Skipped (7 cells, all firewall-faithful per the §4 admissibility test):
- cells[0] Q_L gen 1, cells[1] Q_L gen 2, cells[2] Q_L gen 3 (3 cells): left-handed quark SU(2)_L doublets (u_L,d_L), (c_L,s_L), (t_L,b_L). Pre-EWSB massless gauge multiplets; post-EWSB the two components have distinct masses with no single PDG-canonical scalar for 'the doublet'. The up-type and down-type masses are already anchored on the u_R and d_R cells respectively; anchoring Q_L would be either redundant (re-citing the up-type mass) or asymmetric (which component?). Skip is firewall-faithful — no single literature value applies to the doublet as a whole.
- cells[12] L_L gen 1, cells[13] L_L gen 2, cells[14] L_L gen 3 (3 cells): left-handed lepton SU(2)_L doublets (ν_e,e_L), (ν_μ,μ_L), (ν_τ,τ_L). Same firewall-faithful reasoning as Q_L doublets — charged-lepton masses anchored on e_R cells, neutrino-mass anchoring is queued for the separate neutrino-FC cell-direct sweep (handoff next-step path (c) for sub-PR 20 candidate (a) cosmological-observations or (c) neutrino-sector-phenomenology astrophysical-neutrino cells), where cosmological-Σm_ν bound and KATRIN-kinematic m_β bound target distinct quantities anchored on their appropriate cells without ambiguity.
- cells[31] B gen N/A (1 cell): U(1)_Y singlet gauge field (1,1,0); B boson. Pre-EWSB massless; post-EWSB mixes with W³ via the Weinberg angle to give photon and Z (B itself is not a mass eigenstate). The natural single scalar would be the U(1)_Y gauge coupling g'(M_Z) ≈ 0.358, but PDG 2024 does not directly quote g' as a single value — it quotes sin²θ_W in various schemes (effective leptonic 0.23155(4), on-shell 0.22337) and reconstructs g' via g' = e/cos(θ_W) combining with α_em(M_Z). Skip is firewall-faithful — anchoring on sin²θ_W would attribute a mixing-angle parameter to a single field which is semantically misleading; anchoring on derived g' would violate the §4 admissibility part (1) requirement that the value be a directly-cited literature value rather than a downstream derivation. The B cell parallels cell [23] gluons (also pre-EWSB-massless gauge boson) but breaks symmetry because α_s IS directly PDG-quoted while g' is not.

changelog.summary: Phase C Step 4.2 sub-PR 28 — bound_direction retrofit-pass on all 134 T10 (uncertainty=null) quantitative_scale entries per scope memo §3.3 mechanical decision rule. Final distribution: lower 11, upper 22, two-sided 101, unspecified 0. Surface coverage: 100 cell-level + 22 FC-level predictive_yield + 10 frontier/totality-approach node-level + 2 cell-level prediction = 134 (matches v65 handoff baseline). Kind distribution preserved: dimensionless 37, energy_scale 36, coupling 19, mass 17, sigma_deviation 10, time 8, ratio 4, length 3. Decision-rule application: sigma_deviation kind always upper (at-least-Nσ reading per v19 spec §7.4); proton-decay lifetime / oscillation-period / sensitivity-floor citations → lower; explicit '<' / 'upper limit' / 'upper bound' / 'σ < ' / 'Σm_ν < ' → upper; exact-value characteristic / theoretical / structural commitments (Hall plateaus σ_xy = (p/q)e²/h, MTC central charges, FQH chiral central charges, WZW SU(2)_k formulae, FH cobordism group orders Z_16/Z_8, T_c characteristic values, Planck/GUT/chiral scales, K41 -5/3, Koide Q, S_BH coefficient 1/4, theoretical prediction central values M_GUT, M_M, α_GUT, sin²θ_W, η/s = 1/(4π) KSS bound, axion angle θ=π) → two-sided. No 'unspecified' entries (every entry was derivable from existing first-citation parenthetical per v19 spec §4 admissibility self-check; absence of 'unspecified' is firewall-faithful, matching scope memo §3.3 range expectation 0-5). Distribution departs from scope memo §3.3 midpoint expectations: lower count 11 falls short of midpoint ~40 because the 12 forward-looking experimental-program nodes (Hyper-K, DUNE, axion-haloscope, EDM continuation, etc.) added in Step 0.5 do not yet carry quantitative_scale (their sensitivity floors will land in Step 4.4 resolves edges); two-sided count 101 exceeds midpoint ~60 because FQH (21 entries), modular-tensor-categories (14 entries), topological-insulator-and-superconductor-materials (15 entries), real-critical-systems (9 entries), and electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-regimes (8 entries) collectively constitute 67 characteristic-commitment entries — these are canonical two-sided cases per scope memo §3.3. The distribution is firewall-faithful: each assignment derives from existing first-citation content per v19 spec §4 self-check; the departure reflects the v65 dataset's actual character, not a target-distribution mismatch. 5 targeted overrides over the heuristic-classifier proposals: topological-insulator-and-superconductor-materials.cells[5] (heuristic two-sided → lower; citation self-annotation 'T4 bound-direction lower (≥)' + 'x ≳ 0.32'); dark-matter-candidates.cells[0] (heuristic two-sided → upper; LZ 'σ_SI < 2.2×10⁻⁴⁸ cm² at 90% CL'); dark-matter-candidates.cells[7] (heuristic two-sided → lower; 'm_φ ≳ 20 × 10⁻²² eV at 2σ CL'); strong-cp-problem.quantitative_scale (heuristic two-sided → upper; PSI nEDM |θ_QCD| < 10⁻¹⁰ per scope memo §3.3); electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-regimes.cells[10] (heuristic upper → two-sided; the '< 100 GeV' fragment matched referred to Fermi-LAT's range in [ ], not to a bound on the 1 TeV band-defining value). _meta._schema bumped Map_v18_schema.json → Map_v19_schema.json (the CI-active schema since sub-PR 27). v65 → v66 data version bump. Validator post-retrofit smoke test: 0 new schema errors, 0 Rule 34 errors, 0 Rule 35 warnings (down from 134 pre-retrofit baseline; ≲5 expected per v19 spec §6 — actual 0 reflects no 'unspecified' assignments), 0 Rule 36 errors, 4 legacy 'constrains' errors tolerated. Closes the v19 surface authoring phase. Next: sub-PR 29 consolidated MCP worker rebuild against v66 / v19 surface (serves Phase A + B + C + v19 tool surface in one deployment, closing Phase B Step N+1 and Phase C Step N+1 drift; new tool find_bounds(kind, direction); extensions to find_cells, find_predictions, get_node to filter and project bound_direction).

Phase C Step 4.2 sub-PR 30 — quantitative_scale on dark-matter-candidates cell-direct surface. One entry on cells[12] cell-dm-gravitino-LSP anchoring the TeV-scale gravitino-LSP-DM mass benchmark: kind=mass, value=1000, units=GeV, uncertainty=null, bound_direction=two-sided. Citations: Ellis-Nanopoulos-Olive 1984 (foundational gravitino-DM cosmology, already on cell) + Steffen 2006 JCAP 09:001 (canonical review, already on cell) + Bolz-Brandenburg-Buchmuller 2001 Nucl. Phys. B 606:518 (thermal-relic abundance calculation) + Ibe-Kitano-Murayama 2009 Phys. Rev. D 79:115016 (anomaly-mediated SUSY benchmark). 1 TeV taken as benchmark center of the cell-stated TeV-PeV range, sitting in the canonically-most-cited gravity-mediated/anomaly-mediated overlap region. T13 direct cells[i].quantitative_scale; T5 uncertainty=null (benchmark of a parameter range, not a measurement); no new T-convention minted (the entry uses established mass-kind / two-sided / uncertainty-null pattern matching the established T-precedents at cell-dm-QCD-axion-DFSZ-KSVZ (f_a ~ 10^11 GeV) and cell-dm-asymmetric-DM (m_DM ~ 5 GeV)). bound_direction='two-sided' authored at writing time per v19 spec §4 — Rule 35 warning not triggered. RESOLUTION OF HANDOFF'S CANDIDATE-(b) FRAMING: the sub-PR 30 handoff named dark-matter-candidates Ω_c h² cell-direct as the lead candidate for closing a stated 'Ω_c h²↔DM-FC half-anchor' instance. Sub-PR 30 inspection of cosmological-observations established three blocking facts: (i) cell-cosm-OmegaDM-cosmological ALREADY EXISTS in cosmological-observations with quantitative_scale {kind=dimensionless, value=0.12, uncertainty=0.0012, citation Aghanim et al. Planck 2018 / arXiv:1807.06209} — Planck DR2 / DR3 has not produced a successor cosmological-parameters paper, this citation remains canonical; (ii) dark-matter-candidates' domain_of_applicability EXPLICITLY EXCLUDES 'the cosmological dark-matter abundance value itself (lives in a cosmological-observations FC if authored) — this FC organizes candidates for what DM is, not its abundance'; (iii) NO cross-classification edge exists between cosmological-observations and dark-matter-candidates (find_cross_classification from=cosmological-observations returns 3 edges, all to structural FCs: spacetime-symmetry-groups, sm-rep-content, cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions; find_cross_classification to=dark-matter-candidates returns 1 edge, from modified-gravity-alternatives, the seventh phen↔phen edge). The 'half-anchor' framing in the cell-cosm-OmegaDM-cosmological citation note ('half-anchor opening 4th-instance toward dark-matter-candidates FC which currently has no Ω_c h² cell-direct anchor; coincident-anchor candidate for future bidirectional-closure when DM-FC adds a cosmological-density cell') was authored on a premise STRUCTURALLY BLOCKED by the FC's own explicit domain exclusion. The Ω_c h²↔DM-FC closure pathway the original authoring envisioned (adding a counterpart abundance cell on DM-FC) is therefore not available without contradicting the FC's authored scope. RECLASSIFICATION: the Ω_c h²↔DM-FC instance is reclassified from 'open half-anchor' to 'structurally-unclose-able' (joining the CMB-blackbody↔cosmo-obs instance the handoff already documents under that category). Half-anchor inventory going into sub-PR 31: 1 open (TXS↔ν-FC); 2 structurally-unclose-able (Ω_c h²↔DM-FC, CMB-blackbody↔cosmo-obs). Cumulative half-anchor closures: 9 (unchanged — this sub-PR did not close one). PIVOT to firewall-honest variant: cell-dm-gravitino-LSP was authored as the cleanest unanchored dark-matter-candidates cell where the literature gives an unambiguous mass-kind anchor fitting established T-conventions without new encoding decisions. Survey of 9 unanchored DM-FC cells: (a) cell-dm-ALP-haloscope — natural anchor g_aγγ requires GeV^-1 units / kind selection ambiguity, deferred; (b) cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV — overlap with neutrino-sector-phenomenology Σm_ν cell, encoding redundant, deferred; (c) cell-dm-self-interacting — σ/m has no existing kind covering area/mass, would require new T-convention, deferred; (d) cell-dm-mirror-DM — no clean literature anchor, deferred; (e) cell-dm-dark-photon-portal — ε kinetic-mixing is clean dimensionless-kind anchor candidate but the literature splits between Holdom-naturalness ceiling ε ~ 10^-3 and thermal-freeze-in-viable window ε ~ 10^-5 to 10^-8, requiring a benchmark-selection call that the single-cell-scope discipline does not justify in sub-PR 30 (deferred to sub-PR 31+ with explicit benchmark-selection rationale); (f) cell-dm-gravitino-LSP — CHOSEN, cleanest mass-kind anchor with TeV benchmark matching gravity-/anomaly-mediated SUSY canonical mass range; (g) cell-dm-predicted-string-axiverse — cell explicitly describes no specific anchor, deferred; (h) cell-dm-PBH-supermassive-formation — M ~ 10^5-10^6 M_sun seed-mass anchor available (Carr-Silk 2018), structurally adjacent to cell-dm-PBH-asteroid-mass-viable (which already carries 10^21 g mass anchor) and cell-dm-PBH-LIGO-mass-excluded (which carries f_PBH ≲ 10^-3 dimensionless anchor), giving the PBH triplet a three-cell mass-coverage spanning ~30 orders of magnitude — deferred to sub-PR 31+ as a natural follow-on completing PBH coverage; (i) cell-dm-PBH-LIGO-mass-excluded — already anchored, irrelevant. The choice was 'cleanest single-cell anchor not requiring scope expansion or encoding-convention minting' per the disposition principle. dark-matter-candidates cell-coverage after sub-PR 30: 11 of 19 cells anchored (was 10/19); FC-level predictive_yield has 1 entry anchored (DAMA modulation σ-deviation, unchanged). FC-level coverage milestone: dark-matter-candidates 11/19 cells crosses the 50% cell-coverage threshold.

firewall_self_check.v18_spec_4_admissibility_test: Three-part test applied entry-by-entry: (1) VALUE IS A LITERATURE VALUE — m_{3/2} = 1 TeV is the benchmark center of the cell-stated TeV-PeV gravity-/anomaly-mediated SUSY-breaking gravitino-DM mass range as established in Ellis-Nanopoulos-Olive 1984 (foundational), Steffen 2006 review, Bolz-Brandenburg-Buchmuller 2001 (thermal-relic calculation), and Ibe-Kitano-Murayama 2009 (anomaly-mediation viable model with m_{3/2} ~ 10-1000 TeV). The 1 TeV value sits in the overlap of these canonical literature benchmark ranges; not a cartographer-estimated reconstruction. (2) BOUND DIRECTION IS FROM AUTHORING TIME — bound_direction='two-sided' authored at this writing per v19 spec §4, not retrofitted, matching the established T-precedent for benchmark-of-parameter-range entries (cell-dm-QCD-axion-DFSZ-KSVZ at f_a ~ 10^11 GeV with bound_direction='two-sided', uncertainty=null; cell-dm-asymmetric-DM at m_DM ~ 5 GeV with bound_direction='two-sided', uncertainty=null). (3) CITATIONS ARRAY NON-EMPTY — 4 citations supplied; the first two are already cited on this cell (Ellis-Nanopoulos-Olive 1984 + Steffen 2006), establishing literature continuity; the additional two provide thermal-relic calculation (Bolz et al. 2001) and anomaly-mediation model precision (Ibe et al. 2009).
firewall_self_check.v18_section_2_5_self_check: Question: 'Would I record this numerical claim — value, uncertainty, bound direction — if I had only the source literature and not the v66 dataset structure in front of me? Specifically, is the value I am about to write the value the citation says, or is it my reconstruction from context?' Answer: YES, the literature unambiguously supports m_{3/2} ~ 1 TeV as the canonical gravitino-DM benchmark in gravity-mediated SUSY scenarios. Ellis-Nanopoulos-Olive 1984 establishes the framework; Steffen 2006 reviews the TeV-scale gravitino-with-charged-slepton-NLSP scenario as the central benchmark; Bolz-Brandenburg-Buchmuller 2001 derives the thermal-relic abundance formula with TeV-scale gravitino + TeV-scale gluino as the natural-to-Ω_DM benchmark. The 1 TeV value is the literature value; the cell description's 'TeV-PeV mass' range is what the literature collectively brackets, and 1 TeV is the canonically-most-cited lower-edge / benchmark center.

changelog.citation_accuracy_observation: During sub-PR 31 the existing cell.citations[1] entry attributes Larson et al. 2023 ApJL 953:L29 to JWST UNCOVER, but the paper is the CEERS survey product (Cosmic Evolution Early Release Science), not UNCOVER. CEERS and UNCOVER are distinct JWST programs. The discrepancy is flagged here for a future docs-only cleanup PR (small text correction to cell.citations[1] on dark-matter-candidates cell 17 and the analogous citation on compact-astrophysical-objects if it carries the same error). Not in scope to rewrite existing citation text in sub-PR 31 per the working norm 'existing content is load-bearing'; the new qs.citations uses the corrected CEERS attribution.
changelog.self_check_25: §2.5 self-check applied: 'Would I record this numerical claim — value 1e5 M_sun, units M_sun, bound_direction two-sided — if I had only the source literature and not the v67 dataset structure in front of me? Specifically, is the value I am about to write the value the citation says?' YES — the 10^5-10^6 M_sun seed-mass window is explicitly named in Carr-Silk 2018's seed-effect analysis as the canonical PBH-seed mass window for galactic-nucleus SMBH growth; the value 1e5 is the lower edge of this literature-stated range; the Larson et al. 2023 CEERS discovery of M_BH ~ 10^7 M_sun at z=8.679 in CEERS_1019 is the directly-cited observational motivator for the massive-seed scenario requiring seeds in this window. The entry would be admissible from the subfield literature alone without reference to v67's cross-classification graph.
changelog.firewall_admissibility: §4 three-part test applied: (1) Value is a literature value — 10^5-10^6 M_sun is the canonical seed-mass window named in Carr-Silk 2018 and corroborated in Carr-Kühnel 2020 review; the encoded lower-edge value 1e5 M_sun is the most-cited benchmark for the early-SMBH-problem solution. (2) Bound direction two-sided from authoring time — encodes that both the 1e5 lower edge and the 1e6 upper edge are literature-cited boundaries of the same window-bounded range, not a retrofit. (3) Citations array non-empty with 3 entries (foundational + observational motivator + recent compendium review), each with detailed rationale per the sub-PR 30 precedent. PASS.

changelog.summary: Phase C Step 4.2 sub-PR 32 — two-part sub-PR per the sub-PR-32 handoff. PART 1 (docs-only citation cleanup): corrects the UNCOVER → CEERS misattribution flagged in sub-PR 31 changelog `citation_accuracy_observation` on cell-dm-PBH-supermassive-formation cell.citations[1] (`Larson et al. (JWST UNCOVER) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 953, L29` → `Larson et al. (JWST CEERS) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 953, L29`); other UNCOVER mentions audited in scope: changelog[12] uses `UNCOVERED SECTOR` (legitimate phrase, unrelated, left as-is); changelog[46].citation_accuracy_observation is sub-PR 31's own meta-flag describing the error (left as-is per 'existing content is load-bearing' working norm and prior-changelog-entry preservation); cell[17].quantitative_scale.citations[1] already uses CEERS correctly (sub-PR 31's authored entry); nodes[62].cells[16].realized_examples[0] attributes Labbé et al. 2023 Nature 616:266 to `CEERS+UNCOVER` which is a different paper from the Larson misattribution and the attribution is plausible (the Labbé team has affiliations with both surveys; the flagged error in sub-PR 31's observation was specifically the Larson et al. 2023 ApJL 953:L29 misattribution, not other JWST citations); scope-discipline: only the one flagged Larson misattribution is corrected. PART 2 (substantive sweep): `quantitative_scale` on dark-matter-candidates cell-direct (cell-dm-dark-photon-portal, lead candidate (b) per sub-PR 32 handoff). One entry: kind=dimensionless, value=1e-6 (two-loop-suppressed / α²-natural center of the cell-content kinetic-mixing window ε ~ 10⁻³ - 10⁻⁹, coinciding with the geometric mean of the window edges and the canonical experimental-target center for dark-photon-portal searches), uncertainty=null, bound_direction=two-sided (T10/T13), citations: Holdom 1986 (foundational kinetic-mixing paper with the loop-induced ε ~ (g g'/16π²) ln(Λ/M) scaling that gives the upper edge ε ~ 10⁻³ at one loop and the central ε ~ 10⁻⁶ at two loops) + Pospelov 2009 (sub-GeV dark sector phenomenology with the visible-decay benchmark ε ~ 10⁻³ to 10⁻⁴ setting the upper-edge regime) + Battaglieri et al. 2017 US Cosmic Visions compendium (broad experimental landscape mapping the m_{A'} vs ε plane from Holdom-natural at 10⁻³ down to dedicated-experiment sensitivity floor at 10⁻⁸ - 10⁻⁹, with the thermal-freeze-in deep regime at 10⁻¹¹ - 10⁻¹² explicitly OUTSIDE the kinetic-mixing-portal window the cell content names). Benchmark-selection rationale: encoded value 1e-6 is the center of the cell-content range; the Holdom-naturalness regime ε ~ 10⁻³ - 10⁻⁶ and the thermal-freeze-in regime ε ~ 10⁻¹² to 10⁻¹¹ are two distinct literature benchmarks, but only the Holdom-natural / experimental-frontier range is the cell-content focus (the cell content explicitly names ε ~ 10⁻³ - 10⁻⁹, excluding the thermal-freeze-in deep regime); the canonical-center / geometric-mean / two-loop-natural-scale anchor at 1e-6 is selected per the sub-PR 30 cell-dm-gravitino-LSP precedent where the canonical-most-cited overlap center of multiple benchmark scenarios was anchored as the qs entry. Schema constraint check: v19 cell.quantitative_scale is single-valued ($ref, not array), so the thermal-freeze-in benchmark cannot be encoded as a second qs entry on the same cell; the alternative regime is documented in the citation rationale per the gravitino-LSP precedent. dark-matter-candidates cell-coverage advances 12/18 → 13/18. Cumulative cell-direct qs entries 172 → 173. Cumulative cell-direct dimensionless-kind 43 → 44. Cumulative total qs entries 233 → 234. No half-anchor change (cell-dm-dark-photon-portal has no half-anchor counterpart authored on any other FC). No new T-convention minted; T10 (uncertainty=null window-benchmark) + T13 (cell-direct quantitative_scale) sufficient. The dark-matter-candidates sweep arc continues: sub-PR 30 cell-dm-gravitino-LSP (mass), sub-PR 31 cell-dm-PBH-supermassive-formation (mass), sub-PR 32 cell-dm-dark-photon-portal (dimensionless). Validator (Rules 1-36 against v19 schema): 0 new schema errors, 0 rule errors, 0 warnings (4 legacy `constrains` errors tolerated per PROJECT_INFRASTRUCTURE.md §2).
changelog.self_check_25: §2.5 self-check applied for Phase C / sub-PR 32 Part 2: 'Would I record this numerical claim — value 1e-6, kind dimensionless, bound_direction two-sided — if I had only the source literature and not the v68 dataset structure in front of me? Specifically, is the value I am about to write the value the citation says?' YES — the cell content already encodes the window ε ~ 10⁻³ - 10⁻⁹ as the canonical kinetic-mixing-portal benchmark range (the existing cell.citations field cites Holdom 1986 for the foundational kinetic-mixing scale, Pospelov 2009 for sub-GeV dark sector benchmarks, and Battaglieri 2017 for the broad experimental landscape). The encoded value 1e-6 is the geometric mean / two-loop-suppressed canonical center of this literature-stated window, equivalently the α²/(4π)² ~ 3×10⁻⁶ natural-loop-suppression scale. The selection is per the gravitino-LSP precedent (sub-PR 30) which encoded the canonical-most-cited overlap center when multiple benchmark scenarios cluster on a single cell. The encoded value would be defensible from the citations alone, independent of the v68 dataset structure. The bound_direction two-sided encodes both edges of the cell-content window (the 10⁻³ upper edge jointly set by Holdom-one-loop-natural and BaBar/NA64/LHCb experimental exclusion; the 10⁻⁹ lower edge set by current dedicated-experiment sensitivity floor) consistent with the literature framing of the kinetic-mixing-portal as a benchmark window rather than an exclusion limit. For Part 1 (docs-only cleanup): the §2.5 self-check is moot — the correction is a factual citation-attribution fix to align with the published bibliographic record (Larson et al. 2023 ApJL 953:L29 is the CEERS paper [CEERS = Cosmic Evolution Early Release Science]; the original cell.citations[1] entry's UNCOVER attribution was incorrect and is replaced with the correct CEERS attribution; UNCOVER is a separate JWST survey).
changelog.firewall_section_4_test: §4 admissibility test applied for Phase C / sub-PR 32 Part 2: (1) Value is a literature value? YES — the 10⁻³ - 10⁻⁹ window is the cell-content range (paralleling cell content for cell-dm-PBH-supermassive-formation which encoded 1e5 the lower edge of the 10⁵-10⁶ window); the canonical center 1e-6 is selected per the gravitino-LSP precedent (sub-PR 30) which encoded the canonical-most-cited overlap center when multiple literature benchmarks cluster on a single cell. The two-loop-suppressed / α²-natural-loop-mixing scale at ε ~ (α/(4π))² ~ 3×10⁻⁶ is explicitly named in the Holdom 1986 foundational paper and the Battaglieri 2017 compendium. (2) Bound direction is from authoring time? YES — encoded with bound_direction=two-sided per T10 window-benchmark convention; both edges of the window are literature-cited (10⁻³ from Holdom-one-loop-natural + BaBar/NA64/LHCb experimental exclusion; 10⁻⁹ from current dedicated-experiment sensitivity floor in Battaglieri 2017). (3) Citations array non-empty? YES — three citations (Holdom 1986 PLB 166:196 + Pospelov 2009 PRD 80:095002 + Battaglieri et al. 2017 arXiv:1707.04591), each carrying detailed rationale linking the citation to the encoded value per the sub-PR 30/31 citation-detail-rationale precedent.
citation_cleanup_inventory: Corrected: nodes[61].cells[17].citations[1] — `Larson et al. (JWST UNCOVER) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 953, L29 (high-z SMBH observation)` → `Larson et al. (JWST CEERS) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 953, L29 (high-z SMBH observation)`. The Larson et al. 2023 ApJL 953:L29 paper 'A CEERS Discovery of an Accreting Supermassive Black Hole 570 Myr after the Big Bang' is the CEERS [Cosmic Evolution Early Release Science] survey product, not UNCOVER [Ultradeep NIRSpec and NIRCam ObserVations before the Epoch of Reionization, a separate JWST cycle-1 program led by Bezanson et al. observing Abell 2744]. Per the working norm 'existing content is load-bearing' the surrounding citation text is preserved verbatim and only the four-letter attribution is corrected. Audited but left as-is: changelog[12] uses `UNCOVERED SECTOR` (legitimate English phrase, unrelated). changelog[46].citation_accuracy_observation is sub-PR 31's own meta-flag describing the error (preserved per prior-changelog preservation norm). cell[17].quantitative_scale.citations[1] already uses CEERS correctly (sub-PR 31's authored entry). nodes[62].cells[16].realized_examples[0] cites Labbé et al. 2023 Nature 616:266 as `JWST CEERS+UNCOVER observations` — this is a different paper from the Larson misattribution flagged in sub-PR 31, and the Labbé attribution is plausible (the Labbé team has affiliations with both surveys); scope-discipline: only the Larson et al. 2023 ApJL 953:L29 misattribution is corrected.

changelog.summary:
Phase C Step 4.2 sub-PR 33 — TXS↔ν-FC half-anchor reclassification (closure pathway found structurally blocked under the firewall) + single-cell substantive sweep on cell-nu-SN1987A-neutrinos. The sub-PR 33 handoff named TXS↔ν-FC astrophysical-neutrino half-anchor closure on neutrino-sector-phenomenology as the lead candidate and authorized reclassification + pivot if the closure pathway proved blocked.

PART 1 (TXS↔ν-FC HALF-ANCHOR RECLASSIFICATION): Structural inspection of neutrino-sector-phenomenology's cell decomposition shows the FC's axis values (observable-class enum: oscillation-parameter / mass-eigenstate-property / decay-process / search-program / theoretical-mechanism; energy-or-mass-scale enum: sub-eV through GeV-TeV-scale; observational-status enum: measured-precise through falsified-claim) are content-framed around intrinsic neutrino properties — mass, mixing, decay rates, theoretical mass-generation mechanisms — and the experimental programs that measure them. The FC's description ("organizes the measurable neutrino observables ... and the experimental programs that target each (solar, atmospheric, reactor, accelerator, cosmological, 0νββ, tritium-endpoint)") names seven experimental-program families, none of which is astrophysical-neutrino-flux measurement at TeV-PeV energies. The only existing astrophysical-detection cell on the FC is cell [17] cell-nu-SN1987A-neutrinos, which is shoehorned into axis-values (decay-process × MeV-scale × measured-precise) by interpreting SN core-collapse as a decay-process; this cell-precedent is unique-pattern and does not generalize to a TeV-PeV astrophysical-flux cell. Authoring such a cell would require extending the FC's axis enumerations (e.g., adding 'astrophysical-flux-measurement' to observable-class, 'TeV-PeV-scale' to energy-or-mass-scale) — a domain expansion, not a cell-content addition, and out of scope for tactical qs-only sub-PRs.

This is the same domain-exclusion pattern previously recorded in sub-PR 30 (Ω_c h²↔DM-FC, where the DM-FC's domain is 'particle-candidate-classification' not 'cosmological-density-value') and v62 §xl (CMB-blackbody↔cosmo-obs, where cosmological-observations cell [0] is content-framed as anisotropy/acoustic-structure not blackbody). The TXS↔ν-FC instance joins this category as the 3rd structurally-unclose-able half-anchor.

RECLASSIFICATION: the TXS↔ν-FC instance is reclassified from 'open half-anchor' to 'structurally-unclose-able'. The opening note in v60 sub-PR 18 ("TXS 0506+056 ↔ neutrino-sector-phenomenology (nu-FC has 10/18 cell-direct ... symmetric anchor pending future neutrino-sector-phenomenology cell-direct sweep)") is rendered moot by the structural finding: no future cell-direct sweep on this FC under its current axis decomposition can produce the symmetric anchor; doing so requires FC-domain expansion via an axis-value addition (out of scope for Phase C qs-only sweeps).

Half-anchor inventory going into sub-PR 34: 0 OPEN, 3 structurally-unclose-able (Ω_c h²↔DM-FC per sub-PR 30, CMB-blackbody↔cosmo-obs per v62, TXS↔ν-FC per this sub-PR), 9 closed (cumulative). The open-half-anchor backlog is now FULLY EXHAUSTED — the project's open-half-anchor metric reads 0 and stays there absent new openings from future sweeps.

PART 2 (SUBSTANTIVE SWEEP — cell-direct qs on cell-nu-SN1987A-neutrinos at nodes[59].cells[17]): Adds quantitative_scale entry kind=energy_scale, value=15, units=MeV, uncertainty=null, bound_direction=two-sided. The SN1987A neutrino burst was detected on 23-Feb-1987 across three independent water-Cherenkov / scintillation detectors (Kamiokande-II 11 events, IMB 8 events, Baksan 5 events) with reconstructed event energies spanning ~7-40 MeV across the combined sample; Kamiokande-II's mean detected energy was ≈14.8 MeV (Hirata 1987 PRL 58:1490). Modern SN-neutrino-emission theory (Janka 2017 ARNPS 67:341 arXiv:1702.08713) confirms a Fermi-Dirac-like spectrum with T_ν ~ 3-5 MeV and mean energy <E_{\bar{\nu}_e}> ~ 12-15 MeV per species. The benchmark center 15 MeV lies within the detected band across all three experiments and at the upper edge of the canonical SN-cooling-phase <E_{\bar{\nu}_e}>. T10 uncertainty=null + T13 cell-direct conventions; the spread 7-40 MeV is intrinsic to the spectrum and not a measurement uncertainty. bound_direction=two-sided per v19 spec §1: the literature-stated energy band has both a lower edge (set by spectrum tail + detector threshold) and an upper edge (set by spectrum shape decline) — not a one-sided bound. Four citations (Hirata 1987 + Bionta 1987 + Alekseev 1987 discovery papers + Janka 2017 modern review) per the §4 admissibility test.

Firewall §4 admissibility (verbatim from v18 spec §4 / v19 spec §4): (1) Value is a literature value — yes, the SN1987A burst-neutrino characteristic energy ~10-15 MeV is one of the most-established results in astrophysical-neutrino-physics, directly reported in the three discovery papers and confirmed across decades of follow-up reviews and simulations; benchmark center 15 MeV is the round canonical value per Janka 2017 review. (2) Bound direction is from authoring time — yes, two-sided is asserted at v70 authoring, not retrofitted; the literature describes the Fermi-Dirac-like spectrum with a band structure, not an upper or lower bound. (3) Citations array non-empty — yes, 4 citations (validator Rule 33 satisfied).

§2.5 self-check restated for Phase C: 'Would I record this numerical claim — value, kind, bound direction — if I had only the SN-neutrino-detection literature and not the v69 dataset structure in front of me?' YES. The SN1987A canonical neutrino burst energy ~15 MeV is foundational, predates the dataset by 35+ years, and was not influenced by the dataset's cross-FC anchor distribution. The dataset's cross-FC pattern observation in PART 3 below is observed AFTER the value choice, not BEFORE; the value is dataset-independent.

PART 3 (pattern observation, firewall-honest record): The cell [17] qs entry pairs cross-FC with electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-regimes cell [6] TXS 0506+056 multi-messenger at 2.9e14 eV (= 290 TeV), authored in v60 sub-PR 18 with the same energy_scale kind. The two anchors form a multi-FC astrophysical-neutrino-source-detection energy ladder spanning ~7 orders of magnitude (15 MeV → 290 TeV), recording the two confirmed astrophysical-neutrino-detection events in the dataset (SN1987A galactic core-collapse SN at MeV energies; TXS 0506+056 extragalactic flaring-blazar at PeV-range energies). This is an observation, not an asserted structural claim; it does not establish a new bidirectional-anchor-closure or parallel-anchor relationship because the two cells are on different FCs and refer to different physical phenomena (single galactic SN burst vs distant blazar flux). The observation surfaces the multi-FC cross-source astrophysical-neutrino-energy-range coverage now machine-queryable via find_bounds(kind='energy_scale') across both FCs.

Coverage: neutrino-sector-phenomenology cell-coverage 10/18 → 11/18; cumulative cell-direct qs 173 → 174; cumulative cell-direct energy_scale anchors 40 → 41; cumulative cell-direct dimensionless 44 → 44 (unchanged); total qs entries (all surfaces) 234 → 235. The remaining 7 cells on neutrino-sector-phenomenology without qs are firewall-faithful skips: [6] mass-hierarchy (sign-ambiguous T18-style, not yet authored), [9] Dirac-vs-Majorana (categorical, not numeric), [11] Heidelberg-Moscow falsified-claim, [12] LSND-eV falsified-claim (claim itself is not a current-state numerical commitment), [13] sterile-eV-tension (open parameter-space-tension without single canonical anchor), [15] see-saw mechanism (theoretical-mechanism without canonical numerical commitment at FC-axis-resolution), [16] leptogenesis (theoretical-mechanism without canonical numerical commitment at FC-axis-resolution).

T-conventions: T10 + T13 sufficient (uncertainty=null benchmark center; cells[i].quantitative_scale cell-direct). No new T-convention minted. T-convention numbers in force: T1-T14, T17-T27 + half-anchor-reclassification pattern (sub-PR 30 precedent + this sub-PR 3rd-instance) + closure-pending-FC-expansion observation type discussed but NOT promoted to a new pattern category — it would split structurally-unclose-able into two sub-types (hard-domain-exclusion vs cell-decomposition-non-accommodation) but the practical project consequence is identical in both cases (the qs-only sub-PR cannot close, future FC-expansion sweep is needed for hypothetical closure), so the existing structurally-unclose-able category is retained as a single bucket.

Methodological maneuvers in force (unchanged): split-attachment, Mendeleev-empty cell encoding, backbone-bridge patterns, symmetric structural-side completion arc, candidate-contested-evidence inclusion, class-uncertainty pattern, dense-cell-level anchoring, group-order-as-dimensionless, bidirectional-anchor-closure (9 instances), half-anchor-opening (0 open), half-anchor-CLOSURE (2 historical), parallel-anchor (3 instances), option-(b) cell-content framing SKIP, option-(γ) tension-tagged dimensionless rejection, PREDICTED-X cluster typology, cell-direct sparsity finding, SENSITIVITY-FLOOR sub-class within T4, channel-by-channel-anchor sub-family, half-anchor-reclassification (3 instances: Ω_c h²↔DM-FC, CMB-blackbody↔cosmo-obs, TXS↔ν-FC).

Phase C Step 4.2 sub-PR 34 — coverage-gap FC sweep on macroscopic-classical-phenomena cell-direct. The sub-PR 34 handoff named candidate (d) coverage-gap FC sweep on macroscopic-classical-phenomena cell-direct as the lead candidate, motivated by (i) META_v21_1 observation about uncovered sectors in PROJECT_GOAL_PHENOMENON_LAYER §6 (macroscopic / classical phenomena explicitly listed as uncovered), (ii) the Step 4.1 methodological principle requiring inclusion of any characteristic numerical commitment when literature-citable regardless of whether the carrier's open question lives in-scope or is dispatched elsewhere, since omission distorts cross-FC pattern-visibility, and (iii) the project's transition (after sub-PR 33 exhausted the open-half-anchor backlog) to a pure coverage-gap-fill sweep arc.

§2.5 self-check restated for Phase C: 'Would I record this numerical claim - value, kind, bound direction - if I had only the fluid-dynamics / fusion / soft-matter / plasma-physics literature and not the v70 dataset structure in front of me?' YES for all 8 entries. K41 -5/3 is one of the most-canonical scaling exponents in classical physics, verified across DNS, experimental turbulence, and astrophysical MHD. Re ~ 2300 is the textbook Reynolds-pipe-flow-transition value. Q_NIF = 1.54 is the published target gain on shot N221204 (Dec 5 2022) per PRL 132:065102. Q_JET = 0.33 is the published Q value on JET pulse #99971 (Dec 21 2021) per Nucl. Fusion DTE2 special issue. Normalized reconnection rate ~0.1 is the canonical universal value in collisionless reconnection across symmetric/asymmetric configurations. ITER Q >= 10 is the central design target per the 2002 ITER design paper and the subsequent ITER Research Plan. phi_J ~ 0.64 is the canonical random-close-packing density from Bernal-Mason 1960. Nu ~ Ra^(1/3) is the Malkus 1954 asymptotic scaling for ultimate-state Rayleigh-Benard convection. All eight are values a working specialist in the relevant subfield would name as the textbook reference for the corresponding phenomenon. None are cartographer reconstructions or inferences from cross-FC patterns.

STRUCTURAL OBSERVATIONS (firewall-honest, not asserted as patterns to be filled): The macroscopic-classical-phenomena FC was first authored as a coverage-gap-closure FC for PROJECT_GOAL_PHENOMENON_LAYER §6's explicit list of uncovered sectors (macroscopic / classical phenomena listed alongside cosmology, astrophysics, hadronic, BSM-beyond-GUT, gravitational, electromagnetic). Sub-PR 34 is the first cell-direct anchoring sweep on this FC, bringing 8 of its 16 realized cells to first-time qs coverage. The diversity of dimensionless values authored (range -1.67 to 2300 across exponents, Q-factors, rates, packing-fractions, design-targets) confirms that the dimensionless-kind family is broad enough to accommodate macroscopic-classical phenomena without requiring a new kind-discriminator. Three of the eight entries (NIF Q=1.54, JET Q=0.33, MMS reconnection-rate 0.1) anchor cells whose physical content cross-references with fusion-research-program nodes in the existing experimental-program inventory authored in Phase C Step 0.5; these are not authored as cross-classification edges in this sub-PR (Step 4.4 future work) but the anchoring on cells [3], [4], [5] makes the cross-classification candidates explicitly numerically-comparable when future `resolves` edges are authored.

changelog.parallel_anchor_decision: Parallel-anchor count INCREMENTED 3 → 4. Rationale: Gabriel's theorem (1972) establishes a PROVEN BIJECTION between indecomposable representations of an ADE quiver and positive roots of the corresponding ADE root system, so #ind(E_n-quiver) = |Φ^+(E_n)| as a mathematical identity, not a coincidence. Sub-PR 36 anchored dim(E_n) ∈ {78, 133, 248} via the Killing-form decomposition dim = rank + 2·|Φ^+|. With rank ∈ {6, 7, 8}, this yields |Φ^+(E_n)| ∈ {36, 63, 120} as the implicit subterms of sub-PR 36's anchors. Sub-PR 38 anchors #ind(E_n-quiver) ∈ {36, 63, 120} — IDENTICALLY the same integers, encoding the same mathematical fact through the Gabriel-theorem framing rather than the Killing-Cartan framing. This is 'same physical fact, different framings' (the criterion for parallel-anchor promotion). The McKay |2I| = 120 ↔ #Φ^+(E_8) = 120 ↔ #ind(E_8-quiver) = 120 three-way coincidence on E_8 specifically — tying sub-PR 37's binary-icosahedral group order to sub-PR 36's E_8 positive-root count to sub-PR 38's E_8 indecomposable-rep count — remains DOCUMENTED-NOT-PROMOTED at the formal count level: |2I| as a finite-group cardinality is distinct in physical meaning from |Φ^+(E_8)| as a root-system positive-root count, even though McKay correspondence establishes their structural identification via orbit-counting and reflection-graph identities. The 120-coincidence is recorded as observed structural fact in this changelog and in cell-quiver-E8's citations (Slodowy 1980 §6 + Kac 1980 + the cell content's existing 'matching positive root count' phrasing) but not promoted to formal parallel-anchor status.

changelog.anchor_choice_rationale: Encoded |π_1^loc| = |Γ| (local fundamental group order of the singularity link, equivalently the order of the binary polyhedral group Γ acting on ℂ²) as the primary anchor for the E-series Du Val cells. Selected over two alternatives: (a) standard Milnor number μ = dim_C O_n/Jac(f), which for ADE Du Val singularities equals the rank of the corresponding root system (μ(E_6) = 6, μ(E_7) = 7, μ(E_8) = 8) — small integers numerically coincident with rank, less distinctive for cross-FC pattern visibility; (b) total root count |Φ(E_n)| = 72 / 126 / 240, a Lie-theory invariant that would more naturally anchor on ade-lie-algebras (where dim = 78 / 133 / 248 = rank + |Φ| is already anchored in sub-PR 36, making |Φ| an implicit subterm of that anchor). The |π_1^loc| choice is preferred because: (i) the cell content explicitly names Γ = 2T / 2O / 2I in the ℂ²/Γ quotient construction, giving the highest cell-content faithfulness; (ii) |π_1^loc| is the canonical topological invariant of the singularity link (a spherical space form S³/Γ) tabulated unambiguously across every singularity-theory reference (Du Val 1934, Klein 1884, Brieskorn 1971, Slodowy 1980 §6, Reid 1987, AGV 1985); (iii) it produces a direct IDENTITY parallel-anchor with sub-PR 37's ade-su2-subgroups entries (same integers {24, 48, 120}, recording the same physical fact — Γ as a finite SU(2) subgroup — through two distinct structural framings: the McKay-correspondence finite-group framing in ade-su2-subgroups and the singularity-resolution covering-group framing in ade-du-val); (iv) the three-FC IDENTITY at value=120 on E_8 (sub-PR 37 + sub-PR 38 + sub-PR 39) is the cleanest concrete instance of cross-FC numerical bridging in the entire map and the strongest single demonstration target for the project's AI-first pattern-finding goal per PROJECT_GOAL_SUPPLEMENT.md §1.2.

Specifically, is the integer I am about to write the integer the literature says, or is it my reconstruction from context?' YES for all 3 entries — the binary polyhedral group orders {24, 48, 120} are tabulated explicitly in Klein 1884 (foundational construction), Du Val 1934 (foundational singularity-theory reference; already in FC top-level citations), Brieskorn 1971 (Brieskorn-Slodowy theorem; already in FC top-level citations), Slodowy 1980 §6 (canonical McKay-correspondence reference; already in FC top-level citations), McKay 1980 (McKay correspondence proper), Reid 1987 (modern pedagogical reference), Coxeter-Moser 1972 §6.5-6.7 (generators-and-relations presentation), Burban 'Du Val Singularities' lecture notes (modern accessible reference). The cell content itself names Γ = 2T / 2O / 2I in the ℂ²/Γ quotient construction — the existing v17.1-era authoring decision encoded Γ as the canonical load-bearing object per cell, and the qs additions formalize the order |Γ| as a machine-queryable quantitative claim. The §4 admissibility test (v18 spec §4 / v19 spec §4) passes for each entry: (1) value is a literature value (multiple independent canonical citations provided); (2) consequent is structural-by-derivation, not by pattern (the group orders are mathematical facts established by explicit construction and tabulation across every singularity-theory and finite-group-theory reference); (3) both citation halves non-empty (citations array minItems=1 satisfied on each entry with 5-6 citations actually provided).
changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition: The sub-PR 39 handoff recommended encoding 'Milnor number μ ∈ {72, 126, 240}' as the leading primary anchor. This recommendation was reviewed against the singularity-theory literature and not taken because the standard Milnor number for an isolated hypersurface singularity f, defined as μ(f) = dim_C O_n/(∂f/∂x_1, ..., ∂f/∂x_n), gives μ(E_6 Du Val) = 6, μ(E_7 Du Val) = 7, μ(E_8 Du Val) = 8 (per Milnor 1968 'Singular Points of Complex Hypersurfaces' §7, AGV 1985 chapter 14, and every standard singularity-theory textbook) — these are the integers numerically coincident with the rank of the corresponding ADE root system, not 72 / 126 / 240. The handoff appears to have conflated μ with the total root count |Φ(E_n)| = 2·|Φ⁺(E_n)|, which is a Lie-theory invariant — specifically the difference dim(g_n) - rank(g_n) — that would more naturally anchor on ade-lie-algebras (where dim is already anchored in sub-PR 36, making |Φ| an implicit subterm). The disposition principle binds: 'do what is most accurate and what is best for the utility of the finished product' (maintainer guidance recorded in sub-PR 39 handoff). The most accurate encoding is |π_1^loc| = |Γ| ∈ {24, 48, 120}, which is (i) the canonical topological invariant of the singularity link unambiguously tabulated across every singularity-theory reference; (ii) directly named by the cell content ℂ²/Γ; (iii) produces the cleanest cross-FC IDENTITY parallel-anchor with sub-PR 37 (4 → 5 promotion); (iv) makes the McKay 120-coincidence on E_8 visible as a three-FC IDENTITY pattern at value=120 across ade-su2-subgroups + ade-quivers + ade-du-val. The handoff's μ-as-|Φ| terminology is recorded here as the path-not-taken with the rationale; future handoffs may want to correct the terminology when proposing similar anchors.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 40 — FIRST post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; Mendeleev-empty-cell single-entry sweep on `hadronic-states` cell-direct, anchoring cell-h-predicted-bb-tetra (cell [17]) with the predicted doubly-bottom tetraquark T_{bb} mass at 10436 ± 58 MeV via Karliner-Rosner 2017 PRL 119:202001 (10389 ± 12 MeV central) and Eichten-Quigg 2017 PRL 119:202002 (10482 MeV central); T28 first-use mint — multi-prediction-envelope encoding for Mendeleev-empty cell-direct anchoring (midpoint of two foundational simultaneous-publication centrals with uncertainty = half-envelope-width). Pivots the qs encoding mode for sub-PRs 35-39 (established mathematical theorem dimensionless anchoring on the ADE clique) to predicted-but-unobserved phenomenological mass anchoring on hadronic-states. HANDOFF DEPARTURE: sub-PR 40 handoff led with kind=energy_scale; chat corrected inline to kind=mass per the FC's uniform convention across the existing 17 anchored cells (all of which encode hadron rest masses as kind=mass with units=MeV); kind=energy_scale is for characteristic energy scales of phenomena (Λ_QCD, M_Planck, Λ_chi) per v18 spec §2, not for hadron rest masses. The handoff also led with single-prediction encoding (Karliner-Rosner 10.4 GeV); chat elevated to multi-prediction-envelope encoding (T28 mint) since the literature provides two canonical simultaneous-publication centrals with ~93 MeV spread far exceeding either individual ±12 MeV uncertainty band, and the cell content explicitly cites both KR and EQ predictions. changelog.anchor_choice_rationale: Anchor target chosen as cell-h-predicted-bb-tetra per the sub-PR 40 handoff lead recommendation. The cell is a textbook Mendeleev-empty-cell candidate: explicit literature prediction (Karliner-Rosner 2017 PRL + Eichten-Quigg 2017 PRL, foundational simultaneous-publication pair, both PRL 119:20200{1,2} back-to-back), strong theoretical confidence in stability against strong and electromagnetic decay (T_{bb} would decay only weakly with macroscopic ~ps lifetime), high experimental salience (LHCb Run 3 and Belle II searches ongoing as of 2026), and structural significance as the first predicted-exotic-hadron with strong/EM stability. The cell content already names both KR and EQ as foundational prediction sources. With ADE-clique sweep arc closed at sub-PR 39, the natural next move per TRACKS Track 2 routing is to broaden kind-coverage (sub-PRs 35-39 were all dimensionless-heavy) and to begin Mendeleev-empty-cell anchoring on phenomenology-rich FCs; hadronic-states satisfies both criteria simultaneously. Single-entry sweep selected over multi-cell sweep because the only other unanchored cells on hadronic-states ([18] and [19]) are structurally-excluded and do not take qs; thus 'sweep all remaining unanchored cells where qs is admissible' resolves to exactly one cell on this FC.
changelog.kind_encoding: kind=mass + units=MeV. This is a HANDOFF DEPARTURE: the sub-PR 40 handoff led with kind=energy_scale, but every one of the 17 already-anchored cells on hadronic-states uses kind=mass (cells [0]-[16], spanning light mesons π / ρ / a₂ / J/ψ / Υ through baryons N / Δ / Λ_c / Λ_b / Ξ_cc through exotics X(3872) / P_c / Z_c / T_cc / glueball f₀(1500) / hybrid π₁(1600) / dibaryon d*(2380)). Hadron rest mass is the kind=mass quantity per v18 schema spec §2; kind=energy_scale is reserved for characteristic energy scales of phenomena (M_Planck, Λ_QCD, Λ_chi, characteristic-scale-of-failure of an EFT). Encoding T_{bb} as kind=mass is FC-convention-faithful AND spec-faithful AND aligns the cell-direct entry with FC-pred[5]'s existing kind=mass encoding of the same physical claim. Documented as the handoff-recommendation-departure inline correction precedent from sub-PR 39 (handoff_recommendation_disposition field below).
changelog.bound_direction_encoding: bound_direction OMITTED, per Rule 34: forbidden when uncertainty is non-null (the entry carries uncertainty=58 MeV). This mirrors the encoding of all 17 already-anchored hadronic-states cells (each has non-null uncertainty and no bound_direction). The new entry adds 0 to the cumulative cell-direct two-sided counter (which stays at 107 from v76). Rule 35 (warning-level: bound_direction recommended on uncertainty=null entries) does not apply since uncertainty is explicitly populated.
changelog.constructive_status_cleanup: cell-h-predicted-bb-tetra carries constructive_status='realized' in v76 (already populated, no cleanup performed). Per FC convention across hadronic-states the constructive_status='realized' label marks 'cell-slot is realized in the FC's structural decomposition' rather than 'particle has been experimentally observed' (cf. cell-h-glueball-scalar and cell-h-hybrid-1mp which also carry cs=realized despite contested or non-unique experimental observations). The phenomenon-physical interpretation that T_{bb} has not been observed as of 2026 is captured in the cell content prose ('Mendeleev empty cell — strong theoretical prediction, no experimental observation as of 2026'). An alternative encoding would be constructive_status='conjectured-by-pattern', which would more strictly mark T_{bb} as predicted-but-unobserved at the field level; that encoding however requires populating forced_by per v16 spec §2-§3 (the conjectured-by-pattern status implies the cell is forced into existence by structural constraints), which is a deeper authoring move out of scope for sub-PR 40's qs-anchoring focus. Decision: leave constructive_status='realized' as-is; flag the constructive_status harmonization question across the FC (cell-h-glueball-scalar, cell-h-hybrid-1mp, cell-h-dibaryon-d2380, cell-h-predicted-bb-tetra all carry cs=realized despite varying empirical statuses) as a future-sub-PR coverage opportunity alongside the [18]/[19] structurally-excluded cleanup.
changelog.skip_decisions: Two cells were not anchored: [18] cell-h-excl-charged-glueball (content='structurally-excluded', missing constructive_status) and [19] cell-h-excl-baryon-0++ (content='structurally-excluded', missing constructive_status). Structurally-excluded cells encode forbidden axis-tuple combinations and do not take quantitative_scale per v18 spec §2; they should carry constructive_status='forbidden-by-pattern' plus forced_by per v16 spec §2-§3, which is a separate authoring move out of sub-PR 40 scope. Flagged for a future single-purpose constructive_status cleanup sub-PR. No other cells on hadronic-states were unanchored. No fallback FC was reached for since the lead candidate proved fully usable for a single-entry sweep.
changelog.T_convention_decision: T28 MINTED — Multi-prediction-envelope encoding for Mendeleev-empty cell-direct anchoring. When a cell encodes a predicted-but-unobserved phenomenon AND the literature provides multiple canonical predictions with distinct centrals AND the inter-central spread substantially exceeds any individual prediction's own uncertainty band, the cell-direct quantitative_scale encodes the midpoint of the literature centrals with uncertainty equal to the half-width of the envelope (each prediction's central ± its own quoted uncertainty). T28 differs from the existing FC-pred convention (which anchors a single publication's prediction with that publication's own quoted uncertainty) because the cell-direct surface represents the cell's overall characteristic scale rather than a publication-specific claim; T28 also differs from T11 (stat ⊕ syst quadrature: combining systematics of a single experiment) by combining centrals from disjoint theoretical methodologies. T28 first-use parameters for cell-h-predicted-bb-tetra: KR central 10389 ± 12 MeV (Karliner-Rosner 2017 PRL 119:202001, diquark-antidiquark model) + EQ central 10482 MeV (Eichten-Quigg 2017 PRL 119:202002, heavy-quark symmetry; no quoted uncertainty in PRL paper) → envelope [KR-low 10377, EQ-central 10482], midpoint 10435.5 → rounded value 10436 MeV, half-width 58 MeV (envelope half-width 52.5 expanded to 58 to safely bracket KR's full ±12 band on the low edge: 10436-58 = 10378 covers KR-low 10377 within 1 MeV). The PDG 2024 'Tetraquarks' review compilation is cited as the third citation acknowledging the broader literature consensus on T_{bb} mass which extends beyond the KR+EQ envelope to include QCD-sum-rules predictions (Agaev-Azizi-Sundu 10035 ± 260 MeV) and lattice-QCD predictions (typical range 10400-10550 MeV). T28 scope is bounded to cell-direct on Mendeleev-empty cells where two-or-more canonical literature predictions span a wider scale than any single prediction's own uncertainty band; T28 is not applicable to observed particles (where PDG-average is the canonical anchor) or to single-prediction cells (where the canonical prediction's central is the anchor). T28 is queryable post-rebuild via the cumulative-cell-direct-mass counter incrementing 55 → 56 with envelope-encoded uncertainty as the distinguishing signature.

The T_{bb} prediction does not have an obvious parallel anchor on any other FC (no related cells on dark-matter-candidates, compact-astrophysical-objects, or other phenomenon-FCs encoding mass values near ~10.4 GeV in a structurally-related framework); the FC-pred[5] entry on hadronic-states itself is the split-attachment partner, not a parallel anchor on a different FC. The McKay 120-coincidence three-FC IDENTITY pattern at value=120 (sub-PRs 37+38+39 on E_8 cells across ade-su2-subgroups + ade-quivers + ade-du-val) is preserved untouched. The protect-and-preserve directive from the sub-PR 39 handoff is satisfied.

changelog.firewall_self_check: v18 §4 / v19 §4 admissibility test applied entry-by-entry (only one entry in this sub-PR). PASS — full transcript: (1) [value is a literature value] KR central 10389 ± 12 MeV from PRL 119:202001 explicitly quoted by Karliner-Rosner from their diquark-antidiquark model calculation; EQ central 10482 MeV from PRL 119:202002 explicitly stated by Eichten-Quigg from their heavy-quark-symmetry analysis (no quoted uncertainty in PRL paper); the cell-direct value 10436 is the trivially-computed midpoint of these two literature centrals — a literature-faithful summary statistic, not a cartographer reconstruction; uncertainty 58 MeV is the half-width of the literature envelope [KR-low 10377, EQ-central 10482], expanded by 5 MeV to safely bracket KR's full uncertainty band on the low edge. (2) [§2.5 self-check] Would I record this numerical claim — value, kind, units, uncertainty — if I had only the doubly-heavy-tetraquark phenomenology literature and not the v76 dataset structure in front of me? YES — given only the Karliner-Rosner and Eichten-Quigg PRLs plus the PDG 2024 compilation review, I would still anchor cell-h-predicted-bb-tetra's mass scale at the midpoint of the foundational simultaneous-publication pair with uncertainty bracketing the full prediction envelope; the cell-content text explicitly identifies KR and EQ as the foundational prediction sources, and the midpoint-with-envelope-uncertainty is the natural literature-faithful summary when no single canonical central predominates; the encoding decision is independent of any v76 dataset structural observation. (3) [citations array non-empty per Rule 33] Three citations populated: KR PRL (canonical foundational prediction central + uncertainty), EQ PRL (alternative canonical foundational prediction central, no quoted uncertainty), PDG 2024 (broader literature review context including QCD-sum-rules and lattice-QCD alternatives extending beyond the KR+EQ envelope). All three citations cite published canonical references; none cites the v76 dataset itself.
changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition: The sub-PR 40 handoff made TWO recommendations the chat departed from and corrected inline, mirroring the sub-PR 39 inline-correction precedent. (1) The handoff led with 'kind=energy_scale' as the encoding kind. Reviewed against the v18 schema spec §2 (kind definitions) and the hadronic-states FC's existing convention across 17 anchored cells: all 17 use kind=mass with units=MeV. Hadron rest mass is the kind=mass quantity per spec; kind=energy_scale is reserved for characteristic energy scales of phenomena. Chat encoded kind=mass per FC-convention-faithfulness AND spec-faithfulness AND match with FC-pred[5]'s existing kind=mass. (2) The handoff led with single-prediction encoding ('Karliner-Rosner ~10.4 GeV value') as the simplest path. Reviewed against the cell content (which explicitly names BOTH KR and EQ as foundational prediction sources) and against the inter-central spread (~93 MeV, far exceeding either individual ±12 MeV uncertainty band): single-prediction encoding would have misrepresented the actual literature window. Chat elevated to multi-prediction-envelope encoding (T28 mint) as a more accurate and more informative representation of the cell's overall characteristic predicted mass scale. The handoff's third suggestion (constructive_status='conjectured-by-pattern' with forced_by authoring) was acknowledged but not taken: changing cs to conjectured-by-pattern requires authoring forced_by per v16 spec, which expands sub-PR 40 scope beyond qs-anchoring. cs left at 'realized' matching the FC's existing convention; constructive_status harmonization across hadronic-states cells flagged for a future sub-PR. Both corrections are recorded here per the sub-PR 39 META-precedent for handling handoff errors gracefully under the disposition principle.

Continues the phenomenology-rich FC Mendeleev-empty-cell anchoring momentum from sub-PR 40 (hadronic-states T_{bb} mass anchor) and pivots qs-encoding mode from sub-PR 40's symmetric-uncertainty mass anchoring to mixed-kind bounds-heavy anchoring with bound_direction populated where appropriate (3 of 5 new entries are bd=upper; 2 of 5 carry non-null uncertainty with bd absent per Rule 34). Coverage: dark-matter-candidates cell-direct anchored count 13/18 → 18/18 (FULLY ANCHORED on the qs-admissible surface — all cells encoding particle/non-particle DM candidates carry a literature-anchored characteristic numerical commitment). Cumulative cell-direct qs 198 → 203; cell-direct mass 56 → 58; cell-direct coupling 17 → 18; cell-direct length 4 → 5; cell-direct dimensionless 67 → 68; cell-direct bd=upper 9 → 12; cell-direct bd=two-sided 107 unchanged; total qs all surfaces 259 → 264. Parallel-anchor count unchanged at 5 (sub-PR 41 does not touch the ADE clique or create new same-value/same-kind/same-physical-claim parallel-anchor relationships per the sub-PR 38/39 criterion — preserves the McKay-correspondence five-FC parallel-integer-set and the three-FC IDENTITY pattern on |E_8^(1)| = 120 unmolested). Validator (Rules 1–36 against v19 schema): 0 new errors, 0 new warnings (4 legacy `constrains` errors tolerated per PROJECT_INFRASTRUCTURE.md §2; 0 Rule 35 warnings on v78 baseline — every uncertainty=null new entry carries bound_direction populated).
changelog.anchor_choice_rationale: Target FC chosen as dark-matter-candidates per the sub-PR 41 handoff lead recommendation. Five unanchored cells named in v77 changelog `remaining_after_this_PR` and confirmed at indices [2], [4], [8], [10], [16] on direct inspection. All five cells are Mendeleev-empty / bounds-heavy DM phenomenology with strong canonical-literature anchors (ADMX collaboration papers; LSND/MiniBooNE/IceCube/Planck for sterile-eV; Bullet-Cluster + Tulin-Yu review for SIDM; Berezhiani + Foot for mirror-DM; Arvanitaki et al. 2010 axiverse foundational paper). Multi-cell sweep (all 5 in one sub-PR) selected over partial-3-cell scope after inspection confirmed each cell has a single dominant literature-canonical anchor with no contested encoding decisions that would require deferring entries to sub-PR 42 — the disposition principle directs the chat to author all 5 in one pass when literature support is uniformly strong. This fully anchors dark-matter-candidates' qs-admissible surface (18/18 cells) in a single sub-PR, parallel to sub-PR 40's hadronic-states fully-anchored state (18/20 cells with qs; remaining 2 are structurally-excluded and don't take qs). The sub-PRs 40 → 41 arc establishes a pattern of fully-anchoring phenomenology-rich FCs in single-pass sweeps, raising the per-FC qs-coverage signal-to-noise for downstream pattern-finding queries.
changelog.kind_encoding: Mixed kind coverage per cell — encoding choices follow FC convention where possible, departing only where the cell's physical content forces a different kind: [2] cell-dm-ALP-haloscope: kind=coupling. Matches v18 spec §7 worked example pattern exactly (haloscope axion-photon coupling g_{aγγ} encoded as kind=coupling with units=GeV^-1 and log10=true). Introduces kind=coupling to the dark-matter-candidates FC for the first time (the FC's 13 prior cells used mass / dimensionless / energy_scale / length). [4] cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV: kind=mass. Matches FC convention (cells [3], [5], [7], [9], [12], [14], [15], [17] all use kind=mass for particle masses), and matches the spec §2 definition of kind=mass as 'particle/hadronic mass'. [8] cell-dm-self-interacting: kind=length (T29 MINT). The most accurate kind for cross-section-per-mass σ/m is debatable: (a) kind=ratio is dimensionally forbidden (cm²/g is not strictly dimensionless after canonical reduction); (b) kind=coupling permits units to record normalization per v18 spec §5.2 note but is conventionally for interaction-strength parameters dimensionless after normalization; (c) kind=length with units=cm² is the existing FC convention for cross-sections per cell [0] cell-dm-WIMP-canonical (T23 cross-section-as-area precedent). The chat extends T23 to T29 — cross-section-per-mass-as-length-with-units cm²/g — keeping cross-section-related quantities under a uniform kind across the FC. T29 is reserved for cross-section-per-mass cell-direct entries on DM FCs; T23 remains the convention for pure cross-sections. [10] cell-dm-mirror-DM: kind=dimensionless. Matches FC convention (cells [6], [11], [13] use kind=dimensionless for fractional / mixing / ratio parameters). The mirror-sector temperature ratio T'/T is a dimensionless quantity by construction. [16] cell-dm-predicted-string-axiverse: kind=mass with log10=true. Matches the broader FC convention for particle masses (cells [3], [5], [7], [9], [12], [14], [15], [17] all kind=mass) and the natural way to encode the axiverse's logarithmically-distributed mass range. T28 reuse — see T_convention_decision field below.
changelog.bound_direction_encoding: Mixed: 3 entries carry bd=upper, 2 entries have bd absent. [2] ALP-haloscope: bd=upper (ADMX exclusion limit at 90% CL is unambiguously upper on g_{aγγ}). uncertainty=null. Rule 35 satisfied (bd populated). [4] sterile-nu-eV: bd ABSENT per Rule 34 (uncertainty=0.5 non-null). The LSND/MiniBooNE preferred central is a two-sided measurement with symmetric global-fit uncertainty; recording as uncertainty=0.5 + bd absent matches the established convention for cells with non-null uncertainty (cf. cell [3] cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm uses unc=None + bd=two-sided as an alternative pattern; sub-PR 41 chose the uncertainty-non-null variant here because the global-fit literature genuinely reports a spread). [8] SIDM: bd=upper (Bullet-Cluster σ/m bound is unambiguously upper at ~1 cm²/g). uncertainty=null. Rule 35 satisfied. [10] mirror-DM: bd=upper (BBN constraint on T'/T is unambiguously upper). uncertainty=null. Rule 35 satisfied. [16] string-axiverse: bd ABSENT per Rule 34 (uncertainty=16.5 non-null, T28 envelope half-width in log10 space). The axiverse claim is about a logarithmically-distributed range, not a one-sided bound; T28 envelope encoding with non-null uncertainty correctly captures both endpoints (10^-33 to 10^0 eV) and disallows bd by the schema's binary semantic (Rule 34). Net counter update: cell-direct bd=upper +3 (cells 2, 8, 10); cell-direct bd=ABSENT +2 (cells 4, 16); cell-direct bd=two-sided unchanged at 107.
changelog.constructive_status_cleanup: All 5 touched cells already carry constructive_status='realized' in v77 (none required cleanup). Per FC convention across dark-matter-candidates the constructive_status='realized' label marks 'cell-slot is realized in the FC's structural decomposition' rather than 'candidate has been experimentally observed' — every DM-candidate cell is realized in the structural decomposition regardless of whether the candidate has been confirmed (most haven't; the entire FC's purpose is to organize unobserved/partially-excluded DM candidates). A stricter encoding would use constructive_status='conjectured-by-pattern' for predicted-but-unobserved DM candidates (cell-dm-predicted-string-axiverse most especially) and 'forbidden-by-pattern' or 'realized' for excluded/observed-only cells, but this would require `forced_by` authoring for each cell (Phase A mechanism on cells) which is out of scope for sub-PR 41's qs-anchoring focus. constructive_status harmonization across dark-matter-candidates cells flagged as a future cleanup opportunity (parallel to the same flag carried in sub-PR 40's changelog for hadronic-states).
changelog.skip_decisions: No cells skipped on dark-matter-candidates — all 5 unanchored cells anchored in this sub-PR. FC fully anchored on qs-admissible surface after this sweep (18/18). Two structurally-excluded cells from sub-PR 40's hadronic-states sweep (cells [18] cell-h-excl-charged-glueball, [19] cell-h-excl-baryon-0++) remain out-of-scope for qs anchoring across the map and are deferred to a future single-purpose constructive_status cleanup sub-PR per sub-PR 40's standing flag. No similar structurally-excluded cells exist on dark-matter-candidates.
changelog.T_convention_decision: T28 reused on cell [16] cell-dm-predicted-string-axiverse. T28 was first-minted in sub-PR 40 as multi-prediction-envelope encoding for Mendeleev-empty cell-direct anchoring (midpoint of literature window with uncertainty = half-envelope-width). T28 first-use in sub-PR 40 was on linear-scale T_{bb} mass (10377–10482 MeV envelope → value=10436 MeV, unc=58 MeV). T28 reuse in sub-PR 41 extends the convention to log10-space for the axiverse, where the literature window spans 33 decades (10^-33 to 10^0 eV); in log10 space the midpoint is -16.5 and the half-width is 16.5. This is the natural log10 generalization of T28: when the literature predicts a logarithmically-distributed range, encode with log10=true and use log10-space midpoint and half-width. T29 NEWLY MINTED on cell [8] cell-dm-self-interacting: cross-section-per-mass-as-length-with-units cm²/g. T29 extends T23 (cross-section-as-area encoded as kind=length per cell [0] cell-dm-WIMP-canonical) to mass-normalized cross-sections (kind=length, units=cm²/g). T29 is RESERVED for cross-section-per-mass cell-direct entries where the literature commitment is on σ/m rather than σ. Not applicable to pure cross-sections (use T23) or generic interaction-strength parameters (use kind=coupling). Cumulative T-conventions in force: T1–T14, T17–T20 (with T16 dropped, T15 retired), T21–T22 deferred, T23–T28 active, T29 minted in this sub-PR. T-convention numbers continue monotonically from sub-PR 40's T28.
changelog.parallel_anchor_decision: parallel_anchor_count remains 5 (unchanged from sub-PR 39's three-FC IDENTITY pattern at |E_8^(1)|=120 plus the cumulative four prior parallel anchors). Sub-PR 41 surveyed the 5 new entries against the existing cross-FC numerical landscape for potential parallel-anchor relationships matching the sub-PR 38/39 criterion ('two cells across distinct FCs encoding the SAME numerical value via the SAME kind in citation-anchored fashion'): (i) cell-dm-ALP-haloscope g_{aγγ} ≲ 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹ has no cross-FC kind=coupling counterpart at this value; (ii) cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV m_s ≈ 1 eV has potential parallel with neutrino-sector-phenomenology cells (cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED and cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension are named in the cell content as cross-FC counterparts), but sub-PR 41 does not author entries on those neutrino-sector cells in this PR, so the parallel-anchor relationship is potential rather than realized; flagged as a future cross-FC same-value-anchor opportunity; (iii) cell-dm-self-interacting σ/m ≲ 1 cm²/g has no cross-FC kind=length counterpart; (iv) cell-dm-mirror-DM T'/T ≲ 0.5 has no cross-FC kind=dimensionless counterpart at this value; (v) cell-dm-predicted-string-axiverse log10 m_a midpoint -16.5 eV has no cross-FC kind=mass counterpart in the envelope-encoding mode. No new parallel-anchor relationships are realized in sub-PR 41; the McKay-correspondence five-FC parallel-integer-set (sub-PR 39) and the three-FC IDENTITY pattern (sub-PRs 37/38/39) remain unmolested.
changelog.cross_FC_pattern_status: Cross-FC pattern observation for downstream attention (not action in sub-PR 41): cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV (DM FC) shares physical content with cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED and cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension on the neutrino-sector-phenomenology FC (the cell content names this explicitly). When the neutrino-sector-phenomenology FC is anchored in a future sub-PR with kind=mass + same value (~1 eV), a same-value/same-kind/same-physical-claim cross-FC parallel-anchor relationship will be realized — flagged as a Mendeleev-style cross-FC anchor target. Similarly, cell-dm-PBH-asteroid-mass-viable (existing v77 entry, kind=mass=1e21 g) and cell-dm-PBH-LIGO-mass-excluded (existing v77 entry, kind=dimensionless=0.001) share physical content with corresponding compact-astrophysical-objects FC cells per the explicit content references; some of these cross-FC anchors may already be realized — flagged for verification in a future cross-FC audit pass. Cell-dm-predicted-string-axiverse (kind=mass with log10=true, value=-16.5, unc=16.5) has no current cross-FC kind=mass counterpart in envelope-encoding mode but is structurally connected via cross-classification edges to BSM-non-GUT and to candidate-foundational-program nodes (string-phenomenology); cross-FC anchoring opportunities for axiverse-related claims may surface on those FCs in future sub-PRs.

firewall_self_check.principle: v18 §4 / v19 §4 admissibility test applied per entry: (1) value is a literature value (not cartographer-estimated or pattern-inferred); (2) bound_direction is from authoring time per Rule 34/35/36; (3) citations array non-empty per Rule 33. The §2.5 self-check restated: 'Would I record this numerical claim — value, kind, bound direction, units, uncertainty — if I had only the DM-phenomenology literature and not the v77 dataset structure in front of me?' YES for all 5 entries.
firewall_self_check.cell_2_ALP_haloscope: kind=coupling, value=-16, log10=true, units=GeV^-1, bd=upper. YES — ADMX Bartram et al. 2021 PRL 127:261803 explicitly reports DFSZ-level g_{aγγ} exclusion at ~10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹ in the 3.3–4.2 μeV mass window, and the v18 spec §7 worked example uses exactly this encoding pattern for haloscope axion-photon coupling. The value is from the literature; the encoding pattern is from the spec. No cartographer inference.
firewall_self_check.cell_4_sterile_nu_eV: kind=mass, value=1.0 eV, uncertainty=0.5 (symmetric), bd absent. YES — LSND Aguilar 2001 + MiniBooNE Aguilar-Arevalo 2018 + Dentler et al. 2018 global fit converge on m_s ≈ 1 eV (Δm² ~ 1 eV²) as the central preferred value with a 99% CL range roughly [0.5, 2] eV. The symmetric ±0.5 eV uncertainty captures this spread without favoring either edge. bd absent per Rule 34 (uncertainty non-null). No cartographer inference.
firewall_self_check.cell_8_SIDM: kind=length, value=1.0 cm²/g, bd=upper. YES — Randall et al. 2008 ApJ 679:1173 reports σ/m < 0.7 cm²/g at 68% CL from refined Bullet-Cluster analysis; the round-figure σ/m ≲ 1 cm²/g order-of-magnitude bound is the canonical cluster-scale ceiling on SIDM cross-sections per Tulin-Yu 2018 review. The choice of round-figure value=1.0 rather than literal 0.7 is consistent with the FC's order-of-magnitude anchoring convention (cf. cell [0] WIMP cross-section 2.2e-48 cm² is order-of-magnitude per XENON1T result, not a single-experiment best-fit). T29 mint documented in T_convention_decision field above. No cartographer inference.
firewall_self_check.cell_10_mirror_DM: kind=dimensionless, value=0.5, bd=upper. YES — Berezhiani-Comelli-Villante 2001 PLB 503:362 and Foot 2014 review confirm T'/T < ~0.5 as the canonical mirror-cosmology BBN bound. The value is from the literature; encoding as kind=dimensionless matches FC convention. No cartographer inference.
firewall_self_check.cell_16_string_axiverse: kind=mass with log10=true, value=-16.5, units=eV, uncertainty=16.5. YES — Arvanitaki et al. 2010 PRD 81:123530 foundational axiverse paper predicts ALP masses logarithmically distributed from ~Hubble scale (10⁻³³ eV) to ~eV. Encoding the literature window via T28 envelope encoding in log10 space (midpoint -16.5, half-width 16.5) is the most faithful representation of an explicitly-logarithmic predicted distribution. Stott-Marsh 2018 and Svrcek-Witten 2006 corroborate the range. No cartographer inference.

changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition: Sub-PR 41 handoff led with 'all 5 cells in a single sub-PR (matching the recommended scope; aggressive but tractable)' as the chat's autonomous-pick recommendation; chat accepted this scope after first-action inspection confirmed all 5 cells have strong canonical-literature anchors and no contested encoding decisions. Handoff specifically named per-cell encoding guidance with multiple options for some cells (e.g., 'SIDM → kind=length OR kind=ratio OR kind=coupling — chat picks based on FC convention and v18 spec'). The chat picked: (i) cell [2] ALP-haloscope: kind=coupling (matching v18 spec §7 worked example for haloscope axion-photon coupling, with units=GeV^-1, log10=true, value=-16). The handoff's alternative kind=mass (haloscope mass window) was rejected because kind=mass would record the axion mass scale (~μeV) rather than the haloscope-specific sensitivity claim (g_{aγγ} reach); the coupling-reach is the more distinctive and informative claim for this cell. (ii) cell [8] SIDM: kind=length with units=cm²/g (T29 mint extending T23 cross-section-as-area precedent from cell [0] cell-dm-WIMP-canonical). The handoff's alternatives kind=coupling and kind=ratio were both considered: kind=ratio is dimensionally forbidden by the v18 schema (cm²/g is not strictly dimensionless after canonical reduction); kind=coupling is conventionally for dimensionless interaction-strength parameters with units permitted only to record non-canonical normalization per v18 §5.2 — for σ/m the units cm²/g are not a normalization choice but genuine dimensions, making kind=coupling less faithful. kind=length with units=cm²/g extends T23 in a coherent direction and keeps cross-section-related quantities under a uniform kind across the FC. T29 minted explicitly per handoff invitation. No handoff-recommendation DEPARTURE inline corrections required in sub-PR 41 — the handoff's per-cell recommendations were accurate; the chat picked from the offered options per disposition principle. The two-sub-PR consecutive inline-correction precedent (sub-PRs 39+40) is reinforced as the expected behavior when the handoff makes an error, but sub-PR 41's handoff was correct on every per-cell question and the precedent therefore was not exercised. Future handoffs should expect that the inline-correction precedent is invoked when needed and dormant otherwise.

Smoke test against v78 data: 0 new errors, 0 new warnings (4 legacy `constrains` errors tolerated per PROJECT_INFRASTRUCTURE.md §2). 0 Rule 35 warnings on v78 baseline (every new uncertainty=null entry carries bd populated: cell [2] bd=upper, cell [8] bd=upper, cell [10] bd=upper; the 2 new uncertainty-non-null entries on cells [4] and [16] correctly omit bd per Rule 34).

Encoding mix: 5 exact values (T10 uncertainty=null + T26 rational-fraction expressed as decimal + bd=two-sided per Rule 35 + Onsager precedent), 2 bootstrap-precision values with non-null symmetric uncertainty (bd absent per Rule 34), 2 mathematical bounds (T10 uncertainty=null + T26 rational-fraction + bd=lower/upper per Rule 35).
