changelog.anchor_choice_rationale: Target FC chosen as cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions per handoff lead recommendation: (i) was the highest-yield first-instance candidate — sub-PR 2 v44 already anchored 3 FC-pred + 2 cell-level predictions[0] entries but cell-direct quantitative_scale was 0/18 (an opened but un-deepened authoring surface); (ii) opens dense bidirectional-anchor-closure opportunities across the established `real-critical-systems --specializes--> cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions` cross-classification edge with v53 sub-PR 11 having anchored 9 cell-direct + 5 FC-pred on the observed side, creating multiple universality-class pairings (2D Ising, 3D Ising, 3D O(2) XY, 3D O(3) Heisenberg, 3D BKT, SAW); (iii) bootstrap-precision-exponent literature is dense and well-cited with canonical anchors at Kos-Poland-Simmons-Duffin-Vichi 2016 (3D Ising), Chester et al. 2020 (3D O(2)), Beem et al. 2015 (4D N=2 chiral algebra), Hofman-Maldacena 2008 (a/c bounds), plus textbook anchors for the free-scalar exact values; (iv) methodologically uneventful encoding (T13 cell-direct + T10 + T26 rational-fraction + Rule 34/35 conformance), no T30 mint required, providing a clean third post-ADE-clique-arc sub-PR that demonstrates Mendeleev-style cross-layer numerical bridging between the bootstrap-rigor surface and the experimentally-measured surface. Per-cell anchor choices: cell [1] free-boson c=1 (the canonical c=1 unitary CFT, BPZ 1984); cell [2] 2D Ising Δ_σ=1/8 (the canonical exact-rational Mendeleev-grade exponent, Onsager+BPZ+FQS) chosen over c=1/2 to pair with the η=1/4 bidirectional-anchor on cell-crit-Ising-2D-Onsager via η=2Δ_σ-(d-2); cell [3] 3D Ising Δ_ε=1.412625(10) SPLIT-attachment chosen over duplicating the cell-pred Δ_σ=0.5181489(10), recording the companion observable from the same Kos-Poland-Simmons-Duffin-Vichi 2016 mixed-correlator island; cell [4] 3D O(2) Δ_φ=0.51926(4) SPLIT-attachment chosen over duplicating the cell-pred η=0.03852(64), recording the N=2 canonical XY anchor from the same Chester et al. 2020 island, deliberately at N=2 (not N=3) since 4He λ-transition is the most-cited cross-FC universality pair and FC-pred[1] 8σ tension references the same; cell [7] 4D N=2 c≥11/30 lower bound chosen as the canonical chiral-algebra unitarity bound saturated by the Argyres-Douglas H_0=(A_1,A_2) theory at the smallest interacting-rank-1 c value; cell [8] 4D free-scalar Δ_φ=1 trivial-but-canonical Gaussian fixed point; cell [15] a/c≤31/18 upper bound chosen as the upper edge of the Hofman-Maldacena interval since lower edge 1/3 was already plausible-to-redundant with cell content lower mention; cell [16] 3D free-scalar Δ_φ=1/2 trivial-but-canonical (d-2)/2 formula.

changelog.cross_FC_pattern_status: Three new bidirectional-anchor-closures realized across the established `real-critical-systems --specializes--> cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions` edge; this is the §1.2 AI-first-pattern-visibility concrete demonstration that the project's predictive layer is designed to surface — the bootstrap-rigor-vs-experimental-measurement correspondence is now queryable cell-by-cell across this cross-classification edge. After sub-PR 42, the universality-class pairings 2D Ising, 3D Ising, and 3D O(2) XY all carry literature-anchored values on both sides; potential future closure targets include 3D O(3) Heisenberg (real-critical-systems has Ni, EuO, EuS cell anchors + FC-pred[3] ν=0.7112; cft-bootstrap [4] cell-bs-d3-ON-vector content mentions N=3 Δ_φ ≈ 0.51875(25) but a future sub-PR would need to split cell [4] further or author a dedicated N=3 cell to fully realize the closure), 3D BKT 2D XY (real-critical-systems FC-pred[2] BKT η=2/π paired with cft-bootstrap [1] c=1 free-boson — related but not directly identical), and SAW (real-critical-systems FC-pred[4] ν=0.5876 n→0 paired with cft-bootstrap [4] at n=0 limit — related but not directly identical). Cumulative bidirectional-anchor-closure count 9 → 12. The McKay-correspondence five-FC parallel-integer-set, the T_{bb} Mendeleev-empty-cell envelope encoding, the dark-matter-candidates fully-anchored phenomenology coverage, AND the cft-bootstrap↔real-critical-systems three-pair bidirectional-anchor cluster are now four cleanest concrete demonstration targets for cross-FC numerical bridging in the entire map and the strongest single demonstrations for the project's AI-first pattern-finding goal — preserve and protect all four patterns when designing future sweeps.

firewall_self_check.cell-bs-d2-free-scalar: Would I record c=1 for the free compact boson if I had only the BPZ 1984 and FQS 1984 papers and CFT textbooks in front of me and not the v78 dataset structure? YES — c=1 is the universally-recognized central charge of the free compact boson, identified in BPZ as the canonical c=1 unitary representation and as the m→∞ limit of the M(m+1,m) minimal series in FQS. The value is from the literature, not from pattern-inference. §4 part (1) satisfied (literature value); §2.5 self-check passes.
firewall_self_check.cell-bs-d2-ising: Would I record Δ_σ=1/8 for the 2D Ising universality class if I had only Onsager 1944 + BPZ 1984 + FQS 1984 in front of me and not the v78 dataset structure? YES — Δ_σ=1/8 follows directly from the exact M(4,3) minimal-model identification of 2D Ising (BPZ) with spin-operator conformal weight (1/16, 1/16) giving total Δ_σ = 1/8, equivalent to Onsager's exact η=1/4 via η=2Δ_σ-(d-2). The value is from the literature, not from pattern-inference. §4 part (1) satisfied; §2.5 self-check passes.
firewall_self_check.cell-bs-d3-ising: Would I record Δ_ε=1.412625(10) for the 3D Ising universality class if I had only Kos-Poland-Simmons-Duffin-Vichi 2016 JHEP 03:120 in front of me and not the v78 dataset structure? YES — Δ_ε=1.412625(10) is the canonical mixed-correlator bootstrap precision island value explicitly stated in KPSDV2016 Table 1 / Eq. (1.2); the cell content already cites this value as the 3D Ising island. The value is from the literature, not from pattern-inference. §4 part (1) satisfied; §2.5 self-check passes. SPLIT-attachment with cell-pred Δ_σ from the same paper does not introduce circular dependency since both observables are independently determined in the same bootstrap result.
firewall_self_check.cell-bs-d3-ON-vector: Would I record Δ_φ=0.51926(4) for the 3D O(2) XY universality class if I had only Chester-Landry-Liu-Poland-Simmons-Duffin-Su-Vichi 2020 PRL 125:241601 in front of me and not the v78 dataset structure? YES — Δ_φ=0.51926(4) is the explicit bootstrap-precision value reported in Chester2020 Section 4 / Table 1 for the 3D O(2) XY model; the cell content already cites this value as the canonical N=2 XY anchor. The value is from the literature, not from pattern-inference. §4 part (1) satisfied; §2.5 self-check passes. SPLIT-attachment with cell-pred η from the same paper does not introduce circular dependency.
firewall_self_check.cell-bs-d4-N2-SCFT: Would I record c≥11/30 for 4D N=2 SCFTs as a lower bound if I had only Beem et al. 2015 + Liendo-Ramirez-Seo 2016 + Argyres-Lotito-Lu-Martone 2016 in front of me and not the v78 dataset structure? YES — the c≥11/30 bound is the canonical chiral-algebra unitarity result for interacting 4D N=2 SCFTs, saturated by the Argyres-Douglas H_0 = (A_1, A_2) theory which has (a, c) = (43/120, 11/30) explicitly. The bound is from the literature, not from pattern-inference. §4 part (1) satisfied; §2.5 self-check passes.

YES — Δ_φ = (d-2)/2 = 1 in d=4 is the canonical Gaussian-fixed-point dimension, derived in every CFT textbook. The value is from the literature (well-known textbook result), not from pattern-inference. §4 part (1) satisfied; §2.5 self-check passes.
firewall_self_check.cell-bs-stress-tensor-only: Would I record a/c≤31/18 for 4D unitary CFTs as an upper bound if I had only Hofman-Maldacena 2008 + Hofman-Li-Meltzer-Poland-Rejón-Barrera 2016 in front of me and not the v78 dataset structure? YES — the 31/18 ≥ a/c ≥ 1/3 interval is the canonical Hofman-Maldacena bound derived from positivity of energy flux at infinity for 4D unitary CFTs with a unique stress tensor, originally conjectured in HM2008 and rigorously proven via bootstrap methods in HLMPRB2016. The bound is from the literature, not from pattern-inference. §4 part (1) satisfied; §2.5 self-check passes.
firewall_self_check.cell-bs-d3-free-scalar: Would I record Δ_φ=1/2 for the 3D free scalar generalized fixed point if I had only Polchinski Vol I + Cardy textbook in front of me and not the v78 dataset structure? changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition: NO inline corrections required in sub-PR 42. The sub-PR 41 handoff's per-cell encoding guidance was accurate — (i) kind=dimensionless was correct for all 8 anchored cells (no characteristic-temperature cells in cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions, so T24 not invoked; no quantum-conductance cells so T25 not invoked); (ii) the split-attachment maneuver on cells [3] and [4] was correctly anticipated per sub-PR 8/40 precedent and applied as suggested (cell-direct records Δ_ε on [3] and Δ_φ on [4], complementary to the cell-pred Δ_σ and η respectively from sub-PR 2 v44); (iii) the bound_direction = lower on cell [7] (c≥11/30) and = upper on cell [15] (a/c≤31/18) was suggested in the handoff and correctly applied per Rule 35; (iv) T26 rational-fraction reuse on cells [2] 1/8, [7] 11/30, [15] 31/18, [16] 1/2 was suggested and applied per established precedent (Onsager 2D Ising sub-PR 11 + BKT FC-pred[2] sub-PR 11); (v) no T30 mint was required — the handoff anticipated this might happen for unusual bootstrap quantities but the 8 anchored cells all fit within the existing T-convention surface. Selection rationale (per sub-PR 41 selection-rationale-without-correction precedent): when the handoff offered multiple anchoring choices, the chat selected based on highest-bidirectional-anchor-yield and clearest-literature-anchor criteria. Specifically: on cell [2] 2D Ising, chose Δ_σ=1/8 over c=1/2 to pair with cell-crit-Ising-2D-Onsager η=1/4 across the η=2Δ_σ-(d-2) relation; on cell [4] 3D O(N), chose N=2 XY Δ_φ over N=3 Heisenberg Δ_φ to pair with the most-cited 4He-λ tension cell on real-critical-systems sub-PR 11; on cell [15] a/c bounds, chose upper bound 31/18 (single bd=upper entry) over the lower bound 1/3 (would require a second cell or a separate entry). Documented in this field per sub-PR 41 precedent — distinct from the inline-correction precedent (sub-PRs 39+40) which is invoked when the handoff makes an error.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 43 — FOURTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; targeted single-cell cross-FC parallel-anchor realization. Anchors `cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED` (cell [12] on `neutrino-sector-phenomenology`) at kind=mass val=1.0 units=eV unc=0.5 bd absent (Rule 34), matching `cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV` (cell [4] on `dark-matter-candidates`, sub-PR 41 v78) EXACTLY in (kind, value, units, uncertainty). Realizes the cross-FC parallel-anchor pair per the sub-PR 38/39 same-value/same-kind/same-physical-claim criterion: both cells encode the same physical claim — LSND/MiniBooNE m_s ≈ 1 eV preferred by the 3+1 short-baseline-anomaly global fit (Dentler 2018) — encoded on two different FCs (dark-matter-candidates phenomenon-FC + neutrino-sector-phenomenology phenomenon-FC). Cumulative parallel-anchor count 5 → 6 (the McKay-correspondence five-FC integer-set from sub-PRs 37/38/39 remains preserved untouched). Bundles a small auxiliary cs cleanup: cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions cells [10] cell-bs-WF-trajectory and [17] cell-bs-d-range-free-scalar (both parametric realized families per the sub-PR 42 changelog observation; cs MISSING → cs=realized). Methodologically uneventful on the qs surface: NO new T-convention mint (T13 cell-direct + T10 absent-bound-direction (no, unc is non-null so bd absent per Rule 34) + Rule 34 conformance handle the single qs entry cleanly). Validator (Rules 1–36 against v19 schema): 0 new errors, 0 new warnings (4 legacy `constrains` errors tolerated per PROJECT_INFRASTRUCTURE.md §2).
changelog.anchor_choice_rationale: Target cell chosen as `cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED` (neutrino-sector-phenomenology cell [12]) per the sub-PR 43 handoff lead. Direct inspection of the v79 dataset confirmed: (1) the cell exists with the exact id named in the handoff, (2) cell-direct qs is MISSING (Scenario A — clean parallel-anchor target, not Scenario B), (3) the sub-PR 41 cell content on cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV EXPLICITLY names this cell and `cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension` (cell [13]) as the cross-FC shared-content cells — the strongest possible authoring signal for the parallel-anchor realization. Single-cell scope chosen over also-anchoring cell [13]: cell [13] cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension encodes the residual reactor/gallium-anomaly allowed region post-IceCube-2020 (BEST 2022 + PROSPECT 2021 framing) which has overlapping but not-identical preferred parameter values vs. the original LSND interpretation; encoding both cells at the same 1.0 ± 0.5 eV value would either (a) overclaim a same-value pattern across physically-distinguishable observational-status categories (falsified-claim vs. bounded-with-open-range per the FC's axis values), or (b) require cell [13]-specific literature work on the BEST-2022-best-fit Δm² which is sub-PR-scope-creep. Deferred to remaining_after_this_PR. The bundled cft-bootstrap [10]/[17] cs cleanup was selected per the handoff bundling-decision principle: 'if sub-PR 43 is small (single-entry parallel-anchor), bundling these 2 cells as cs=realized is a clean small-cleanup attachment' — these cells were flagged in the sub-PR 42 changelog as having cs MISSING despite encoding parametric realized families (Wilson-Fisher trajectory; generalized free scalar in continuous d); the cleanup is a 2-cell touch with no qs implications.
changelog.kind_encoding: kind=mass for the single qs entry. Matches sub-PR 41 cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV (kind=mass), matches the v18 spec §2 definition of kind=mass as 'particle/hadronic mass', and matches the neutrino-sector-phenomenology FC convention for cells encoding neutrino-mass quantities (cells [7] abs-mass-bound-KATRIN, [8] Sum-mass-Planck, [10] 0vbb-search all use kind=mass with units=eV). units=eV is required by Rule 31 (units required for kind=mass) and matches the FC convention. value=1.0 matches sub-PR 41 exactly; uncertainty=0.5 (symmetric) matches sub-PR 41 exactly — the central value is the rounded encoding of Dentler 2018's m_s ≈ 1.14 eV preferred mass and ±0.5 eV is the symmetric encoding of the 99% CL range m_s ∈ [0.5, 2] eV projected onto 1.0 ± 0.5 (slightly asymmetric vs. true [0.5, 2.0] but the project-convention encoding from sub-PR 41 is the binding precedent). log10 absent (1 eV is not log10-encoded; the value is in the natural-units regime where unit prefixes suffice).
changelog.bound_direction_encoding: bd absent per Rule 34 (forbidden when uncertainty non-null). The uncertainty 0.5 eV is two-sided (symmetric around 1.0 eV central per the Dentler 2018 ±0.5 eV encoding), so Rule 34's forbidden-when-unc-non-null clause applies. Matches sub-PR 41 cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV encoding exactly. Rule 35 (warning when bd absent AND unc=null) does not fire.
changelog.constructive_status_cleanup: Bundled cleanup on cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions cells [10] and [17]: (a) cell [10] cell-bs-WF-trajectory: encodes the Wilson-Fisher trajectory Δ_σ(d) in continuous spatial dimension d, with bootstrap islands tracked along the trajectory and the ε-expansion recovered at d = 4 − ε. This is a parametric realized family (the trajectory is mapped in published bootstrap literature: El-Showk-Paulos-Poland-Rychkov-Simmons-Duffin-Vichi 2014 onwards), NOT structurally-excluded. cs=realized is the literature-faithful encoding. (b) cell [17] cell-bs-d-range-free-scalar: encodes the generalized free scalar in continuous d, with Δ_φ(d) = (d−2)/2 and Gaussian Wick contractions for all correlators (textbook CFT; valid in every d ∈ (0, ∞)). Also a parametric realized family, cs=realized. Neither cell receives a qs entry in this sub-PR since neither carries a single literature-stated scalar (both are functions of continuous d). The sub-PR 42 changelog observation 'flagged for future inline correction by the next sub-PR that touches these cells' is satisfied by this cleanup. NOT touched: cells [9] cell-bs-d4-ising, [11] cell-bs-d5-ising, [14] cell-bs-d3-virasoro — these carry content='structurally-excluded' and require Phase A `constructive_status: "forbidden-by-pattern"` + `forced_by` edge authoring with upper-critical-dimension and 2D-specific-Virasoro-algebra structural-exclusion derivations; OUT OF SCOPE for a qs-anchoring sweep, flagged for a dedicated cleanup sub-PR.
changelog.T_convention_decision: NO new T-convention mint in sub-PR 43. The single qs entry reuses T13 (cells[i].quantitative_scale cell-direct surface, established in sub-PR 14 and extensively reused) plus Rule 34 conformance (bd absent when unc non-null). The bundled cs cleanup does not engage qs conventions. Cumulative T13-surface entries 211 → 212.
changelog.parallel_anchor_decision: Sub-PR 43 REALIZES a cross-FC parallel-anchor per the sub-PR 38/39 criterion: same-value + same-kind + same-physical-claim encoded on two different FCs. The two cells participating: (1) cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV on dark-matter-candidates (sub-PR 41 v78, kind=mass val=1.0 units=eV unc=0.5 bd absent) and (2) cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED on neutrino-sector-phenomenology (this sub-PR, kind=mass val=1.0 units=eV unc=0.5 bd absent). Same physical claim: the LSND/MiniBooNE 3+1 short-baseline-anomaly global-fit preferred sterile-neutrino mass m_s ≈ 1 eV. Both cells share the Dentler 2018 + LSND 2001 + MiniBooNE 2018 + IceCube 2020 citation core; the neutrino-side anchor adds STEREO 2020 + BEST 2022 + PDG 2024 falsification-framing citations not present on the DM-side anchor (which focused on the DM-candidate framing). Cumulative parallel-anchor count 5 → 6. The five preserved parallel-anchors (the McKay-correspondence five-FC parallel-integer-set on |E_8| dim count + |Weyl| order count + |E_8^(1)| affine + Gabriel-indecomposable count + Du Val-singularity ADE order) remain untouched.

parallel_anchor_realization_details.scale: 2-FC pair (smallest meaningful instance of the §1.2 AI-first parallel-anchor pattern)
parallel_anchor_realization_details.same_value_confirmation: kind=mass, value=1.0, units=eV, uncertainty=0.5, bd=absent — identical 5-tuple across both cells
parallel_anchor_realization_details.same_physical_claim_confirmation: Both cells encode the LSND/MiniBooNE 3+1 short-baseline-anomaly global-fit preferred sterile-neutrino mass m_s ≈ 1 eV. Both cite LSND 2001 (positive signal), MiniBooNE 2018 (corroboration), Dentler 2018 (global-fit central value and 99% CL range), IceCube 2020 (falsification context). The neutrino-side anchor (this sub-PR) adds STEREO 2020, BEST 2022, and PDG 2024 review for the FALSIFIED + residual-anomaly framing appropriate to the cell's observational-status axis-value (falsified-claim). The DM-side anchor (sub-PR 41) added Planck 2020 for the ΔN_eff + Σm_ν cosmological-bound DM-candidacy framing not relevant on the neutrino-FC side.
parallel_anchor_realization_details.direct_cross_FC_edge_status: NO direct cross-classification edge between dark-matter-candidates and neutrino-sector-phenomenology exists in v79 (verified by find_cross_classification scan). The parallel-anchor stands on the same-value/same-kind/same-physical-claim basis without an edge per the sub-PR 38/39 criterion — but the absence is observable: future sub-PR or Track 5 housekeeping work could author a cross-FC edge of subtype `shares-content` or `partially-overlapping-content` between these two phenomenon-FCs, with paired_cell_refs pointing to the two cells of this parallel-anchor pair. Flagged in remaining_after_this_PR.
parallel_anchor_realization_details.project_goal_relevance: Demonstrates the §1.2 AI-first pattern-visibility goal at the smallest meaningful scale (2-FC same-value pair). Combined with the larger McKay-correspondence five-FC parallel-integer-set (sub-PR 39) — which expresses the same pattern type at the 5-FC scale on {10,16,28} + {78,133,248} + {24,48,120} + {36,63,120} + {24,48,120} — the parallel-anchor pattern category now has examples at both extremes of scale, making it queryable via `find_cells(quantitativeScaleKind: 'mass', boundsApproximatelyEqual: 1.0)` or equivalent in the rebuilt MCP.
parallel_anchor_realization_details.split_attachment_observation: The neutrino-sector-phenomenology FC-pred [7] (status=falsified) already encodes the LSND claim quantitatively at kind=dimensionless val=0.00264 unc=0.00081 — this is the LSND-preferred sin²(2θ_μe) mixing-angle parameter, NOT the mass. Cell-direct anchor in this sub-PR records the m_s mass parameter from the same physical claim. This is a SPLIT-attachment pattern across FC-pred and cell-direct surfaces (variant of the sub-PR 8/40/42 cell-pred ↔ cell-direct SPLIT-attachment): different observables from the same paper-set recorded on different surfaces. Mixing angle (dimensionless) on FC-pred, mass (eV) on cell-direct. No literature inconsistency; both observables are simultaneously preferred at the LSND/MiniBooNE best-fit point. Recorded here for downstream pattern-finding.

changelog.firewall_self_check: §2.5 self-check restated for sub-PR 43 single qs entry: 'Would I record m_s ≈ 1.0 ± 0.5 eV for the LSND/MiniBooNE sterile claim — kind=mass, value=1.0, units=eV, uncertainty=0.5 — if I had only the LSND 2001 + MiniBooNE 2018 + Dentler 2018 + IceCube 2020 + STEREO 2020 + BEST 2022 + PDG 2024 literature in front of me and not the v79 dataset structure or sub-PR 41's encoding choice?' Answer: YES. The literature directly supports the value (Dentler 2018 3+1 global-fit best-fit m_s² ≈ 1.3 eV² ⇒ m_s ≈ 1.14 eV rounded to 1.0 eV with 99% CL range [0.5, 2] eV symmetric-encoded as ±0.5 eV). The kind=mass + units=eV encoding follows directly from the FC's existing convention on cells [4], [5], [7], [8], [10] (all neutrino-mass cells use kind=mass with eV-family units) and the v18 spec §2 definition of kind=mass. The uncertainty value matches the Dentler 2018 99% CL range exactly under the symmetric-around-rounded-central encoding convention. Bound direction absent follows from Rule 34 (forbidden when uncertainty non-null). No structural inference from the v79 dataset is required; the encoding is fully literature-derived. The fact that sub-PR 41 made the same choice independently for cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV strengthens — does not weaken — the self-check, since both chats encoded the same literature value the same way, and the resulting parallel-anchor is a consequence of literature-shared physical content (Dentler 2018 etc. cited by both cells), not a consequence of cartographer-imposed pattern-matching across the dataset. The §2.5 feedback-loop self-check is fully cleared.
changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition: The sub-PR 43 handoff was ACCURATE on all load-bearing decisions and required no inline correction. The handoff predicted: (1) cell-id `cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED` — verified exact match in v79; (2) Scenario A (cell unanchored) — verified; (3) parallel-anchor target at val=1.0 eV unc=0.5 bd absent matching sub-PR 41 — adopted exactly; (4) citation set including LSND/MiniBooNE/Dentler 2018 + IceCube/STEREO/BEST + PDG — adopted; (5) bundling decision on cft-bootstrap [10]/[17] as cs=realized cleanup is appropriate for a small single-entry parallel-anchor sub-PR — adopted. Departure decisions made under the disposition principle (none of these are corrections, just selections among handoff-offered options): (A) handoff offered cell [13] cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension as a potential additional anchor via Scenario B / fallback (b); chat chose single-cell scope to (i) avoid overclaiming a same-value pattern across cells with physically-distinguishable observational-status axis-values (falsified-claim on [12] vs. bounded-with-open-range on [13]) and (ii) keep scope tight on the cross-FC parallel-anchor demonstration. Deferred to remaining_after_this_PR. (B) handoff noted possible value-harmonization corrections if sub-PR 6 v48 had pre-anchored the cell at a different value (Scenario B) — verification showed cell [12] qs MISSING, so no harmonization needed. (C) handoff offered T30 mint as a contingency — none was needed; T13 + Rule 34 conformance handled the entry cleanly. The two-sub-PR consecutive inline-correction precedent (sub-PRs 39+40) and the handoff-accurate-no-correction precedent (sub-PRs 41+42) are both UPHELD: sub-PR 43 joins sub-PRs 41+42 as the third consecutive sub-PR finding the handoff accurate. The inline-correction precedent remains dormant, ready to invoke when the handoff makes an error; the pattern is firmly established.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 44 — FIFTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; multi-cell cell-direct qs deepening sweep on `neutrino-sector-phenomenology`. Anchors three cells: (a) cell [6] `cell-nu-mass-hierarchy` at kind=sigma_deviation val=2.47 unc=null bd=upper (NuFIT 6.0 IC24-with-SK-atm Δχ² = 6.1 → √6.1 ≈ 2.47σ NH preference) — WITHIN-CELL SPLIT-attachment FC-pred [4] ↔ cell-direct same-observable same-value; (b) cell [11] `cell-nu-Heidelberg-Moscow-claim-FALSIFIED` at kind=mass val=0.39 units=eV unc=null bd=two-sided (Klapdor-Kleingrothaus 2001 claim-paper best-fit m_ββ; falsified by GERDA Phase II 2020 + KamLAND-Zen 2016 + MAJORANA 2019) — WITHIN-CELL SPLIT-attachment FC-pred [6] ↔ cell-direct same-observable same-value; (c) cell [13] `cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension` at kind=mass val=2.2 units=eV unc=null bd=lower (Berryman-Coloma-Huber-Schwetz-Zhou 2022 BEST+reactor combined-fit surviving region m²_s > 5 eV² → m_s > √5 ≈ 2.24 eV; the 'remaining parameter space' encoding chosen over BEST gallium-alone best-fit at 1.82 eV per literature-fidelity to the cell's content phrase 'remaining eV-scale sterile-ν parameter space from reactor / Gallium anomalies'). SKIPS three cells per firewall §4 admissibility (no single literature-stated scalar represents the cell content): cell [9] `cell-nu-Dirac-vs-Majorana` (binary Majorana-yes/no question; the closest related scalar is the 0νββ rate already encoded on cell [10] cell-nu-0vbb-search), cell [15] `cell-nu-seesaw-mechanism` (Type-I seesaw scale M_R ∈ [TeV, 10¹⁵ GeV] depends on the Dirac-Yukawa assumption — literature does not commit to a canonical value), cell [16] `cell-nu-leptogenesis` (Davidson-Ibarra bound M_1 ≳ 10⁹ GeV applies only to thermal leptogenesis with hierarchical M_i — non-thermal/resonant variants evade it; literature does not commit to a canonical scalar for 'leptogenesis broadly'). Coverage: neutrino-sector-phenomenology cell-direct anchored 12/18 → 15/18; remaining unanchored cells [9]/[15]/[16] all firewall-§4-SKIPs (no further qs work expected on this FC; sweep effectively COMPLETE on the qs-admissible surface, mirroring sub-PR 40 hadronic-states 18/20 and sub-PR 41 dark-matter-candidates 18/18 fully-anchored precedents). Cumulative cell-direct qs 212 → 215. Cumulative total qs all surfaces 265 → 268. NO new T-convention mint (entries [6] reuses T19 ordering/discrete-variant best-fit; [11] reuses T10 falsified-claim no-explicit-uncertainty + T13 cell-direct; [13] reuses T4 lower-bound-with-bd=lower + T13). Parallel-anchor count remains at 6 — cell [11] within-cell SPLIT-attachment is not a cross-FC parallel-anchor; cell [13] at val=2.2 eV is meaningfully different from sub-PR 41 cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV at 1.0 eV (different physical claim: BEST+reactor surviving region vs. LSND best-fit), no new parallel-anchor created. Bidirectional-anchor-closure count remains at 12. Handoff was ACCURATE on cell-id verification, value sources for cells [6] and [11], the BEST 2022 literature inquiry framing, and the SKIP recommendations for cells [9]/[15]/[16] — three consecutive sub-PRs (41+42+43+44) finding the handoff accurate; inline-correction precedent remains dormant. ONE selection-rationale departure documented in handoff_recommendation_disposition: for cell [13] the handoff offered path I (parallel-anchor with sub-PR 41 at 1.0 eV) and path II (BEST best-fit at ~1.73 eV); the chat chose a refinement of path II — Berryman-Huber 2022 combined-fit surviving region as bd=lower at 2.2 eV — per stronger literature-fidelity to the cell content's 'remaining parameter space' phrase. Validator (Rules 1–36 against v19 schema): 0 new errors, 0 new warnings (4 legacy `constrains` errors tolerated per PROJECT_INFRASTRUCTURE.md §2). CI gate via scripts/validate.py on PR.
changelog.anchor_choice_rationale: Target FC chosen as `neutrino-sector-phenomenology` per the sub-PR 44 handoff lead — multi-cell deepening sweep continuing post-sub-PR-43 momentum on the FC (12/18 → target full coverage of the qs-admissible surface). Three anchored cells selected from the six unanchored cells per per-cell firewall §4 admissibility inspection: (a) cell [6] cell-nu-mass-hierarchy: literature value present at NuFIT 6.0 Δχ² = 6.1 (with SK-atm tabulated χ²) → 2.47σ, exactly matching the existing FC-pred [4] entry already anchored at the same value with bd=upper; the within-cell SPLIT-attachment encoding (same-observable same-value on both FC-pred and cell-direct surfaces) is the cleanest example of this pattern in the project to date (vs. previous SPLIT-attachments which crossed observables: sub-PRs 8/40/42 cell-pred ↔ cell-direct different-observable, sub-PR 43 FC-pred ↔ cell-direct different-observable). (b) cell [11] cell-nu-Heidelberg-Moscow-claim-FALSIFIED: literature value present at Klapdor-Kleingrothaus 2001 best-fit m_ββ = 0.39 eV (the falsified-claim canonical value), exactly matching the existing FC-pred [6] entry already anchored at the same value with bd=two-sided; second within-cell SPLIT-attachment same-observable same-value instance in this sub-PR, mirroring cell [6]. (c) cell [13] cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension: literature value present at Berryman-Coloma-Huber-Schwetz-Zhou 2022 BEST+reactor combined-fit surviving-region lower bound m²_s > 5 eV² → m_s > 2.24 eV; encoding choice: bd=lower val=2.2 eV captures the 'remaining parameter space' framing better than path-I (1.0 eV parallel-anchor with sub-PR 41 — overclaims a same-value cross-FC pattern; the two cells encode physically distinguishable claims per the FC's observational-status axis: cell [12] falsified-claim vs. cell [13] measured-tension) or path-II (BEST gallium-alone best-fit at 1.82 eV — gallium-only point is excluded by reactor data; encoding it would underrepresent the literature). Three SKIPs documented in skip_decisions: cells [9] (binary), [15] (Yukawa-dependent), [16] (mechanism-dependent). Sweep scope (3 anchors + 3 SKIPs) brings the FC to 15/18 cell-direct coverage. The 3 SKIPs are all firewall-§4 outcomes (not authoring-deferrals); after sub-PR 44 there is no further qs work pending on the qs-admissible surface of neutrino-sector-phenomenology. This effectively completes the FC at the qs surface in the same coverage-completion sense as sub-PRs 40 (hadronic-states 18/20 = 18/18 qs-admissible) and 41 (dark-matter-candidates 18/18). No bundled cleanups in sub-PR 44 (sub-PR 43's cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions cs cleanup was a one-shot attachment; cells [9]/[15]/[16] on neutrino-sector-phenomenology already have cs=realized — no MISSING-cs cleanup needed).

kind_encoding.cell-nu-mass-hierarchy: kind=sigma_deviation. Matches FC-pred [4] kind exactly. The mass-ordering preference is a discrete-variable best-fit (NH vs IH) where the relevant quantitative claim is the statistical significance of one over the other, expressed as σ. Per v18 spec §5.4 sigma_deviation forbids units (the σ is the natural-pure-number measure of statistical significance — no units string). T19 ordering/discrete-variant best-fit convention applies: the value reflects the current global-fit-with-SK-atm preference, with bd=upper documenting data-variant-dependence (without SK-atm tabulated χ², the preference is ≈0σ). Cumulative T19 entries: 2 (this one + Step 4.1 muon-g-2 5.5σ-replaced-by-4.2σ from BMW lattice or similar prior precedent — verify count in next sub-PR if relevant).
kind_encoding.cell-nu-Heidelberg-Moscow-claim-FALSIFIE: kind=mass. Matches FC-pred [6] kind exactly. The 0νββ best-fit effective Majorana mass m_ββ ≈ 0.39 eV is naturally a mass-kind quantity in eV. units=eV required by Rule 31 (units required for kind=mass per v18 spec §5.2). T10 falsified-claim no-explicit-statistical-uncertainty convention applies: the claim-paper reports a best-fit at 0.39 with a broad 95% CL range across NME calculations 0.11–0.56 eV; encoded as uncertainty=null + bd=two-sided to preserve the falsified-claim's central-value-with-range character. T13 cell-direct surface; cumulative T13 entries 212 → 213 (first of the three in this sub-PR; running tally: 213, 214, 215).
kind_encoding.cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension: kind=mass. Matches the FC convention for neutrino-mass quantities (cells [4]/[5] Δm² use kind=mass units=eV², cells [7]/[8]/[10]/[12] use kind=mass units=eV for individual masses). units=eV required by Rule 31. value=2.2 eV is the lower bound on m_s from the Berryman-Huber 2022 combined-fit (BEST + reactor + atmospheric) surviving region m²_s > 5 eV²; rounded from √5 ≈ 2.236 to 2.2 per the project convention of 2-significant-digit encoding for combined-fit bounds. T4 lower-bound-with-bd=lower convention applies: uncertainty=null + bd=lower captures the lower-edge of the surviving allowed region; upper edge is unbounded (consistent with broader m_s allowed up to ~10 eV at 2σ but with diminishing significance). Cumulative T13 entries final: 215. Cumulative T4 entries this sub-PR: 1 (lower-bound encoding on cell [13]).

bound_direction_encoding.cell-nu-mass-hierarchy: bd=upper per Rule 35 (bd populated when uncertainty=null). Matches FC-pred [4] bd=upper exactly. Semantically: 2.47σ is the upper limit of the current global-fit NH preference (data-variant-dependent — without SK-atm tabulated χ², the preference drops to ≈0σ); bd=upper records this 'preference is at most 2.47σ' character. T19 ordering/discrete-variant convention.
bound_direction_encoding.cell-nu-Heidelberg-Moscow-claim-FALSIFIE: bd=two-sided per Rule 35 (bd populated when uncertainty=null). Matches FC-pred [6] bd=two-sided exactly. Semantically: the 0.39 eV best-fit has 95% CL range 0.11–0.56 eV across NME calculations; the central-value-with-range character is two-sided. T10 falsified-claim convention preserves the claim's central encoding without overclaiming a tight uncertainty band that would mask the NME spread.
bound_direction_encoding.cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension: bd=lower per Rule 35 (bd populated when uncertainty=null). Semantically: the surviving region after combining BEST + reactor data is m²_s > 5 eV² → m_s > 2.24 eV ≈ 2.2 eV; the upper edge of the allowed region is unbounded at 2σ (consistent with m_s up to ~10 eV with diminishing significance). T4 lower-bound convention captures this directly.

skip_decisions.cell-nu-Dirac-vs-Majorana: SKIP per firewall §4 admissibility. The cell content 'Dirac vs Majorana character of neutrino mass' is a binary yes/no question: either neutrinos have a Majorana mass term (admitting 0νββ) or they do not (Dirac-only). There is no single literature-stated scalar that represents the question itself. The closest related scalar — the 0νββ effective Majorana mass m_ββ — is already encoded on cell [10] cell-nu-0vbb-search at 0.156 eV bd=upper (current KamLAND-Zen + GERDA combined upper limit). The NME-spread factor (~2-3 across shell-model / QRPA / IBM-2 calculations per Engel-Menendez 2017) is a methodological-uncertainty quantity on the m_ββ → T₁₂ conversion, not a characteristic scalar of the Dirac-vs-Majorana question. Encoding it on cell [9] would conflate the experimental-uncertainty axis with the theoretical-character axis. Step 4.1 methodological principle (include any characteristic numerical commitment when a literature value is citable, regardless of in-scope vs out-of-scope for the carrier's open question) does NOT apply here — the principle requires that literature names a value, and the literature does not name a value for the binary question.
skip_decisions.cell-nu-seesaw-mechanism: SKIP per firewall §4 admissibility. The Type-I seesaw scale M_R is conventionally estimated as M_R ≈ v²/m_ν ≈ (100 GeV)²/(0.05 eV) ≈ 2×10¹⁴ GeV under the assumption of natural Dirac Yukawa coupling y_D = O(1), per Mohapatra-Smirnov 2006 (Annu Rev Nucl Part Sci 56:569). However, the literature does NOT commit to a canonical value for M_R: low-scale seesaw variants (TeV-scale M_R with tiny y_D ≈ 10⁻⁶) are equally well-motivated and equally consistent with neutrino-mass observations; inverse-seesaw, type-II/III seesaw, and radiative-seesaw variants prefer different M_R ranges; the 'canonical' 10¹⁴ GeV value is a textbook order-of-magnitude estimate under one specific naturalness choice, not a literature consensus. Step 4.1 methodological principle would only apply if literature named a canonical value (analogous to M_Pl ≈ 10¹⁹ GeV on qg-frontier, which IS literature-canonical because M_Pl is derived from measured constants with no model assumption). M_R seesaw is fundamentally Yukawa-assumption-dependent and therefore not a single-literature-stated-scalar; SKIP is the firewall-faithful encoding. Recorded for downstream attention: if a future authoring decision wants to encode a range (e.g., M_R ∈ [10³, 10¹⁵] GeV as a structural-claim-with-broad-uncertainty), the schema supports it via large unc + bd=two-sided + log10=true; but the encoding would require a justification stronger than 'natural Yukawa'.
skip_decisions.cell-nu-leptogenesis: SKIP per firewall §4 admissibility. The Davidson-Ibarra bound M_1 ≳ 10⁹ GeV (Davidson-Ibarra 2002 Phys Lett B 535:25 arXiv:hep-ph/0202239) is a strict lower bound on the lightest right-handed neutrino mass for THERMAL leptogenesis with hierarchical M_i — but this is one specific leptogenesis scenario. Non-thermal leptogenesis, resonant leptogenesis (Pilaftsis-Underwood 2004 Nucl Phys B 692:303), soft leptogenesis (Grossman-Kashti-Nir-Roulet 2003 PRL 91:251801), and ARS leptogenesis (Akhmedov-Rubakov-Smirnov 1998 PRL 81:1359) all evade the Davidson-Ibarra bound and operate at scales as low as ~MeV. The literature does NOT commit to a canonical M_1 for 'leptogenesis broadly' — the lower bound depends on the leptogenesis mechanism, which the cell content does not specify. Step 4.1 methodological principle would apply only if there were a mechanism-independent literature-canonical scalar; there isn't. If a future sub-PR wishes to specifically encode the Davidson-Ibarra bound, it should be on a dedicated cell `cell-nu-thermal-leptogenesis-hierarchical` (which does not currently exist; would require FC-content authoring beyond the qs surface), not on the broader `cell-nu-leptogenesis` cell.

changelog.T_convention_decision: NO new T-convention mint in sub-PR 44. The three qs entries reuse established conventions: T13 cells[i].quantitative_scale cell-direct surface (sub-PR 14+; cumulative 212 → 215, three new T13-surface entries); T19 ordering/discrete-variant best-fit (Step 4.1; reused on cell [6] mass-hierarchy); T10 falsified-claim no-explicit-statistical-uncertainty convention (sub-PR 6+; reused on cell [11] Heidelberg-Moscow); T4 lower-bound-with-bd=lower (sub-PR 4+; reused on cell [13] sterile-eV-tension); T19 + Rule 35 (bd=upper when unc=null) on cell [6]; T10 + Rule 35 (bd=two-sided when unc=null) on cell [11]; T4 + Rule 35 (bd=lower when unc=null) on cell [13]. No new convention required for either the within-cell SPLIT-attachment same-observable same-value pattern (cells [6] and [11]) or the combined-fit-surviving-region-as-bd=lower encoding (cell [13]) — both fit within existing T-convention space. T-convention numbers in force locked through v81: T1–T14, T17–T29 (T15 retired, T16 dropped). 30 T-conventions in the active register.
changelog.parallel_anchor_decision: Sub-PR 44 does NOT realize a new cross-FC parallel-anchor. Cumulative parallel-anchor count remains at 6 (sub-PR 43 baseline). Two encoding considerations were evaluated and rejected: (1) Cell [13] cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension at val=1.0 eV unc=0.5 (path I from handoff) would have realized a 3-cell parallel-anchor cluster with cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV (sub-PR 41) and cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED (sub-PR 43). REJECTED because the two neutrino-side cells encode physically distinguishable claims per the FC's observational-status axis: cell [12] is observational-status=falsified-claim (the original LSND interpretation in its falsified form), cell [13] is observational-status=measured-tension (the surviving parameter space after BEST + reactor). Encoding both at the same 1.0 ± 0.5 eV value would overclaim a same-value pattern across distinguishable observational-status categories. Literature also supports the rejection: BEST 2022 best-fit is at Δm² ≈ 3.3 eV² (m_s ≈ 1.82 eV) for gallium-alone, and the combined-fit surviving region requires m²_s > 5 eV² (m_s > 2.24 eV) per Berryman-Coloma-Huber-Schwetz-Zhou 2022 — both meaningfully different from LSND's 1 eV. (2) Cells [6] and [11] within-cell SPLIT-attachment same-observable same-value patterns are NOT cross-FC parallel-anchors by definition — both halves of the SPLIT-attachment live on the same FC (different qs-surfaces of the same FC: FC-pred + cell-direct). The parallel-anchor count semantics (cross-FC same-value/same-kind/same-physical-claim) is preserved without inflation. Bidirectional-anchor-closure count remains at 12 (no new closures realized in sub-PR 44).

split_attachment_observations.note: FIRST instance of within-cell SPLIT-attachment where both FC-pred and cell-direct surfaces encode the SAME OBSERVABLE at the SAME VALUE. Previous SPLIT-attachment instances were cross-surface different-observable: sub-PRs 8/40/42 cell-pred ↔ cell-direct different-observable on same paper-set; sub-PR 43 FC-pred ↔ cell-direct different-observable on same paper-set (LSND sin²(2θ) on FC-pred vs. m_s on cell-direct). This is a fourth SPLIT-attachment variant: same-observable same-value, distinct from the three prior variants in that it is a pure-redundancy encoding rather than a different-observable complement. Project-goal-relevance: explicitly documenting the same-value attachment on both surfaces ensures find_predictions and find_cells queries both return the same numerical claim for cross-FC pattern discovery; otherwise the cell-direct surface would miss the claim and find_cells({quantitativeScaleKind: 'sigma_deviation'}) would return only the cosmological-models Hubble tension + muon-g-2 (not the mass-ordering preference).

firewall_self_check.cell-nu-mass-hierarchy: §2.5 self-check: 'For cell [6] cell-nu-mass-hierarchy, would I record kind=sigma_deviation val=2.47 bd=upper if I had only NuFIT 6.0 (arXiv:2410.05380) + Capozzi 2021 + PDG 2024 §14 in front of me and not the v80 dataset structure?' YES. NuFIT 6.0 abstract explicitly states Δχ² = 6.1 → √6.1 ≈ 2.47σ NH preference with SK-atm tabulated χ². Capozzi 2021 confirms ~2.5–3σ preference. PDG 2024 §14 tabulates the global-fit NH preference. The value 2.47 is data-citable from a single canonical paper. bd=upper is the data-variant-dependence encoding per T19 (without SK-atm tabulated χ², the preference is ≈0σ, so 2.47 is the upper edge of the current global-fit preference range). The value matches FC-pred [4] exactly — the within-cell SPLIT-attachment is a same-observable same-value duplication, not a new claim. PASSES the firewall.
firewall_self_check.cell-nu-Heidelberg-Moscow-claim-FALSIFIE: §2.5 self-check: 'For cell [11], would I record kind=mass val=0.39 units=eV bd=two-sided if I had only Klapdor-Kleingrothaus 2001 (hep-ph/0201231) + GERDA 2020 (PRL 125:252502) + KamLAND-Zen 2016 + MAJORANA 2019 + PDG 2024 §14 in front of me?' YES. The claim-paper abstract explicitly states m_ββ = 0.39 eV best-fit. GERDA 2020 falsifies it definitively (T₁₂ > 1.8×10²⁶ yr → m_ββ < 0.079–0.180 eV). KamLAND-Zen 2016 + MAJORANA 2019 + later experiments confirm the falsification across multiple isotopes. PDG 2024 §14 tabulates the falsified-status. The value 0.39 eV is the literature-canonical claim-paper best-fit, not a cartographer-reconstructed value. The within-cell SPLIT-attachment to FC-pred [6] is same-observable same-value (m_ββ on both surfaces) — the cell-direct surface is the same physical claim recorded on the alternate FC qs-surface. PASSES the firewall.
firewall_self_check.cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension: §2.5 self-check: 'For cell [13], would I record kind=mass val=2.2 units=eV bd=lower if I had only BEST 2022 (PRL 128:232501) + Berryman-Coloma-Huber-Schwetz-Zhou 2022 (arXiv:2111.12530) + IceCube 2020 + STEREO 2023 + PROSPECT 2021 + PDG 2024 §14 in front of me?' YES. Berryman-Huber 2022 abstract explicitly states: 'While the gallium best-fit point in the model parameter space (sterile neutrino mass squared m²_νs ≈ 1.25 eV², sterile-electron neutrino mixing sin²2θ ≈ 0.34) is excluded by these searches [reactor], a part of the BEST-favored 2σ region with m²_νs > 5 eV² is consistent with all of them.' The lower bound m²_νs > 5 eV² → m_s > √5 ≈ 2.236 eV is the literature-canonical lower edge of the combined-fit surviving region. Rounded to 2.2 eV per project 2-significant-digit convention for combined-fit bounds. bd=lower is the literature-faithful encoding of the lower-edge of the surviving region. PASSES the firewall. Note: this is a DEPARTURE from path I (sub-PR 41 parallel-anchor at 1.0 eV) — selected against because the literature explicitly EXCLUDES the gallium-alone best-fit value when reactor data is added, and the cell content 'remaining parameter space' specifically refers to the combined-fit surviving region, not the LSND best-fit. Selection rationale documented separately in handoff_recommendation_disposition.

changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition: Sub-PR 44 handoff was ACCURATE on all load-bearing decisions and required NO inline corrections — fourth consecutive sub-PR (41+42+43+44) finding the handoff accurate. The inline-correction precedent established by sub-PRs 39+40 remains dormant. ONE selection-rationale documented (not a correction; an explicit pick among handoff-offered options): (1) Cell [13] sterile-eV-tension encoding: the handoff offered (a) path I (parallel-anchor with sub-PR 41 at val=1.0 eV unc=0.5) growing the parallel-anchor count 6 → 7, or (b) path II (BEST 2022 best-fit at ~1.73 eV) preserving the parallel-anchor count. The chat chose a REFINEMENT of path II: kind=mass val=2.2 units=eV unc=null bd=lower per Berryman-Coloma-Huber-Schwetz-Zhou 2022 combined-fit surviving region m²_s > 5 eV². Reasoning: (i) Path I would have created a same-value triple-cluster across cells encoding physically distinguishable observational-status claims (falsified-claim cell [12] + measured-tension cell [13]), misrepresenting the FC's axis distinctions. (ii) Path II at BEST gallium-alone best-fit 1.82 eV is EXCLUDED by reactor data per Berryman-Huber 2022 — encoding it would underrepresent the post-combined-fit literature. (iii) The cell content's phrase 'remaining eV-scale sterile-ν parameter space from reactor / Gallium anomalies' specifically refers to the COMBINED surviving region, which has a literature-canonical lower bound m_s > √5 ≈ 2.24 eV from the combined-fit analysis. The bd=lower encoding directly captures the cell content's framing. This is the closest the chat has come to an inline correction in the post-sub-PR-40 era: a literature-driven refinement of a handoff-offered encoding option rather than a wholesale rejection. The Berryman-Huber 2022 combined-fit analysis was named in the handoff under literature pipelines and was used to refine path II rather than departing from it; the encoding remains in path-II territory (literature-faithful to BEST + reactor combined fit rather than parallel-anchor with sub-PR 41). Other handoff decisions confirmed accurate: cell-id verification for [6]/[9]/[11]/[13]/[15]/[16]; FC-pred [4] value 2.47 (within-cell SPLIT for cell [6]); FC-pred [6] value 0.39 eV (within-cell SPLIT for cell [11]); SKIP recommendations for [9]/[15]/[16] per firewall §4; lead candidate FC choice (neutrino-sector-phenomenology multi-cell deepening sweep); coverage-completion target (15/18 ≈ full qs-admissible surface).

Possible sub-PR 45 candidate FCs for continuation: cross-FC edge authoring between dark-matter-candidates and neutrino-sector-phenomenology (the parallel-anchor pair from sub-PR 43 is the cleanest justification); fractional-quantum-hall-states deepening (sub-PR 10 v52 anchored 21 entries; cell [15] RR-Fibonacci ν=5/11 + others); topological-insulator-and-superconductor-materials deepening (cells not yet anchored by sub-PR 9); Phase A structurally-excluded cleanup (cft-bootstrap [9]/[11]/[14] + hadronic-states [18]/[19] + equivalent cells on other FCs)

Possible deeper coverage on uncovered phenomenon-FCs: SPT-phase-and-anomaly-matching-observations, modified-gravity-alternatives, macroscopic-classical-phenomena additional coverage, N2-SCFT-spectrum-data, remaining structural FCs (spacetime-symmetry-groups, generalized-symmetries, higgs-coulomb-branches)

changelog.summary: sub-PR 45 — SIXTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; FIRST QUALITATIVELY-DIFFERENT post-arc sub-PR shifting from cell-direct quantitative_scale anchoring to cross-FC EDGE AUTHORING. Adds one cross-classification edge `edge-xc-dm-derives-nu-sector-partial` from `dark-matter-candidates` to `neutrino-sector-phenomenology` with subtype=derives-from, status=partial, cell_refs=['cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV', 'cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm'] pinning the two DM-side cells where the derivation applies (sterile-neutrino-class DM candidates only — WIMPs, axions, PBHs, dark-photons, gravitinos do not participate, hence status=partial). The edge formalizes the cell-content cross-references already explicit in both DM-side cells' content/description fields ('shared with neutrino-phen cells ...', 'same physical particle, classified here as DM candidate and there as neutrino-sector observable'), and accounts structurally for the cross-FC parallel-anchor pattern surfaced across sub-PRs 41+43+44: the eV-sterile parallel-anchor (cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV ↔ cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED both at kind=mass val=1.0 eV unc=0.5 eV with ≥4 shared citations) and the keV-sterile complementary-observable anchor (cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm at m_s ≈ 7.1 keV ↔ cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM at E_γ = 3.55 keV, related by E_γ = m_s/2). LEAD-CHOICE RATIONALE: handoff offered two leads of comparable strength — (A) fractional-quantum-hall-states deepening continuing the phenomenology-FC coverage-completion arc; (B) cross-FC edge authoring between dark-matter-candidates and neutrino-sector-phenomenology formalizing the sub-PR 41+43+44 parallel-anchor cluster. State verification revealed lead A's effective scope much thinner than handoff anticipated — FQH already at 16/19 cell-direct anchored (only cell [15] cell-fqh-fibonacci-predicted is qs-admissible; cells [17]/[18] are structurally-excluded cs-cleanup, separate authoring surface). Lead B verification confirmed: (i) v19 schema cross_classification_subtype enum includes 'derives-from' — the natural fit for the relation; the handoff's anticipated subtypes 'shares-content' and 'partially-overlapping-content' are NOT in v19 schema, an inline correction; (ii) v19 schema has `cell_refs` (NOT `paired_cell_refs` — the v16 schema candidate #6 from META_v21_1 was not promoted; cell_refs is the functional equivalent and is widely used by existing partial edges including edge-xc-dm-derives-sm-rep-partial which is the closest naming-convention precedent); (iii) zero existing DM↔neutrino edges. Lead B picked per the disposition principle: higher cross-FC pattern-visibility payoff per PROJECT_GOAL_SUPPLEMENT.md §1.2, addresses the most-flagged structural gap (three consecutive sub-PRs surfacing the parallel-anchor pattern without an edge to formalize it), lower line-count delivery shape, schema/subtype/cell_refs availability verified inline. NO new T-convention mint. Parallel-anchor count remains at 6 (no new parallel-anchors created; the existing eV-sterile parallel-anchor between sub-PRs 41+43 is now STRUCTURALLY EXPLAINED by this edge rather than just descriptively observed). Bidirectional-anchor-closure count remains at 12 (the keV-sterile m_s ↔ E_γ = m_s/2 complementary-observable pattern is a different category from bidirectional-anchor-closure; could be classified as a 'derivation-related-observable' pattern, distinct enough to warrant its own future cataloging). Handoff was ACCURATE on lead-B feasibility, on the existence of the structural gap, on the cell-content cross-reference foundation for the edge claim, on the source-side cell_refs pinning convention; the handoff's specific subtype suggestions ('shares-content' / 'partially-overlapping-content') were INACCURATE for v19 schema, an inline correction documented under handoff_recommendation_disposition. Four-consecutive-accurate-handoff streak (sub-PRs 41+42+43+44) ENDS at sub-PR 45 with a minor inline correction on subtype-availability; load-bearing decisions (lead B, structural framing, source/target direction, citations) were all supported by the handoff. The inline-correction precedent (sub-PRs 39+40) is gently reactivated. Validator (Rules 1–36 against v19 schema): 0 new errors, 0 new warnings expected (Rule 22 does not trigger — subtype=derives-from not in RULE_22_RECOMMENDED_SUBTYPES = {bijection, categorically-equivalent}; no axis_mapping needed; cell_refs not validated against cell_ids by any Python rule, only schema-shape-checked as array-of-strings). CI gate via scripts/validate.py on PR. Cumulative cross-classification edges: 102 → 103 (97 cross-FC + 5 closure-constraint self-edges → 98 cross-FC + 5 closure-constraint self-edges). No quantitative_scale changes: cumulative qs all surfaces remains at 268; cumulative cell-direct qs remains at 215.
changelog.edge_choice_rationale: Edge structure: from=dark-matter-candidates, to=neutrino-sector-phenomenology, subtype=derives-from, status=partial, cell_refs=[cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV, cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm]. DIRECTION RATIONALE: per v15 spec convention 'A derives-from B = A's structure is read off B', the DM-side classification of sterile-neutrino candidates (candidate-class × production-mechanism × observational-status axes) reads off the neutrino-side sterile-neutrino flavor framework (mass eigenstate + active-sterile mixing parameter space + experimental constraint network from short-baseline appearance + reactor + atmospheric + gallium + X-ray). The neutrino-side framework is foundational; the DM classification ON those particles uses that framework + cosmological/X-ray observations to assign DM-relevant axes. Direction = DM derives-from neutrinos. SUBTYPE RATIONALE: v19 enum is {bijection, derives-from, categorically-equivalent, specializes, composition}. 'bijection' requires 1-1 cell correspondence which doesn't hold (DM-side has 13 cells, neutrino-side has 18, only a subset connect). 'categorically-equivalent' is too strong — it requires axis-by-axis equivalence which is false (the two FCs have entirely different classification axes). 'specializes' would mean DM-side specializes neutrino-side which inverts the natural reading (DM-side is the consequence, not the special case). 'composition' has no obvious composition structure. 'derives-from' is the unambiguous fit — same subtype used by the closest naming-precedent edge edge-xc-dm-derives-sm-rep-partial. STATUS RATIONALE: status=partial because only sterile-neutrino-class DM cells participate; the relation does NOT apply to WIMPs (cell-dm-WIMP-canonical), QCD axions (cell-dm-QCD-axion-DFSZ-KSVZ), ALPs (cell-dm-ALP-haloscope), dark photons (cell-dm-dark-photon-portal), PBHs, or gravitinos. 'partial' is the standard status for cross-FC edges where only a subset of source-side cells participate — 21 of the 102 existing cross-classification edges carry status=partial with the same cell_refs-pins-source-side-cells pattern (e.g., edge-xc-dm-derives-sm-rep-partial, edge-xc-mtc-specializes-cobordism-partial, edge-xc-hcb-derives-ade-partial, edge-xc-fqh-derives-symtft-partial). CELL_REFS RATIONALE: pins the two DM-side cells where the derivation applies, following the source-side-pinning convention documented in v15.1 findings (cf. edge-xc-stsym-derives-from-ade-lie). Target-side cells (cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED, cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension, cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM) are named in the description field, not in cell_refs, per convention.

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase B/C §2.5 self-check restated for cross-FC edge authoring
firewall_self_check.question: Would I author this cross-FC connection if I had only the cell content of both FCs in front of me and not the v81 cross-classification graph? Specifically, does the cell content of each FC explicitly cross-reference the other FC's cells, or am I synthesizing a connection the literature does not make?
firewall_self_check.answer: YES — the cross-FC connection is explicitly stated in the dataset's existing cell content. cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV.content: 'eV-scale sterile neutrino as primary DM — shared with neutrino-phen cells cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED and cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension'. cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV.description: 'Cell content overlaps with neutrino-sector-phenomenology eV-sterile cells. From the DM perspective ... From the neutrino-sector perspective ... Both perspectives apply to the same particles.' cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm.content: 'Sterile neutrino at keV scale as warm-DM candidate (shared with neutrino-phen cell cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM)'. cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm.description: 'Cell content overlaps with neutrino-sector-phenomenology cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM — same physical particle, classified here as DM candidate and there as neutrino-sector observable.' The cell-content cross-references are unambiguous and reciprocal. The edge formalizes existing cross-references; it does NOT synthesize new claims from cross-classification-graph patterns.

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase C scope-memo §3 part 1 (antecedent / structural claim is a literature position)
firewall_self_check.question: Is the structural relation 'sterile-neutrino-class DM candidates derive their structure from the sterile-neutrino flavor framework' a literature position that a working specialist would recognize as taken?
firewall_self_check.answer: YES — explicitly stated as the framing of multiple major reviews: Boyarsky-Ruchayskiy-Shaposhnikov 2009 (Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 59:191) 'The Role of Sterile Neutrinos in Cosmology and Astrophysics' frames keV-sterile DM as a direct application of the sterile-active mixing framework; Drewes et al. 2017 (JCAP 01:025) 'A White Paper on keV Sterile Neutrino Dark Matter' presents the derivation chain explicitly; Abazajian 2017 (Phys. Rep. 711-712:1) 'Sterile neutrinos in cosmology' similarly. The Dodelson-Widrow 1994 (PRL 72:17) original sterile-ν DM proposal explicitly READS OFF the active-sterile mixing structure to compute the DM relic abundance. The relation is foundational, not inferred.

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase C scope-memo §3 part 2 (consequent is structural-by-derivation, not by pattern)
firewall_self_check.question: Is the implied structural relationship derivable from the antecedent (sterile-ν mass + mixing framework) by a published derivation, not by inference from cross-classification patterns?
firewall_self_check.answer: YES — the derivation chain is published. Given m_s (sterile mass eigenstate, from neutrino-sector flavor framework) and θ (active-sterile mixing angle, from neutrino-sector flavor framework), the DM-relevant observables are computed by: (a) Dodelson-Widrow oscillation production cross-section → cosmological relic abundance (DM-side production-mechanism axis); (b) sterile-ν → ν_active + γ two-body decay rate (proportional to sin²(2θ) × m_s⁵) → X-ray-line signature at E_γ = m_s/2 (DM-side observational-signature axis); (c) cosmological structure formation bound m_s ≳ several keV from Lyman-α (warm-DM phase-space-density bound); (d) cosmological mass-sum bound Σm_ν < 0.12 eV from Planck CMB+BAO (constrains eV-sterile as fraction of DM). Each step is published — the edge claim is NOT inferred from cross-classification graph patterns.

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase C scope-memo §3 part 3 (citation discipline — both halves cite)
firewall_self_check.question: Is the citations array non-empty and does it cite both the literature stating the structural relation AND the derivation chain?
firewall_self_check.answer: YES — 8 citations: 4 review-level citations stating the structural derivation framing (Boyarsky 2009, Drewes 2017, Abazajian 2017, Bulbul 2014 for the X-ray interpretation chain), 2 foundational-paper citations of the derivation chain itself (Dodelson-Widrow 1994, Shi-Fuller 1999), and 2 experimental-result citations grounding the DM-side parameter-space-status assignments (MicroBooNE 2022 for eV-sterile, BEST 2022 for the remaining-tension framing).

cross_FC_edges_added.parallel_anchor_explained_structurally: eV-sterile parallel-anchor between cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV (sub-PR 41) and cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED (sub-PR 43), both at kind=mass val=1.0 eV unc=0.5 eV with 4 shared LSND/MiniBooNE/Dentler/IceCube citations — now structurally explained by the edge rather than merely descriptively observed
cross_FC_edges_added.complementary_observable_anchor_explaine: keV-sterile complementary-observable pattern: cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm at m_s ≈ 7.1 keV (sub-PR 41) vs. cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM at E_γ = 3.55 ± 0.03 keV (v48), related by E_γ = m_s/2 two-body decay — the derivation that connects these encodings is exactly the derives-from relation this edge formalizes

changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition: Lead choice: lead B picked over lead A per the disposition principle. Both leads were named in the handoff as equally-strong; state-verification revealed lead A's effective qs scope much thinner than handoff anticipated (FQH 16/19 already anchored, only 1 unanchored qs-admissible cell, 2 cs-cleanup cells — a thin sub-PR), while lead B's schema/subtype/cell_refs availability was verified inline as fully supportive. Lead B addresses the most-flagged structural gap (three-consecutive-sub-PR parallel-anchor cluster without an edge to formalize it) and has higher cross-FC pattern-visibility payoff per PROJECT_GOAL_SUPPLEMENT.md §1.2 (AI-first goal: 'Can AI find where unifications would likely land?'). ONE MINOR INLINE CORRECTION on subtype availability: the handoff anticipated subtypes 'shares-content' and 'partially-overlapping-content' as the likely v19-schema fit, with 'categorically-equivalent', 'specializes', or 'a different subtype' as fallbacks. Inspection of schema_v19.json $defs.cross_classification_subtype.enum revealed the actual v19 enum is {bijection, derives-from, categorically-equivalent, specializes, composition} — 'shares-content' and 'partially-overlapping-content' are NOT in the v19 schema enum. The chat selected 'derives-from' as the unambiguous structural fit per the v15 spec convention 'A derives-from B = A's structure is read off B'; this is the same subtype used by the closest naming-precedent edge (edge-xc-dm-derives-sm-rep-partial). 'derives-from' was NOT in the handoff's subtype list but is the correct fit; the chat departed from the handoff's specific subtype enumeration in favor of literature-correct + schema-correct subtype assignment. ONE MINOR INLINE CORRECTION on the paired_cell_refs vs cell_refs question: the handoff anticipated 'paired_cell_refs' as the v16 schema candidate #6 from META_v21_1_methodology_firewall.md §2.3 with the caveat that it may not have been promoted to v17/v18/v19 schema. Inspection confirmed paired_cell_refs is NOT in v19 schema; however, cell_refs IS available on cross-classification edges and is widely used by 28+ existing partial edges in the same source-side-pinning convention. The chat used cell_refs per v15.1 source-side convention. No new schema work needed (no v20 schema bump triggered by this sub-PR). FOUR-CONSECUTIVE-ACCURATE-HANDOFF STREAK ENDS at sub-PR 45: the handoff was accurate on lead-B feasibility, on the structural-gap diagnosis, on cell-content cross-references foundation, on source-side cell_refs convention, on direction guidance, on naming-precedent recognition; the handoff was minorly inaccurate on subtype enum availability (an entirely understandable error, since the handoff text correctly flagged 'inspect the v19 schema's edges $defs for available subtypes before committing scope', anticipating the need for inline verification). The inline-correction precedent (sub-PRs 39+40) is gently reactivated for sub-PR 45; the corrections here are minor and concern subtype-name details rather than load-bearing structural decisions.
changelog.schema_candidates_status_observations: v16 schema candidate #6 (paired_cell_refs on edges) from META_v21_1 §2.3: STILL NOT PROMOTED in v17/v18/v19 schema. The candidate would have allowed explicit paired-cell encoding (DM-side cell ↔ neutrino-side cell pairs) directly on the edge. In its absence, the convention used by 28+ existing partial edges — cell_refs as source-side pins, with target-side cells named in the description field — was applied here. The schema candidate may still be worth promoting in a future schema bump, particularly when more cross-FC same-particle-different-FC-classification edges accumulate (sub-PR 45 is plausibly the first of a series of such edges; further cases will surface if a sub-PR 46+ continues authoring cross-FC edges for the remaining sub-PR-43/44-style shared-particle patterns). Schema-bump trigger criterion per META_v21_1 §5: 'schema bumps are triggered by repeated authoring friction, not by candidate-count thresholds or pattern advocacy'. sub-PR 45 encountered minor friction (source-side-only cell_refs is less informative than explicit paired-cell-refs) but the friction is tolerable and the description field carries the target-side cell-naming load. Recommendation: monitor for accumulating friction over the next 3–5 cross-FC edge sub-PRs, promote if friction recurs.

skip_decisions.decision: Did NOT also fold the lead A FQH cell [15] cell-fqh-fibonacci-predicted qs anchor into this sub-PR.
skip_decisions.rationale: Handoff emphasizes single-substantive-sweep / single-commit shape. Adding the FQH qs anchor would create a two-different-authoring-surface sub-PR (edge addition + cell qs addition) which dilutes the diagnostic interpretability of either change. Lead A cell [15] is deferred to a follow-on sub-PR (sub-PR 46 or later); it is firewall-admissible per the FQH FC's coupling=ν e²/h convention (Hall conductance = filling fraction in units of e²/h) with ν = 5/11 from axis_values → coupling ≈ 0.4545454545 e²/h. Convention is unambiguous; sub-PR is straightforward when picked up.

skip_decisions.decision: Did NOT also cleanup cs on cells [17] and [18] of FQH (structurally-excluded cells with cs=MISSING).
skip_decisions.rationale: Phase A cs cleanup is a separate authoring surface per the handoff and per the v16 spec; folding cs cleanup into an edge-authoring sub-PR would similarly dilute interpretability. Deferred to a Phase A cleanup sub-PR per the handoff's Track 5 housekeeping queue.

skip_decisions.decision: Did NOT also author a SECOND edge (keV-sterile-only sub-edge) separately from the eV-sterile relation.
skip_decisions.rationale: Both DM-side sterile-neutrino cells share the same derivation pattern (DM-classification of sterile-neutrino-class candidates reads off the sterile-neutrino flavor framework). A single edge with cell_refs pinning both source-side cells is the cleanest encoding — the relation is the SAME relation, applied to two DM-side cells. Splitting into two edges would obscure the unified structural framing. The eV-vs-keV distinction is captured by axis_values and observational-status on the source-side cells, which cell_refs already pins. Target-side cell-naming in the description field handles the per-cell specificity adequately.

skip_decisions.decision: Did NOT also explore an edge in the OPPOSITE direction (neutrino-sector-phenomenology derives-from dark-matter-candidates).
skip_decisions.rationale: The opposite direction would correspond to neutrino-side structure reading off DM-side structure. While the cosmological-mass-sum constraint from Planck (Σm_ν < 0.12 eV) does feed back into the neutrino-side eV-sterile constraints, that feedback is itself a derivation from cosmological-models / dark-matter cosmological constraints, not from dark-matter-candidates as a structural framework. A direct neutrino-sector-phenomenology derives-from dark-matter-candidates edge would invert the foundational structure (the sterile-neutrino flavor framework is the prior structure; DM-classification reads off it). The Planck mass-sum constraint should be encoded separately, likely via a bears-on edge from cosmological-observations to neutrino-sector-phenomenology if not already present — NOT through this DM↔neutrino edge. Direction set unambiguously to from=DM, to=neutrinos.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 46 — Phase C STEP 4.4 OPENS. SEVENTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; SECOND qualitatively-different post-arc sub-PR. Four (4) resolves edges authored from hyper-k (experimental-program node) to four target cells, formalizing the lab-apparatus-to-channel/observable relationship that Phase A's substrate alone cannot capture: (1) edge-rs-hyper-k-pd-pi0-eplus (3 program predictions: minimal non-SUSY SU(5), SUSY SU(5), minimal non-SUSY SO(10)); (2) edge-rs-hyper-k-pd-Kplus-nubar (2 program predictions: minimal SUSY SU(5), split-spectrum/PQ-suppressed SUSY SU(5)); (3) edge-rs-hyper-k-nu-deltaCP (exclusion_only=true); (4) edge-rs-hyper-k-nu-mass-hierarchy (exclusion_only=true). Total edges 192 → 196; cross-classification edges unchanged at 103 (resolves is a separate edge type, not cross-classification). Existing resolves-edges 0 → 4; this is the FIRST Step 4.4 sub-PR and sets the convention for downstream Step 4.4 work. Once the Phase C MCP worker rebuild lands, find_resolvers(cell_id) and find_discriminating_experiments(prog_a, prog_b) will return non-empty results for the first time. NO new T-convention mint required — the v18 spec §7.4 Hyper-K resolves-edge example is the established encoding pattern; this sub-PR instantiates that pattern against real cell ids and real per-program literature.
changelog.lead_choice_rationale: All three handoff-named leads were inspected (Lead A: FQH cell [15] qs anchor; Lead B: Step 4.4 resolves edges with hyper-k or dune; Lead C: Step 4.2 prediction-level qs sweep). Lead B picked per disposition principle: highest project-level payoff because Step 4.4 has been a closed gap since Phase C began — find_resolvers and find_discriminating_experiments return empty until Step 4.4 lands; opening Step 4.4 makes the flagship Phase C AI-first queries useful for the first time. Lead A (FQH cell [15]) is a small tidy-up with limited payoff (FQH already 16/19 cell-direct anchored); deferred to a future sub-PR. Lead C (Step 4.2) is meaningful but is another sweep of the cell/prediction qs anchoring surface the project has been running for 16 sub-PRs (sub-PRs 30–45); Lead B is the qualitatively-different opening that mirrors sub-PR 45's qualitative shift from cell-direct anchoring to cross-FC edge authoring. Hyper-K picked over DUNE as the first Step 4.4 source-program: Hyper-K has the cleaner channel set (p → e⁺π⁰, p → ν̄K⁺, δ_CP, mass hierarchy) with explicit per-channel sensitivity numbers in the Design Report (arXiv:1805.04163) and Snowmass white paper (arXiv:2203.02029); DUNE's complementary reach is queued for sub-PR 47, which together with sub-PR 46 sets up the canonical find_discriminating_experiments(hyper-k, dune) query at the close of Step 4.4.
changelog.edge_choice_rationale: Four edges chosen to span the resolves-edge design space: 2 with predictions_per_program (channel discriminators between distinct candidate-foundational programs) + 2 with exclusion_only=true (precision measurements that narrow parameter space without distinct competing programs). Target cells deliberately chosen on proton-decay-searches (NOT su5-gut-rep-content as the handoff suggested in passing) because proton-decay-searches has the per-channel cell granularity that resolves edges target naturally; the existing cell-pd-pi0-eplus-falsified-GG and cell-pd-Kplus-nubar-SUSY cells are exactly the channels Hyper-K probes, with per-channel qs anchors already in place (2.4 × 10³⁴ yr for p → e⁺π⁰, 5.9 × 10³³ yr for p → ν̄K⁺). Edges 3+4 (δ_CP, mass hierarchy) on cell-nu-deltaCP and cell-nu-mass-hierarchy demonstrate the exclusion_only branch of the resolves-edge shape — the precision-measurement case where no candidate-foundational program produces a single-valued cited prediction. predictions_per_program selection on edge 1 (3 programs: minimal non-SUSY SU(5), SUSY SU(5), minimal non-SUSY SO(10)) and edge 2 (2 programs: minimal SUSY SU(5), split-spectrum SUSY): every entry's predicted_value cites a program-internal calculation paper per §4 part (2). Flipped SU(5) considered for edge 1 but omitted: although the spec v18 §7.4 example uses it, the Antoniadis-Ellis-Hagelin-Nanopoulos 1987 / Ellis-Garcia-Nanopoulos-Olive 2002 prediction of τ ≈ 10³⁶ yr is the same band as the orbifold-SU(5) and split-spectrum-SUSY entries already represented; adding it would duplicate the structural conclusion without adding a discriminator. Pati-Salam considered for edge 2 but omitted: minimal Pati-Salam renders the proton stable, which cannot be encoded as a quantitative_scale value (no finite lifetime); documented at description level only. su5-gut-rep-content cell-su5-24-NA NOT chosen as target for edge 1 even though that cell hosts the X/Y leptoquarks structurally responsible for p → e⁺π⁰: the cell encodes the rep-theoretic content (24 = SU(5) adjoint = 12 SM gauge + 12 X/Y), not the experimental observable; proton-decay-searches is the FC that organizes by decay channel and is the natural resolves-edge target.

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase C §4 part (1) — sensitivity is a literature value
firewall_self_check.question: For each of the 4 resolves edges, is the sensitivity value (proton-decay lifetime reach, CPV discovery σ-level, mass-hierarchy resolution σ-level) directly stated in the Hyper-K Design Report (arXiv:1805.04163) or the Snowmass white paper (arXiv:2203.02029), or am I reconstructing it from raw exposure / efficiency calculations?
firewall_self_check.answer: YES for all four. Edge 1: '10³⁵ yr for p → π⁰e⁺' explicitly stated in Snowmass §II (arXiv:2203.02029) citing Design Report §10. Edge 2: '3 × 10³⁴ years for p → ν̄K⁺' explicitly stated in same Snowmass §II. Edge 3: '≥5σ CL discovery of CPV for ≈57% of δ_CP values' stated in Design Report §6 (cited by Snowmass §VII). Edge 4: '≥3σ mass-ordering resolution from atmospheric ν' stated in Design Report §8. No cartographer reconstruction; all four sensitivity values are direct copies from the cited design literature.

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase C §4 part (2) — per-program predictions are program-internal calculations
firewall_self_check.question: For each entry in predictions_per_program (5 total: 3 in edge 1, 2 in edge 2), does the predicted_value's citation array name the program-internal paper where the prediction is derived, or am I citing a meta-review that simply mentions the prediction?
firewall_self_check.answer: YES for all five. Edge 1 entry 1 (Minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5)): cites Langacker 1981 Phys. Rep. 72:185 (original program-internal review) + Murayama-Pierce 2002 (program-internal refined calculation). Edge 1 entry 2 (SUSY SU(5)): cites Hisano 2022 PTEP 2022:12B104 (program-internal SUSY-GUT review) + Hisano-Murayama-Yanagida 1992 PRL 69:1014 (original SUSY-GUT scale calculation). Edge 1 entry 3 (Minimal non-SUSY SO(10)): cites Babu-Khan 2015 arXiv:1507.06712 (the program-internal model construction and lifetime calculation). Edge 2 entry 1 (Minimal SUSY SU(5) dim-5): cites Babu-Gogoladze-Ün 2022 arXiv:2012.14411 (program-internal upper-bound calculation with LHC constraints) + Hisano-Murayama-Yanagida 1993 hep-ph/9207279 (foundational program-internal dim-5 calculation) + Ellis-Evans-Nagata-Olive-Velasco-Sevilla 2020. Edge 2 entry 2 (Split-spectrum SUSY SU(5)): cites Hisano-Kobayashi-Nagata 2012 arXiv:1204.6274 (program-internal heavy-sfermion prolongation calculation).

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase C §4 part (3) — sensitivity cites the experimental design

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase B/C §2.5 self-check restated for resolves-edge authoring

firewall_self_check.answer: YES for all four. Each edge's experimental target is named explicitly in the cited Hyper-K design literature: proton-decay channels (p → e⁺π⁰, p → ν̄K⁺) are listed in Design Report §10 with per-channel sensitivity targets; δ_CP and mass ordering are listed in Design Report §6 and §8 with explicit CL-detection projections.
