The cross-FC structure (proton-decay-searches FC, neutrino-sector-phenomenology FC) provides the cells but does not motivate the experiment-to-cell choice; the experimental design does.

resolves_edges_added.discriminator_role: HK reach (10³⁵ yr) sits in the SUSY-SU(5) prediction band (10³⁵⁻³⁶ yr) and the SO(10) upper-bound band (few × 10³⁵ yr); minimal non-SUSY SU(5) is already excluded; positive HK signal would discriminate SUSY-SU(5)/SO(10) from extra-dim SU(5) (~10³⁶ yr, out of reach).
HK reach (3 × 10³⁴ yr) sits below minimal SUSY SU(5)'s upper bound (1.1 × 10³⁵ yr) and above split-spectrum SUSY's prediction (~10³⁶ yr): the experiment discriminates which branch of SUSY survives.

resolves_edges_added.sensitivity: ≥5σ CL CPV discovery for ~57% of δ_CP true values; σ(δ_CP) ≲ 7° near maximal
resolves_edges_added.rationale_for_exclusion_only: leptonic δ_CP is being measured directly, not predicted to a single value by any candidate-foundational program admissible under §4 part (2); the precision measurement narrows parameter space without distinguishing competing per-program predictions.

resolves_edges_added.sensitivity: ≥3σ CL ordering resolution from atmospheric ν (10-yr run); ≥5σ in joint atmospheric+beam analysis (θ_23-octant-dependent)
resolves_edges_added.rationale_for_exclusion_only: NH vs IH is a binary determination of mass ordering; specific flavor-symmetry models prefer one ordering or the other under additional assumptions but do not generically produce §4-part-(2)-admissible per-program citations.

changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition: Five inline observations/corrections documented: (1) HANDOFF NAMED su5-gut-rep-content cell-su5-24-NA as the proton-decay target for the resolves edge, but the actual proton-decay-searches FC — not named in the handoff — hosts per-channel cells (cell-pd-pi0-eplus-falsified-GG, cell-pd-Kplus-nubar-SUSY, plus cell-pd-Hyper-K-future, cell-pd-DUNE-K-channel-sensitivity, cell-pd-SO10, cell-pd-flipped-SU5, cell-pd-Pati-Salam, etc.) that are the natural resolves-edge targets. The proton-decay-searches FC was authored at some earlier point and is not surfaced in the handoff prose. Decision: target the per-channel cells on proton-decay-searches, which gives cleaner cell-level alignment between experimental observable and FC structure. su5-gut-rep-content cells host rep-theoretic content (24 = adjoint = 12 gauge + 12 X/Y), not experimental channels. (2) HANDOFF NAMED 'around 1.3 × 10³⁵ yr sensitivity for p → e⁺π⁰' inline; the Snowmass white paper and Design Report state 10³⁵ yr (the 1.3 × 10³⁵-yr figure appears elsewhere as a less-cited variant). Decision: use the canonically-cited 10³⁵-yr figure with citations to both Design Report §10 and Snowmass white paper §II. (3) HANDOFF NAMED timeline 'planned-by-2030' for Hyper-K (HK starts 2027); the spec §7.4 example uses 'planned-by-2035'. The timeline enum description specifies 'planned-by-YYYY = first physics expected by the named year', so planned-by-2030 is the correct bucket for first physics 2027. Decision: use planned-by-2030 across all four edges, matching the enum's stated semantics rather than the spec example. The spec example may itself be using a 'sensitivity-fully-achieved' rather than 'first-physics' convention; the explicit enum description is binding when it disagrees with an example. (4) HANDOFF SUGGESTED 5–10 resolves edges for hyper-k (juno+atmospheric, supernova-burst, etc.); decision is 4 edges (the two cleanest proton-decay channels + the two cleanest neutrino-oscillation observables). Supernova-burst not authored because no open frontier or cell is currently structured as 'future galactic supernova ν burst observation' — the existing cell-nu-SN1987A-neutrinos is the historical event, not a future observable. Solar-ν and reactor-ν channels not authored because no cell directly hosts the open-question precision-measurement state they would resolve. Atmospheric θ_23 octant not authored as a separate edge because the cell-nu-theta23-atmospheric is a precision-measurement cell already at 'measured-tension' status with a small qs anchor (sin²θ_23 = 0.561); a resolves edge would duplicate the shape of edges 3+4 without adding discriminator power. (5) HANDOFF SUGGESTED 'consider supernova-burst observation, matter-antimatter-asymmetry frontier'; supernova-burst skipped per (4); matter-antimatter-asymmetry frontier skipped because Hyper-K's connection to leptogenesis is indirect (via δ_CP correlation with high-scale CP) and the Hyper-K Design Report does not publish a leptogenesis-specific sensitivity — §4 part (3) would fail (sensitivity cannot be cited from the design report). The structural δ_CP↔leptogenesis connection is real but lives on the leptogenesis-side literature, not on the Hyper-K-experiment side, so authoring it as a resolves edge would invert the firewall. All five inline corrections recorded per the active inline-correction precedent (sub-PRs 39+40 major scale; sub-PR 45 minor scale) and the disposition principle — the chat makes all per-entry decisions autonomously.
changelog.schema_candidates_status_observations: v16 schema candidate #6 (paired_cell_refs on edges) remains NOT PROMOTED. Phase C and resolves edges in particular do not encounter friction with cell_refs absence (resolves edges have a single target-cell semantics, not a paired-cell-refs need; the from/to + the four substructure fields fully capture the edge content). Sub-PR 45 noted mild friction; sub-PR 46 sees none. Recommendation: continue monitoring; not promotable yet. No new schema candidates identified by sub-PR 46. The v18 spec §3 resolves-edge structure is sufficient as authored. One narrow observation: the v18 timeline enum has half-decade granularity (2030/2035/2040) which is coarser than many design-report timelines (which often specify by-year). This is an acceptable coarseness because the project's audience is structural-pattern recognition over decades, not project-management timeline tracking; promotion to finer granularity would be premature.
skip_decisions: DID NOT author lead A (FQH cell [15] cell-fqh-fibonacci-predicted qs anchor): deferred to a future sub-PR; FQH FC is already 16/19 cell-direct anchored, and the structural lift is modest. The handoff's noted bundled cs cleanup on FQH cells [17][18] (currently cs=MISSING, content='structurally-excluded') is also deferred; would require either Phase-A-only cs assignment OR a forced_by edge authoring against an upstream structural constraint, both of which expand scope away from Step 4.4 opening.

DID NOT author lead C (Step 4.2 prediction-level quantitative_scale sweep on a high-prediction-density FC): remains the most-neglected Phase C step, but with lower payoff than opening Step 4.4. Queued for after Step 4.4 closes (probably sub-PRs 50+).

Authoring such a cell on a compact-astrophysical-objects FC would be valuable but expands Step 4.4 scope; queued separately.

DID NOT author edges to matter-antimatter-asymmetry frontier: Hyper-K's δ_CP measurement informs leptogenesis indirectly, but the Design Report does not publish a leptogenesis-specific sensitivity; §4 part (3) admissibility fails because the sensitivity would not be cited from the experimental program's design.

DID NOT author the Pati-Salam predictions_per_program entry on edge 2 (p → ν̄K⁺): minimal Pati-Salam predicts the proton stable, which has no finite quantitative_scale value (infinite lifetime cannot be encoded); documented at description-level only.

The Step 4.4 sub-PRs need a worker rebuild to surface find_resolvers and find_discriminating_experiments; this is queued for after Step 4.5 closes (consolidated rebuild).

changelog.summary: sub-PR 47 — Phase C STEP 4.4 continues. EIGHTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; SECOND Step-4.4 sub-PR. Four (4) resolves edges authored from dune (experimental-program node) to the same four target cells already resolved by hyper-k in sub-PR 46, completing the first discriminating-resolves-pair cluster (HK ↔ DUNE) on the project's most-cited Phase C demonstration targets: (1) edge-rs-dune-pd-pi0-eplus to cell-pd-pi0-eplus-falsified-GG (3 program predictions, transferred from sub-PR 46 HK edge per T30 detector-independence principle); (2) edge-rs-dune-pd-Kplus-nubar to cell-pd-Kplus-nubar-SUSY (2 program predictions, transferred from sub-PR 46 HK edge); (3) edge-rs-dune-nu-deltaCP (exclusion_only=true); (4) edge-rs-dune-nu-mass-hierarchy (exclusion_only=true). Total edges 196 → 200; resolves edges 4 → 8; cross-classification edges unchanged at 103. FIRST instance of the discriminating-resolves-pair structural pattern anticipated in sub-PR 46's handoff: 4 cells now carry parallel resolves edges from HK and DUNE with quantitatively-distinct sensitivity profiles, sourced from independent detector technologies. Once the consolidated Phase C MCP worker rebuild lands at the close of Step 4.5, find_discriminating_experiments(hyper-k, dune) will return all four target cells as its first non-empty result — the FIRST concrete realization of the AI-first discriminating-experiments query that PROJECT_GOAL_SUPPLEMENT §1.2 and §2.4 specify as a flagship Phase C output. T30 (sub-PR 46 resolves-edge encoding) applied without modification on edges 1+2; sigma_deviation sensitivity encoding applied on edges 3+4 (no units, no log10 per v19 schema Rules 28+29). Inline-correction-precedent reactivated at major scale: FOUR corrections to the handoff documented in handoff_recommendation_disposition (DUNE δ_CP coverage actually 50% not 75%; mass-hierarchy timeline 2 years not 6 years; DUNE TDR arXiv numbers were wrong in handoff; 'DUNE stronger on p → ν̄K⁺' framing not supported by published 90% CL sensitivities). Methodological observation surfaced: same-target-multi-source resolves pattern is now first-class, expected to recur for every shared-target experiment pair (LIGO + LISA on gravitational-wave cells; CMB-S4 + Simons-Observatory + Planck on cosmology cells; future axion + EDM experiments on strong-CP cells). The category is named 'discriminating-resolves-pair'; sub-PR 47 establishes 4 instances. Validator: 0 new schema errors, 0 new rule errors, 0 warnings (4 legacy `constrains` errors tolerated per PROJECT_INFRASTRUCTURE §2).
changelog.lead_choice_rationale: Lead A (DUNE resolves edges paired with sub-PR 46's HK edges) picked per disposition principle — strongly favored in handoff and confirmed at inspection: Lead A directly enables the AI-first discriminating-experiments query precedent that Phase C's project goal calls for. The HK-DUNE pair on proton-decay channels + neutrino-oscillation observables is the cleanest concrete demonstration target the project has had at any point; it is the first time find_discriminating_experiments will return non-empty results when the worker rebuilds. Lead B (different experimental-program node: LISA / CMB-S4 / JUNO / DESI / Rubin-LSST) was inspected: each is structurally valuable but defers the discriminating-experiments precedent by 1+ sub-PRs. Diversification across physics domains is welcome but the discriminating-experiments precedent is a once-only opening; doing it before further diversification is the higher-payoff sequencing. Lead C (carry-over qs anchor on FQH cell [15] or Step 4.2 prediction-level sweep) was inspected: smaller scope, lower project-level payoff than either A or B; deferred. The handoff's prediction of 'fewer corrections than sub-PR 46 (perhaps 0-2 minor)' turned out to be optimistic: 4 corrections were needed (3 numerical, 1 framing). All were inline-corrected during authoring, consistent with the disposition principle.
changelog.edge_choice_rationale: Four edges chosen as direct same-target counterparts to sub-PR 46's HK edges: each DUNE edge resolves the same cell as the corresponding HK edge, with sensitivity and citations updated to reflect DUNE's design report and EPJ C oscillation paper. Per-program prediction substructures on edges 1+2 are transferred without modification from the HK edges, per T30's detector-independence principle: program-internal predictions (minimal non-SUSY GG SU(5), SUSY SU(5), minimal non-SUSY SO(10) for p → e⁺π⁰; minimal SUSY SU(5), split-spectrum SUSY for p → ν̄K⁺) are calculations of what each theory predicts the proton lifetime to be — they do not depend on which experiment is doing the measuring. What differs between the HK and DUNE edges is only the experimental sensitivity. This is the cleanest illustration of why find_discriminating_experiments is a meaningful AI-first query: the per-program predictions are the COMMON REFERENCE that two experiments' sensitivities resolve against. Edges 3+4 (δ_CP, mass hierarchy) use exclusion_only=true, mirroring the HK edges 3+4 for the same firewall reason: these are SM-parameter measurements, not program discriminators with single-valued cited predictions. The descriptions on edges 3+4 articulate how DUNE and HK form a discriminating-resolves-pair in the systematics-orthogonal sense (DUNE matter-effect-driven; HK statistical-power-driven) without claiming program-internal discriminator content.

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase C §4 part (1) — sensitivity is a literature value
firewall_self_check.question: For each of the 4 DUNE resolves edges, is the sensitivity value (proton-decay lifetime reach, CPV discovery σ-level, mass-hierarchy resolution σ-level) directly stated in the DUNE TDR Vol II (arXiv:2002.03005) or the Abi et al. 2020 EPJ C 80:978 oscillation paper (arXiv:2006.16043), or am I reconstructing it from raw exposure / efficiency calculations?
firewall_self_check.answer: YES for all four. Edge 1: 'τ > ~10³⁴ yr at 400 kt·yr (90% CL)' from TDR Vol II §5 with companion Monte-Carlo confirmation in Alt-Radics-Rubbia 2021 JHEP 04:243 quoting 0.9-1.1 × 10³⁴ yr at the same exposure. Edge 2: 'DUNE sets a 90% CL lower limit on proton decay lifetime [p → ν̄K⁺] at 1.3 × 10³⁴ years' directly quoted from TDR Vol II §5. Edge 3: '3σ (5σ) after exposure of 5 (10) years, for 50% of all δ_CP values' directly from Abi 2020 EPJ C abstract. Edge 4: 'resolve neutrino mass ordering to 5σ for all δ_CP after 2 years' directly from Abi 2020 EPJ C abstract. No cartographer reconstruction; all four sensitivity values are direct copies from the DUNE collaboration's own published projections.

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase C §4 part (2) — per-program predictions are program-internal calculations
firewall_self_check.question: For each entry in predictions_per_program (5 total: 3 in edge 1, 2 in edge 2), does the predicted_value's citation array name the program-internal paper where the prediction is derived, or am I citing a meta-review that simply mentions the prediction? Note: per-program substructures are transferred from sub-PR 46 HK edges without modification per T30 detector-independence.
firewall_self_check.answer: YES for all five. The transferred substructures carry their original program-internal citations: Langacker 1981 Phys. Rep. 72:185 + Murayama-Pierce 2002 (minimal GG SU(5)); Hisano 2022 PTEP 2022:12B104 + Hisano-Murayama-Yanagida 1992 PRL 69:1014 (SUSY SU(5)); Babu-Khan 2015 arXiv:1507.06712 (minimal non-SUSY SO(10)); Babu-Gogoladze-Ün 2022 arXiv:2012.14411 + Hisano-Murayama-Yanagida 1993 hep-ph/9207279 + Ellis et al. 2020 (minimal SUSY SU(5) dim-5); Hisano-Kobayashi-Nagata 2012 arXiv:1204.6274 (split-spectrum SUSY). The detector-independence principle is the rationale for transfer without re-derivation: program-internal calculations like 'minimal SUSY SU(5)'s dim-5 proton lifetime' are properties of the program, not properties of how it is measured. T30 (sub-PR 46 mint) formalizes this; sub-PR 47 instantiates it.

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase C §4 part (3) — sensitivity cites the experimental design
firewall_self_check.question: Does each edge's sensitivity.citations array name the DUNE TDR or DUNE-collaboration-published projection (the experimental-program-internal documentation), rather than a third-party paper that simply reports the design specs?
firewall_self_check.answer: YES for all four. Every sensitivity.citations array primarily cites DUNE collaboration publications: TDR Vol II (arXiv:2002.03005, all 4 edges); EPJ C 80:978 (arXiv:2006.16043, edges 3+4 — the dedicated DUNE oscillation paper). The companion citation on edges 1+2 (Alt-Radics-Rubbia 2021 JHEP 04:243, arXiv:2010.06552) is authored by DUNE-affiliated scientists and provides the full-simulation neural-network sensitivity study that the TDR Vol II §5 cites as its underlying analysis basis; it is collaboration-internal in the relevant sense. No third-party sensitivity-estimate citations.

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase B/C §2.5 self-check restated for resolves-edge authoring
firewall_self_check.question: Would I author each of these resolves edges if I had only the DUNE TDR Vol II + Abi 2020 EPJ C + Alt-Radics-Rubbia 2021 JHEP + the same program-internal calculation papers used in sub-PR 46 in front of me, and not the v83 cross-classification graph? Specifically, would the DUNE-to-target-cell connection be defensible from the experimental design alone, without reasoning from sub-PR 46's HK precedent?
firewall_self_check.answer: YES for all four. Each edge's experimental target is named explicitly in the cited DUNE design literature: proton-decay channels (p → e⁺π⁰, p → ν̄K⁺) are listed in TDR Vol II §5 with explicit per-channel sensitivity projections; δ_CP and mass ordering are the headline scientific deliverables of the long-baseline oscillation analysis in Abi 2020 EPJ C abstract and TDR Vol II §3. The connection from dune node to each target cell is sourced from DUNE's own documentation; sub-PR 46's HK edges provide encoding-pattern continuity (T30) and the cell-id structure (which is fixed independently of either experiment), but the DUNE-to-cell links would be authorable from the DUNE literature alone. The cross-FC structure (proton-decay-searches FC, neutrino-sector-phenomenology FC) provides the target cells but does not motivate the experiment-to-cell choice; the experimental design does.

The mass-ordering discriminating-resolves-pair is the strongest of the four: DUNE's baseline gives a systematic advantage that exposure-scaling cannot close for HK. Fourth discriminating-resolves-pair instance.

changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition:
FOUR inline corrections to the sub-PR 47 handoff documented here per the inline-correction precedent (sub-PRs 39+40 major scale; sub-PR 45 minor scale; sub-PR 46 major scale; sub-PR 47 major scale, continuing the major-scale escalation when handoffs anticipate uncharted-territory work):

(1) Handoff said DUNE δ_CP sensitivity is '5σ over 75% of δ_CP values for ≥10 years'. ACTUAL canonical value from Abi et al. 2020 EPJ C 80:978 (arXiv:2006.16043) abstract: '3σ (5σ) after exposure of 5 (10) years, for 50% of all δ_CP values.' The 75% framing appears in some joint-DUNE+T2HK or 3σ-coverage discussions in the literature but is NOT the canonical Abi 2020 single-experiment 5σ figure. Encoded as 5σ (sensitivity floor); the 50% coverage statement appears in the description and citation text. Rationale: faithful rendering of the cited DUNE-collaboration projection.

(2) Handoff said DUNE mass-hierarchy timeline is '>5σ at 6 years from baseline matter effects'. ACTUAL canonical value from Abi 2020 EPJ C 80:978 abstract: '5σ for ALL δ_CP values after 2 years of running with the nominal detector design.' DUNE achieves this MUCH faster than the handoff anticipated (2 yr, not 6 yr). The 6-year number may be from a low-exposure DUNE study (arXiv:2109.01304) under non-nominal beam configurations. Encoded as 5σ with the 2-year exposure stated in the description; this is also faster than HK's ≥3σ-at-10-yr atmospheric-only reach, sharpening the DUNE-HK discriminating-pair narrative on this observable.

(3) Handoff cited DUNE TDR vol arXiv numbers as '2002.02967 (vol 1), 2002.02968 (vol 2), 2002.02969 (vol 3), 2002.02970 (vol 4)'. ACTUAL arXiv numbers per the DUNE experimental-program node's own published citations and confirmed by independent web search: Vol I 2002.02967 (handoff correct); Vol II **2002.03005** (handoff said 2002.02968 — WRONG); Vol III **2002.02980** (handoff said 2002.02969 — WRONG); Vol IV **2002.03010** (handoff said 2002.02970 — WRONG). Encoded with the correct arXiv numbers. Rationale: citations must be accurate per §4 admissibility; the handoff's incorrect numbers would have produced non-resolving arXiv identifiers in a reader's hands.

(4) Handoff's framing 'DUNE stronger on p → ν̄K⁺ due to LAr K⁺ identification' is not supported by the published 90% CL sensitivities. ACTUAL numbers: HK 3 × 10³⁴ yr at 20-yr exposure (sub-PR 46 edge), DUNE 1.3 × 10³⁴ yr at 10 yr / 400 kt·yr (this sub-PR). HK is MARGINALLY HIGHER in 90% CL sensitivity terms. DUNE's LAr K⁺ advantage is real but lies in DETECTION QUALITY (quasi-background-free per-event identification) rather than raw 90% CL reach. Encoded faithfully in the description: 'DUNE's 1.3 × 10³⁴ yr reach at 10 yr is modestly below Hyper-K's 3 × 10³⁴ yr reach at 20 yr, but DUNE's near-zero-background regime makes any DUNE detection a clean discovery rather than a statistical excess.' Rationale: the firewall test does not permit cartographer narrative overclaim; the published numbers are what go in.

Total: 4 inline corrections at major scale. Anticipated count from sub-PR 47 handoff was 0-2 minor; actual was 4 major. The handoff itself flagged this scaling concern with 'sub-PR 47 SHOULD see fewer corrections than sub-PR 46 (perhaps 0-2 minor)' as conditional expectation rather than firm projection; the conditional did not hold, but the inline-correction precedent governed the response. Disposition principle applied throughout: literature precedence over handoff narrative.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 48 — Phase C STEP 4.4 continues. NINTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; THIRD Step-4.4 sub-PR. Four (4) resolves edges authored from juno (experimental-program node) to four target cells across two FCs: neutrino-sector-phenomenology (cell-nu-mass-hierarchy, cell-nu-theta12-solar, cell-nu-Dm21-squared) and proton-decay-searches (cell-pd-Kplus-nubar-SUSY). Edges 1+2 (mass hierarchy and p → ν̄K⁺) complete the FIRST three-way discriminating-resolves clusters in the project: HK ↔ DUNE ↔ JUNO via three orthogonal detector technologies (water-Cherenkov, LAr-TPC, liquid-scintillator), with three orthogonal methodologies (statistical-power atmospheric, matter-driven beam, vacuum-interference reactor). Edges 3+4 (sin²θ_12 and Δm²_21 precision) record JUNO's world-leading sub-0.5% precision targets and introduce a new precision-measurement-resolves sensitivity encoding (T31 mint). T30 (sub-PR 46) applied without modification on edge 2's predictions_per_program (transferred verbatim from HK/DUNE per detector-independence). Cumulative T-conventions: 32 active (T1–T14, T17–T31; T15 retired, T16 dropped). 0 new validator errors, 0 new warnings.
changelog.lead_choice_rationale: Lead A (JUNO resolves edges) picked per disposition principle — strongly favored in handoff and confirmed at inspection: Lead A directly enables the strongest concrete realization of the AI-first goal yet possible (three-way discriminating-resolves cluster query through `find_discriminating_experiments(hyper-k, dune)` returning 4 cells, `(hyper-k, juno)` returning 2 cells, `(dune, juno)` returning 2 cells, and `find_resolvers(cell-nu-mass-hierarchy)` returning three experiments with three orthogonal physical channels after the next worker rebuild). Lead B (gw-ground-network + LISA pair) deferred to sub-PR 49+ — opens a new physics domain but creates only 2-way clusters. Lead C (other standalone) and Lead D (carry-over) lower per-sub-PR payoff than the three-way-cluster completion.
changelog.edge_choice_rationale: Four edges chosen as the full headline JUNO physics program: (1) mass ordering via vacuum-oscillation interference — the JUNO flagship measurement; (2) p → ν̄K⁺ via three-fold coincidence — second flagship; (3) sin²θ_12 sub-0.5% precision — JUNO is the world-leading experiment; (4) Δm²_21 sub-0.5% precision — JUNO is the world-leading experiment. Edges 1+2 are the structurally-headline deliverables (three-way discriminating-resolves cluster completions). Edges 3+4 are JUNO-specific precision targets with no HK/DUNE analogues at these cells (no cluster forms) but they establish the precision-measurement-resolves encoding pattern (T31 mint) for future precision experiments (KATRIN absolute neutrino mass, future neutrino-precision experiments, future kaon precision experiments). The fifth potential edge on |Δm²_31| precision was skipped because JUNO is sub-percent on this parameter rather than the world-leading 0.5% reached for the solar-sector parameters, and |Δm²_31| is more naturally a beam-experiment discriminator (best determined by DUNE-style long-baseline experiments).

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase C §4 part (1) — sensitivity is a literature value
firewall_self_check.question: For each of the 4 JUNO resolves edges, is the sensitivity value (mass-ordering σ-level, proton-decay lifetime reach, precision target on sin²θ_12 and Δm²_21) directly stated in the JUNO collaboration's own published projections, or am I reconstructing it from raw exposure / efficiency calculations?
firewall_self_check.answer: YES for all four. Edge 1: '3σ median sensitivity to reject the wrong mass ordering hypothesis can be reached with an exposure to approximately 6.5 years × 26.6 GW thermal power' directly quoted from Abusleme et al. 2025 Chin. Phys. C 49:033104 (arXiv:2405.18008) abstract. Edge 2: 'The estimated sensitivity based on 200 kton-years exposure is 9.6 × 10³³ years' directly quoted from Abusleme et al. 2023 Chin. Phys. C 47:113002 (arXiv:2212.08502) abstract. Edges 3+4: 'the Δm²_21 and sin²θ_12 oscillation parameters will be determined to 0.5% precision or better in six years of data collection' directly quoted from Abusleme et al. 2022 Chin. Phys. C 46:123001 (arXiv:2204.13249) abstract. No cartographer reconstruction; all four sensitivity values are direct copies from the JUNO collaboration's own published projections.

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase C §4 part (2) — per-program predictions are program-internal calculations
firewall_self_check.question: For edge 2 (p → ν̄K⁺), the per-program predictions cite Babu-Gogoladze-Ün 2022, Hisano-Murayama-Yanagida 1993, and Hisano-Kobayashi-Nagata 2012. Are these program-internal calculations of the SUSY-SU(5) p → ν̄K⁺ lifetime, or are they reviews/syntheses?
firewall_self_check.answer: Program-internal calculations. Babu-Gogoladze-Ün 2022 derive the minimal SUSY SU(5) upper bound τ ≤ 1.1 × 10³⁵ yr from the SUSY-SU(5) dim-5 operator structure with explicit scalar-mass dependence. Hisano-Murayama-Yanagida 1993 is the foundational SUSY-SU(5) dim-5 calculation. Hisano-Kobayashi-Nagata 2012 derives the heavy-sfermion enhancement of the SUSY-SU(5) p → ν̄K⁺ lifetime. All three are program-internal SUSY-SU(5) papers. The per-program prediction blocks on edge 2 are transferred without modification from the matching HK edge (edge-rs-hyper-k-pd-Kplus-nubar, sub-PR 46) and DUNE edge (edge-rs-dune-pd-Kplus-nubar, sub-PR 47) per the T30 detector-independence principle: the program-internal predicted lifetime for minimal SUSY SU(5) is 1.1 × 10³⁵ yr regardless of which detector is measuring. Edges 1, 3, 4 carry exclusion_only=true with empty predictions_per_program — no candidate-foundational program makes a discriminating program-internal prediction of mass-ordering or PMNS-matrix precision values at the required §4 admissibility level.

firewall_self_check.test_part: Phase C §4 part (3) — timeline is documented
firewall_self_check.question: Why is the timeline encoded as 'running' rather than 'planned-by-2030' as the HK/DUNE edges use?
firewall_self_check.answer: JUNO's operational_period in the experimental-program node payload records start_year=2025 with ongoing=True. The node description states 'Filling completed late 2024; physics operations underway.' Further confirmation from the November 2025 JUNO collaboration preprints (arXiv:2511.14590 'Initial performance results of the JUNO detector' and arXiv:2511.14593 'First measurement of reactor neutrino oscillations at JUNO'). Per the v18 schema's resolves_timeline enum (deployed = already taking data; running = currently operating with scheduled runs ahead; planned-by-YYYY = funded/approved with first physics expected by the named year), the correct encoding for JUNO is 'running' — it is currently operating with scheduled future runs (the 6.5-yr mass-ordering goal still ahead). The HK ↔ DUNE ↔ JUNO three-way cluster therefore spans two timeline buckets: JUNO is 'running' now, HK and DUNE are 'planned-by-2030' (HK first physics 2027 per Design Report; DUNE first physics ~2029-2030).

firewall_self_check.test_part: §2.5 self-check — independence from existing graph structure
firewall_self_check.question: For each new edge: would I author this JUNO-resolves connection if I had only the JUNO Yellow Book + 2022 JUNO Physics and Detector + 2022 Sub-percent Precision paper + 2023 Proton Decay paper + 2025 Mass Ordering paper and the cell-content of the target cell in front of me, and not the v84 cross-classification graph or the sub-PR 46+47 HK+DUNE edges? Specifically, is the sensitivity claim sourced from each named JUNO paper (in §-named sections / abstract), is each per-program prediction sourced from the same program-internal paper sub-PRs 46+47 used, and does the JUNO-vs-DUNE-vs-HK three-way sensitivity comparison emerge structurally from independent literature rather than being asserted by me?
firewall_self_check.answer: YES. Each edge would be authorable from the four JUNO collaboration papers alone: edge 1 from arXiv:2405.18008 abstract; edge 2 from arXiv:2212.08502 abstract; edges 3+4 from arXiv:2204.13249 abstract. The per-program predictions on edge 2 cite the same program-internal SUSY-SU(5) papers used at sub-PRs 46+47 (Babu-Gogoladze-Ün 2022, Hisano-Murayama-Yanagida 1993, Hisano-Kobayashi-Nagata 2012) — these predictions are NOT inferred from the existing HK/DUNE edges; they are the SAME program-internal calculations cited THERE because the underlying SUSY-SU(5) framework is one program with one lifetime prediction. The three-way comparison narrative in the descriptions does reference the HK and DUNE edges, but each independently sourced sensitivity is what makes the three-way structural comparison emerge — the cartographer is noting an emergent structural fact, not asserting one.

JUNO's mechanism (quasi-vacuum oscillation interference at 53-km baseline, completely δ_CP- and θ_23-independent) is methodologically ORTHOGONAL to HK (statistical-power atmospheric ν) and DUNE (matter-driven 1285-km beam). Completion of FIRST three-way discriminating-resolves cluster in the project.

JUNO's detection mechanism (three-fold timing-coincidence in liquid scintillator: K⁺ pulse + K⁺ → μ⁺ν delayed pulse + μ⁺ → e⁺ν̄ν further-delayed pulse) is the THIRD detection technology after water-Cherenkov (HK) and LAr-TPC (DUNE). JUNO's 90% CL reach (9.6 × 10³³ yr) is the lowest of the three, but the systematic profile is independent. Completion of SECOND three-way discriminating-resolves cluster in the project.

resolves_edges_added.T31_mint_purpose: First instance of precision-measurement-resolves sensitivity encoding: kind matches the cell's qs kind (dimensionless), value records the current central value (0.307 from NuFIT 6.0), uncertainty records the achievable projected precision (±0.0015 = 0.5% relative); bound_direction omitted because this is a precision target, not a discovery floor. Distinguishes from the sigma-deviation / discovery-floor encoding pattern of edges 1+2 (and all sub-PR 46+47 edges).
