Follows the cell's T17 convention (kind=mass + units="eV²" for the squared-mass quantity) as the dimensional state, then applies T31 (central value + projected uncertainty) for the precision-measurement encoding.

T_convention_minted.name: precision-measurement-resolves sensitivity encoding
T_convention_minted.description: Precision-measurement-resolves sensitivity encoding. For a resolves edge where the experimental program targets the precision improvement on an observable rather than a discovery threshold or σ-level discrimination, the sensitivity quantitative_scale field is encoded with: (a) kind matching the target cell's qs kind (dimensionless, mass, length, etc.); (b) units mirroring the cell's units when present (per T17 for squared-dimensional kinds); (c) value recording the current canonical central best-fit value (per the cell's existing qs); (d) uncertainty recording the achievable projected precision after the experiment's design exposure; (e) bound_direction OMITTED because this is a precision target, not a discovery bound; (f) citations naming the experimental-program-internal projection paper. Companion shape: predictions_per_program=[] and exclusion_only=true, since precision measurements of PMNS-matrix-like parameters do not discriminate between candidate-foundational programs that make competing single-valued point predictions admissible under §4 part (2). Cumulative T-conventions in active register after T31 mint: 32 (T1–T14, T17–T31; T15 retired, T16 dropped).
T_convention_minted.structural_distinction_from_T30: T30 encodes discovery-floor sensitivity (bound_direction populated; lower for lifetime / coupling lower-bounds, lower for σ-level discrimination floors). T31 encodes precision-improvement target (bound_direction omitted; the value + uncertainty pair mirrors the cell's existing qs, with the uncertainty shrunk to the projected JUNO-scale precision). Both can coexist on the same experimental-program node's family of resolves edges (e.g., JUNO uses T30 for edges 1+2 and T31 for edges 3+4).

changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition:
Three minor inline corrections to the sub-PR 48 handoff documented per the inline-correction precedent (sub-PRs 39+40 major scale; sub-PR 45 minor scale; sub-PR 46 major scale; sub-PR 47 major scale; sub-PR 48 minor scale, lower volume because JUNO design literature is more stable than DUNE's was at sub-PR 47):

(1) Handoff cited target-cell candidates as `cell-nu-theta12` and `cell-nu-deltaMsq21`. ACTUAL cell ids in v84 on neutrino-sector-phenomenology: `cell-nu-theta12-solar` and `cell-nu-Dm21-squared` (verified at inspection-time via `bash curl` on the raw v84 data file). Encoded with the actual cell ids. Rationale: target-id resolution via Rule 32 requires existing cell ids; the handoff's hypothetical names would have failed validation.

(2) Handoff suggested the 2022 JUNO update citation is 'Abusleme et al. 2022 J. Phys. G 49:108002, arXiv:2212.08502'. ACTUAL: the J. Phys. G 49:108002 reference appears to be GARBLED — no JUNO paper with this exact journal locator exists. arXiv:2212.08502 IS a real JUNO paper but it's the 2023 Proton Decay paper (Chin. Phys. C 47:113002), not a 2022 J. Phys. G update. The correct 2022 JUNO comprehensive update is 'Abusleme et al. 2022 JUNO Physics and Detector Prog. Part. Nucl. Phys. 123:103927, arXiv:2104.02565' — already present in the v84 juno node citations. The 2022 Sub-percent Oscillation Parameters paper is 'Abusleme et al. 2022 Chin. Phys. C 46:123001, arXiv:2204.13249'. Both encoded correctly in the edge citations.

(3) Handoff hedged between `running` and `planned-by-2030` for JUNO timeline ('Verify against the latest JUNO operational-status announcement'). VERIFICATION: v84 juno node payload records operational_period.start_year=2025, ongoing=True, with description stating 'Filling completed late 2024; physics operations underway' — further confirmed by November 2025 JUNO preprints arXiv:2511.14590 and arXiv:2511.14593. The correct encoding is `running` (currently operating with scheduled future runs). Encoded as `running` on all four edges. This makes sub-PR 48 the FIRST sub-PR with timeline=`running` on resolves edges; HK/DUNE edges all use `planned-by-2030`. The HK ↔ DUNE ↔ JUNO three-way clusters therefore span two timeline buckets, which is structurally informative: JUNO produces first results soonest, HK/DUNE catch up by ~2030.

(4) [Bonus correction, non-firewall:] Handoff's mass-ordering sensitivity anticipation '3-4σ at 6 years' is the OLDER Yellow Book / 2022 Physics and Detector projection. CURRENT canonical JUNO projection: '3σ at approximately 6.5 years × 26.6 GW thermal power' (Abusleme et al. 2025 Chin. Phys. C 49:033104, arXiv:2405.18008) — the most recent collaboration-internal paper on this observable. Encoded as 3σ (value=3, bd=lower) with the 2025 paper cited. The differences from the older 3-4σ-at-6-yr value reflect TAO satellite-detector incorporation, refined site-overburden modeling, and updated reactor-spectrum constraints. This is a minor correction since the encoded value (3σ) is compatible with both old and new projections; the citation update is what makes it firewall-faithful.
changelog.schema_candidates_status_observations: Schema candidate #6 (paired_cell_refs on edges) from META_v21_1 §2.3: NO friction observed during sub-PR 48. Sub-PR 48 added 4 resolves edges (each with single-target-cell semantics) plus extended the precision-measurement-resolves shape; paired_cell_refs friction has now failed to recur for THREE consecutive sub-PRs (46, 47, 48). Sub-PR 45 remains the only friction observation. Recommendation: continue monitoring across the next 3-5 cross-FC edges; promotion to v20 not warranted until friction recurs. Schema candidates 1-5, 7 from META_v21_1 §2.4: no friction observed.
changelog.methodological_observations_recorded: Three-way discriminating-resolves cluster category (introduced provisionally as a structural-observation possibility in sub-PR 47's changelog) is now CONCRETELY INSTANTIATED at sub-PR 48: two first instances on cell-nu-mass-hierarchy and cell-pd-Kplus-nubar-SUSY. The category is structurally distinct from the 2-way discriminating-resolves-pair (sub-PR 47's first instances): three-way clusters expose the observable through three independent physical channels with three independent systematic dependencies, enabling the cleanest possible structural richness in the AI-first `find_discriminating_experiments` and `find_resolvers` queries. After the next worker rebuild, querying find_discriminating_experiments (hyper-k, dune) returns 4 cells, (hyper-k, juno) returns 2 cells (mass hierarchy + p → ν̄K⁺), (dune, juno) returns the same 2 cells, and find_resolvers(cell-nu-mass-hierarchy) and find_resolvers(cell-pd-Kplus-nubar-SUSY) each return three experimental programs. This is the SINGLE strongest outreach demonstration target after sub-PR 48 — the cleanest concrete realization of the AI-first goal yet achieved in the project.
changelog.skip_decisions: Skipped a fifth potential edge on cell-nu-absDm31-squared (|Δm²_31| precision via reactor-ν spectrum analysis). JUNO is sub-percent (not sub-0.5%) on this parameter, and |Δm²_31| is more naturally a beam-experiment domain (DUNE will achieve comparable precision via long-baseline oscillation analysis). The skip is consistent with sub-PR 48's focus on JUNO's WORLD-LEADING precision targets (mass ordering, p → ν̄K⁺, sin²θ_12, Δm²_21) rather than its full physics program. Future sub-PRs may revisit |Δm²_31| when authoring DUNE precision-measurement-resolves edges using T31. Lead B (gw-ground-network + LISA pair) and Lead C (other standalones: CMB-S4, DESI, Rubin-LSST, FCC, axion-haloscope-network, edm-program, muon-g-2-continuation) deferred to sub-PR 49+. Lead D (carry-over: FQH cell [15] qs anchor OR Step 4.2 prediction-level sweep) also deferred.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 49 — Phase C STEP 4.4 continues. TENTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; FOURTH Step-4.4 sub-PR. SIX (6) resolves edges authored OPENING the gravitational-wave physics domain after three consecutive neutrino-sector sub-PRs (46-47-48). Three edges from gw-ground-network (timeline=running) targeting compact-astrophysical-objects cells: cell-compact-stellar-BH-GW (BBH catalog at A+/O5 design, 2.6 Gpc detection range), cell-compact-NS-massive (BNS tidal-deformability bound Λ̃₁.₄ ≲ 400 with predictions_per_program for stiff/intermediate/soft EOS), cell-compact-NS-merger-remnant (post-merger HMNS oscillation spectroscopy, CE/ET-era prospective). Three edges from lisa (timeline=planned-by-2035) targeting cell-compact-IMBH-gap (10³–10⁵ M_sun mergers to z≈15 with predictions_per_program for light-seed/heavy-seed/hierarchical-cluster seed-formation scenarios), cell-compact-SMBH-galactic-center (10⁵–10⁷ M_sun SMBHB mergers to z≈20 with predictions_per_program for light-seed/heavy-seed), cell-compact-stellar-BH-GW (early-inspiral 10⁴ yr before LIGO-band coalescence). Edge 6 (LISA early-inspiral) creates the FIRST GW-domain DISCRIMINATING-RESOLVES-PAIR with Edge 1 (ground-network IMR catalog) on cell-compact-stellar-BH-GW via frequency-band + epoch complementarity. Also the FIRST mixed-timeline sub-PR (gw-ground-network running + lisa planned-by-2035), establishing the precedent that discriminating-resolves clusters can span operational timelines. Step 4.4 now spans THREE physics domains: hep-ph (proton decay), neutrino-sector (oscillations + mass ordering), gravitational-wave (compact-binary mergers + multiband astronomy).
changelog.lead_choice_rationale: Lead A (gw-ground-network + lisa GW-domain pair) chosen per the sub-PR 48 handoff's STRONGLY-FAVORED recommendation. Three structural reasons made lead A the highest-payoff lead at sub-PR 49: (i) opens a new physics-domain after three consecutive neutrino-sector sub-PRs (46-47-48), broadening Step 4.4 coverage as required for the eventual review pathway (Track 4) — the outreach narrative will now span hep-ph, neutrino-sector, AND GW subfields; (ii) two GW-detector experimental programs with complementary frequency ranges (ground: 10 Hz–1 kHz; LISA: 10⁻⁴–10⁻¹ Hz) and source-class coverage (stellar-mass BBH + BNS; IMBH; SMBHB), enabling both same-target discriminating-resolves-pair and non-overlapping coverage in one sub-PR; (iii) both source nodes were Step-0.5-authored (verified) with stable comprehensive design and result literature. Lead B (IceCube-Upgrade + KM3NeT 4-way cluster deepening) was structurally weaker since four-way clusters in one observable don't extend the cluster-deepening lesson beyond sub-PR 48's three-way demonstration; lead B also required Step-0.5 authoring of new experimental-program nodes, heavier than lead A. Leads C and D were diversification/cleanup options without lead A's domain-opening payoff.
changelog.edge_choice_rationale: Six edges chosen to span the GW-detector science envelope while keeping the diff-shape within typical sub-PR scope. Edge 1 (ground-network → stellar-BH-GW catalog): the LIGO/Virgo/KAGRA realized population, encoded with A+/O5 design BBH detection range 2.6 Gpc per Abbott et al. 2020 LRR 23:3; exclusion_only because catalog enumeration doesn't directly discriminate between candidate-foundational programs. Edge 2 (ground-network → NS-massive via tidal-deformability): the dense-nuclear-EOS bound from BNS inspiral observations, encoded with projected A+/O5 Λ̃₁.₄ ≲ 400 90% CL upper bound and 3 EOS programs (stiff DD2 ≈850, intermediate APR ≈350, soft SLy ≈240) — first EOS-program-class discriminating-resolves edge. Edge 3 (ground-network → NS-merger-remnant): the post-merger HMNS oscillation cell, encoded with A+/O5 BNS range 330 Mpc; exclusion_only because CE/ET-era prospective with overlapping EOS-class f_peak predictions; cell-population graduates from candidate-individual to confirmed-individual in CE/ET era. Edge 4 (LISA → IMBH-gap): the 10³–10⁵ M_sun BH mass window, encoded with LISA reach to z≈15 (30 Gpc comoving) and 3 seed-formation programs (light-seed ≈20/yr at high z, heavy-seed ≈3/yr at intermediate z, hierarchical-cluster ≈5/yr at low z) — first seed-formation-program discriminating-resolves edge. Edge 5 (LISA → SMBH-galactic-center): the SMBHB merger cell, encoded with LISA reach to z≈20 (40 Gpc comoving) and 2 seed-formation programs (light-seed ≈30/yr high z low-mass, heavy-seed ≈10/yr intermediate z higher-mass). Edge 6 (LISA → stellar-BH-GW early-inspiral): the multiband-GW complementary observation, encoded with LISA-band horizon ≈3 Gpc; exclusion_only because the same-target discriminating-resolves-pair structure with edge 1 lives in frequency-band+epoch complementarity rather than per-program prediction differences. Cell-compact-SMBH-AGN explicitly NOT targeted because AGN cell describes 10⁷–10¹⁰ M_sun population heavier than LISA's 10⁵–10⁷ M_sun sweet spot.
firewall_self_check: edge-rs-gw-ground-network-stellar-BH-GW: Sensitivity (2.6 Gpc detection range) sourced from Abbott et al. 2020 LRR 23:3 Table 2, with realized catalog reach from Abbott et al. 2023 GWTC-3 Phys. Rev. X 13:041039. Independent of v85 cross-classification graph — derives from ground-network design and results papers alone. The cell's exclusion_only framing reflects firewall faithfulness: no per-program citation exists for predicted catalog SIZE that would survive §4 part (2) admissibility, since catalog-size depends on integrated source rate × horizon volume rather than program-internal calculation. PASS.

edge-rs-gw-ground-network-NS-EOS: Sensitivity (Λ̃₁.₄ ≲ 400) sourced from Abbott et al. 2018 PRL 121:161101 (GW170817 first measurement) and projected from Abbott et al. 2020 LRR 23:3 (A+/O5 reach). Each EOS-program prediction cites the program-internal nuclear-physics calculation: APR (Akmal-Pandharipande-Ravenhall 1998 PRC 58:1804), DD2 (Typel et al. 2010 PRC 81:015803; Hempel-Schaffner-Bielich 2010 NPA 837:210), SLy (Douchin-Haensel 2001 A&A 380:151; Annala et al. 2020 Nature Phys. 16:907 for hybrid). All three program predictions derivable from nuclear-physics literature alone. PASS.

edge-rs-gw-ground-network-NS-merger-remnant: Sensitivity (330 Mpc BNS range) sourced from Abbott et al. 2020 LRR 23:3 Table 2 with explicit post-merger detection floor referenced to Branchesi et al. 2023 JCAP 07:068 (CE/ET projections). The cell's exclusion_only framing reflects firewall faithfulness: EOS-class predictions for HMNS f_peak (2.5–3.5 kHz, Bauswein-Stergioulas-Janka 2014 PRD 90:023002) overlap heavily within current EOS uncertainty, making the per-program discriminating prediction less crisp than for inspiral-tidal-deformability. PASS.

edge-rs-lisa-IMBH-gap: Sensitivity (LISA reach to z≈15 / 30 Gpc comoving) sourced from Amaro-Seoane et al. 2023 LRR 26:2 §6 and Colpi et al. 2024 LISA Definition Study Report. Each seed-formation program prediction cites program-internal merger-rate calculation: light-seed (Klein et al. 2016 PRD 93:024003; Sesana-Volonteri-Haardt 2007 MNRAS 377:1711), heavy-seed (Begelman-Volonteri-Rees 2006 MNRAS 370:289 + Klein et al. 2016), hierarchical-cluster (Portegies-Zwart-McMillan 2002 ApJ 576:899 + Antonini-Gieles-Gualandris 2019 MNRAS 486:5008). All three predictions derivable from astrophysics literature alone. PASS.

edge-rs-lisa-SMBH-mergers: Sensitivity (LISA reach to z≈20 / 40 Gpc comoving for 10⁶ M_sun canonical SMBHB) sourced from Amaro-Seoane et al. 2023 LRR 26:2 §5 and Klein et al. 2016 PRD 93:024003. Each seed-formation program prediction cites Klein et al. 2016 (program-internal rate calculation) and Volonteri-Habouzit-Colpi 2021 Nature Rev. Phys. 3:732 (review constraints). Both predictions derivable from astrophysics literature alone. PASS.

edge-rs-lisa-stellar-BH-early-inspiral: Sensitivity (3 Gpc LISA-band horizon for canonical 30+30 M_sun stellar BBH) sourced from Sesana 2016 PRL 116:231102 (the multiband-GW-astronomy proposal). The exclusion_only framing reflects firewall faithfulness: per-source astrophysical-formation-channel discrimination (isolated-binary-evolution vs dense-cluster vs AGN-disk) happens at population level via residual eccentricity statistics, not at single-source LISA-band detection level. The discriminating-resolves-pair structure with edge 1 lives in frequency-band+epoch complementarity, not in per-program predicted-value differences. PASS.

resolves_edges_added.structural_role: Ground-network → stellar-mass BBH catalog cell. A+/O5 design BBH detection range. Paired with edge 6 (LISA early-inspiral) to form first GW-domain DISCRIMINATING-RESOLVES-PAIR on same target.

Ground-network → NS-EOS via tidal-deformability Λ̃₁.₄. Projected A+/O5 90% CL upper bound from ≈10 BNS detections. First EOS-program-class discriminating-resolves edge with stiff/intermediate/soft EOS predictions.
Ground-network → post-merger HMNS oscillation cell. A+/O5 BNS detection range; CE/ET-era prospective for routine 2–4 kHz post-merger spectroscopy.

LISA → IMBH 10³–10⁵ M_sun mass window. Reach to z≈15 (full cosmologically-accessible volume for IMBH mergers). First seed-formation-program discriminating-resolves edge with light-seed/heavy-seed/hierarchical-cluster predictions.
LISA → SMBHB merger cell. Reach to z≈20 for 10⁶ M_sun canonical SMBHB at SNR>100. Seed-formation-program discriminating-resolves edge with light-seed/heavy-seed predictions.

LISA → multiband stellar-mass BBH early-inspiral on same target as edge 1. Creates first GW-domain DISCRIMINATING-RESOLVES-PAIR via frequency-band + epoch complementarity (LISA early-inspiral 10⁴ yr before ground-band coalescence).

changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition:
Six minor-to-moderate inline departures from the sub-PR 49 handoff are documented below.

(1) NO T32 MINT. The handoff anticipated that T32 mint 'IS likely' for events-per-year encoding on GW edges. Closer inspection found that the existing T30 (discovery-style sensitivity with bound_direction) cleanly fits the SENSITIVITY field on all 6 edges (kind=length for detection-horizon, kind=dimensionless for tidal-deformability), and that the PREDICTIONS_PER_PROGRAM predicted_value substructures can use kind=dimensionless with the per-year rate semantics in the description (76 existing dimensionless qs entries on cells in v85 follow this pattern). Per Rule 28, kind=dimensionless forbids units; per Rule 29, kind=dimensionless forbids log10; both constraints are met. Minting T32 would have been premature convention-proliferation per the disposition principle. T-convention register stays at 32 active (T1–T14, T17–T31).

(2) TARGET CELL FOR LISA SMBHB MERGERS: chose cell-compact-SMBH-galactic-center over cell-compact-SMBH-AGN. The handoff listed both as candidate targets. Cell-compact-SMBH-AGN describes 10⁷–10¹⁰ M_sun AGN-powering SMBHs — the high-mass end is beyond LISA's f-band (10⁻³–10⁻¹ Hz peaks at 10⁵–10⁷ M_sun mergers). Cell-compact-SMBH-galactic-center covers galactic-nucleus SMBHs at 10⁶–10⁹ M_sun, which overlaps LISA's sweet spot. The SMBH-AGN cell remains candidate for a future steady-state-AGN-imaging resolves edge (e.g., from EHT or future-EHT) but not a LISA merger edge.

(3) NO TESTS-OF-GR CELL EXISTS in v85 data. The handoff suggested an optional third ground-network edge to a tests-of-general-relativity cell if such cell/FC exists. After inspection, no tests-of-GR FC or cell exists in v85; the spacetime-symmetry-groups FC organizes symmetry structure, not GW tests-of-GR observables. Replaced the optional edge with edge-rs-gw-ground-network-NS-merger-remnant (cell-compact-NS-merger-remnant), which IS on compact-astrophysical-objects and is the natural CE/ET-era prospective target. Authoring a tests-of-GR FC or cell would be a separate authoring step, not a sub-PR 49 deliverable.

(4) TIMELINE FOR GW-GROUND-NETWORK = 'running'. The handoff anticipated a 'running vs deployed' judgment-call. The node's operational_period = {start_year: 2015, ongoing: true}; per the v18 schema timeline enum description, 'running = currently operating with scheduled runs ahead', which matches O4 ongoing + O5/A+ scheduled. 'deployed' would mean 'already taking data' without future scheduled runs, which fits less well given the continuing run plan. The sub-PR 48 JUNO precedent (JUNO filling completed late 2024, ongoing=true) used 'running'; consistency favors 'running' here.

(5) TIMELINE FOR LISA = 'planned-by-2035'. The handoff anticipated a 'planned-by-2035 vs proposed' judgment-call. ESA L3 adoption occurred January 2024 (per Colpi et al. 2024 LISA Definition Study Report); launch targeted for mid-2030s. 'proposed' applies to concept-stage missions; ESA-adopted L3 missions with funded definition study are 'planned-by-YYYY' per the schema. Used 'planned-by-2035'.

(6) PER-PROGRAM PREDICTION SHAPE FOR EOS / SEED-FORMATION: each predicted_value uses kind=dimensionless (no units field) since Λ̃ is dimensionless and event-rates are encoded as pure numbers with per-year semantics in the description. Symmetric/asymmetric uncertainty objects are used per quantitative_scale_uncertainty schema, which permits both {low, high} symmetric and asymmetric forms. Verified by validator on sample drafts.
changelog.schema_candidates_status_observations: Schema candidate #6 (paired_cell_refs on edges) friction did not recur for the FOURTH consecutive sub-PR (46, 47, 48, 49). Sub-PR 49 added 6 resolves edges and saw zero friction with paired_cell_refs: all edges target single cells (cell_refs would be a one-element array, no pairing structure needed). The friction in sub-PR 45 (the cross-FC edge case) remains the only documented friction observation. Recommendation: continue to defer promotion of #6 to v20 until friction recurs in 2+ further sub-PRs; current 4-sub-PR-streak without friction suggests the original observation may have been case-specific to cross-FC edges rather than indicating a load-bearing schema feature.
changelog.methodological_observations_recorded:
PHYSICS-DOMAIN-OPENING FIRST INSTANCE: sub-PR 49 introduces the first gravitational-wave-domain resolves edges in the project, opening a third major Step-4.4 physics subdomain after hep-ph (proton decay via HK + DUNE + JUNO) and neutrino-sector (oscillations + mass ordering via HK + DUNE + JUNO). This establishes 'physics-domain-opening' as a distinct structural move from 'cluster-deepening' — the Step 4.4 trajectory now combines both. Cumulative coverage across physics subdomains is a key project-level metric for the eventual review pathway (Track 4).

FIRST MIXED-TIMELINE SUB-PR: gw-ground-network (running) + lisa (planned-by-2035) ship in the same sub-PR. The discriminating-resolves-pair on cell-compact-stellar-BH-GW (edges 1 + 6) is the first cluster that spans operational timelines: ground-network produces results NOW, LISA opens complementary multiband observations after 2035. This establishes that discriminating-resolves clusters CAN span timelines, not just methodologies or detector technologies. Future GW outreach narrative emphasizes 'today + after 2035' complementary observations of the same source population.

FIRST EOS-PROGRAM-CLASS DISCRIMINATING-RESOLVES EDGE: edge 2 (NS-EOS via tidal-deformability) is the first resolves edge whose predictions_per_program substructure encodes microphysical-EOS-class predictions (stiff DD2, intermediate APR, soft SLy + hybrid). This extends the pattern beyond cosmology/particle-physics candidate-foundational programs (the SUSY-SU(5) variants used in sub-PRs 46-48) and the seed-formation astrophysical scenarios used in edges 4-5 of this sub-PR. The pattern is now demonstrated across three program-type families: candidate-foundational-BSM (hep-ph), microphysical-EOS-class (nuclear physics), astrophysical-formation-scenario (astrophysics).
changelog.skip_decisions:
Five potential additional edges were considered and skipped in sub-PR 49 to keep the diff-shape within typical scope.

(a) edge-rs-gw-ground-network-stellar-BH-mass-gap (→ cell-compact-stellar-BH-mass-gap): SKIP. The 3–5 M_sun lower mass-gap region IS being populated by ground-network observations (GW230529 and similar candidates), but the discriminating physics is at the population statistics level rather than at the per-event detection level. Resolves edge would duplicate the catalog-enumeration story of edge 1 without adding distinctive program-discrimination structure. Deferred for a future population-level sub-PR.

(b) edge-rs-gw-ground-network-PBH-LIGO-window (→ cell-compact-PBH-LIGO-window): SKIP. The cell is explicitly excluded-as-DM-fraction; the resolves edge would be a falsified-prediction-style edge, not a forward-resolves. The existing falsification is already recorded on cell-compact-PBH-LIGO-window via the v85 cell-direct qs and prediction fields. Adding a resolves edge would duplicate the exclusion content.

(c) edge-rs-lisa-galactic-binaries (→ Galactic compact-binary cells on AGN or NS-canonical cells): SKIP. LISA's Galactic-binary catalog (≈10⁴ resolvable double-WD and BH-WD systems) is a major science target but maps to multiple cells (NS-canonical, WD-canonical, stellar-BH-XRB-equivalent) without a clean single target. Deferred until cell structure for Galactic-binary classification is articulated.

(d) edge-rs-lisa-EMRI (→ EMRI-specific cell): SKIP. Extreme-mass-ratio inspirals are a major LISA target but no EMRI-specific cell exists in v85; EMRIs naturally fit on cell-compact-SMBH-galactic-center (the SMBH side) but with a secondary stellar-mass-BH that doesn't pin cleanly. Authoring an EMRI cell would be Phase A cell-direct work, not Step 4.4. Deferred.

(e) edge-rs-lisa-stochastic-background (→ cosmological-observations stochastic-GW cell): SKIP. LISA's stochastic-background sensitivity is a real science target (Ω_GW ≈ 10⁻¹² at 10⁻³ Hz from inflation-era GW), but verification confirmed no stochastic-GW cell exists on cosmological-observations FC. Authoring such a cell would be Phase A work or a cosmological-observations FC extension. Deferred.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 50 — Phase C STEP 4.4 continues. ELEVENTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; FIFTH Step-4.4 sub-PR. THREE (3) resolves edges authored OPENING the cosmology physics domain after one consecutive gravitational-wave sub-PR (49) and three consecutive neutrino-sector sub-PRs (46–48). All three edges from desi (timeline=running) targeting cosmology surfaces: (1) cc-frontier (open-frontier node) for the dark-energy equation-of-state (w_0, w_a) measurement — the PROJECT'S FIRST FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE (all 18 prior resolves edges target cells); (2) cell-cosm-BAO-sound-horizon-DESI for sub-percent BAO standard-ruler precision (T31 precision-improvement); (3) cell-cosm-H0-CMB-Planck for the BAO+CMB inverse-distance-ladder Hubble-constant anchor that sets the precision floor for the Hubble tension's CMB-side (T31 precision-improvement). Step 4.4 now spans FOUR physics domains: hep-ph (proton decay), neutrino-sector (oscillations + mass ordering), gravitational-wave (compact-binary mergers + multiband astronomy), AND cosmology (dark energy + Hubble tension + BAO standard ruler).
changelog.lead_choice_rationale:
Lead A (DUNE + Hyper-K long-baseline-neutrino pair) from the sub-PR 50 handoff was DISCARDED on inspection: the handoff's program-node inventory is stale. Live v86 data shows hyper-k already attached with 4 resolves edges in sub-PR 46 (including cell-nu-deltaCP and cell-nu-mass-hierarchy, the exact targets Lead A proposed), and dune already attached with 4 resolves edges in sub-PR 47 (same targets). The handoff was authored from a planning snapshot that did not track sub-PR 47–49 deliveries. The actual unattached experimental-program inventory after v86 is 7 nodes (not the handoff's listed 8): cmb-s4, rubin-lsst, desi, axion-haloscope-network, edm-program, muon-g-2-continuation, fcc.

Switched to Lead B (DESI single-node on cc-frontier / cosmological-observations) — the 'genuinely close runner-up' the sub-PR 50 handoff named. Three structural reasons for choosing Lead B at sub-PR 50: (i) opens a new physics-domain (cosmology) after one GW sub-PR (49) and three preceding neutrino sub-PRs, continuing the broadening of Step 4.4 coverage required for the eventual review pathway (Track 4) — the outreach narrative now spans hep-ph + neutrino-sector + GW + cosmology subfields, four major physics domains; (ii) DESI Y1 (2024) is named in the Phase C scope memo Step 0.5 notes as 'the cleanest current empirical-tension example available to Phase C' — the 2.6–3.9σ evolving-DE tension is the most concrete current empirical anomaly the project can attach to via Step 4.4; (iii) the natural cc-frontier target enables the project's FIRST frontier-target resolves edge, demonstrating the v18 spec's Rule-32 permission that resolves edges may target open-frontier nodes when the experimental program's structural payoff sits at frontier level rather than cell level. Lead C (axion-haloscope-network on strong-cp-problem) was structurally weaker because (a) it duplicates the single-source-on-single-frontier pattern with less domain-opening payoff than Lead B's cosmology opening, and (b) the strong-CP frontier already has a rich Phase A + Phase B structural backbone; the marginal Step 4.4 payoff is smaller than opening cosmology from zero.
changelog.edge_choice_rationale:
Three edges chosen to span DESI's primary scientific payoffs while keeping the diff-shape at the low end of the handoff's 2–4 range. Edge 1 (desi → cc-frontier): the flagship dark-energy equation-of-state measurement, encoded with T30 sigma_deviation (value=5, bound_direction=lower) at the experimental design's 5-year-exposure deviation-from-ΛCDM discovery floor. Exclusion_only because (w_0, w_a) candidate-foundational programs are hypothesis classes (ΛCDM with w ≡ -1 is the only point predictor; quintessence/early-DE/modified-gravity are parameter-free hypothesis classes). FIRST frontier-target resolves edge in the project. Edge 2 (desi → cell-cosm-BAO-sound-horizon-DESI): the BAO standard-ruler precision-improvement target, encoded with T31 length/Mpc (value=147.09 mirroring the cell's existing qs, uncertainty=0.5 projected Y5+CMB combined r_d-equivalent precision). Exclusion_only because r_d is a derived pre-recombination quantity that standard cosmological models share at Planck-precision; no candidate-foundational programs shift r_d at distinguishable values DESI could discriminate at this surface. Edge 3 (desi → cell-cosm-H0-CMB-Planck): the BAO+CMB inverse-distance-ladder Hubble-constant anchor, encoded with T31 time/Gyr (value=14.52 mirroring the cell's existing qs, uncertainty=0.07 projected Y5+CMB Hubble-time precision ≈0.3 km/s/Mpc on H_0). Exclusion_only because DESI's contribution to this cell is precision-improvement on the CMB-side anchor of the Hubble tension; the discriminating physics between candidate Hubble-tension resolutions lives on a different observational axis (late-time distance ladders, GW standard sirens, time-delay cosmography) covered by other experimental programs in future sub-PRs.

Five potential additional edges considered and skipped (documented in skip_decisions below): σ_8 / S_8 RSD probe of structure-growth tension; specific cosmological-observations cells already DESI-anchored at the cell-direct qs level; primordial-non-Gaussianity f_NL bounds; Σm_ν cosmological sum-of-neutrino-masses; spectral-index n_s precision. None of these would have added a distinct structural mechanism beyond what edges 1–3 already establish for the cosmology-domain opening.
firewall_self_check: edge-rs-desi-cc-frontier: Sensitivity (5σ deviation-from-ΛCDM discovery floor from 5-year DESI BAO+CMB+SN exposure) sourced from DESI Collaboration 2013 (arXiv:1308.0847) Science Requirements + DESI 2016 (arXiv:1611.00036) Part I forecast + Adame et al. 2024 (arXiv:2404.03002) Year-1 result projection. Independent of v86 cross-classification graph — derives from DESI design and results papers alone. Exclusion_only=true documented under §4 part (2) admissibility: ΛCDM is the only point-predictive program for (w_0, w_a) = (-1, 0); evolving-DE alternatives (quintessence, early-DE, modified-gravity) are hypothesis classes with parameter freedom, not point predictors that satisfy the §4 admissibility test for predictions_per_program entries. Firewall self-check (§2.5): if presented with the DESI literature and the v86 cosmological-observations FC alone, the cc-frontier resolves edge with 5σ sensitivity and exclusion_only framing is reconstructable as a structural-by-derivation claim from the DESI papers. PASS.

edge-rs-desi-BAO-sound-horizon: Sensitivity (147.09 ± 0.5 Mpc projected r_d-equivalent precision at DESI Y5+CMB combination) sourced from Adame et al. 2024 (arXiv:2404.03000) Year-1 D_M/r_d and D_H/r_d 0.5–1.4% per-bin precision + DESI 2016 design forecast for <0.3% combined precision over the 5-year survey. Independent of v86 graph — derives from DESI design and results papers alone. Exclusion_only=true documented under §4 part (2): r_d is a derived pre-recombination quantity (baryon/photon ratio, matter/radiation equality, recombination history) shared at Planck-precision across standard cosmological models; no candidate-foundational programs predict r_d at distinguishably different values that DESI could discriminate. Firewall self-check (§2.5): the cell's existing qs (Planck-derived r_d = 147.09 ± 0.26 Mpc) provides the canonical anchor; DESI's role is precision-improvement, citable directly from the DESI Year-1 paper and 2016 design forecast. PASS.

edge-rs-desi-H0-CMB-precision: Sensitivity (14.52 ± 0.07 Gyr Hubble-time precision ≈0.3 km/s/Mpc on H_0 at DESI Y5+CMB combination) sourced from Adame et al. 2024 (arXiv:2404.03002) Year-1 H_0 = 68.52 ± 0.62 km/s/Mpc + DESI 2016 Table 2.3 design forecast. The Y1 Hubble-time precision is ≈0.13 Gyr; the Y5 projection is ≈0.07 Gyr. Independent of v86 graph — derives from DESI design and results papers + Planck 2018 CMB prior alone. Exclusion_only=true documented under §4 part (2): DESI's contribution to cell-cosm-H0-CMB-Planck is precision-improvement on the CMB-anchored side of the Hubble tension; the candidate resolutions of the Hubble tension (early-DE, varying-fundamental-constants, modified pre-recombination physics) are hypothesis classes with parameter freedom, not point predictors at this cell's surface. Firewall self-check (§2.5): the cell's existing qs (Planck-derived Hubble time 14.52 ± 0.11 Gyr) provides the canonical anchor; DESI tightens it through BAO+CMB combination, citable directly from the DESI 2024 cosmology paper. PASS.

resolves_edges_added.structural_role: DESI → cc-frontier (dark-energy equation-of-state). FIRST FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE in the project (all 18 prior resolves edges target cells). 5-year design exposure 5σ deviation-from-ΛCDM discovery floor; DESI Year-1 already at 2.6–3.9σ tension. Exclusion_only: (w_0, w_a) candidate-foundational programs are hypothesis classes, not point predictors.

DESI → BAO sound-horizon-at-drag-epoch cell. T31 precision-improvement target (value mirrors cell qs; uncertainty mirrors DESI Y5+CMB projected r_d-equivalent precision). Exclusion_only: r_d is a derived pre-recombination quantity shared at Planck-precision across standard cosmological models.

DESI → CMB-anchored H_0 cell (Hubble-time encoding). T31 precision-improvement target on the inverse-distance-ladder side of the Hubble tension. Exclusion_only: DESI's contribution is precision-improvement on the CMB-side anchor; discriminating physics between candidate Hubble-tension resolutions lives on different observational axes.

changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition:
Six minor-to-major inline departures from the sub-PR 50 handoff are documented below.

(1) LEAD CHOICE FLIPPED — MAJOR DEPARTURE. The handoff named Lead A (DUNE + Hyper-K LBL-pair) as STRONGLY-FAVORED with expected 4–7 edges. Live v86 inspection shows both source nodes already attached: hyper-k carries 4 resolves edges from sub-PR 46 (targeting cell-pd-pi0-eplus-falsified-GG, cell-pd-Kplus-nubar-SUSY, cell-nu-deltaCP, cell-nu-mass-hierarchy), dune carries 4 resolves edges from sub-PR 47 (same targets). The handoff's Lead A would have authored REDUNDANT edges to already-attached source-target pairs, not new authoring. The handoff was authored from a planning snapshot that did not track sub-PR 47–49 deliveries. Switched to Lead B (DESI cosmology single-node) per the handoff's runner-up option. RECORDED for future sub-PR handoff authoring: verify program-node attachment status before naming a lead, by querying the live data for existing resolves edges grouped by source.

(2) PROGRAM-NODE INVENTORY CORRECTION. Handoff listed 8 unattached programs: dune, cmb-s4, rubin-lsst, desi, axion-haloscope-network, edm-program, muon-g-2-continuation, fcc. Actual unattached after v86: 7 (excluding dune which is attached). Updated inventory recorded for the sub-PR 51 handoff.

(3) NO T-CONVENTION MINT. The handoff explicitly recommended 'DO NOT mint T32 unless the literature forces a structurally novel register' and noted T30 + T31 (originally anticipated for the Lead A neutrino case) would be sufficient. Confirmed for Lead B: T30 (discovery-floor sigma_deviation, value=5, bound_direction=lower) covers edge 1 (cc-frontier); T31 (precision-improvement, value mirrors cell qs, uncertainty mirrors design-projected precision, bound_direction omitted) covers edges 2 and 3. T-convention register stays at 32 active (T1–T14, T17–T31).

(4) EDGE COUNT 3, NOT 2–4 RANGE'S UPPER END. The handoff's Lead B sketch named 2–4 edges with a 'concrete tension showcase' framing. Settled on 3 (cc-frontier + BAO + H_0) to maximize structural distinctness per edge: the FIRST frontier-target resolves edge + the precision-improvement BAO ruler + the Hubble-tension CMB anchor. Adding a fourth edge (e.g., σ_8 / S_8 RSD probe) was considered but skipped — see skip_decisions — because no clean single target cell exists for DESI RSD's S_8 role; the existing σ_8-CMB and σ_8-WL cells are method-specific and DESI provides a third independent probe rather than directly resolving either, which would have required either authoring a new cell (out of scope) or attaching with awkward semantics.

(5) ALL THREE EDGES EXCLUSION_ONLY. The handoff's Lead A speculation said 'Predictions_per_program populated on δ_CP edges' for the neutrino case. For Lead B (cosmology), the analogous predictions_per_program populations are not §4-admissible: ΛCDM is the only point-predictive program for (w_0, w_a) and H_0; the evolving-DE / Hubble-tension candidate-foundational programs are hypothesis classes with parameter freedom, not point predictors that satisfy §4 part (2). Matches the precedent set by the hyper-k δ_CP and mass-hierarchy edges from sub-PRs 46–48 (both exclusion_only for the same structural reason: PMNS-like measured parameters don't have point-predictive candidate-foundational programs). Documented in each edge's description.

(6) FIRST FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE. Recorded as a methodological observation (see methodological_observations_recorded below). The handoff did not anticipate this consequence of choosing Lead B — the desi → cc-frontier edge is the first resolves edge to target an open-frontier node (Rule 32 permits cell, open-frontier, and totality-approach targets; all 18 prior resolves edges targeted cells). Demonstrates that the v18 spec's Rule-32 permission is exercisable in practice.
changelog.schema_candidates_status_observations: Schema candidate #6 (paired_cell_refs on edges) friction did not recur for the FIFTH consecutive sub-PR (46, 47, 48, 49, 50). Sub-PR 50 added 3 resolves edges and saw zero friction with paired_cell_refs: all edges target single cells or a single open-frontier node. The friction in sub-PR 45 (the cross-FC edge case) remains the only documented friction observation. Recommendation: continue to defer promotion of #6 to v20 until friction recurs in 2+ further sub-PRs; current 5-sub-PR-streak without friction reinforces the original observation may have been case-specific to cross-FC edges rather than indicating a load-bearing schema feature for resolves edges.
changelog.methodological_observations_recorded:
PHYSICS-DOMAIN-OPENING FIFTH INSTANCE: sub-PR 50 opens the cosmology physics domain in Step 4.4 — the fourth distinct subdomain after hep-ph (proton decay; sub-PR 46–48), neutrino-sector (oscillations + mass ordering; sub-PR 46–48), and gravitational-wave (compact-binary mergers + multiband astronomy; sub-PR 49). The cumulative physics-subdomain coverage of Step 4.4 is now: hep-ph + neutrino + GW + cosmology, four major subdomains. This positions the eventual Track 4 review pathway to reach physicists across all four subdomains with substantive Step 4.4 content to evaluate.

FIRST FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE: edge-rs-desi-cc-frontier is the project's first resolves edge to target an open-frontier node directly, rather than a cell. The v18 spec's Rule 32 explicitly permits resolves edges to target cell, open-frontier, or totality-approach ids; sub-PR 50 is the first instance exercising the open-frontier permission. This demonstrates that resolves edges may attach at the frontier level when the experimental program's most direct structural payoff is the frontier's empirical content rather than a specific cell's value. A future sub-PR could exercise totality-approach as a resolves-edge target (e.g., a precision measurement on muon-g-2 or koide-formula totality-approach nodes), completing the Rule-32 permission demonstration across all three target-type categories.

HANDOFF-INVENTORY-DRIFT FIRST FORMAL CASE: this sub-PR documents a major handoff departure (Lead A flipped to Lead B because Lead A's source nodes were already attached). The proximate cause is that the sub-PR 50 handoff was authored from a planning snapshot that predated sub-PRs 47–49 deliveries, so the unattached-program-inventory was stale on commit. Recorded recommendation for the sub-PR 51 handoff: the unattached-program inventory should be VERIFIED at handoff authoring time by listing existing resolves-edge source-nodes from the live data, not inherited from a prior planning document. The sub-PR 51 handoff inventory should show 7 unattached programs (cmb-s4, rubin-lsst, axion-haloscope-network, edm-program, muon-g-2-continuation, fcc, plus one of {desi-attached-as-of-sub-PR-50}). Inventory drift was the only major handoff departure in sub-PR 50; all other departures were minor or anticipated.

EXCLUSION_ONLY PATTERN GENERALIZATION: the precedent that PMNS-like measured parameters (δ_CP, mass-ordering, θ₁₂) take exclusion_only=true (sub-PRs 46–48) extends naturally to cosmologically-measured parameters ((w_0, w_a), r_d, H_0) for the same structural reason: candidate-foundational programs in these spaces are hypothesis classes with parameter freedom, not point predictors satisfying §4 part (2). The point-predictive cases (proton-decay GUT-class τ_p, NS-EOS-class Λ̃, BH-seed-formation merger rates) remain the structurally-distinct minority that populate predictions_per_program. The exclusion_only / predictions_per_program split across all 21 resolves edges in v87 is now: 13 exclusion_only / 8 with populated predictions_per_program (verified post-commit).
changelog.skip_decisions:
Five potential additional edges were considered and skipped in sub-PR 50 to keep the diff-shape at the low end of the handoff's 2–4 range and to avoid forcing edges where the structural fit is not clean.

(a) edge-rs-desi-sigma8-RSD (→ cell-cosm-sigma8-CMB or cell-cosm-sigma8-weak-lensing): SKIP. DESI's full-shape redshift-space-distortion (RSD) measurements deliver fσ₈(z) across the survey-z range, bearing on the S₈ tension between CMB-inferred (σ₈ ≈ 0.81) and weak-lensing-inferred (S₈ ≈ 0.77) growth amplitudes. The cells cell-cosm-sigma8-CMB and cell-cosm-sigma8-weak-lensing are method-specific (CMB-anchored vs WL-anchored), and DESI provides an INDEPENDENT third probe rather than directly resolving either cell's measurement. Attaching DESI to EITHER cell would mis-frame DESI's RSD as a precision-improvement on a method DESI doesn't use; attaching to BOTH cells with exclusion_only and 'independent third probe' framing would be a coherent move but requires either authoring a new σ₈-RSD-DESI cell (out of Step 4.4 scope) or accepting awkward semantics across two cells for one experiment. Deferred for a future sub-PR that authors the σ₈-RSD cell or pairs DESI RSD with Rubin LSST and Euclid for a 3-way S₈-probe-comparison.

(b) edge-rs-desi-fNL (→ cell-cosm-primordial-non-Gaussianity-fNL): SKIP. DESI's high-z LRG and quasar large-scale clustering bears on f_NL bounds (DESI projects σ(f_NL^local) ≈ 4 at full survey, comparable to Planck's |f_NL^local| < 5.0). However, the cell is currently populated by Planck CMB-derived bounds; the natural evolution would be cell-direct qs updates (Phase C Step 4.3 work) rather than a resolves edge. The resolves-edge framing would also require predictions_per_program populated with inflation-class predictions (single-field slow-roll: f_NL^local ~ 10^-2, multi-field: O(1), curvaton: O(1–10)), which is a separate authoring task with literature complexity beyond a single sub-PR. Deferred to a dedicated inflation-cosmology sub-PR or to Step 4.3 cell-direct work.

(c) edge-rs-desi-Sigma-mass-nu (→ cell-cosm-Sigma-mass-nu): SKIP. DESI's BAO+CMB combination tightens the cosmological sum-of-neutrino-masses Σm_ν bound (DESI Y1 + Planck gives Σm_ν < 0.072 eV at 95% CL, tightening the Planck-alone bound of Σm_ν < 0.12 eV). However, the cell-cosm-Sigma-mass-nu already explicitly cross-references cell-nu-Sum-mass-Planck and the structural payoff is best captured by a cell-direct qs update on cell-nu-Sum-mass-Planck (neutrino-sector-phenomenology FC), not a separate resolves edge. The neutrino-sector FC's existing sub-PR 41/44 qs anchoring already records the cosmological-sum bound. Adding a resolves edge would duplicate the structural content. Deferred to potential Phase C Step 4.3 deepening on cell-nu-Sum-mass-Planck if a DESI-specific tightening warrants separate recording.

(d) edge-rs-desi-spectral-index-ns (→ cell-cosm-spectral-index-ns): SKIP. DESI's BAO+CMB combination tightens n_s precision modestly (DESI Y1 + Planck gives n_s = 0.9649 ± 0.0035, slightly tightening the Planck-alone 0.965 ± 0.004). The precision-improvement is real but marginal; the cell's existing Planck-derived value remains the dominant anchor. The structural payoff per edge is too thin to warrant a separate resolves edge over a cell-direct qs uncertainty update. Deferred.

(e) edge-rs-desi-curvature-OmegaK (→ cell-cosm-curvature-OmegaK): SKIP. DESI's BAO+CMB combination tightens spatial-curvature bounds (DESI Y1 + Planck gives |Ω_K| < 0.003 vs the Planck-alone |Ω_K| < 0.005). Same structural reasoning as (d): marginal precision improvement on an already-strongly-constrained parameter, with no candidate-foundational program discrimination. Deferred.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 51 — Phase C STEP 4.4 continues. TWELFTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; SIXTH Step-4.4 sub-PR. THREE (3) resolves edges authored OPENING the axion-dark-matter physics domain after one consecutive cosmology sub-PR (50) and the preceding gravitational-wave and neutrino sub-PRs. All three edges from axion-haloscope-network (timeline=running) targeting two surfaces: (1) strong-cp-problem (open-frontier node) for the haloscope-program's bear-down on the Peccei-Quinn-axion resolution of strong-CP — the PROJECT'S SECOND FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE (after edge-rs-desi-cc-frontier in sub-PR 50); (2) cell-dm-QCD-axion-DFSZ-KSVZ for the canonical QCD-axion-as-DM cell at the DFSZ benchmark in the few-μeV band; (3) cell-dm-ALP-haloscope for the broader generic-ALP haloscope envelope across neV–meV m_a coverage. Step 4.4 now spans FIVE physics domains: hep-ph (proton decay), neutrino-sector (oscillations + mass ordering), gravitational-wave (compact-binary mergers + multiband astronomy), cosmology (dark energy + Hubble tension + BAO standard ruler), AND axion-dark-matter (haloscope coupling sensitivity for QCD axion + ALP parameter space). FIRST resolves edges using sensitivity kind = coupling (previously: time, sigma_deviation, dimensionless, mass, length); sensitivity-kind diversity now spans 6 of the 8 available kinds (only ratio and energy_scale remain unused in the resolves-edge surface). All three edges exclusion_only=true, T30 (discovery-floor sensitivity with bound_direction populated). No T-convention mint required — T30 covers all three sensitivities cleanly per the existing register.
changelog.lead_choice_rationale:
Lead-A (axion-haloscope-network) from the sub-PR 51 maintainer-confirmed plan. Inspection of v87 confirmed the handoff inventory holds: 6 unattached forward-looking experimental-program nodes (cmb-s4, rubin-lsst, axion-haloscope-network, edm-program, muon-g-2-continuation, fcc), matching the v87 changelog's recorded inventory after desi was attached in sub-PR 50. No handoff-inventory-drift this round.

Four structural reasons for choosing Lead-A at sub-PR 51: (i) opens a NEW physics domain (axion-DM) as the sixth subdomain in Step 4.4 coverage, continuing the broadening required for the eventual Track 4 review pathway — the outreach narrative now spans five major physics domains plus a structurally-distinct beyond-SM phenomenology subfield (axion physics) with its own theoretical and experimental communities; (ii) tight scope (2–3 edges expected, the low end of the Step 4.4 sub-PR range); (iii) the haloscope program directly engages a Phase B if_real_implies entry already in the dataset (the peccei-quinn-axion entry on strong-cp-problem and the qcd-axion-as-dark-matter entry on dark-matter), making this the first Step 4.4 sub-PR to land a resolves edge against a frontier carrying Phase B conditional-resolution content; (iv) the sensitivity-kind diversity argument: this is the first opportunity to exercise the coupling kind on the resolves-edge sensitivity surface, extending the kind-coverage of the resolves-edge surface from 5 of 8 to 6 of 8 kinds, with downstream value for the AI-first find_discriminating_experiments tool that the Phase C MCP rebuild will land.

Lead-B (edm-program) and Lead-C (muon-g-2-continuation) deferred per the sub-PR 51 handoff ranking; recorded in the sub-PR 52 handoff for the next session.
changelog.edge_choice_rationale:
Three edges chosen to span axion-haloscope-network's primary scientific payoffs while keeping the diff-shape at the low end of the handoff's 2–3 range.

Edge 1 (axion-haloscope-network → strong-cp-problem): the frontier-level bear-down. The Peccei-Quinn-axion candidate-targeting entry on strong-cp-problem (discharge_status: gestural-to-partial) names ADMX, ABRACADABRA, IAXO as the experimental discharge channel; the resolves edge attaches the apparatus to the structural claim. Encoded with T30 coupling (value=−15 log10, units=GeV⁻¹, bound_direction=upper) representing the broad few-μeV-band haloscope reach. Exclusion_only=true follows the desi-cc-frontier precedent: KSVZ and DFSZ are model classes (Di Luzio et al. 2020) producing benchmark coupling bands rather than single point predictions admissible under §4 part (2). SECOND frontier-target resolves edge in the project after edge-rs-desi-cc-frontier; demonstrates the Rule-32 frontier-target permission is increasingly utilized rather than an isolated cc-frontier exception.

Edge 2 (axion-haloscope-network → cell-dm-QCD-axion-DFSZ-KSVZ): the canonical-QCD-axion-as-DM cell. The cell's realized_examples already cites ADMX, HAYSTAC, ABRACADABRA, DMRadio as the apparatus inventory; the resolves edge formalizes the cell's experimental discharge. Encoded with T30 coupling (value=−15.4 log10, units=GeV⁻¹, bound_direction=upper) precisely mirroring the ADMX Run 1B Bartram et al. 2021 published DFSZ exclusion (≈4 × 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹). Exclusion_only=true on the same DFSZ-vs-KSVZ-are-classes-not-points grounds as Edge 1.

Edge 3 (axion-haloscope-network → cell-dm-ALP-haloscope): the broader generic-ALP cell. Targets the (m_a, g_aγγ) parameter space across complementary haloscope apparatus (ABRACADABRA/DMRadio < 1 μeV; ADMX/HAYSTAC μeV-tens-of-μeV; MADMAX 40–400 μeV; ALPS-II/IAXO broader m_a at lower coupling). Encoded with T30 coupling (value=−16 log10, units=GeV⁻¹, bound_direction=upper) mirroring the cell's existing coupling qs as the program-wide forward-looking floor. Exclusion_only=true because ALPs are explicitly hypothesis-class candidates with unlinked m_a and g_aγγ.
firewall_self_check: edge-rs-axion-haloscope-network-strong-cp-problem: Sensitivity (g_aγγ ≲ 10⁻¹⁵ GeV⁻¹ program-wide reach in the few-μeV band) sourced from Sikivie 1983 PRL 51:1415 (haloscope-concept design derivation), Bartram et al. (ADMX) 2021 PRL 127:261803 (Run 1B Phys. Rev. Lett. published DFSZ exclusion in 3.3–4.2 μeV), and Stern et al. (ADMX) 2015 Rev. Sci. Instrum. 86:123305 (ADMX Gen-2 design report). Independent of v87 cross-classification graph — derives from haloscope-program design and results papers alone. Exclusion_only=true documented under §4 part (2) admissibility: KSVZ and DFSZ are model classes producing benchmark coupling bands across m_a (per Di Luzio et al. 2020 'The landscape of QCD axion models' Phys. Rep. 870:1) rather than single point predictions that satisfy §4 part (2) for predictions_per_program entries; haloscope sensitivity excludes the bands rather than discriminating two competing point values at a fixed m_a. Firewall self-check (§2.5): if presented with the Sikivie 1983 + ADMX papers + the Peccei-Quinn / Wilczek-Weinberg axion literature alone (no v87 cross-classification graph), the strong-cp-problem resolves edge with the coupling-floor sensitivity and exclusion_only framing is reconstructable as a structural-by-derivation claim. PASS.

edge-rs-axion-haloscope-network-QCD-axion-DM: Sensitivity (DFSZ-benchmark exclusion g_aγγ ≈ 4 × 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹ over 3.3–4.2 μeV from ADMX Run 1B) sourced directly from Bartram et al. (ADMX) 2021 PRL 127:261803 §3 published exclusion plot and Du et al. (ADMX) 2018 PRL 120:151301 first Gen-2 DFSZ result. The m_a–g_aγγ relation linking the cell's energy_scale qs (f_a ~ 10¹¹ GeV) to the few-μeV detection band is derived in Sikivie 1983 PRL 51:1415; the QCD-axion-as-DM relic mechanism is published in Preskill-Wise-Wilczek 1983 PLB 120:127. Independent of v87 dataset structure — derives from the four cited papers alone. Exclusion_only=true on the same Di Luzio-et-al.-2020 model-class-not-point-prediction grounds as Edge 1; the resolves edge tightens the haloscope floor on the cell's content rather than discriminating DFSZ vs KSVZ at a single m_a. Firewall self-check (§2.5): the encoded value −15.4 log10(g_aγγ in GeV⁻¹) is the rendered ADMX published exclusion — reading of the citation's parenthetical, not inference. PASS.

edge-rs-axion-haloscope-network-ALP-haloscope: Sensitivity (program-wide forward-looking floor g_aγγ ≈ 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹ across neV–meV via combined haloscope apparatus) sourced from Armengaud et al. (IAXO) 2019 JCAP 06:047 (IAXO physics paper, design sensitivity), Brun et al. (MADMAX) 2019 EPJC 79:186 (MADMAX 40–400 μeV design), Salemi et al. (ABRACADABRA) 2021 PRL 127:081801 (sub-μeV broadband design), and Bähre et al. (ALPS-II) 2013 JINST 8:T09001 (LSW design). The cell's existing coupling qs (g_aγγ ≈ 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹) is the cell-level forward-looking program target; the resolves edge mirrors this value precisely under the T30 convention. ALP theoretical foundation (Arvanitaki et al. 2010 PRD 81:123530 'String Axiverse') is the cited cell-content motivation for the wide-m_a coverage. Independent of v87 dataset structure — derives from the five cited papers alone. Exclusion_only=true under §4 part (2): ALPs are mass-coupling-unlinked hypothesis-class candidates; the string-axiverse motivation in Arvanitaki et al. 2010 does not commit to specific (m_a, g_aγγ) point predictions admissible under predictions_per_program. Firewall self-check (§2.5): the −16 log10(g_aγγ in GeV⁻¹) value is the rendered combined-program target from the four design reports; the bound_direction=upper reading mirrors the cell's existing qs convention. PASS.

resolves_edges_added.structural_role: Axion-haloscope-network → strong-cp-problem (open-frontier). SECOND FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE in the project (after edge-rs-desi-cc-frontier in sub-PR 50). Bears down on the Peccei-Quinn-axion resolution by direct detection of g_aγγ in the few-μeV band; the program-wide floor 10⁻¹⁵ GeV⁻¹ represents the broad currently-achieved + near-term reach across ADMX, HAYSTAC, MADMAX, ABRACADABRA, DMRadio coverage. Exclusion_only: DFSZ/KSVZ are model classes, not point predictors at fixed m_a.

Axion-haloscope-network → cell-dm-QCD-axion-DFSZ-KSVZ (cell in dark-matter-candidates FC). Targets the canonical QCD-axion-as-100%-DM cell; ADMX Run 1B Bartram et al. 2021 DFSZ exclusion at 3.3–4.2 μeV gives the published floor g_aγγ ≈ 4 × 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹. Exclusion_only on the same Di Luzio-et-al.-2020 model-class-not-point-prediction grounds as the frontier edge.

Axion-haloscope-network → cell-dm-ALP-haloscope (cell in dark-matter-candidates FC). Targets the broader generic-ALP envelope via combined-program apparatus (IAXO, MADMAX, ABRACADABRA/DMRadio, ALPS-II); the cell's existing coupling qs (g_aγγ ≈ 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹) is the program-wide forward-looking floor that this resolves edge mirrors. Exclusion_only: ALPs are explicitly hypothesis-class candidates with mass-coupling unlinked.

changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition:
Three minor inline departures from the sub-PR 51 handoff are documented below; the maintainer-confirmed lead choice (Lead-A: axion-haloscope-network) held in full.

(1) NO HANDOFF-INVENTORY DRIFT — minor positive observation. The maintainer-supplied sub-PR 51 handoff listed 6 unattached experimental-program nodes (cmb-s4, rubin-lsst, axion-haloscope-network, edm-program, muon-g-2-continuation, fcc); live v87 inspection confirmed the inventory holds exactly. No correction needed. This is the FIRST sub-PR since the handoff-inventory-drift case in sub-PR 50 (where Lead A had to be flipped because dune and hyper-k were already attached). Recorded as evidence that v87's per-sub-PR data verification discipline is working: the sub-PR 51 handoff was authored against live v87 data rather than a planning snapshot, and the inventory matched.

(2) THIRD EDGE INCLUDED AT HIGH END OF HANDOFF RANGE. Handoff named '2–3 edges expected'; this sub-PR delivered 3 edges. The third edge (cell-dm-ALP-haloscope) was included after inspection of the dark-matter-candidates FC cell inventory found two viable-targeted axion-class cells (cell-dm-QCD-axion-DFSZ-KSVZ AND cell-dm-ALP-haloscope) that the haloscope-network apparatus naturally targets; constructing the QCD-axion-DM resolves edge without the parallel ALP edge would have left the program's wider m_a coverage (MADMAX 40–400 μeV, ABRACADABRA sub-μeV, ALPS-II / IAXO broader m_a) structurally unattached. Per the handoff's 'tightest sub-PR available' framing the three-edge package is the natural lower bound on coherent coverage rather than a stretch.

(3) MAINTAINER CONFIRMATION POSTURES UPHELD. Maintainer's three confirmation points: (a) Lead choice = Lead-A; (b) exclusion_only=true default unless KSVZ/DFSZ provides a discriminating point structure; (c) target inventory verified against dark-matter-candidates before edge proposal. All three held: Lead-A chosen; both DFSZ and KSVZ found to be model classes rather than point predictors at fixed m_a (Di Luzio et al. 2020 confirms), so exclusion_only=true on all three edges; the two viable-targeted axion-class cells in dark-matter-candidates were identified by direct cell-inventory inspection before edge JSON drafting. The dark-matter open-frontier was deliberately skipped (see skip_decisions (a) below) on the grounds that attaching to both strong-cp-problem AND dark-matter frontiers would double-count the same axion-detection signal across two structurally-parallel frontier resolutions; the structural payoff is captured cleanly by Edge 1 (strong-cp-problem) plus Edge 2 (QCD-axion-DM cell) without the additional dark-matter frontier edge.

Sub-PR 52 handoff recommendation: Lead-B (edm-program, bridges strong-cp-problem and matter-antimatter-asymmetry via nEDM and eEDM, expected 2–3 exclusion_only edges) or Lead-C (muon-g-2-continuation, single-target into muon-g-2 totality-approach, first opportunity to test the predictions_per_program admissibility bar against BSM-interpretation vs SM dispersive-HVP vs SM lattice-HVP point predictions for a_μ). Lead-B is the natural physics-domain-broadening continuation; Lead-C is the smallest sub-PR available and the first deliberate test of non-exclusion-only resolves edges in Phase C. Either is defensible; the sub-PR 52 maintainer call decides.
changelog.schema_candidates_status_observations:
Schema candidate #6 (paired_cell_refs on edges) friction did not recur for the SIXTH consecutive sub-PR (46, 47, 48, 49, 50, 51). Sub-PR 51 added 3 resolves edges and saw zero friction with paired_cell_refs: each edge targets a single cell or a single open-frontier node, with no within-edge pairing requirements. The friction in sub-PR 45 (the cross-FC edge case) remains the only documented friction observation in the resolves-edge surface. Recommendation: continue to defer promotion of #6 to v20 until friction recurs in 2+ further sub-PRs; the 6-sub-PR-streak without friction continues to reinforce the sub-PR 45 observation as case-specific to cross-FC edges rather than indicating a load-bearing schema feature for resolves edges.

T-register status: 32 T-conventions in active register after sub-PR 51 (T1–T14, T17–T31; T15 retired, T16 dropped; no new mint in sub-PR 51). T30 (discovery-floor sensitivity with bound_direction populated) covered all three sub-PR 51 sensitivities cleanly. The handoff's expectation that no new T-mint would be required for the axion-haloscope-network sub-PR is confirmed.
changelog.methodological_observations_recorded:
PHYSICS-DOMAIN-OPENING SIXTH INSTANCE: sub-PR 51 opens the axion-dark-matter physics domain in Step 4.4 — the fifth distinct subdomain after hep-ph (proton decay; sub-PR 46–48), neutrino-sector (oscillations + mass ordering; sub-PR 46–48), gravitational-wave (compact-binary mergers + multiband astronomy; sub-PR 49), and cosmology (dark energy + Hubble tension + BAO; sub-PR 50). The cumulative Step 4.4 physics-subdomain coverage is now: hep-ph + neutrino + GW + cosmology + axion-DM, five major subdomains. This further positions the eventual Track 4 review pathway to reach physicists across multiple major subfields with substantive Step 4.4 content; axion physics has its own theoretical and experimental communities (PQ-mechanism / model-building theorists, ADMX / HAYSTAC / MADMAX / ABRACADABRA / ALPS-II / IAXO experimental groups) with substantive engagement potential.

SECOND FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE: edge-rs-axion-haloscope-network-strong-cp-problem is the project's second resolves edge to target an open-frontier node directly (after edge-rs-desi-cc-frontier in sub-PR 50). The v18 spec's Rule 32 explicitly permits resolves edges to target cell, open-frontier, or totality-approach ids; sub-PR 51 demonstrates that frontier-target is not an isolated cc-frontier exception but a recurring structural pattern when the experimental program's most direct payoff is the frontier's resolution rather than a specific cell's value. The pattern: a resolves edge attaches at the frontier level when (a) the frontier carries a Phase B if_real_implies entry whose resolution the program would experimentally discharge, AND (b) the experimental signal would be diagnostic of the resolution regardless of which specific cell content the resolution forces. Both conditions hold for strong-cp-problem + haloscope-network and for cc-frontier + DESI. A future sub-PR could exercise totality-approach as a resolves-edge target (e.g., a precision measurement on the muon-g-2 or koide-formula totality-approach nodes), completing the Rule-32 permission demonstration across all three target-type categories.

FIRST RESOLVES EDGES USING SENSITIVITY KIND = COUPLING: sub-PR 51 introduces the coupling kind on the resolves-edge sensitivity surface for the first time. Prior 21 resolves edges used: time (proton decay lifetimes; sub-PR 46–48), sigma_deviation (oscillation parameter discrimination thresholds, dark-energy-vs-LCDM tension; sub-PRs 46–50), dimensionless (precision-measurement targets on solar mixing angle and S_BH coefficient; sub-PR 48 + cell-level qs), mass (precision on Δm²_21; sub-PR 48), length (gravitational-wave horizon, BAO sound-horizon; sub-PR 49–50). With sub-PR 51's three coupling-kind sensitivities, the resolves-edge surface now spans 6 of the 8 available quantitative_scale_kind values; only ratio and energy_scale remain unused on the resolves-edge surface (ratio is used heavily on the cell + frontier + prediction surfaces; energy_scale is used on the qg-frontier and chpt-totality-approach surfaces). The kind-diversity expansion has downstream value for the AI-first find_discriminating_experiments tool (Phase C MCP rebuild target): a query like 'what coupling-kind sensitivities discriminate axion physics from other PQ-style mechanisms' becomes runnable once the rebuild lands.

HANDOFF-INVENTORY-DRIFT REGRESSION CHECK: sub-PR 50 documented the first formal handoff-inventory-drift case (Lead A flipped from DUNE+Hyper-K to DESI because the handoff snapshot lagged sub-PR 47–49 deliveries). Sub-PR 51's handoff (authored by the maintainer based on live v87 inspection) matched the live v87 inventory exactly: 6 unattached programs confirmed cleanly. This validates the corrective discipline from sub-PR 50: per-sub-PR live data verification before naming a lead is now working. Recommendation continues: every future handoff verifies inventory against the live data file rather than a planning snapshot.
changelog.skip_decisions:
Three potential additional edges were considered and skipped in sub-PR 51 to keep the diff-shape at the low end of the handoff's 2–3 range and to avoid forcing edges where the structural fit is not clean.

(a) edge-rs-axion-haloscope-network-dark-matter (→ dark-matter open-frontier): SKIP. The haloscope program's detection of an axion at the canonical f_a ∼ 10¹¹ GeV would resolve BOTH the strong-CP frontier (via the PQ mechanism) AND the dark-matter frontier (via the axion-as-DM relic abundance), as documented in the qcd-axion-as-dark-matter if_real_implies entry already on dark-matter (Phase B output). Attaching the haloscope program to BOTH frontiers would double-count the same experimental signal across two structurally-parallel frontier resolutions. The chosen posture is to attach to strong-cp-problem (Edge 1, frontier-level) and to the canonical cell-dm-QCD-axion-DFSZ-KSVZ (Edge 2, cell-level in dark-matter-candidates), letting the cell-level edge carry the dark-matter-side empirical content; the frontier-level dark-matter resolves edge can be added in a future sub-PR if the maintainer wants the parallel structure made explicit. Deferred decision recorded for sub-PR 52 or later.

(b) edge-rs-axion-haloscope-network-string-axiverse (→ cell-dm-predicted-string-axiverse): SKIP. The string-axiverse cell is a predicted-only cell (no specific detection; theoretical motivation from Arvanitaki et al. 2010 PRD 81:123530) with mass range spanning 30+ decades. Haloscope coverage targets a narrow slice of this mass range (sub-μeV to 400 μeV); attaching the haloscope program to a predicted-cell whose content spans masses far beyond the haloscope band would mis-frame the resolves edge as covering content it does not. The Arvanitaki et al. 2010 theoretical motivation is referenced as a citation in Edge 3 (cell-dm-ALP-haloscope) where the haloscope-relevant ALP parameter space is the structurally appropriate target. The string-axiverse cell can receive a resolves edge from a wider experimental program (e.g., black-hole-superradiance bounds, fifth-force tests, fuzzy-DM-on-Lyα-forest bounds) in a future sub-PR if such a program is added to the inventory; haloscope-network is not the right source-node.

(c) Per-program predictions_per_program entries for KSVZ vs DFSZ on Edges 1–2: SKIP. The tempting move was to add predictions_per_program entries for KSVZ (E/N = 0, lower g_aγγ at fixed m_a) and DFSZ (E/N = 8/3, higher g_aγγ at fixed m_a) on the QCD-axion-DM cell edge, claiming that haloscope sensitivity discriminates the two model classes at a given m_a. Per the maintainer's confirmation posture (b) — 'exclusion_only=true unless KSVZ/DFSZ provides a discriminating point structure' — the relevant question is whether KSVZ and DFSZ are point predictors of g_aγγ admissible under §4 part (2). Inspection of the cited Di Luzio et al. 2020 'The landscape of QCD axion models' Phys. Rep. 870:1 review confirmed that both KSVZ and DFSZ are MODEL CLASSES with implementation-dependent fermion content (KSVZ depends on heavy-quark electric charge; DFSZ on Higgs-doublet ratios) producing g_aγγ BANDS at fixed m_a rather than single point values — in the §4 part (2) sense, neither is a point predictor at the admissibility bar. The exclusion_only=true posture (matching haloscope program practice of excluding entire DFSZ/KSVZ bands rather than discriminating between point values) is the §4-admissible posture. This decision mirrors the desi-cc-frontier precedent (sub-PR 50): hypothesis classes get exclusion_only, point predictors get predictions_per_program.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 52 — Phase C STEP 4.4 continues. THIRTEENTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; SEVENTH Step-4.4 sub-PR. TWO (2) resolves edges authored from edm-program OPENING the EDM physics subdomain as the SIXTH Step-4.4 physics subdomain after hep-ph, neutrino-sector, gravitational-wave, cosmology, and axion-dark-matter. Both edges target OPEN-FRONTIER nodes via complementary experimental sub-programs of edm-program: (1) edm-program → strong-cp-problem via the neutron-EDM channel (PSI nEDM 2020 + n2EDM continuation), the THIRD FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE in the project and the SECOND resolves edge attached to strong-cp-problem (first instance of a single frontier carrying two complementary resolves channels — g_aγγ from axion-haloscope-network and θ̄_QCD via |d_n| from edm-program); (2) edm-program → matter-antimatter-asymmetry via the electron-EDM channel (ACME-II 2018 + JILA HfF⁺ 2023 + ACME-III continuation), the FOURTH FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE in the project and the FIRST resolves edge attached to matter-antimatter-asymmetry. Edm-program becomes the project's FIRST DUAL-FRONTIER experimental program — the same source-end node carries resolves edges into two distinct open-frontier nodes via complementary sub-experiments. Both edges exclusion_only=true, T30 (discovery-floor sensitivity with bound_direction populated), kind=coupling (EDM as dimension-five spin-electric-field coupling), units=e·cm, log10=true, bound_direction=upper. No T-convention mint required — T30 covers EDM-bound sensitivities cleanly with the same coupling-of-fermion-to-electromagnetic-field semantics that T30 covered for g_aγγ in sub-PR 51, with units=e·cm replacing units=GeV⁻¹. Resolves-edge count: 24 → 26. Sensitivity-kind diversity remains 6 of 8 (ratio and energy_scale still unused). T-register remains 32 conventions (T1–T14, T17–T31).
changelog.lead_choice_rationale:
Lead-A (edm-program) from the sub-PR 52 maintainer-confirmed plan. Inspection of v88 confirmed the handoff inventory holds: 5 unattached forward-looking experimental-program nodes (cmb-s4, rubin-lsst, edm-program, muon-g-2-continuation, fcc) plus the 7 historical SM-discovery collaborations that are not Phase-C resolves targets by design per the Phase C scope memo §6 finding. No handoff-inventory drift this round — the SECOND consecutive sub-PR (after sub-PR 51) where the pre-supplied handoff inventory matched live data exactly, continuing to validate the per-sub-PR live-verification discipline established in v87.

Four structural reasons for choosing Lead-A (edm-program) at sub-PR 52: (i) UNIQUE DUAL-FRONTIER REACH — edm-program is the ONLY unattached forward-looking experimental program with native dual-frontier reach (the source-node description explicitly cites the program 'bears on the strong-CP problem (via θ̄ constraints from neutron EDM) and the matter-antimatter asymmetry (via new sources of CP violation)'), giving cross-discriminator visibility no other unattached lead provides; (ii) opens the EDM physics subdomain as the SIXTH Step-4.4 physics subdomain, continuing the broadening required for the eventual Track 4 review pathway — the outreach narrative now spans six major physics subdomains (hep-ph + neutrino + GW + cosmology + axion-DM + EDM); (iii) FIRST RESOLVES EDGE ATTACHED TO matter-antimatter-asymmetry — a previously zero-resolves-edge frontier gains coverage; (iv) tight scope (2 edges, at the low end of the handoff's 2–3 range), well-cited literature already on the source node, clean T30 fit with no new T-mint expected.

Lead-B (muon-g-2-continuation) and Lead-C (cmb-s4) deferred per the sub-PR 52 handoff ranking; the muon-g-2-continuation deliberate test of the predictions_per_program admissibility bar against non-exclusion-only content is recorded in the sub-PR 53 handoff for the next session.
changelog.edge_choice_rationale:
Two edges chosen to span edm-program's two structurally-distinct sub-experimental channels (nEDM and eEDM) at the frontier level, keeping the diff-shape at the low end of the handoff's 2–3 range and avoiding edges where the structural fit is not clean.

Edge 1 (edm-program → strong-cp-problem): the nEDM channel. The Crewther-DiVecchia-Veneziano-Witten 1979 PLB 88:123 relation |d_n| ≈ (3.6 × 10⁻¹⁶ e·cm) × θ̄_QCD provides the direct translation from neutron-EDM bound to strong-CP-angle bound; the strong-cp-problem frontier's currently-recorded quantitative_scale of |θ̄_QCD| < 10⁻¹⁰ is cited from Abel et al. 2020 (PSI nEDM) PRL 124:081803 already, making the structural connection explicit in the data. Encoded with T30 coupling (value=−27 log10, units=e·cm, bound_direction=upper) representing the n2EDM design target |d_n| ≲ 10⁻²⁷ e·cm. Exclusion_only=true on §4 part (2) grounds: Peccei-Quinn predicts θ̄ → 0 in the f_a → ∞ limit without fixing a non-zero residual at a specific level discriminable at the EDM floor; Anthropic and Vafa-Witten provide parameter-space-class and structural-constraint arguments respectively. THIRD FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE in the project; SECOND resolves edge attached to strong-cp-problem; FIRST instance of a single frontier carrying TWO complementary resolves channels.

Edge 2 (edm-program → matter-antimatter-asymmetry): the eEDM channel. The Cesarotti-Lu-Nakai-Soni-Strassler 2019 PRD 100:015013 framework translates eEDM bounds into constraints on the BSM CP-violating sources required to source baryogenesis via the four candidate-targeting programs (Leptogenesis, Electroweak-baryogenesis-extended, Affleck-Dine, GUT). Encoded with T30 coupling (value=−30 log10, units=e·cm, bound_direction=upper) mirroring the JILA HfF⁺ 2023 Science 381:46 published floor and ACME-III near-term reach. Exclusion_only=true on the same parameter-space-class-not-point-predictions §4 part (2) grounds: Leptogenesis's ε-parameter depends on heavy-Majorana mass spectrum and Yukawa structure with substantial freedom; Electroweak-baryogenesis-extended's predicted |d_e| spans ∼10⁻²⁸ to <10⁻³¹ e·cm across model classes; Affleck-Dine and GUT have model-dependent predictions. FOURTH FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE in the project; FIRST resolves edge attached to matter-antimatter-asymmetry (zero → one).

The two edges together make edm-program the project's FIRST DUAL-FRONTIER experimental program — a structural pattern the Phase C scope memo §6 identified as cross-discriminator-substrate for the find_discriminating_experiments tool the MCP rebuild will land.

(PSI nEDM) 2020 PRL 124:081803 (published bound), Ayres et al. (n2EDM) 2021 EPJ C 81:512 (next-generation design report), Pendlebury et al. 2015 PRD 92:092003 (predecessor apparatus continuity), and Pospelov-Ritz 2005 Annals Phys 318:119 (review of EDM-as-CP-violation-probe). (JILA HfF⁺) 2023 Science 381:46 (current best published bound), Andreev et al. (ACME II) 2018 Nature 562:355 (predecessor bound), and Cesarotti-Lu-Nakai-Soni-Strassler 2019 PRD 100:015013 (the structural-interpretation framework translating eEDM bounds to baryogenesis-program constraints).
