PASS.

resolves_edges_added.structural_role: Edm-program → strong-cp-problem (open-frontier). THIRD FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE in the project (after edge-rs-desi-cc-frontier sub-PR 50 and edge-rs-axion-haloscope-network-strong-cp-problem sub-PR 51). SECOND resolves edge attached to strong-cp-problem — first instance of a single frontier carrying TWO complementary resolves channels (axion-haloscope direct-detection of g_aγγ in sub-PR 51; nEDM indirect-bound on θ̄_QCD via |d_n| in this sub-PR). The nEDM channel translates |d_n| bound to θ̄_QCD bound via the Crewther-DiVecchia-Veneziano-Witten 1979 PLB 88:123 relation |d_n| ≈ 3.6 × 10⁻¹⁶ e·cm × θ̄_QCD; the n2EDM target |d_n| ≲ 10⁻²⁷ e·cm tightens the existing strong-cp-problem frontier quantitative_scale |θ̄_QCD| < 10⁻¹⁰ by another order of magnitude. Exclusion_only on §4(2) admissibility for the three candidate-targeting programs (PQ/axion, Anthropic, Vafa-Witten).

Edm-program → matter-antimatter-asymmetry (open-frontier). FOURTH FRONTIER-TARGET RESOLVES EDGE in the project. FIRST RESOLVES EDGE ATTACHED TO matter-antimatter-asymmetry — previously zero. The eEDM channel translates |d_e| bound to constraints on the BSM CP-violating phases required to source baryogenesis, via the Cesarotti-Lu-Nakai-Soni-Strassler 2019 PRD 100:015013 framework; the JILA HfF⁺ 2023 floor at |d_e| < 4.1 × 10⁻³⁰ e·cm essentially closes light-stop EW baryogenesis and substantially constrains two-Higgs-doublet light-pseudoscalar variants; ACME-III continuation extends reach. Exclusion_only on §4(2) admissibility for the four candidate-targeting programs (Leptogenesis, EW-baryogenesis-extended, Affleck-Dine, GUT-baryogenesis). Pairing with Edge 1 makes edm-program the project's FIRST DUAL-FRONTIER experimental program.

changelog.T_convention_minted: None. T30 (discovery-floor sensitivity with bound_direction populated) covers both EDM sensitivities cleanly. The conceptual extension is that T30's coverage extends from kind=coupling with units=GeV⁻¹ (the axion-haloscope-network case in sub-PR 51) to kind=coupling with units=e·cm (the EDM case in this sub-PR), reflecting the dimension-five spin-electric-field-coupling semantics shared by g_aγγ (axion-photon) and d_n / d_e (nucleon-EDM and lepton-EDM). The handoff's expectation that no new T-mint would be required for the edm-program sub-PR is confirmed.
changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition:
Two minor inline observations from the sub-PR 52 handoff are documented below; the maintainer-confirmed lead choice (Lead-A: edm-program) held in full.

(1) NO HANDOFF-INVENTORY DRIFT — SECOND consecutive sub-PR (after sub-PR 51) where the pre-supplied handoff inventory matched live data exactly. The sub-PR 52 handoff listed 5 unattached forward-looking experimental-program nodes (cmb-s4, rubin-lsst, edm-program, muon-g-2-continuation, fcc); live v88 inspection confirmed the inventory holds exactly (the additional 7 'unattached' nodes are the historical SM-discovery collaborations that are not Phase-C resolves targets by design per the Phase C scope memo §6 finding, not a drift case). The two-sub-PR streak of clean handoff inventories continues to validate the per-sub-PR live-verification discipline established in v87.

(2) HANDOFF UNIT-CHOICE QUESTION SETTLED — sub-PR 52 handoff explicitly raised the question of whether T30's 'discovery-floor with bound_direction' convention handles EDM upper bounds cleanly or whether a new 'exclusion-at-CL' register entry is needed. Settled: T30 covers cleanly. The EDM bound at 90% CL is semantically equivalent to a discovery-floor sensitivity — the experiment WOULD detect an EDM if |d| > floor, and not having seen one, sets an upper bound at the floor. The bound_direction=upper field expresses the 'exclusion above the floor' semantics that the haloscope-style 'exclusion above the coupling floor' also uses. No new T-mint required. The handoff's primary anticipated outcome (T30 sufficient, no mint) is confirmed.

Two edges delivered (handoff named '2–3 edges expected'). No third edge included because a third edge would have been forced rather than physics-clean: the natural physics of edm-program splits into exactly two frontier-target channels (nEDM → strong-CP; eEDM → baryogenesis), with the two sub-experiments (PSI nEDM/n2EDM; ACME-II/III + JILA HfF⁺) each cohesively grouped under its own frontier target. A cell-level edge would have required choosing a single representative cell from candidate-targeting programs that are themselves not yet cell-rendered in the data; deferred to a future sub-PR if a maintainer-orchestration pattern emerges.
changelog.schema_candidates_status_observations:
Schema candidate #6 (paired_cell_refs on edges) friction did not recur for the SEVENTH consecutive sub-PR (46, 47, 48, 49, 50, 51, 52). Sub-PR 52 added 2 resolves edges and saw zero friction with paired_cell_refs: each edge targets a single open-frontier node, with no within-edge pairing requirements. The friction in sub-PR 45 (the cross-FC edge case) remains the only documented friction observation in the resolves-edge surface. Recommendation: continue to defer promotion of #6 to v20 until friction recurs in further sub-PRs; the 7-sub-PR-streak without friction continues to reinforce the sub-PR 45 observation as case-specific to cross-FC edges rather than indicating a load-bearing schema feature for resolves edges.

T-register status: 32 T-conventions in active register after sub-PR 52 (T1–T14, T17–T31; T15 retired, T16 dropped; no new mint in sub-PR 52). T30 (discovery-floor sensitivity with bound_direction populated) covered both EDM sensitivities cleanly with the new units=e·cm string. The handoff's expectation that no new T-mint would be required for the edm-program sub-PR is confirmed.

Incidental schema-friction observation: the units string 'e·cm' is the first non-SI-derived units string used on the resolves-edge sensitivity surface (previous strings: GeV, TeV, MeV, eV, GeV⁻¹, fm, m, s, yr, dimensionless-via-omission). The schema's units field is a free-form string; the 'e·cm' string is the community-standard EDM units convention and validates cleanly under Rule 31 (which requires units to be PRESENT for dimensional kinds, not from a fixed vocabulary). No schema modification required.
changelog.methodological_observations_recorded:
PHYSICS-DOMAIN-OPENING SEVENTH INSTANCE: sub-PR 52 opens the EDM physics subdomain in Step 4.4 — the sixth distinct subdomain after hep-ph (proton decay; sub-PR 46–48), neutrino-sector (oscillations + mass ordering; sub-PR 46–48), gravitational-wave (compact-binary mergers + multiband; sub-PR 49), cosmology (dark energy + Hubble tension + BAO; sub-PR 50), and axion-dark-matter (haloscope coupling for QCD axion + ALP; sub-PR 51). The cumulative Step 4.4 physics-subdomain coverage is now: hep-ph + neutrino + GW + cosmology + axion-DM + EDM, SIX major subdomains. This further positions the eventual Track 4 review pathway to reach physicists across multiple major subfields with substantive Step 4.4 content; EDM physics has its own theoretical and experimental communities (low-energy precision-CP theorists, n2EDM at PSI / ACME at Harvard-Yale-Northwestern / JILA HfF⁺ at Cornell-JILA experimental groups) with substantive engagement potential.

FIRST DUAL-FRONTIER EXPERIMENTAL PROGRAM: edm-program is the project's first source-end node that carries resolves edges into TWO distinct open-frontier nodes via complementary sub-experiments. The pattern — a single experimental program targeting structurally-disjoint frontiers via independent sub-channels — is the AI-first cross-discriminator substrate the Phase C scope memo §6 identified as load-bearing for the find_discriminating_experiments tool the MCP rebuild will land. Specifically: a query like 'which experiments would discriminate between Peccei-Quinn-axion (strong-CP resolution) and electroweak-baryogenesis-extended (matter-antimatter resolution)?' now resolves to edm-program covering BOTH frontiers, with the nEDM channel discriminating in the strong-CP frontier and the eEDM channel discriminating in the baryogenesis frontier — a structural fact that emerges from the dataset only via the dual-edge attachment.

FIRST FRONTIER WITH TWO COMPLEMENTARY RESOLVES CHANNELS: strong-cp-problem is the project's first open-frontier node carrying TWO resolves edges from independent experimental programs (axion-haloscope-network in sub-PR 51 via the g_aγγ direct-detection channel; edm-program in this sub-PR 52 via the |d_n| → θ̄_QCD indirect channel). Both channels probe the SAME Peccei-Quinn-axion candidate-targeting program from complementary directions: haloscope detects the axion as a physical particle; nEDM detects its dynamical-relaxation residual. The two-channel coverage is itself a finding — strong-CP is the only project frontier where TWO Phase-C resolves edges intersect a single candidate-targeting program from independent experimental methods. Future sub-PRs may add a third edge from helioscopes (IAXO) or fifth-force-search programs to deepen the multi-channel coverage further.

UNITS-STRING DIVERSITY ON SENSITIVITY SURFACE: sub-PR 52 introduces 'e·cm' as the first non-SI-derived units string on the resolves-edge sensitivity surface. The schema's free-form units field accommodates this without modification; the practice of letting community-standard units strings populate the field (rather than restricting to a fixed vocabulary) is consistent with the existing convention (GeV, fm, yr, etc. already in use). Recorded as a precedent for future sub-PRs that may introduce further community-standard units strings (e.g., 'A' for ALP magnetic-moment bounds, 'erg' or 'Jy' for astrophysical observable bounds).
changelog.skip_decisions:
Three potential additional edges were considered and skipped in sub-PR 52 to keep the diff-shape at the low end of the handoff's 2–3 range and to avoid forcing edges where the structural fit is not clean.

(a) edge-rs-edm-program-cp-violation-via-cell-targeting (→ a cell on some FC representing CP-violation phenomenology): SKIP. No FC in v88 currently has a cell whose content is specifically 'BSM CP-violating phase parameters constrained by EDM bounds' — the CP-violation structural content is distributed across multiple candidate_targeting program entries on matter-antimatter-asymmetry and across not-yet-fully-rendered cells in BSM-phenomenology-adjacent FCs (e.g., the EW-baryogenesis-extended program's parameter space is not a single cell). Attaching the edge to a single representative cell would have forced a choice; the cleaner posture is to attach at the frontier level (Edge 2) where the candidate-targeting program list IS rendered, and let future cell-level coverage emerge as BSM-phenomenology FCs are authored more deeply. Deferred decision recorded for sub-PR 53 or later.

(b) edge-rs-edm-program-strong-cp-via-cell (→ a cell representing the canonical PQ-mechanism phenomenology): SKIP. The strong-cp-problem frontier IS the locus where the PQ-mechanism candidate_targeting entry lives (discharge_status: gestural-to-partial); attaching edm-program to a cell representing PQ phenomenology would double-count the same structural connection at finer granularity. The strong-cp-problem frontier-level attachment (Edge 1) is the cleanest single point of contact. Deferred decision recorded for sub-PR 53 or later if a cell-level structural payoff emerges.

(c) edge-rs-edm-program-dark-matter (→ dark-matter open-frontier): SKIP. EDMs can probe CP-violating couplings in dark-sector mediators (per recent literature, e.g., Berger-Chen-Tait-Wang 2014 PRD 90:115029 'Direct detection of dark forces from EDMs'), but the connection is to specific BSM dark-sector models rather than to dark-matter-as-such; attaching to the dark-matter frontier would conflate the EDM-as-CP-violation-probe role (Edge 2's domain) with EDM-as-dark-sector-probe role (a structurally-distinct future possibility). The current literature does not yet treat eEDM bounds as a primary dark-matter discharge channel comparable to direct/indirect detection or accelerator searches. Skip; revisit if a future BSM-dark-sector resolves-edge pattern emerges in the data.

All three skip decisions are firewall-faithful per the §2.5 self-check: the chosen edge inventory (2 frontier-target edges) is the structurally-clean subset; the skipped edges would either force structure that is not yet rendered (a, b) or conflate distinct probe-channels (c). The skips are not absence-of-evidence concessions — they are structurally-driven decisions to wait for the dataset to develop the surrounding cells before attaching further edges.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 53 — Phase C STEP 4.4 continues. FOURTEENTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; EIGHTH Step-4.4 sub-PR. ONE (1) resolves edge authored from muon-g-2-continuation OPENING the muon-anomalous-magnetic-moment physics subdomain as the SEVENTH Step-4.4 physics subdomain after hep-ph, neutrino-sector, gravitational-wave, cosmology, axion-dark-matter, and EDM. Targets the muon-g-2 totality-approach carrier (NOT an open-frontier — muon-g-2 is the project's first totality-approach-targeted resolves edge), the FIFTH FRONTIER/CARRIER-LEVEL RESOLVES EDGE in the project after edge-rs-desi-cc-frontier (sub-PR 50) and the three sub-PR 51/52 frontier-edges. TWO methodological FIRSTS land in this edge: (1) PROJECT'S FIRST exclusion_only=false RESOLVES EDGE — §4(2) admissibility passes for both Theory Initiative 2020 dispersive HVP (Aoyama et al. 2020 Phys Rep 887:1; a_μ^SM = 116 591 810(43) × 10⁻¹¹) and BMW 2021 lattice HVP (Borsanyi et al. 2021 Nature 593:51; a_μ^SM ≈ 116 591 954(55) × 10⁻¹¹), giving PROJECT'S FIRST PPP-POPULATED edge with TWO ENTRIES drawn from a totality-approach carrier's competing SM-side candidate-targeting programs. (2) T33 MINT — 'precision-measurement sensitivity with bound_direction=two-sided', structurally distinct from T30 (discovery-floor sensitivity with bound_direction=upper/lower); first Phase C T-mint since T31 in sub-PR 49 and the FIRST T-mint that broadens semantic categories rather than refining existing patterns. Edge: kind=dimensionless, value=1.48 × 10⁻¹⁰ (127 ppb of a_μ ≈ 1.166 × 10⁻³ from Aguillard et al. 2025 PRL 135:101802 final Run-1–6 combined result), bound_direction=two-sided, timeline=running (Fermilab E989 has published final result; J-PARC E34 under construction). BSM-loop interpretation excluded from PPP under §4(2) parameter-freedom test (Athron et al. 2021 JHEP 09:080; Lindner-Platscher-Queiroz 2018 Phys Rep 731:1) matching the axion/EDM precedent. THIRD CONSECUTIVE SUB-PR WITH NO HANDOFF-INVENTORY DRIFT. POST-2021 LITERATURE DRIFT FINDING RECORDED: Aliberti et al. 2025 Phys Rep 1143:1 (TI 2025 update) and Boccaletti et al. 2024 Nature (BMW hybrid) have substantially evolved the empirical picture; the muon-g-2 carrier's quantitative_scale (4.2σ) and if_real_implies.muon-g2-bsm-loop entry are frozen in the 2021 picture and lag current consensus — carrier-refresh deferred to a separate follow-up sub-PR per the methodological-test-purity scope discipline.
changelog.lead_choice_rationale:
Lead-A (muon-g-2-continuation) from the sub-PR 53 maintainer-confirmed plan. Inspection of v89 confirmed the handoff inventory holds exactly: 4 unattached forward-looking experimental-program nodes (cmb-s4, rubin-lsst, muon-g-2-continuation, fcc) plus the 7 historical SM-discovery collaborations that are not Phase-C resolves targets by design per the Phase C scope memo §6 finding. NO handoff-inventory drift this round — the THIRD CONSECUTIVE sub-PR (after sub-PRs 51 and 52) where the pre-supplied handoff inventory matched live data exactly, continuing to validate the per-sub-PR live-verification discipline established in v87.

Three structural reasons for choosing Lead-A (muon-g-2-continuation) at sub-PR 53, ordered by binding force: (i) METHODOLOGICAL-TEST RATIONALE — the SM-side candidate-targeting programs on the muon-g-2 carrier (TI 2020 dispersive HVP, BMW 2021 lattice HVP) supply two competing program-internal SM PREDICTIONS for the same observable (a_μ), structurally different from any prior resolves-edge case in the dataset; this is the smallest-scope sub-PR available to deliberately test whether §4(2) admits SM-side competing-prediction edges as exclusion_only=false with PPP populated, before later sub-PRs (fcc, cmb-s4 potentially) encounter analogous decisions without precedent to draw on. (ii) T-CONVENTION PRECEDENT — the muon-g-2 measurement is a precision-measurement sensitivity (two-sided window around a_μ^exp) rather than a discovery-floor sensitivity (one-sided exclusion above/below floor) handled by T30; minting T33 now codifies the T30/T33 distinction before future precision-measurement sub-PRs (FCC-ee Higgs couplings the most likely candidate) need it. The handoff anticipated and asked for this mint decision. (iii) PHYSICS-DOMAIN-OPENING SEVENTH INSTANCE — opens the muon-anomalous-magnetic-moment subdomain as the seventh distinct Step 4.4 physics subdomain. The cumulative coverage now reaches across seven major subdomains (hep-ph + neutrino + GW + cosmology + axion-DM + EDM + muon-anomaly), supporting the eventual Track 4 review pathway with substantive Step 4.4 content reaching multiple subfield-specialist communities.

Reason (i) outweighs (iii) here, in the maintainer's framing: the methodological-test value of this sub-PR sits above subdomain broadening because precedent fixed now (PPP-populated on a totality-approach with competing SM-side calculations + exclusion_only=false) cascades into later Step 4.4 sub-PRs and into the eventual Phase C MCP rebuild's find_discriminating_experiments tool semantics. Reasons (ii) and (iii) compound (i) but do not displace it.
changelog.edge_choice_rationale:
ONE edge chosen — the smallest-scope sub-PR in the Step 4.4 sweep so far, keeping the diff-shape minimal and the methodological-test purity maximal.

Edge (muon-g-2-continuation → muon-g-2): the program's discrimination among the muon-g-2 totality-approach's three candidate-targeting programs proceeds via precision measurement of a_μ = (g_μ - 2)/2. The Fermilab E989 storage-ring experiment has reached 127 ppb relative precision at design completion (Aguillard et al. 2025 PRL 135:101802, absolute uncertainty ∼1.48 × 10⁻¹⁰ on a_μ ≈ 1.166 × 10⁻³); J-PARC E34 (Abe et al. 2019 PTEP 2019:053C02, under construction) provides a systematics-independent cross-check via the ultra-cold muon storage-ring technique without electric focusing. Encoded with T33 (new mint: precision-measurement sensitivity with bound_direction=two-sided), kind=dimensionless, value=1.48 × 10⁻¹⁰, bound_direction=two-sided, timeline=running.

PPP populated with TWO entries (project's first exclusion_only=false resolves edge):

  (a) Theory Initiative 2020 dispersive HVP: a_μ^SM = (116 591 810 ± 43) × 10⁻¹¹ per Aoyama et al. 2020 Phys Rep 887:1. Clean §4(2) pass — the consensus paper is the program-internal calculation of the 'dispersive-method refinement (e⁺ e⁻ → hadrons)' candidate-targeting entry on the carrier (currently 'active investigation' in the loose-ends).

  (b) BMW 2021 lattice HVP: a_μ^SM ≈ (116 591 954 ± 55) × 10⁻¹¹ per Borsanyi et al. 2021 Nature 593:51 (LO-HVP at 707.5(5.5) × 10⁻¹⁰, combined with non-HVP SM components from Aoyama 2020 §8). Clean §4(2) pass — the Nature paper is the program-internal calculation of the 'lattice-QCD HVP refinement' candidate-targeting entry on the carrier (currently 'active investigation' in the loose-ends).

The third candidate-targeting program on the carrier ('new-physics interpretations — dark photons, sleptons, etc.', currently discharge_status: gestural-to-constrained) is EXCLUDED from PPP on §4(2) parameter-freedom test grounds: BSM-loop predictions of Δa_μ depend on model-parameter values (mediator mass, coupling strength, dark-photon mixing, slepton spectrum) and supply no single point prediction at the experimental floor; the BSM-loop literature (Athron et al. 2021 JHEP 09:080; Lindner-Platscher-Queiroz 2018 Phys Rep 731:1) works in model-parameter space, matching the parameter-space-class pattern that axion-haloscope-network (sub-PR 51) and edm-program (sub-PR 52) handled with exclusion_only=true. The muon-g-2 case differs in that the SM-SIDE candidate programs DO supply discriminable point predictions, so the BSM-side exclusion is local to PPP rather than globalizing the entire edge to exclusion_only=true.

FIRST PPP-populated resolves edge on a totality-approach carrier: all 8 prior PPP-populated edges target specific cells (hyper-k/dune/juno on sm-rep-content / su5-gut-rep-content / neutrino-sector-phenomenology cells; gw-ground-network/lisa on compact-astrophysical-objects cells). The muon-g-2 carrier is a totality-approach, and the SM-side candidate programs deliver point predictions for the same observable rather than per-cell predictions for distinct phenomena. This establishes the precedent for the 'competing SM-side calculations on a totality-approach' pattern. FIFTH FRONTIER/CARRIER-LEVEL RESOLVES EDGE in the project (after desi/cc-frontier sub-PR 50; axion-haloscope/strong-cp-problem sub-PR 51; edm-program/strong-cp-problem sub-PR 52; edm-program/matter-antimatter-asymmetry sub-PR 52).

Single-edge sub-PR by design: muon-g-2-continuation has exactly one carrier it discriminates (the muon-g-2 totality-approach itself); a second edge targeting a cell would force a choice of cell (no FC currently has a cell whose content is specifically 'a_μ as the discriminating observable between dispersive-HVP and lattice-HVP SM predictions'). The single-edge shape keeps the methodological-test purity maximal and avoids the structural-fit-not-clean problem the skip_decisions section of sub-PR 52 flagged for analogous cell-targeting candidates.
firewall_self_check: edge-rs-muon-g-2-continuation-muon-g-2: Sensitivity (a_μ absolute uncertainty 1.48 × 10⁻¹⁰ = 127 ppb of a_μ ≈ 1.166 × 10⁻³ at Fermilab E989 design completion) sourced from Aguillard et al. 2025 PRL 135:101802 (final Run-1–6 combined published precision), Aguillard et al. 2023 PRL 131:161802 (Run-1+2+3 intermediate at 0.20 ppm), Abi et al. 2021 PRL 126:141801 (Run-1 initial at 0.46 ppm), and Abe et al. 2019 PTEP 2019:053C02 §3 (J-PARC E34 design document for systematics-independent cross-check). Independent of v89 cross-classification graph — derives from the experimental design and result papers alone, with the SM-prediction comparison drawn from Aoyama 2020 + Borsanyi 2021 which are also already cited on the carrier's existing quantitative_scale and if_real_implies entries. Firewall self-check (§2.5): if presented with the four sensitivity citations + the two SM-prediction citations + the BSM-loop literature (Athron 2021, Lindner-Platscher-Queiroz 2018) alone (no v89 cross-classification graph), the resolves edge with kind=dimensionless precision sensitivity, two PPP entries for the SM-side dispersive/lattice programs, BSM-loop exclusion from PPP on parameter-freedom grounds, and exclusion_only=false framing is reconstructable as a structural-by-derivation claim. §4(2) admissibility passes for TI 2020 (single program-internal point prediction in Aoyama 2020) and BMW 2021 (single program-internal point prediction in Borsanyi 2021 combined with Aoyama 2020 non-HVP components); §4(2) admissibility fails for BSM-loop on the parameter-freedom test (model-space rather than point-prediction structure), matching the axion/EDM precedent in sub-PRs 51–52 — the difference is that here only the BSM-side fails so exclusion is local to PPP rather than global to the entire edge. T33 mint documented in T_convention_minted field; structurally distinct from T30 by virtue of precision-measurement vs discovery-floor semantic register. PASS.

resolves_edges_added.structural_role: Muon-g-2-continuation → muon-g-2 (totality-approach). FIFTH FRONTIER/CARRIER-LEVEL RESOLVES EDGE in the project (after desi/cc-frontier sub-PR 50; axion-haloscope/strong-cp-problem sub-PR 51; edm-program/strong-cp-problem sub-PR 52; edm-program/matter-antimatter-asymmetry sub-PR 52). FIRST resolves edge targeting a totality-approach carrier (all prior carrier-level edges targeted open-frontier nodes; muon-g-2 is the first totality-approach-typed target). PROJECT'S FIRST exclusion_only=false RESOLVES EDGE — PPP populated with two SM-side competing point predictions (TI 2020 dispersive HVP from Aoyama et al. 2020 Phys Rep 887:1; BMW 2021 lattice HVP from Borsanyi et al. 2021 Nature 593:51). PROJECT'S FIRST PPP-POPULATED RESOLVES EDGE on a non-cell target. BSM-loop interpretation excluded from PPP on §4(2) parameter-freedom grounds (Athron 2021 + Lindner-Platscher-Queiroz 2018), local to PPP rather than globalizing the edge to exclusion_only=true. The Fermilab E989 final Run-1–6 precision (127 ppb, Aguillard 2025 PRL 135:101802) corresponds to absolute uncertainty ∼1.48 × 10⁻¹⁰ on a_μ — roughly 10× smaller than the TI–BMW gap of ∼144 × 10⁻¹¹, so the experiment cleanly discriminates the two SM-side programs at design completion. T33 mint (precision-measurement sensitivity with bound_direction=two-sided) codifies the semantic distinction from T30 (discovery-floor with bound_direction=upper/lower).

changelog.T_convention_minted:
T33 — 'precision-measurement sensitivity with bound_direction=two-sided' for resolves-edge sensitivity. STRUCTURALLY DISTINCT from T30 (discovery-floor sensitivity with bound_direction=upper/lower as used in proton-decay/axion-haloscope/EDM exclusion edges in sub-PRs 46–52).

Semantic anchor: T33 records the ACHIEVED ABSOLUTE PRECISION of a measurement on a observable's central value — the experiment establishes a two-sided window |observable_measured − observable_true| ≤ σ around the measured central value, and the relevant 'sensitivity' is the value of σ itself. By contrast T30 records the value AT WHICH a one-sided exclusion is set — the experiment WOULD detect a signal if some quantity exceeds (or falls below) a floor, and having not detected one, sets a bound at the floor.

Why mint rather than extend T30 to bound_direction=two-sided: the conceptual register differs in two ways the mint preserves. (1) Semantic clarity: 'discovery floor' and 'achieved precision' are structurally distinct sensitivity concepts; precision is a window around a measured CENTRAL VALUE whereas discovery floor is a threshold on the EXISTENCE of a signal. (2) Future-proofing: future Phase C sub-PRs are likely to include precision-measurement programs (FCC-ee Higgs-coupling precision, future electron g-2, possibly future tau g-2 if it becomes part of muon-g-2-continuation's downstream scope, and the find_discriminating_experiments tool the Phase C MCP rebuild will land needs to distinguish 'experiments that discriminate via precision measurement' from 'experiments that discriminate via signal exclusion'). T33 makes this distinction first-class.

First Phase C T-mint since T31 in sub-PR 49 (4 sub-PRs ago). T-register status after sub-PR 53: 33 T-conventions in active register (T1–T14, T17–T31, T33; T15 retired, T16 dropped, T32 not present in active mints; the T32 mention in the v89 changelog regex was a spurious extraction — verified via the v89 T_convention_minted field which records 'None' and the schema_candidates_status_observations field which lists 'T1–T14, T17–T31' as the 32-convention active register pre-mint). Post-sub-PR-53 T-register active list: T1–T14, T17–T31, T33 = 33 active conventions; sub-PR 53's mint of T33 is correctly distinguished from any prior T32 drift-mention.
changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition:
Three observations from the sub-PR 53 handoff are documented below; the maintainer-confirmed lead choice (Lead-A: muon-g-2-continuation), T33 mint decision, edge ID, and BMW uncertainty verification all held in full.

(1) NO HANDOFF-INVENTORY DRIFT — THIRD CONSECUTIVE sub-PR (after sub-PRs 51 and 52) where the pre-supplied handoff inventory matched live data exactly. The sub-PR 53 handoff listed 4 unattached forward-looking experimental-program nodes (cmb-s4, rubin-lsst, muon-g-2-continuation, fcc); live v89 inspection confirmed the inventory holds exactly (the additional 7 'unattached' nodes are the historical SM-discovery collaborations that are not Phase-C resolves targets by design per the Phase C scope memo §6 finding, not a drift case). The three-sub-PR streak of clean handoff inventories continues to validate the per-sub-PR live-verification discipline established in v87. NOTE: this is the natural endpoint of the inventory-drift-monitoring observation — with three consecutive clean inventories the discipline is established as load-bearing; subsequent sub-PRs (54+) need only verify the inventory live without continuing to flag the streak as a methodological observation in its own right.

(2) HANDOFF METHODOLOGICAL-TEST RATIONALE CONFIRMED — the handoff's anticipated outcome held: TI 2020 dispersive HVP and BMW 2021 lattice HVP both pass §4(2) admissibility cleanly as program-internal point-prediction calculations; the BSM-loop family fails §4(2) on parameter-freedom grounds; the resolved edge has exclusion_only=false with two PPP entries. The handoff's deliberate-test framing — 'either outcome is methodologically valuable per the sub-PR 52 handoff's deliberate-test rationale' — lands on the positive case (the first non-exclusion_only=true edge), which is the more consequential precedent for future sub-PRs (fcc, cmb-s4 potentially) encountering analogous decisions. The negative-case alternative (BSM-loop somehow passing §4(2)) would have been a methodological surprise; the positive case (BSM-loop excluded cleanly) extends the established sub-PRs 51–52 pattern without disturbing it.

(3) HANDOFF T-CONVENTION DECISION SETTLED — the sub-PR 53 handoff explicitly raised the T30 vs T33-mint decision question and indicated it was for the authoring chat to settle. SETTLED with T33 mint, on the two grounds documented in the T_convention_minted field: semantic-register distinction (precision vs discovery-floor) and future-proofing for FCC-ee Higgs-coupling-precision-style sub-PRs. The handoff's alternative ('extend T30 to cover two-sided') was considered and rejected on the grounds that conflating semantically-distinct sensitivity categories under one T-convention with a bound_direction discriminator alone reduces query-answerability for the find_discriminating_experiments tool the Phase C MCP rebuild will land.
changelog.schema_candidates_status_observations:
Schema candidate #6 (paired_cell_refs on edges) friction did not recur for the EIGHTH consecutive sub-PR (46, 47, 48, 49, 50, 51, 52, 53). Sub-PR 53 added 1 resolves edge targeting a single totality-approach node with no within-edge pairing requirement — paired_cell_refs remains case-specific to the cross-FC edge case observed in sub-PR 45. Recommendation: continue to defer promotion of #6 to v20 until friction recurs in further sub-PRs.

T-register status after sub-PR 53: 33 T-conventions in active register (T1–T14, T17–T31, T33; T15 retired, T16 dropped; T33 newly minted this sub-PR). T33 (precision-measurement sensitivity with bound_direction=two-sided) is the first Phase C T-mint since T31 in sub-PR 49.

Incidental schema observation: the sensitivity kind=dimensionless on a resolves edge (sub-PR 53 is the first such case among the resolves-edge surface; prior dimensionless sensitivities appear on 104 quantitative_scale entries elsewhere in the dataset, mostly on cell-level critical-exponent values and on the koide-formula carrier's frontier-level quantitative_scale) validates cleanly under v18/v19 Rules 28 (units forbidden on dimensionless: PASS, units field omitted) and 29 (log10 permitted only on six dimensional/ratio/coupling kinds: PASS, log10 field omitted). The dimensionless sensitivity is a structurally novel choice for a resolves edge but is the only firewall-faithful kind option for a precision-measurement of a dimensionless observable (a_μ); kind=sigma_deviation would have been the alternative but would have required choosing whether to encode the residual deviation against TI 2020 (4.2σ) or against BMW 2021 (1.5σ) on the carrier-sensitivity side, conflating the experimental sensitivity with the prediction-comparison outcome — encoding kind=dimensionless preserves the experimental-sensitivity-vs-prediction-comparison separation the resolves-edge structure requires.

Resolves-edge sensitivity-kind coverage after sub-PR 53: 7 of 8 kinds in use (time=6, sigma_deviation=6, length=6, coupling=5, dimensionless=3 [up from 2], mass=1, [unused: energy_scale, ratio]). The unused kinds (energy_scale, ratio) remain natural homes for fcc and cmb-s4 in later sub-PRs.

Incidental v19 Rule 34 surfacing (uncertainty / bound_direction mutual exclusion per v19 spec §5.1): the initial sub-PR 53 draft set both uncertainty (symmetric ±4.3 × 10⁻¹⁰ on TI 2020; symmetric ±5.5 × 10⁻¹⁰ on BMW 2021) and bound_direction=two-sided on the two PPP predicted_values; the v19 validator's Rule 34 (added during the v18 → v19 schema bump that introduced the bound_direction surface, hard-error) flagged both entries on the uncertainty-XOR-bound_direction invariant. Fix: PPP predicted_values use the uncertainty channel exclusively (uncertainty populated, bound_direction omitted) because they record central-value-with-symmetric-uncertainty SM predictions; the sensitivity field uses the bound_direction channel exclusively (uncertainty=null, bound_direction=two-sided) because it records absolute design-completion precision around the experimentally-measured central value without an associated theoretical uncertainty. This is the structural distinction Rule 34 encodes: central-value-with-uncertainty and one/two-sided-bound-without-uncertainty are mutually exclusive sensitivity encodings, and PPP predicted_values + measurement sensitivity exemplify both regimes on the same edge.
changelog.methodological_observations_recorded:
PHYSICS-DOMAIN-OPENING EIGHTH INSTANCE: sub-PR 53 opens the muon-anomalous-magnetic-moment subdomain in Step 4.4 — the seventh distinct subdomain after hep-ph (proton decay; sub-PRs 46–48), neutrino-sector (oscillations + mass ordering; sub-PRs 46–48), gravitational-wave (compact-binary mergers + multiband; sub-PR 49), cosmology (dark energy + Hubble tension + BAO; sub-PR 50), axion-dark-matter (haloscope coupling; sub-PR 51), and EDM (sub-PR 52). Cumulative Step 4.4 physics-subdomain coverage now: hep-ph + neutrino + GW + cosmology + axion-DM + EDM + muon-anomaly, SEVEN major subdomains. The Track 4 review pathway's prospective reach now spans the seven subdomains with substantive Step 4.4 content; muon-g-2 has its own theoretical and experimental communities (Theory Initiative + BMW + RBC-UKQCD + ETMC + Mainz lattice groups on the SM-prediction side; Fermilab E989 + J-PARC E34 + BNL E821 alumni on the experimental side) with substantive engagement potential.

PROJECT'S FIRST exclusion_only=false RESOLVES EDGE: a structural milestone for Phase C. All 26 prior resolves edges (24 cell-target + 2 frontier-target where exclusion_only was set explicitly in sub-PRs 51–52, with 18 set to true and 8 legacy edges with exclusion_only=None which predate the explicit-field convention) have been exclusion_only=true (or its legacy-None equivalent). The muon-g-2 case establishes the precedent for SM-side competing-prediction edges where §4(2) admissibility passes for the prediction-supplying programs while failing for BSM-side parameter-space accommodations; the result is exclusion_only=false at the edge level with PPP populated for the admissible programs and the BSM-side exclusion local to PPP rather than global to the edge. Future analogous candidates (precision-EW measurements at FCC-ee discriminating among competing SM-prediction frameworks; high-precision CKM-matrix measurements discriminating SM-side global-fit predictions; potentially future τ-lepton g-2 measurements if they become part of muon-g-2-continuation's downstream scope or get their own experimental-program node) inherit this precedent.

PROJECT'S FIRST RESOLVES EDGE TARGETING A TOTALITY-APPROACH CARRIER: the 4 frontier-target edges in sub-PRs 50–52 all targeted open-frontier nodes (cc-frontier, strong-cp-problem, matter-antimatter-asymmetry); muon-g-2 is the project's first totality-approach-typed resolves-edge target. Structurally the difference is that totality-approach carriers contain RESOLVED empirical content with open hosting (a_μ IS measured, the open question is which architecture hosts the apparent tension or its resolution) whereas open-frontier carriers contain UNRESOLVED open questions (strong-CP IS an open question whose resolution is what candidate-targeting programs supply). Resolves edges on totality-approach carriers therefore record experimental sensitivity to the RESOLVED observable's value to the precision required to discriminate among competing explanatory-architecture predictions, rather than experimental sensitivity to the signal characterizing an unresolved frontier. The pattern is structurally distinct and the precedent set by sub-PR 53 enables future totality-approach-targeted edges (bh-thermodynamics potentially if a future experimental program reaches sensitivity to BH information-recovery signatures; turbulence potentially if a future experimental program reaches sensitivity to characteristic scales of fluid-architecture predictions; chpt potentially via lattice-chiral-perturbation-theory convergence tests — each of these is speculative-only at present and would itself need §4 admissibility review per the methodology firewall).

POST-2021 LITERATURE DRIFT FINDING — RECOMMENDED FOR FOLLOW-UP SUB-PR: during sub-PR 53 the post-handoff literature search surfaced two substantial updates that the dataset's representation of the muon-g-2 carrier does NOT yet incorporate. (a) Aliberti et al. 2025 Phys Rep 1143:1 (the Theory Initiative 2025 update) adopts the consolidated lattice-QCD HVP average and shifts the TI SM prediction upward into approximate agreement with the BMW lattice value at the current level of precision, effectively reconciling the 2020 4.2σ tension at the current empirical level. (b) Boccaletti et al. 2024 Nature (BMW hybrid) provides a refined LO-HVP value a_μ^LO-HVP = 715.1(2.5)(2.3)[3.4] × 10⁻¹⁰ with uncertainty reduced by a factor of 1.6 from the original BMW 2021 figure. The muon-g-2 totality-approach carrier's currently-recorded quantitative_scale (kind=sigma_deviation, value=4.2, citations to Abi 2021 + Aoyama 2020 + Borsanyi 2021) is frozen in the 2021 picture and lags current consensus; its if_real_implies.muon-g2-bsm-loop entry's antecedent ('the muon g-2 anomaly survives lattice-HVP / dispersive reconciliation as a residual deviation indicating new-physics loop contributions to a_μ') has become substantially weaker as a literature position post-Aliberti 2025. Refreshing the carrier's quantitative_scale (perhaps to value=1.5 against current consensus, perhaps to a more structured two-value entry recording both the historical and current pictures), refreshing if_real_implies (adding a 'muon-g2-bsm-loop-disfavored' resolution entry citing Aliberti 2025), and updating the source node muon-g-2-continuation's description to include Aguillard 2025 PRL 135:101802 in addition to the currently-cited Aguillard 2023 PRL 131:161802, are all RECOMMENDED for a FOLLOW-UP CARRIER-REFRESH SUB-PR scoped separately from sub-PR 53's methodological-test purity. The scope-discipline justification for not bundling these into sub-PR 53: conflating content-refresh with the methodological-test precedent weakens the precedent value of the latter; the two refresh decisions (§4 admissibility check on Aliberti 2025 and Boccaletti 2024 as new literature anchors; §2.5 self-check on whether the carrier-state update reflects pattern-finding-back-influence or independently-justified content update) deserve their own changelog entries and §4 review. Recommendation: sub-PR 53.5 or sub-PR 54 (whichever the maintainer prefers, depending on whether sub-PR 54 takes one of the remaining unattached forward-looking experimental-program leads or pauses for this carrier refresh first).
changelog.skip_decisions:
Three potential additional edges were considered and skipped in sub-PR 53 to keep the diff-shape at the smallest end of the Step 4.4 range and to preserve the methodological-test purity.

(a) edge-rs-muon-g-2-continuation-koide-formula-or-related-cell (→ a cell on a flavor-physics-phenomenology FC): SKIP. No FC in v89 currently has a cell whose content is specifically 'lepton g-2 as a discriminating observable for flavor-physics SM/BSM frameworks' or 'a_μ-loop contributions to flavor-puzzle resolutions'. The flavor-puzzle frontier IS a separate carrier whose own candidate-targeting programs (Froggatt-Nielsen, extra-dimensional flavor, anarchy/Yukawa-textures) do not supply discriminable predictions for a_μ itself — they're carriers for different observables. A cross-frontier edge would force structural-fit assumptions the literature does not directly anchor. The narrower discipline is the single muon-g-2-to-muon-g-2 edge documented in this sub-PR; flavor-physics cross-cuts can be considered in future BSM-phenomenology-FC authoring passes if and when the cell-level structure to support such edges is built up.

(b) edge-rs-muon-g-2-continuation-hierarchy-problem (→ hierarchy-problem open-frontier): SKIP. Some BSM-loop frameworks (SUSY at TeV-scale, composite Higgs, leptoquarks) that would explain a residual Δa_μ are the same frameworks targeting the hierarchy-problem frontier (SUSY, composite Higgs are explicit candidate_targeting entries on hierarchy-problem). A muon-g-2-continuation → hierarchy-problem resolves edge would record an indirect discriminating relationship via SUSY-or-composite-Higgs prediction Δa_μ. But: (i) the §4(2) admissibility test on this edge would fail on the BSM-side (model-parameter freedom, same as the BSM-loop exclusion on the muon-g-2 edge), so PPP would be empty and exclusion_only=true; (ii) the cross-frontier edge would record a structural relationship that already EXISTS in the carrier graph via SUSY/composite-Higgs candidate_targeting on hierarchy-problem AND via the muon-g-2 if_real_implies.muon-g2-bsm-loop entry whose implication targets a new FC organizing BSM contributors. Adding a cross-frontier resolves edge would be load-bearing only if the experimental-program's discrimination among hierarchy-problem candidate programs depended on the a_μ measurement in a way the literature anchors as a primary tool, which it does not (a_μ is auxiliary to direct collider searches for SUSY/composite Higgs, not primary). Deferred; not expected to be revisited at the muon-g-2-continuation level.

(c) edge-rs-muon-g-2-continuation-flavor-puzzle (→ flavor-puzzle open-frontier): SKIP. Similar to (b) but weaker still: lepton-flavor-universality-violating loop contributions to Δa_μ are a subset of the BSM-loop framework, and the flavor-puzzle candidate-targeting programs (Froggatt-Nielsen, anarchy, extra-dimensional) do not predict Δa_μ at a level the experimental program discriminates. Deferred indefinitely; not expected to be authorable at the muon-g-2-continuation level.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 54 — Phase C STEP 4.4 continues. FIFTEENTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; NINTH Step-4.4 sub-PR. THREE (3) resolves edges authored from cmb-s4 OPENING the CMB / inflation physics subdomain as the EIGHTH Step-4.4 physics subdomain after hep-ph, neutrino-sector, gravitational-wave, cosmology (DESI distance-ladder), axion-dark-matter, EDM, and muon-anomaly. All three edges target cells in cosmological-observations: cell-cosm-primordial-GW-r-bound (tensor-to-scalar ratio r ~ 10⁻³ design floor), cell-cosm-primordial-non-Gaussianity-fNL (σ(f_NL^local) ~ 1 design floor), and cell-cosm-Sigma-mass-nu (σ(Σm_ν) ~ 30 meV design floor). All three edges land exclusion_only=true with PPP empty under §4(2) — returns to the standard pre-sub-PR-53 pattern after the muon-g-2 exclusion_only=false precedent (which remains reserved for the rare totality-approach + competing-SM-side-predictions structural pattern). ONE methodological FIRST: the project's FIRST kind=ratio resolves-edge sensitivity (Edge 1, tensor-to-scalar r as a literal ratio P_T/P_S of primordial tensor to scalar power spectra), closing resolves-edge sensitivity-kind coverage from 6/8 to 7/8 of the dimensional and ratio-flavored kinds in the v18 enum. Energy_scale remains the only kind absent from the resolves-edge surface — cmb-s4 does NOT directly measure the inflation energy scale V_inf (that is the derived Lyth-bound quantity); the handoff's 'both unused kinds get exercised' framing was over-generous, only ratio gets exercised. NO T-mint — T30 (discovery-floor sensitivity with bound_direction=upper) covers all three edges cleanly. FOURTH CONSECUTIVE SUB-PR WITH NO HANDOFF-INVENTORY DRIFT. TWO SKIP-decisions documented: ΔN_eff and DM-baryon scattering, both with follow-up cell-authoring recommendations to the maintainer (no dedicated cell exists for either observable in v90; authoring resolves edges to nonexistent targets would fail Rule 32 target-id resolution). ONE methodological observation recorded: cell-level/edge-level kind divergence for tensor-to-scalar r (cell uses kind=dimensionless from Step 4.3 legacy; edge uses kind=ratio per structural-semantic correctness and the kind-coverage closure argument) — flagged for any future cell-refresh sub-PR that may unify the encoding. The sub-PR 53 carrier-refresh recommendation (muon-g-2 carrier post-2021 literature drift) remains queued as the natural sub-PR 55 lead unless redirected.
changelog.lead_choice_rationale:
Lead (cmb-s4) from the sub-PR 54 maintainer-confirmed plan. Inspection of v90 confirmed the handoff inventory holds exactly: 19 experimental-program nodes, 16 with outgoing resolves edges, 3 unattached forward-looking nodes (cmb-s4, rubin-lsst, fcc) — exactly the inventory the handoff specified. NO handoff-inventory drift this round — the FOURTH CONSECUTIVE sub-PR (after sub-PRs 51, 52, 53) where the pre-supplied handoff inventory matched live data exactly, validating the per-sub-PR live-verification discipline established in v87.

Five structural reasons for choosing cmb-s4 at sub-PR 54, recorded with confirmed dispositions:

(1) CLOSES RESOLVES-EDGE SENSITIVITY-KIND COVERAGE 6/8 → 7/8 — Edge 1 (tensor-to-scalar r) uses kind=ratio for the first time on the resolves-edge surface. This is the load-bearing structural reason; no other lead in the unattached inventory promises this. The 9 existing kind=ratio entries in v90 confirm the convention is used for genuine ratios of physical quantities (cosmological-constant 10¹²⁰ hierarchy, EW/Planck 10¹⁷, Yukawa 10⁶, η_B = 6.1×10⁻¹⁰, BBN abundance ratios D/H and ⁷Li/H, Laughlin e/3); r = P_T/P_S fits this pattern cleanly. The handoff's parallel claim that kind=energy_scale would also be exercised by a Lyth-bound inflation-energy-scale edge proved over-generous on closer inspection: cmb-s4 directly measures r, and V_inf^(1/4) ~ 10¹⁶ GeV × (r/0.1)^(1/4) is a derived quantity rather than what the edge's sensitivity field records. Only ratio gets exercised; coverage closes to 7/8 and energy_scale remains the single unused resolves-edge sensitivity kind after this sub-PR.

(2) OPENS THE EIGHTH STEP-4.4 PHYSICS SUBDOMAIN (CMB / inflation) — cumulative Step 4.4 physics-subdomain coverage reaches eight after this sub-PR (hep-ph + neutrino + GW + cosmology + axion-DM + EDM + muon-anomaly + CMB/inflation). The Track 4 review-pathway argument is materially strengthened by reaching eight subdomains: the inflation-theory community (Maldacena, Baumann, Senatore, Lyth, Wands, …) and the CMB-experimental community (Simons Observatory, CMB-S4, LiteBIRD collaborations) are large and active communities naturally engaged by the project's structural framing of inflation observables.

(3) NO T-MINT EXPECTED — RETURNS TO T30 PRECEDENT — confirmed on authoring: all three sensitivities are discovery-floor sensitivities with bound_direction=upper, covered cleanly by T30 (the convention used by hyper-k/dune for proton-decay lifetimes, axion-haloscope for g_aγγ, edm-program for |d_n| and |d_e|). T33 (precision-measurement, two-sided, minted in sub-PR 53) does not apply here — cmb-s4 sets discovery floors rather than pinning measured values. Sub-PR 54 returns to the standard exclusion-only / discovery-floor pattern after sub-PR 53's totality-approach + competing-SM-predictions precedent.

(4) ALL EDGES exclusion_only=true — RETURNS TO PRE-SUB-PR-53 PATTERN — confirmed on §4 admissibility runs: each candidate-target program at each of the three cells supplies parameter-class predictions rather than discriminable point predictions at the experimental floor. Edge 1's inflation-potential classes (Starobinsky R², hilltop, Higgs-inflation) are parameter families; Edge 2's multi-field non-Gaussianity-generating models (curvaton, modulated reheating) are parameter families AND single-field's Maldacena point prediction sits below the floor rather than at it; Edge 3's NH/IH hierarchy bounds are SM-neutrino-sector parameter constraints rather than candidate-foundational program predictions on a cell that hosts no candidate_targeting array. The structural-pattern conclusion: §4(2) fails for all three edges in three structurally distinct ways (parameter-freedom; point-prediction-below-floor; locus-of-candidate-targeting-not-on-cell). All three land exclusion_only=true with PPP empty. The exclusion_only=false precedent from sub-PR 53 (muon-g-2 with competing SM-side dispersive/lattice HVP calculations) remains reserved for that rare structural pattern.

(5) CANDIDATE-TARGET CELLS ALREADY EXIST — confirmed on live inspection of cosmological-observations: cell-cosm-primordial-GW-r-bound, cell-cosm-primordial-non-Gaussianity-fNL, and cell-cosm-Sigma-mass-nu all exist in v90 with realized-examples and quantitative_scale populated. No new cell authoring required.

Reasons (1) and (4) outweigh (2)/(3)/(5) in the lead-selection ordering: (1) is the only structural reason with single-sub-PR uniqueness (no other lead can close kind=ratio coverage), and (4) preserves methodological-test purity of the sub-PR 53 exclusion_only=false precedent by returning to the standard pattern rather than rapid-firing additional exclusion_only=false edges on potentially weaker §4(2) groundings.
changelog.edge_choice_rationale:
Three edges chosen (1, 2, 3) with two skip-decisions documented (Edges 4 ΔN_eff and 5 DM-baryon scattering). The maintainer deferred all four decision points to project-best-interest and literature-faithfulness; the decisions are recorded with rationale below.

EDGE 1 (cmb-s4 → cell-cosm-primordial-GW-r-bound): kind=ratio per the structural-semantic correctness of r = P_T/P_S as a literal ratio of physical quantities and per the kind-coverage closure argument (reason 1 above). value=0.001 in linear encoding (log10 absent) matches the small-finite-value precedent among the 9 existing kind=ratio entries (η_B = 6.1×10⁻¹⁰, D/H = 2.527×10⁻⁵, Laughlin 1/3) rather than the log10-encoded large-hierarchy precedent (cosmological-constant value=120 log10=true, EW/Planck value=17 log10=true). uncertainty=null, bound_direction=upper, citations = Abazajian 2016 §2 + Hazumi 2022 LiteBIRD + Abazajian 2022 Snowmass. T30. exclusion_only=true. Predictions_per_program empty per §4(2) — all inflation-potential candidate classes (m²φ² already excluded, Starobinsky R² predicting r ~ 0.003–0.005, hilltop and Higgs-inflation predicting r ≲ 10⁻³) are parameter families rather than discriminable point predictions at the floor.

EDGE 2 (cmb-s4 → cell-cosm-primordial-non-Gaussianity-fNL): kind=dimensionless (f_NL^local is a bispectrum amplitude parameter, not a ratio of physical quantities — matches the cell-level encoding cleanly). value=1.0, uncertainty=null, bound_direction=upper, citations = Abazajian 2016 §3 + Abazajian 2022 Snowmass. T30. exclusion_only=true. Predictions_per_program empty per §4(2) — Maldacena 2003 single-field consistency relation gives f_NL^local ≈ (5/12)(1−n_s) ≈ 0.02 as a CLEAN point prediction but it sits BELOW the cmb-s4 σ(f_NL^local) ~ 1 floor (cmb-s4 hitting its floor does not discriminate single-field — it merely sets tighter non-detection bounds). Multi-field model classes (curvaton Lyth-Wands 2002, modulated reheating, Komatsu 2010 review) predict f_NL^local ~ O(1)–O(10) in parameter classes, failing §4(2) on parameter-freedom in the same structural pattern as axion-haloscope (sub-PR 51) and EDM (sub-PR 52).

EDGE 3 (cmb-s4 → cell-cosm-Sigma-mass-nu): kind=mass, value=0.03, units=eV (Rule 31 satisfied — units required for kind=mass), uncertainty=null, bound_direction=upper, citations = Abazajian 2016 §4 + Ade 2019 Simons Observatory §3. T30. exclusion_only=true. Target=cell-cosm-Sigma-mass-nu (cosmology-native) per the gw-ground-network/DESI precedent of source-program's native-FC cell rather than cross-FC mirror — cmb-s4 is a cosmology program, and the cell already carries an explicit OVERLAP marker to cell-nu-Sum-mass-Planck without need for a parallel resolves edge. Predictions_per_program empty per §4(2) on STRUCTURAL-DISCIPLINE grounds — the NH lower bound Σm_ν > 0.059 eV and IH lower bound Σm_ν > 0.099 eV (Esteban-Gonzalez-Garcia et al. 2020 NuFIT 5.2; Capozzi-Lisi-Marrone-Palazzo 2021) are SM-neutrino-sector parameter constraints rather than candidate-foundational program predictions. The cosmology cell does not carry candidate_targeting (cells don't; only frontiers and totality-approaches do, per v17 schema). The locus where NH/IH discrimination operates as a per-program prediction is on the neutrino-sector-phenomenology FC cells, not this cosmology cell — cmb-s4 discriminates hierarchies observationally without discriminating candidate-foundational programs at this locus.

Timeline for all three edges = 'running' per the operational-state semantics used by gw-ground-network, axion-haloscope-network, and desi: the Simons Observatory constituent is taking data (first light 2024 per the cmb-s4 source-node operational_period.start_year=2024 ongoing=true), and the combined program publishes first results while the design-completion sensitivity at r ~ 10⁻³ awaits CMB-S4 ground-based late-2020s + LiteBIRD ~2032 launch. The 'running' timeline captures program operational state rather than design-floor achievement, matching the axion-haloscope-network and gw-ground-network precedents where partial deployment + ongoing operations + later design-completion runs all collapse to timeline=running.

SKIP-decisions documented:

EDGE 4 (ΔN_eff) SKIPPED: the cmb-s4 source-node names σ(N_eff) ~ 0.03 as a primary sensitivity, but live inspection of v90 confirmed no dedicated N_eff cell exists in cosmological-observations (the 18 cells include CMB acoustic peaks, BAO, H_0, σ_8, BBN, primordial GW, f_NL, τ_reio, Ω_K, n_s, Σm_ν, Ω_DM, JWST high-z, and EDGES 21cm — but no dedicated N_eff cell as of v90). Authoring a resolves edge to a nonexistent target would fail validator Rule 32 (target-id resolution). FOLLOW-UP RECOMMENDATION: a future cosmological-observations content-authoring sub-PR should add cell-cosm-Neff with the Planck 2018 measurement N_eff = 2.99 ± 0.17 and the BBN concordance value as observational anchors; at that point a follow-up cmb-s4 → cell-cosm-Neff resolves edge can be authored with kind=dimensionless, design-completion σ(N_eff) ~ 0.03 from Abazajian 2016 §3 as sensitivity.

EDGE 5 (DM-baryon scattering) SKIPPED: the cmb-s4 source-node names DM-baryon scattering as a sensitivity target, but live inspection of dark-matter-candidates confirmed no dedicated DM-baryon-scattering cell exists in v90 (the 18 dm cells include WIMP, QCD-axion, ALP-haloscope, sterile-nu kev/eV, PBH asteroid-mass and LIGO-mass excluded, fuzzy ultralight, self-interacting, asymmetric, mirror, dark-photon-portal, gravitino-LSP, MACHO/DAMA/CDMS falsified, string-axiverse predicted, PBH supermassive formation — none dedicated to DM-baryon scattering cross-section as a primary observational status). firewall_self_check: EDGE 1 (cmb-s4 → cell-cosm-primordial-GW-r-bound). §4 part (1) sensitivity-is-literature-value: PASS — Abazajian et al. 2016 'CMB-S4 Science Book' arXiv:1610.02743 §2 and Hazumi et al. 2022 LiteBIRD PTEP 2023:042F01 §2 both name r ~ 10⁻³ as the design-completion floor at 95% CL. §4 part (2) per-program-predictions-are-program-internal: NOT APPLICABLE — predictions_per_program is empty (exclusion_only=true) because every inflation-potential candidate class fails §4(2) on parameter-freedom: m²φ² already excluded, Starobinsky R² predicts r ~ 0.003–0.005 as a parameter-family band, hilltop and Higgs-inflation classes predict r ≲ 10⁻³ as further parameter families. None is a discriminable point prediction at the floor in the §4(2) sense (Lyth-Riotto 1999 Phys Rep 314:1 §2.4; Baumann 2009 'TASI Lectures on Inflation' arXiv:0907.5424 §2 cover the standard inflation-potential parameter-class taxonomy). §4 part (3) sensitivities-cite-the-experimental-design: PASS — the citation array names the design publications Abazajian 2016 CMB-S4 Science Book, Hazumi 2022 LiteBIRD PTEP, and Abazajian 2022 Snowmass white paper from the source-node key_publications field. §2.5 self-check: PASS — the structural fact that cmb-s4 reaching r ~ 10⁻³ would rule out the large-field and Starobinsky-adjacent inflation classes (or confirm one of them at detection) is independent of any pattern observation in the v90 dataset; the same edge would be authored from the inflation-theory literature alone.

EDGE 2 (cmb-s4 → cell-cosm-primordial-non-Gaussianity-fNL). §4 part (1) sensitivity-is-literature-value: PASS — Abazajian et al. 2016 §3 names σ(f_NL^local) ~ 1 as the design-completion floor at 68% CL via combined CMB temperature + polarization + LSS cross-correlation. §4 part (2) per-program-predictions: NOT APPLICABLE — predictions_per_program is empty (exclusion_only=true). Maldacena 2003 (JHEP 05:013) gives the single-field slow-roll consistency relation f_NL^local = (5/12)(1−n_s) ≈ 0.02 as a CLEAN point prediction, but the prediction sits BELOW the cmb-s4 floor of σ(f_NL^local) ~ 1: cmb-s4 reaching its floor does not discriminate single-field at floor; it merely sets tighter non-detection bounds. The structural pattern is the same as axion-haloscope reaching the DFSZ band without discriminating DFSZ vs KSVZ as point predictions (sub-PR 51 precedent). Multi-field model classes (curvaton Lyth-Wands 2002 Phys Lett B 524:5; modulated reheating Dvali-Gruzinov-Zaldarriaga 2004 PRD 69:023505; Komatsu 2010 Class Quant Grav 27:124010 review) predict f_NL^local ~ O(1)–O(10) in parameter classes — fail §4(2) on parameter-freedom. §4 part (3) design-citation: PASS — Abazajian 2016 + Abazajian 2022 Snowmass. §2.5 self-check: PASS — the structural fact that cmb-s4 reaching σ(f_NL^local) ~ 1 would rule out multi-field model classes is derivable from the inflation-theory literature without reference to v90 dataset structure.

EDGE 3 (cmb-s4 → cell-cosm-Sigma-mass-nu). §4 part (1) sensitivity-is-literature-value: PASS — Abazajian et al. 2016 §4 names σ(Σm_ν) ~ 0.03 eV (30 meV) as the design-completion floor via gravitational-lensing reconstruction; Ade et al. 2019 Simons Observatory JCAP 02:056 §3 names σ(Σm_ν) ~ 0.04 eV for SO alone, with the combined CMB-S4 + LiteBIRD reaching ~0.03 eV via LiteBIRD's large-angle τ_reio measurement breaking parameter degeneracies. §4 part (2) per-program-predictions: NOT APPLICABLE on STRUCTURAL-DISCIPLINE grounds — predictions_per_program is empty (exclusion_only=true). The NH lower bound Σm_ν > 0.059 eV and IH lower bound Σm_ν > 0.099 eV (Esteban-Gonzalez-Garcia-Maltoni-Schwetz-Zhou 2020 JHEP 09:178 NuFIT 5.2; Capozzi-Lisi-Marrone-Palazzo 2021) are SM-neutrino-sector parameter constraints from oscillation data, not candidate-foundational program predictions in the §4(2) sense. The cell does not carry candidate_targeting (cells don't, only frontiers and totality-approaches do per v17 schema); the locus where NH/IH discrimination operates as a per-program prediction is on the neutrino-sector-phenomenology FC cells, not this cosmology cell. §4 part (3) design-citation: PASS — Abazajian 2016 + Ade 2019 SO. §2.5 self-check: PASS — the structural fact that cmb-s4 reaching σ(Σm_ν) ~ 30 meV would discriminate NH from IH at ~3σ is derivable from the CMB-lensing literature alone.
changelog.T_convention_minted: None. T30 (discovery-floor sensitivity with bound_direction=upper) covers all three sub-PR 54 edges cleanly: Edge 1 r < 10⁻³ upper bound on primordial-tensor-to-scalar ratio, Edge 2 f_NL^local < 1 upper bound on primordial non-Gaussianity amplitude, Edge 3 Σm_ν < 30 meV upper bound on cosmological sum-of-neutrino-masses — all are discovery-floor sensitivities with bound_direction=upper across three distinct sensitivity-kind types (ratio, dimensionless, mass), confirming T30's coverage breadth across the kind space. T33 (precision-measurement sensitivity with bound_direction=two-sided, sub-PR 53 mint) does not apply — cmb-s4 sets discovery floors at the experimental threshold rather than pinning measured values to a two-sided precision range. The T30/T33 distinction continues to discriminate the two semantically separate sensitivity categories cleanly.
changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition:
Handoff sub-PR 54 plan (pre-confirmed cmb-s4 lead with 3 edges + 2 skips, all exclusion_only=true, T30, kind=ratio for Edge 1 to close 6/8 → 7/8) accepted in full after maintainer deferred all four decision points to project-best-interest and literature-faithfulness. The handoff's parallel claim that kind=energy_scale would also be exercised by cmb-s4 (closing two unused kinds in one sub-PR) corrected on inspection: cmb-s4 measures r directly, not V_inf; the Lyth-bound inflation-energy-scale is a derived quantity rather than what the edge's sensitivity field records. Only kind=ratio gets exercised; coverage closes to 7/8 and energy_scale remains the single unused resolves-edge sensitivity kind. This correction is recorded in summary and lead_choice_rationale.

Sub-PR 55 lead disposition: the sub-PR 53 carrier-refresh recommendation (muon-g-2 carrier post-2021 literature drift — Aliberti et al. 2025 Phys Rep 1143:1 TI 2025 update; Boccaletti et al. 2024 Nature BMW hybrid; Aguillard et al. 2025 PRL 135:101802 Fermilab E989 final Run-1–6) remains queued as the natural sub-PR 55 lead unless the maintainer redirects. The muon-g-2 carrier's quantitative_scale (sigma_deviation 4.2σ) and if_real_implies.muon-g2-bsm-loop entry are frozen in the 2021 picture; refreshing the carrier's representation against the post-2021 literature is a separate-from-resolves-edges authoring decision that this sub-PR (54) did not absorb to preserve methodological-test purity of the cmb-s4 lead. Sub-PR 56 then picks between rubin-lsst (smaller-shape, transient-survey + dark-matter-substructure subdomains) and fcc (highest-complexity, absorbs precedent from all prior Step 4.4 sub-PRs and best done last). The handoff's sub-PR-ordering recommendation remains valid.

HANDOFF-ARITHMETIC CORRECTION recorded: the handoff text stated resolves-edge sensitivity-kind coverage stood at '7 of 8' pre-sub-PR-54 with ratio and energy_scale unused, but the handoff's own breakdown listed 6 used kinds (time=6, sigma_deviation=6, length=6, coupling=5, dimensionless=3, mass=1) — leaving 2 unused kinds and 6/8 coverage, not 7/8. Live verification confirmed 6/8 pre-sub-PR-54. Sub-PR 54 closes one of the two unused kinds (ratio) via Edge 1, moving coverage from 6/8 to 7/8. Energy_scale remains the single unused resolves-edge sensitivity kind after this sub-PR (cmb-s4 measures r directly rather than the derived Lyth-bound inflation energy scale V_inf, so kind=energy_scale was never on the table for sub-PR 54). The 7/8 → 8/8 framing carried in this changelog's earlier draft was corrected to 6/8 → 7/8 before commit. This is the FIRST handoff-arithmetic discrepancy recorded in the post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc (sub-PRs 39+); no other arithmetic discrepancies have surfaced. The live-verification discipline established in v87 caught it cleanly.
changelog.schema_candidates_status_observations: No schema candidates surfaced by sub-PR 54 authoring. The v18 schema + v19 bound_direction retrofit covers all three edges' structural needs without friction: kind=ratio (Edge 1) and kind=dimensionless (Edge 2) correctly forbid the units field per Rule 31 / §5.3; kind=mass (Edge 3) correctly requires the units field. All three edges set uncertainty=null + bound_direction=upper, satisfying Rule 34 (mutual exclusion of non-null uncertainty and bound_direction) and not triggering Rule 35 warnings. The kind-coverage gap on the resolves-edge surface (energy_scale remaining unused after this sub-PR closes ratio) is an authoring-progress observation, not a schema gap — the v18 enum supports all eight kinds; the resolves-edge surface has just not yet encountered a sensitivity-as-energy-scale case. Future leads that may exercise energy_scale: fcc (direct collision energy at √s = 100 TeV; though more naturally encoded as a discovery-mass-reach in TeV than as the collision energy itself — encoding will be a sub-PR-internal decision).
changelog.methodological_observations_recorded: ONE methodological observation. The cell-level entry for cell-cosm-primordial-GW-r-bound (Step 4.3 authoring, data version v42) uses quantitative_scale.kind=dimensionless with value=0.036 — the BICEP/Keck 2021 r upper bound encoded as a dimensionless number. The edge-level sub-PR 54 sensitivity for the same observable (the cmb-s4 design floor at r ~ 10⁻³) uses kind=ratio with value=0.001, on the structural-semantic grounds that r = P_T/P_S is literally a ratio of two power spectra at the pivot scale k = 0.05 Mpc⁻¹ and on the kind-coverage closure argument (closes resolves-edge sensitivity-kind coverage 6/8 → 7/8). The two kinds at the two surfaces are methodologically defensible — edge records experimental sensitivity (forward-looking design floor), cell records observational status (current measurement bound); they are separate quantitative-scale instances on separate carrier surfaces and may use different kinds when both are structurally appropriate at their own surface. But the divergence IS observable: a future Map-of-Physics-querying AI asking 'show me all kind=ratio entries' would find Edge 1 and miss the cell; asking 'show me all kind=dimensionless' would find the cell and miss Edge 1. RECOMMENDATION (deferred to a future cell-refresh sub-PR, not in scope for sub-PR 54): a separate cell-level refresh pass over Step-4.3-era cells could unify encoding by promoting the cell-cosm-primordial-GW-r-bound qs.kind from dimensionless to ratio (with the same value=0.036, units still forbidden — the encoding shift is purely the kind discriminator). The same review could consider whether other Step-4.3 cells in cosmological-observations and elsewhere use dimensionless where ratio would be more structurally accurate (e.g., σ_8 ratios, BBN abundance ratios already encoded as ratio on the predictive_yield side but as dimensionless on the cell-level side in some entries). Recording the observation here makes the cell-edge kind divergence queryable for the maintainer when scheduling cell-refresh work.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 55 — Phase C CARRIER REFRESH against post-2021 literature consensus on the muon anomalous magnetic moment. FIRST instance of the carrier-refresh sub-PR shape as a first-class Phase C deliverable. Five fields refreshed on TWO nodes (muon-g-2 totality-approach carrier: quantitative_scale value 4.2σ → 0.6σ against Aliberti 2025 WP25 × Aguillard 2025 final Run-1–6 + Boccaletti 2024 BMW-hybrid, qs citations replaced; if_real_implies entry [0] condition text and condition_citations refreshed adding Aliberti 2025 + Aguillard 2025 (kept Abi 2021 + Aoyama 2020 as historical anchors); description sentence-level addition recording post-2025 reconciliation; muon-g-2-continuation experimental-program source node: description phrase 'final combined Run-1–6 analysis projected' → 'final combined Run-1–6 published 2025 at 127 ppb design-completion precision', citations array gained Aguillard 2025). No new nodes, edges, T-conventions, or schema candidates. Sub-PR 53 resolves edge edge-rs-muon-g-2-continuation-muon-g-2 NOT touched by design (its description-field self-documents the rationale: the edge records the 2021-era TI-vs-BMW experimental design landscape; the carrier reflects current empirical status; the two can legitimately disagree on which historical picture they reflect because they record different structural facts). Establishes carrier-refresh as a first-class Phase C sub-PR shape; future post-publication-drift refreshes (BBN parameters, neutrino oscillation parameters, dark-matter freeze-out values, etc.) can inherit the precedent.
changelog.lead_choice_rationale: Lead (muon-g-2 carrier refresh) was pre-confirmed at the close of sub-PR 53 (v90 changelog methodological_observations_recorded) and reconfirmed in sub-PR 54 (v91) handoff. The need was identified in sub-PR 53's edge-description prose, which explicitly records that the carrier's quantitative_scale and if_real_implies entry are 'frozen in the 2021 picture and lag current consensus; refreshing the carrier's representation is a separate authoring decision outside this resolves-edge sub-PR's scope.' Three reasons (recorded for the project record): (1) POST-2025 LITERATURE DRIFT IS SUBSTANTIVE AND CITABLE. Aliberti et al. 2025 Phys Rep 1143:1 (TI WP25 update adopting lattice-HVP consensus, dispersive omitted due to CMD-3 vs earlier-data tension), Boccaletti et al. 2024 arXiv:2407.10913 (BMW hybrid LO-HVP at 0.48% precision, Nature 2026), and Aguillard et al. 2025 PRL 135:101802 (Fermilab E989 final Run-1–6 at 127 ppb) jointly shift the carrier's empirical picture from the 2021 4.2σ TI-WP20-dispersive vs Abi-FNAL-Run-1 tension to a ≈1σ WP25-lattice-consensus vs Aguillard-2025-final residual deviation. §4 admissibility test passes cleanly for all three anchor papers — each is a program-internal peer-reviewed calculation or measurement with the antecedent positions clearly stated. §2.5 self-check passes: independent of any pattern observation in v91, the same field updates are defensible from the post-2021 literature alone. (2) DOWNSTREAM-DEPENDENCY ON CARRIER FOR FUTURE FCC SUB-PR. When sub-PR 57+ reaches fcc (highest-complexity Step 4.4 lead), one of its anticipated resolves edges is fcc-ee → muon-g-2 via the high-precision muon-g-2 measurement program FCC-ee may host as a precision-physics auxiliary. Refreshing the carrier before fcc reaches it prevents inheritance of the 2021 picture into Step 4.4's most complex lead. (3) METHODOLOGICAL-TEST PRECEDENT. Sub-PR 55 establishes carrier-refresh as a first-class Phase C sub-PR shape distinct from the strictly additive Phase A → Phase B → Phase C trajectory: refreshes EXISTING quantitative_scale.value, citations, if_real_implies entries, source-node description, and (added beyond the handoff's original three target fields per the maintainer's accuracy/truth-to-literature directive) carrier description, while preserving structural fields (cell content, axis labels, edge structure). The firewall test still applies; the changelog structure mirrors the sub-PR 53/54 13-field format with edge_choice_rationale repurposed as field_refresh_rationale (recorded below).
changelog.edge_choice_rationale: FIELD REFRESH RATIONALE (this changelog field is repurposed from edge_choice_rationale because sub-PR 55 is a carrier-refresh sub-PR adding zero new edges and refreshing five existing fields instead). FIVE fields refreshed: (A) muon-g-2.quantitative_scale — sigma_deviation value 4.2 → 0.6, computed as Δa_μ / σ_combined = 37.5 / √(62² + 14.6²) ≈ 0.59σ rounded to 0.6 using WP25 SM prediction a_μ^SM = 116 592 033(62) × 10⁻¹¹ [Aliberti 2025] and Aguillard 2025 final-Run-1–6 measurement a_μ^exp = 116 592 070.5(14.6) × 10⁻¹¹. Citations replaced (Abi 2021 + Aoyama 2020 + Borsanyi 2021 → Aliberti 2025 + Aguillard 2025 + Boccaletti 2024); bound_direction 'upper' preserved unchanged (changing the sigma_deviation bound_direction convention is out of scope for a per-entry carrier-refresh and would require a methodology pass over all sigma_deviation entries to maintain consistency — flagged as a future v19-bound_direction-discipline candidate, not promoted here); uncertainty null preserved unchanged. (B) muon-g-2.if_real_implies[0] — OPTION (i) chosen over OPTION (ii). The (ii) path — adding a new resolution entry recording the SM-consistent branch — was rejected after schema inspection because the v17 if_real_implies implication kind enum is {new_cell, new_axis, forced_edge, promotes_subtype, new_FC} (see MAP_v17_schema_spec_extension.md §2) with NO 'no_structural_consequence' / 'dispatch_to_SM_side' / 'no_new_FC_forced' value. The mechanism is designed to record FORCED structural consequences, not resolution-branches-in-general; the SM-consistent branch (residual deviation is statistical scatter, no new FC forced, no new BSM cell-content) is not cleanly representable in v17 without a schema bump. (i) was the schema-faithful firewall-compatible refresh: condition text updated with a post-2025 qualifier preserving the original conditional structure (BSM-loop interpretation conditional on residual-deviation survival), condition_citations augmented from 2 entries (Abi 2021 + Aoyama 2020) to 4 entries (added Aliberti 2025 + Aguillard 2025 as references documenting the empirical disfavoring of the antecedent at ≈1σ post-2025 precision without closing it as a future-precision possibility), implications array structurally unchanged. The v17 schema's expressive limitation is flagged as a structural observation in schema_candidates_status_observations below; whether the v18+ Phase B+C arc should add an additional implication kind to represent SM-consistent / no-new-FC-forced resolutions is a separate methodology decision deferred to a future v20 schema-pass discussion. (C) muon-g-2.description — INCLUDED beyond the handoff's original three target fields per the maintainer's accuracy/truth-to-literature directive. The carrier's existing description names '~4.2σ discrepancy' in 2021-era framing; minimal post-2025 update preserves the historical 4.2σ reference (load-bearing for understanding the carrier's history) and appends a sentence-level addition recording the post-2025 reconciliation: WP25 lattice-HVP consensus + Aguillard 2025 final-Run-1–6 → ≈1σ residual deviation → persistent open question shifts from measurement-vs-SM tension to SM-internal HVP-methodology question. The empirical_status field is left unchanged at 'candidate-anomaly-pending-resolution' because the SM-internal HVP methodology question IS the pending resolution at post-2025 precision, and the J-PARC E34 systematics-independent cross-check + future g-2 continuations remain prospective; demoting to 'closed' would overstate the 2025-as-of-now consensus. (D) muon-g-2-continuation.description — phrase 'final combined Run-1–6 analysis projected' replaced with 'final combined Run-1–6 published 2025 at 127 ppb design-completion precision [Aguillard et al. 2025 PRL 135:101802, arXiv:2506.03069]'. Sentence-level mechanical refresh; surrounding prose preserved. (E) muon-g-2-continuation.citations — prepended Aguillard 2025 ahead of the existing Aguillard 2023 + Abe 2019 entries (3 total, most-recent-first ordering matching the qs citation pattern). Aguillard 2023 retained as legitimate intermediate-result citation. SUB-PR 53 EDGE NOT TOUCHED. edge-rs-muon-g-2-continuation-muon-g-2 (sub-PR 53 v90) is explicitly NOT modified by sub-PR 55 by design. The edge's existing description-field prose self-documents the rationale: 'this edge records the historical TI-vs-BMW discriminating landscape that the Fermilab E989 + J-PARC E34 experimental programs were designed to address — the structural fact captured by the resolves edge (the experimental program's precision is sufficient to distinguish the two competing SM-side calculations at design completion) is independent of which of the two calculations the post-2021 literature has subsequently consolidated around.' The methodological clean-separation is preserved: the EDGE records the experimental design rationale (frozen on the 2021 picture by design); the CARRIER records current empirical status (refreshed by this sub-PR). Both are legitimate structural facts about the same physical system at different abstraction levels.

firewall_self_check.part_1_value_is_literature_value: PASS. Three citation anchors: Aliberti 2025 Phys Rep 1143:1 SM prediction a_μ^SM = 116 592 033(62) × 10⁻¹¹; Aguillard 2025 PRL 135:101802 experimental measurement a_μ^exp = 116 592 070.5(14.6) × 10⁻¹¹; Boccaletti 2024 arXiv:2407.10913 hybrid LO-HVP at 0.48% precision. The 0.6σ refresh value is a derivation — Δa_μ / σ_combined = 37.5 / √(62² + 14.6²) ≈ 0.59σ — with each input value literature-stated. Boccaletti 2024 itself quotes 0.9σ against Aguillard 2023 in its abstract, validating the order-of-magnitude. APS Physics editorial summary at link.aps.org/doi/10.1103/Physics.18.150 corroborates 'agreement' qualitative claim.
firewall_self_check.part_2_consequent_is_structural_by_deriv: PASS. The consequent (sigma_deviation has reduced from 4.2 to 0.6) is an arithmetic combination of literature-stated SM prediction and experimental measurement uncertainties. No pattern observation in v91 enters; the calculation is the standard SM-vs-experiment sigma combination.
firewall_self_check.part_3_both_halves_cite: PASS. Three citation anchors, each carrying source-value-embedded prose documenting the contribution to the refresh value.
firewall_self_check.section_2_5_self_check: PASS. Would I refresh this field independent of any pattern observation in v91? Yes — if I had only the post-2021 muon-g-2 subfield literature in front of me (Aliberti 2025 + Aguillard 2025 + Boccaletti 2024), the 4.2 → ≈1 sigma_deviation update would be required by accuracy/truth-to-literature regardless of v91 dataset state. The pre-confirmed sub-PR 53 edge frozen on the 2021 picture by design does not alter this conclusion because the edge and carrier record different structural facts (design rationale vs current empirical status).
