changelog.T_convention_minted: None — not a sensitivity-adding sub-PR; T-register unchanged at 33 conventions through v92 (T1–T14, T17–T31, T33; T15 retired, T16 dropped). The carrier-refresh sub-PR shape uses existing v18+v19 surfaces (quantitative_scale, if_real_implies, node description, node citations) without forcing new conventions.
changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition: Handoff sub-PR 55 plan (pre-confirmed muon-g-2 carrier refresh with three target fields + sub-PR 53 edge NOT touched) accepted in full after maintainer's directive 'always do what is accurate and true to the literature and what is best for the goals of the project and utility of the final product' deferred the four explicit decision points to project-best-interest and literature-faithfulness. The author-side resolutions: DECISION 1 (qs value): handoff anticipated '≈1.5σ or below 2σ' figure; the live calculation against WP25 × Aguillard 2025 yields 0.6σ (the handoff's 1.5σ figure was a BMW-2021 × Abi-2021 historical pairing, not the post-2025 consensus pairing). Refresh value set to 0.6σ — more accurate to the literature than the handoff's anticipated figure. DECISION 2 (if_real_implies option): handoff offered (i) refresh citations only, (ii) add new resolution entry for SM-consistent branch, (iii) combine. Option (ii) was rejected after v17 schema inspection — the implication kind enum has no representation for 'no structural consequence forced' resolutions; the SM-consistent branch cannot be added without a schema bump. Option (i) chosen as the schema-faithful refresh. The schema limitation is a structural observation worth surfacing for future v20 methodology pass (see schema_candidates_status_observations below). DECISION 3 (continuation description refresh): handoff confirmed; sentence-level mechanical refresh executed. DECISION 4 (sub-PR 53 edge NOT touched): handoff confirmed; reaffirmed in this changelog's edge_choice_rationale and not modified. HANDOFF CITATION CORRECTIONS: handoff cited 'Boccaletti et al. 2024 Nature 644:78' — my literature search resolves the BMW 2024 paper to arXiv:2407.10913 [hep-lat] with Nature publication as s41586-026-10449-z (Nature 2026); cited accordingly. Aliberti 2025 (Phys Rep 1143:1) and Aguillard 2025 (PRL 135:101802) cited as the handoff specified. HANDOFF FIFTH-FIELD ADDITION: muon-g-2.description (carrier prose) added as a fifth refresh target beyond the handoff's original three (carrier qs + carrier if_real_implies + continuation description) per the maintainer's accuracy/truth-to-literature directive, because the carrier description's '~4.2σ discrepancy' phrasing is 2021-era framing whose refresh follows the same firewall test as the other four fields.
changelog.schema_candidates_status_observations: ONE schema observation surfaced by sub-PR 55 authoring, NOT promoted as a v20 schema candidate in this sub-PR but flagged for future-methodology consideration: The v17 if_real_implies implication kind enum {new_cell, new_axis, forced_edge, promotes_subtype, new_FC} is designed to record forced structural consequences and lacks a representation for resolutions whose realization implies NO new structure (the 'SM-consistent branch' / 'dispatch-to-internal-methodology-refinement' / 'no FC forced' family). The muon-g-2 post-2025 picture exposes this limitation: a complete record of the carrier's resolution landscape would benefit from both branches being structurally encoded — the BSM-loop branch (currently encoded, forces new BSM FC) AND the SM-consistent branch (cannot be encoded under v17). Whether this gap warrants a v20 schema extension (e.g., a new implication kind 'no_new_structure' with description-only target, or a separate carrier-level field 'resolution_dispatched_to' pointing at internal-to-an-existing-FC) is deferred to a future methodology discussion. The post-2025 muon-g-2 picture is the first authoring case to expose this gap concretely; future similar carriers (other 'candidate-anomaly-pending-resolution' carriers whose post-data-update picture lands on SM-consistent rather than new-FC-forcing) would inherit the same friction. Beyond this observation, no schema candidates surfaced by sub-PR 55 authoring. v18 schema + v19 bound_direction retrofit cover all five field-refresh structural needs without further friction.
changelog.methodological_observations_recorded: ONE methodological observation. SUB-PR 55 ESTABLISHES the CARRIER-REFRESH sub-PR shape as a FIRST-CLASS Phase C deliverable, distinct from the strictly additive Phase A → Phase B → Phase C trajectory the v18 spec was authored against. The diff shape: refreshes EXISTING quantitative_scale.value + citations, EXISTING if_real_implies entries' condition + condition_citations, EXISTING node descriptions and citation arrays — manipulates content already in place rather than appending. The firewall test (§4 admissibility per field-refresh; §2.5 self-check per field) applies unchanged. The changelog structure mirrors the sub-PR 53/54 13-field format with edge_choice_rationale repurposed as field_refresh_rationale and T_convention_minted defaulted to 'None — not a sensitivity-adding sub-PR'. Future post-publication-drift refreshes that may inherit this pattern: BBN parameter refreshes when CMB-S4/ACT publish updated cosmology fits; dark-matter freeze-out refreshes when lattice-QCD updates the canonical QCD-axion mass-coupling relation; neutrino oscillation parameter refreshes when NuFIT or T2K publish global-fit updates; any carrier or cell where the LITERATURE-ANCHORED value evolves while the structural content of the carrier/cell is stable. The sub-PR 55 precedent fixes (a) the diff shape (refresh existing values + citations + descriptions without touching structural fields), (b) the firewall test (§4 admissibility applies; §2.5 self-check applies; antecedent → consequent must still follow from published derivation), and (c) the changelog structure (mirrors sub-PR 53/54 13-field structure with field-name repurposing as above). Specific sub-PR 55 observation about scope discipline: the muon-g-2 carrier's empirical_status field was considered for refresh (from 'candidate-anomaly-pending-resolution' to a potentially-less-tentative status given post-2025 reconciliation) but left unchanged on the firewall-faithful reading that the SM-internal HVP-methodology question (dispersive vs lattice; CMD-3 vs earlier e⁺ e⁻ data below 1 GeV) IS the pending resolution at post-2025 precision; demoting empirical_status to 'closed' or similar would overstate the 2025-as-of-now consensus. Future carrier-refresh sub-PRs encountering similar empirical_status questions should apply the same self-check before modifying enum-valued status fields: change only if the literature itself names the carrier as closed.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 56 - Phase C STEP 4.4 continues. SIXTEENTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; TENTH Step-4.4 sub-PR. FOUR (4) resolves edges authored from rubin-lsst OPENING the dark-energy / structure-growth / dark-matter-substructure physics subdomain. Closes the PENULTIMATE unattached forward-looking experimental-program node from sub-PR 0.5's v40 inventory (only fcc remains, queued for sub-PR 57). FIRST INSTANCE of the MIXED-EXCLUSION-FLAVOR sub-PR shape — a single source node attaches resolves edges with BOTH exclusion_only=false (one edge, dark-energy cc-frontier) and exclusion_only=true (three edges, sigma_8 weak-lensing + WDM-mass + PBH-microlensing) based on the per-target structure of candidate-foundational programs. Establishes precedent for sub-PR 57 fcc (mixed gauge-coupling-precision + direct-search) and any future cross-science-case experimental-program node. PROJECT'S SECOND exclusion_only=false PPP-populated resolves edge (first was sub-PR 53 muon-g-2-continuation); first such edge on a cosmological frontier; first cc-frontier resolves edge with populated PPP. PARALLEL-RESOLVES-EDGE pattern signal first instance: two independent experimental programs (rubin-lsst + desi) targeting the same frontier (cc-frontier) with DISTINCT methodologies (3x2pt+SNIa+clustering vs BAO-sound-horizon) at distinct sensitivity-encoding kinds (dimensionless precision-floor vs sigma_deviation discovery-floor) — establishes that multiple resolves edges can target the same node and that the value of the encoding is methodology-distinguishability, not single-edge-per-target. Sensitivity-kind coverage on resolves edges remains 7/8 — energy_scale still unused, as anticipated; sub-PR 57 fcc is the natural closure for 7/8 -> 8/8 via beam-energy or unification-scale sensitivities. No new T-conventions minted (T33 reused on edges A and B; standard one-sided bound encoding on edges C and D). Carrier-refresh sub-PR shape from sub-PR 55 not invoked (sub-PR 56 is a strictly-additive Step-4.4 resolves-edge sub-PR, no carrier fields touched, sub-PR 53 edge byte-identical-preserved precedent extended to all prior edges). Validator clean (Rules 1-36 against v19 schema, 4 legacy `constrains` errors tolerated).
changelog.lead_choice_rationale: Lead (rubin-lsst) was pre-confirmed at the close of sub-PR 55 (v92 changelog NEXT-STEP recommendation): 'the natural sub-PR 56 lead per the handoff queue is rubin-lsst (smaller-shape transient-survey + dark-matter-substructure subdomain opening); sub-PR 57 then reaches fcc.' Three reasons recorded for the changelog: (1) CLOSES THE PENULTIMATE UNATTACHED EXPERIMENTAL-PROGRAM NODE — of the 12 forward-looking experimental-program nodes authored in sub-PR 0.5 (v40), 10 had resolves-edge attachments by close of sub-PR 54 (dune + hyper-k + juno + gw-ground-network + lisa + axion-haloscope-network + edm-program + muon-g-2-continuation + desi + cmb-s4); two remained unattached (rubin-lsst and fcc); sub-PR 56 attaches rubin-lsst as the natural penultimate-attachment per the smaller-shape-before-largest-shape ordering with fcc deferred to sub-PR 57 as the highest-complexity Step-4.4 lead. (2) OPENS THE COSMOLOGICAL-OBSERVATIONS DARK-ENERGY DISCRIMINATION SURFACE WITH PPP-POPULATED EDGES — Rubin/LSST's dark-energy program combining 3x2pt weak-lensing + Type Ia SNe + BAO + cluster counts is designed to discriminate between candidate-foundational dark-energy programs at the point-prediction level: LCDM (w_0=-1, w_a=0 by construction), quintessence-class (specific w(z) trajectories with w_0 > -1 at percent level), modified-gravity (degenerate-in-expansion but distinct-in-growth via 3x2pt growth-vs-expansion split). The DESC SRD §5 Y10 projection σ(w_0) ≈ 0.01 establishes the first cosmological frontier with PPP-populated discriminator coverage. (3) METHODOLOGICAL-TEST PRECEDENT FOR THE MIXED-EXCLUSION-FLAVOR SUB-PR SHAPE — sub-PR 56 is the FIRST Step-4.4 sub-PR whose resolves edges split between exclusion_only=true and exclusion_only=false within a single source node's outgoing-edge set. Prior precedent: sub-PRs 51 (axion-haloscope-network), 52 (edm-program), 54 (cmb-s4) had uniform exclusion_only=true; sub-PR 53 (muon-g-2-continuation) had uniform exclusion_only=false. Sub-PR 56's mixed shape establishes the convention precedent that a single experimental-program node's resolves edges can carry mixed exclusion_only values based on per-target structure: dark-energy targets get PPP-populated exclusion_only=false (LCDM vs quintessence vs MG are point-prediction-discriminable); DM-substructure and microlensing targets get exclusion_only=true (CDM/WDM/PBH parameter spaces don't yield single discriminating point predictions). This is the structurally-natural way to handle real experimental programs spanning multiple science cases with different program-prediction structures. Establishing this precedent in sub-PR 56 gives sub-PR 57+ fcc (the most complex Step-4.4 lead with even more cross-target heterogeneity) a model to inherit.
changelog.edge_choice_rationale: FOUR edges authored covering Rubin's primary science-case attachments. Per the maintainer's accuracy/truth-to-literature directive (sub-PR 55 binding), edge-set decisions were resolved on the merits rather than surfaced piecemeal; resolutions documented here. (A) edge-rs-rubin-lsst-cc-frontier — TARGET cc-frontier (carrier-level) parallel to desi's existing edge-rs-desi-cc-frontier. Decision: carrier-level over cell-level. Rationale: Rubin's combined 3x2pt+SNIa+BAO+cluster-counts methodology hits cc-frontier at the carrier-summary level; there is no cell-level dedicated w_0-w_a cell distinct from cell-cosm-BAO-sound-horizon-DESI (which is BAO-specific and already a desi target). PARALLEL-RESOLVES-EDGE pattern signal first instance — see methodological_observations_recorded. Sensitivity kind=dimensionless, value=0.01, bound_direction=two-sided (T33 reuse from sub-PR 53 muon-g-2-continuation precedent for precision-measurement two-sided bounding deviation from a reference value; reference here is the LCDM w_0=-1). The DESI parallel edge uses kind=sigma_deviation (discovery-floor encoding); Rubin's distinct methodology calls for distinct encoding (precision-floor σ(w_0) ≈ 0.01 dimensionless) — the two encodings are complementary and together establish the parallel-edge-different-encoding precedent. exclusion_only=false; PPP populated with THREE programs (LCDM, quintessence-class, modified-gravity) per §4 admissibility test: each PPP entry cites the program-internal calculation deriving w_0. The third PPP entry (modified-gravity) is included per default — the handoff's edge-choice-rationale notes it as optional but the three-program PPP gives richer cross-FC pattern visibility for AI-queryability and §1.2 distinctive-value purposes; the modified-gravity discriminator works via growth-rate not w_0 directly, which is documented in the description. (B) edge-rs-rubin-lsst-sigma8-weak-lensing — TARGET cell-cosm-sigma8-weak-lensing. Decision: include (handoff's optional B accepted per default recommendation: weak-lensing IS Rubin's native methodology, so excluding sigma_8 would leave Rubin's signature science underrepresented). Sensitivity kind=dimensionless, value=0.005, bound_direction=two-sided (T33 reuse). exclusion_only=true with §4(2) STRUCTURAL-DISCIPLINE failure paralleling sub-PR 54 cmb-s4 Σm_ν edge: candidate-foundational programs for resolving the σ_8/S_8 tension split into late-universe BSM (PPP-eligible in principle) and WL-systematic-resolution (PPP-ineligible); the cell-level resolution doesn't host a candidate_targeting array (cells don't, by schema design — only frontiers and totality-approaches carry candidate_targeting per the v17 spec), so §4(2) fails on STRUCTURAL-LOCUS grounds. PPP empty under the firewall. (C) edge-rs-rubin-lsst-WDM-mass-MWsatellites — TARGET cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm. Decision: target the keV-warm-DM cell (NOT a separate thermal-WDM cell because no dedicated thermal-WDM cell exists; the keV-warm-DM cell IS parametrized by m_WDM ≈ keV-scale, the parameter space Rubin probes). Sensitivity kind=mass, value=6.0, units=keV, bound_direction=lower (Drlica-Wagner et al. 2019 §III Y10 projection from full LSST MW satellite census). exclusion_only=true per parameter-space-without-program-point-predictions grounds (sub-PR 51/52 precedent). PPP empty. The cell hosts the Bulbul 2014 contested-X-ray-line claim as a SUB-CLAIM; Rubin's m_WDM lower bound is a structurally distinct measurement-direction (cosmological-structure constraint on the WDM mass scale) from the X-ray-line direct-detection claim — both belong on the same cell because they probe the SAME parameter (m_WDM ≈ keV-scale). (D) edge-rs-rubin-lsst-PBH-microlensing-asteroid — TARGET cell-dm-PBH-asteroid-mass-viable. Decision: target the asteroid-mass-window cell (NOT the LIGO-mass-window cell — Rubin's microlensing covers asteroid-to-stellar-mass via stellar light-curve monitoring; the LIGO-mass cell content concerns LVK-BBH-derived constraints not microlensing). Sensitivity kind=dimensionless, value=0.01, bound_direction=upper (Drlica-Wagner et al. 2019 §V Y10 projection of ~10× tightening on Niikura 2019 HSC bound). exclusion_only=true per parameter-space-without-program-point-predictions grounds. PPP empty. Edge E (Type Ia SN distance modulus) DEFERRED. Decision: not included. Rationale: no dedicated SN cell exists in cosmological-observations; SN-Ia distance-modulus content folds into the H_0 cells (cell-cosm-H0-CMB-Planck, cell-cosm-H0-local-ladder-SH0ES, cell-cosm-H0-GW-standard-siren) which are H_0-focused not SN-distance-focused. Rubin's SN-Ia program contributes to the cc-frontier edge A via the joint 3x2pt+SNIa probe combination (documented in edge A's sensitivity citation), so the SN content is captured at the carrier-level edge A. Authoring a dedicated SN-distance-modulus cell + resolves edge is a separate content-pass decision deferred.

firewall_self_check.part_1_sensitivity_is_literature_value: PASS. σ(w_0) ≈ 0.01 Y10 floor is documented in The LSST Dark Energy Science Collaboration 2018 DESC Science Requirements Document arXiv:1809.01669 §5 (the FoM ≳ 500 figure-of-merit in the (w_0, w_a) plane corresponds to σ(w_0) ≈ 0.01 and σ(w_a) ≈ 0.04 in the joint 3x2pt+SNIa+clustering+cluster-counts constraint at design-completion 10-year coverage); cross-validated by Ivezic et al. 2019 ApJ 873:111 §13 dark-energy design parameters. The encoded value is a documented design-completion forecast in a peer-reviewed collaboration design document, not a cartographer synthesis.
firewall_self_check.part_2_per_program_predictions_are_progr: PASS. PPP populated with three programs each citing program-internal calculations: (i) LCDM at w_0=-1 cites Aghanim et al. (Planck Collaboration) 2020 §6.3 base-LCDM structural definition; (ii) quintessence-class at w_0 ≈ -0.95 ± 0.05 cites Caldwell-Linder 2005 PRL 95:141301 (thawing/freezing class definitions) and Linder 2003 PRL 90:091301 (canonical w_0-w_a parametrization derivation); (iii) modified-gravity at w_0 ≈ -1.0 ± 0.05 cites Sotiriou-Faraoni 2010 RMP 82:451 §IV.C (f(R) background expansion characterization) and DES Collaboration 2023 PRD 107:083504 §V (current MG parameter constraints). All three are program-internal calculations / structural definitions, not cartographer derivations.

firewall_self_check.section_2_5_self_check: PASS. Question: 'Would I author this resolves edge independent of any pattern observation in the existing dataset? Specifically, if I had only the Rubin/LSST DESC SRD + Ivezic 2019 + Aghanim 2020 + Caldwell-Linder 2005 + Sotiriou-Faraoni 2010 in front of me and the rubin-lsst source node's existing description, would the same edge target cc-frontier and the same PPP composition be the natural attachment to make from the design literature alone?' Answer: YES — Rubin's primary dark-energy science case in the DESC SRD §5 names w_0, w_a as the load-bearing observables; cc-frontier is the natural carrier-level target for the cosmological-constant-problem-resolving methodology that the joint 3x2pt+SNIa+BAO+clusters approach addresses; the LCDM+quintessence+MG three-program PPP set is the standard candidate-foundational-program partition over dark-energy resolutions in the cosmological literature.

Answer: YES — Rubin's signature methodology is weak-lensing (Rubin IS fundamentally a weak-lensing survey); the σ_8/S_8 cell is the natural attachment point for Rubin's WL contribution; the current S_8 tension at 2-3σ between WL surveys and Planck-CMB is the operationally-relevant target that Rubin Y10 σ(S_8) ≈ 0.005 sharpens to ≳ 5σ discrimination. The exclusion_only=true §4(2) STRUCTURAL-LOCUS failure mirrors sub-PR 54 cmb-s4 Σm_ν edge precedent.

changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition: Handoff sub-PR 56 plan (pre-confirmed rubin-lsst lead with 4-edge core A+B+C+D, mixed-exclusion-flavor sub-PR shape) accepted in full. Maintainer-confirmation points (1)-(4) resolved on the merits per the binding accuracy/truth-to-literature directive: (1) target set: A+B+C+D (4 edges) accepted — handoff's default A+C+D plus B per handoff confirmation point 4 default recommendation; E deferred because no dedicated SN cell exists (SN content folds into edge A's joint-probe sensitivity citation). (2) edge A exclusion_only=false with 3-program PPP accepted — default recommendation followed; modified-gravity included as the third PPP entry (handoff noted as optional) for richer cross-FC pattern visibility per §1.2 distinctive-value goals. (3) edge C WDM bound at m_WDM ≳ 6 keV (Drlica-Wagner 2019 §III Y10 projection) accepted as primary literature anchor; alternative literature values (Newton 2021 ≳ 9 keV) preserved in source-value parenthetical of citation. (4) edge B included per default recommendation (Rubin's signature methodology is weak-lensing; excluding sigma_8 would underrepresent Rubin's native science); edge E deferred per handoff's secondary recommendation. All four edges author with one consolidated changelog entry mirroring v90/v91/v92 13-field structure. No new T-numbers; T-register unchanged at 33 conventions. Resolves-edge sensitivity-kind coverage remains 7/8 (energy_scale unused) — sub-PR 57 fcc is the natural closure for 7/8 -> 8/8.
changelog.schema_candidates_status_observations: ONE schema observation reaffirmed by sub-PR 56 authoring, NOT promoted as a new v20 schema candidate in this sub-PR but flagged for future-methodology consideration: The PARALLEL-RESOLVES-EDGE pattern surfaced by sub-PR 56 (rubin-lsst's edge A and desi's existing edge-rs-desi-cc-frontier both target cc-frontier with distinct sensitivity-encoding kinds — dimensionless precision-floor vs sigma_deviation discovery-floor) demonstrates that the resolves-edge schema supports multiple independent edges per target-node by design (no schema constraint forbids multiple edges with the same target). The v19 spec might benefit from an explicit acknowledgment that parallel-resolves-edges at different sensitivity-kind encodings are first-class — currently this is implicit. NOT a schema change candidate; documentation candidate only. The v17 if_real_implies implication kind enum {new_cell, new_axis, forced_edge, promotes_subtype, new_FC} expressibility-gap noted in sub-PR 55 changelog (lacks representation for resolutions whose realization implies NO new structure) remains an unaddressed v20 schema-extension candidate; not relevant to sub-PR 56's resolves-edge authoring.
changelog.methodological_observations_recorded: TWO methodological observations recorded by sub-PR 56. (I) MIXED-EXCLUSION-FLAVOR SUB-PR SHAPE established as a FIRST-CLASS Phase C deliverable. A single experimental-program source node can attach multiple resolves edges with BOTH exclusion_only=true AND exclusion_only=false within the same sub-PR, based on the per-target structure of candidate-foundational programs. Sub-PR 56's pattern: edge A (cc-frontier) exclusion_only=false PPP=3 because dark-energy candidate-foundational programs (LCDM, quintessence, MG) are point-prediction-discriminable; edges B (sigma_8), C (WDM), D (PBH-microlensing) exclusion_only=true PPP=0 because their candidate-foundational programs parameterize the observable rather than supplying single discriminating point predictions. The mixed shape is firewall-faithful as long as the per-edge §4 admissibility test passes for each edge independently — the mixed shape itself is a methodological refinement of the prior uniform-exclusion-flavor sub-PRs (51/52/54 all true; 53 all false). Future sub-PRs likely to inherit: sub-PR 57 fcc (mixed gauge-coupling-precision PPP-populated + direct-search exclusion_only=true); future cross-science-case experimental-program nodes (next-gen neutrino, GW, etc.). (II) PARALLEL-RESOLVES-EDGE PATTERN at first instance. Rubin's edge A and DESI's existing edge-rs-desi-cc-frontier both target cc-frontier with distinct methodologies (Rubin: 3x2pt+SNIa+BAO+clusters joint constraint; DESI: BAO sound-horizon-at-drag-epoch standard ruler) at distinct sensitivity-encoding kinds (Rubin: kind=dimensionless precision-floor σ(w_0) ≈ 0.01; DESI: kind=sigma_deviation discovery-floor 5σ). This is the project's FIRST INSTANCE of two independent experimental-program nodes targeting the SAME frontier node with DIFFERENT sensitivity-encoding kinds — establishing the precedent that parallel-resolves-edges are first-class and that distinct methodologies producing distinct measurement-class encodings can co-exist on the same target. Post-MCP-rebuild (Phase C Step N+1), the find_resolvers(cc-frontier) query will return BOTH edges, surfacing the two-methodology complementary-probe structure. Methodologically distinct from parallel-anchors (sub-PRs 18/20/22 cross-FC qs pattern signals where the same quantity is anchored at multiple cells/frontiers) — parallel-resolves-edges anchor the same target across distinct experimental methodologies rather than across distinct structural-axis-locations. Future sub-PRs may add a third parallel-resolves-edge to cc-frontier (e.g., from cmb-s4 inflation-tensor-r-bound side, though cmb-s4's existing primordial-GW-r-bound edge targets cell-cosm-primordial-GW-r-bound rather than cc-frontier) or to other frontiers where multiple experimental programs share targets. The carrier-refresh sub-PR shape (sub-PR 55 first-instance) is NOT invoked in sub-PR 56 — sub-PR 56 is a strictly-additive Step-4.4 resolves-edge sub-PR with no carrier-field touches. The sub-PR 55 byte-identical-preservation precedent for prior resolves edges is extended: zero pre-sub-PR-56 edges modified.

skip_decisions.rationale: Rubin's H_0 contribution is via the joint cosmological constraint in edge A's combined-probe methodology; a dedicated H_0-cell-targeted edge would duplicate the cc-frontier coverage without adding methodological distinctiveness. Rubin's SN-Ia program could in principle target one of the H_0 cells via standard-candle distance-modulus measurements, but the operational-relevance of Rubin to the H_0 tension specifically is through the late-universe-distance-ladder revision (which folds into the SH0ES-cell content) rather than via a Rubin-distinct H_0 measurement direction. Skip is firewall-faithful: edge A captures Rubin's H_0 indirect contribution; explicit H_0 cell targeting would be cartographer-driven rather than literature-driven.

skip_decisions.skipped_target: Edge E - Type Ia SN distance modulus cell
skip_decisions.rationale: No dedicated SN-Ia distance-modulus cell exists in cosmological-observations FC; SN content folds into the existing H_0 cells (which are H_0-focused) or into the BAO/w_0wa cells (which are BAO-focused). Authoring a dedicated SN-distance-modulus cell + resolves edge is a separate content-pass decision deferred. Rubin's SN-Ia contribution is captured in edge A's joint-probe methodology citation (DESC SRD §5).

Rubin's WL-based σ_8 measurement targets the WL-side cell (edge B); the CMB-side cell records the Planck-inferred σ_8 = 0.811 anchor as the tension comparator. Targeting the CMB-side cell from Rubin would be a category error (Rubin is not a CMB experiment). The cmb-s4 sub-PR 54 edges target Σm_ν (not σ_8) on their direct cell; no current resolves-edge targets the σ_8-CMB cell from any experimental-program node. Skip is firewall-faithful.

The cell's content concerns LVK-BBH-merger-rate-derived constraints on PBH-as-DM in the 1-100 M_sun LIGO mass window, NOT microlensing-derived constraints. Rubin's microlensing methodology covers asteroid-to-stellar-mass via stellar light-curve monitoring; the LIGO-mass-window bounds come from LVK BBH merger rates (gw-ground-network's territory). Skip is firewall-faithful: edge D targets cell-dm-PBH-asteroid-mass-viable which is the methodologically-correct cell for Rubin's microlensing methodology.

Rubin's stellar-mass microlensing in MW bulge/disk could in principle tighten the EROS-2 MACHO bound f_MACHO < 0.08, but the cell's structural status is FALSIFIED (MACHO-as-100%-halo-DM rejected by EROS-2 + OGLE + MACHO surveys); Rubin's marginal improvement on an already-falsified-as-100%-DM cell doesn't open new physics. The asteroid-mass-PBH-viability cell (edge D target) is the structurally distinct opening. Skip is firewall-faithful: edge D captures the operationally-relevant Rubin PBH contribution.

changelog.summary: sub-PR 57 - Phase C STEP 4.4 CLOSES. SEVENTEENTH post-ADE-clique-sweep-arc sub-PR; ELEVENTH and FINAL Step-4.4 sub-PR. FOUR (4) resolves edges authored from fcc OPENING the high-energy collider / Higgs-precision / direct-BSM-search / WIMP-DM-collider-reach physics subdomain. CLOSES THE LAST UNATTACHED forward-looking experimental-program node from sub-PR 0.5's v40 inventory: of the 12 forward-looking experimental-program nodes authored in sub-PR 0.5 (dune, hyper-k, juno, gw-ground-network, lisa, cmb-s4, rubin-lsst, desi, axion-haloscope-network, edm-program, muon-g-2-continuation, fcc), all 12 now carry resolves-edge attachments. SECOND INSTANCE of the MIXED-EXCLUSION-FLAVOR sub-PR shape (sub-PR 56 rubin-lsst was first): three edges (A Higgs self-coupling, C WIMP thermal mass, D m_W precision) exclusion_only=false with PPP populated; one edge (B direct BSM reach) exclusion_only=true. THIRD INSTANCE of THREE-EDGES-TO-A-SINGLE-CARRIER pattern (sub-PR 49 was first with gw-ground-network three edges into compact-astro cells; sub-PR 54 cmb-s4 second with three edges across CMB cells): edges A, B, D all target hierarchy-problem carrier with structurally distinct probes (Higgs-potential shape via λ_HHH; direct production reach; indirect oblique constraint via m_W). PROJECT'S FOURTH-AND-FIFTH exclusion_only=false PPP-populated resolves edges following sub-PR 53 muon-g-2-continuation (first) and sub-PR 56 rubin-lsst edge A (second); cumulative count rises to five PPP-populated edges. SECOND INSTANCE of cell-level-vs-edge-level encoding-divergence (sub-PR 54 cmb-s4 r-bound was first with cell=dimensionless, edge=ratio): edge C cell-dm-WIMP-canonical has cell-level kind=length (σ_SI cross-section from LZ direct-detection) while the resolves edge encodes kind=mass (collider WIMP mass reach) — distinct observables of the same WIMP-DM physical content, complementary rather than redundant. Resolves-edge sensitivity-kind coverage remains 7/8 (energy_scale unused) per accuracy-driven authoring under the firewall — none of FCC's literature-cited deliverables encode naturally as kind=energy_scale (Higgs-self-coupling=dimensionless; direct-BSM-reach=mass; WIMP-thermal-mass=mass; m_W-precision=mass). The handoff's anticipation of energy_scale closure via fcc was a pattern-completion expectation; the firewall-faithful authoring outcome is that energy_scale stays unused until a literature-cited deliverable naturally requires it. No new T-conventions minted (T33 reused on edges A, C, D for precision-measurement two-sided bounding; standard kind=mass bound_direction=lower one-sided exclusion on edge B). Step 4.4 CLOSES with this sub-PR: all 12 forward-looking experimental-program nodes attached; cumulative 38 resolves edges (34 prior + 4 here) distributed across 11 phenomenon-FC cells and 4 frontier/totality-approach targets. Validator clean (Rules 1-36 against v19 schema, 4 legacy 'constrains' errors tolerated). Step 4.5 (quantitative_scale on if_real_implies implications) remains queued as the final Phase C sweep.
changelog.lead_choice_rationale: Lead (fcc) was pre-confirmed at the close of sub-PR 56 (v93 changelog NEXT-STEP recommendation): 'the natural sub-PR 57 lead per the handoff queue is fcc (most complex Step-4.4 lead; closes Step 4.4 attachment surface).' Three reasons recorded: (1) CLOSES THE LAST UNATTACHED EXPERIMENTAL-PROGRAM NODE — 11 of 12 forward-looking experimental-program nodes attached by close of sub-PR 56; fcc was the final unattached node, queued as the most-complex Step-4.4 lead per the handoff doc §3 sub-PR 57 entry. (2) PROGRAM-COMPLEXITY MATCHES THE MIXED-EXCLUSION-FLAVOR PRECEDENT FROM SUB-PR 56 — fcc's two-stage program (FCC-ee precision + FCC-hh direct-search) spans precision EW + Higgs + direct production + DM-mediator + flavour, each with distinct candidate-foundational-program structures. Sub-PR 56 rubin-lsst's mixed-flavor precedent applies: per-target structure of candidate-foundational programs determines per-edge exclusion_only value, not a uniform sub-PR-wide choice. (3) STEP 4.4 CLOSURE MILESTONE — sub-PR 57 closes Step 4.4 (resolves edges from forward-looking experimental-program nodes), leaving only Step 4.5 (quantitative_scale on if_real_implies implications) as the remaining Phase C sweep. The cumulative resolves-edge dataset (38 edges across 12 programs and 15 targets) is now complete at the Step 4.4 level.
changelog.edge_choice_rationale: FOUR edges authored covering FCC's primary science-case attachments. Per the maintainer's binding accuracy/truth-to-literature directive (sub-PR 55+ binding), edge-set decisions were resolved on the merits per the literature without pattern-completion considerations. The handoff's anticipation of 'closing 7/8 -> 8/8 sensitivity-kind coverage via energy_scale anchor' was IDENTIFIED as a pattern-completion expectation and rejected as authoring rationale; the firewall-faithful outcome is that energy_scale stays unused until a literature-cited deliverable naturally requires it. None of FCC's literature-cited deliverables encode naturally as kind=energy_scale: (A) edge-rs-fcc-hierarchy-Higgs-self-coupling — TARGET hierarchy-problem (carrier-level) via FCC-hh di-Higgs Higgs trilinear coupling precision. Decision: carrier-level over cell-level. Rationale: λ_HHH is the load-bearing observable of the Higgs-potential shape, which is the structural probe of hierarchy-problem candidate-foundational programs (SUSY tree-level decoupling vs composite-Higgs ξ-dependent deviation). No cell-level Higgs-potential cell exists in the dataset; carrier-level target is the firewall-correct attachment. Sensitivity kind=dimensionless, value=0.05, bound_direction=two-sided (T33 reuse from sub-PR 53/56 precedents for precision-measurement two-sided bounding deviation from reference value κ_λ^SM = 1). exclusion_only=false; PPP populated with TWO programs (SM at κ_λ = 1; composite-Higgs at κ_λ ≈ 0.85 ± {0.10, 0.05}) per §4 admissibility test part (2): each PPP entry cites program-internal calculation (SM: Frederix et al. 2014 di-Higgs NLO QCD; composite-Higgs: Gillioz et al. 2012 κ_λ = (1-2ξ)/√(1-ξ) for PNGB-Higgs). SUSY's κ_λ prediction is parameter-dependent (tan β + stop spectrum + decoupling limit) and does not yield a single discriminating point prediction at FCC precision; SUSY intentionally not in PPP. (B) edge-rs-fcc-hierarchy-direct-BSM-reach — TARGET hierarchy-problem (carrier-level) via FCC-hh direct production of strongly-interacting BSM particles. Decision: carrier-level over cell-level. Rationale: 'direct BSM reach' spans squarks, gluinos, stops, top partners, Z′ — the carrier-level hierarchy-problem target subsumes these via the candidate_targeting array (SUSY, Composite Higgs, Extra dimensions, etc.). Sensitivity kind=mass, value=30, units=TeV, bound_direction=lower (Ellis-Zheng 2015 PRD 92:075034 squark reach 32 TeV non-compressed / 37 TeV compressed; 30 TeV encoded as conservative lower edge of the range). exclusion_only=true per parameter-space-without-program-point-predictions grounds (sub-PR 51/52/54/56-B-C-D precedents). PPP empty: the candidate-foundational programs for the hierarchy problem parameterize the BSM mass scale rather than supplying single point predictions of squark/gluino/stop masses at FCC precision. (C) edge-rs-fcc-WIMP-thermal-mass-reach — TARGET cell-dm-WIMP-canonical via FCC-hh disappearing-track WIMP-DM search. Decision: target the WIMP-canonical cell (NOT a separate heavy-WIMP cell because no dedicated heavy-WIMP cell exists; the WIMP-canonical cell content explicitly notes 'heavy-WIMP candidates remain viable' covering the thermal-wino-3-TeV and thermal-higgsino-1-TeV regime FCC-hh probes). Sensitivity kind=mass, value=3, units=TeV, bound_direction=lower (Saito et al. 2019 EPJC 79:469 disappearing-track 5σ reach m_χ ≈ 3 TeV wino / 1 TeV higgsino). exclusion_only=false; PPP populated with TWO COMPLEMENTARY (not competing) thermal-WIMP programs (pure-wino LSP at m = 2.87 ± 0.1 TeV; pure-higgsino LSP at m = 1.08 ± 0.05 TeV) per §4(2). Cell-vs-edge encoding-divergence: cell carries kind=length (σ_SI cross-section from LZ); edge carries kind=mass (collider mass reach) — distinct observables of the same WIMP-DM physical content; sub-PR 54 cmb-s4 r-bound first-instance precedent. (D) edge-rs-fcc-hierarchy-mW-precision — TARGET hierarchy-problem (carrier-level) via FCC-ee m_W precision measurement at WW threshold. Decision: carrier-level over cell-level. Rationale: m_W precision is an INDIRECT oblique-parameter (S, T, U) constraint on BSM at TeV-decade — operates at the hierarchy-problem candidate-foundational-program level, not on a specific cell of any phenomenon-FC. Sensitivity kind=mass, value=0.0005, units=GeV (0.5 MeV), bound_direction=two-sided (T33 reuse — precision-measurement two-sided bounding deviation from SM-fit reference value). exclusion_only=false; PPP populated with ONE program (SM EW global fit at m_W^SM = 80.3536 ± 0.0061 GeV) per §4(2); singleton because SM is the unique candidate-foundational program with a structural point prediction for m_W. NO EDGE TO MATTER-ANTIMATTER-ASYMMETRY FRONTIER: my earlier draft proposed a flavour-CP-violation edge (φ_s precision at FCC-ee) but the FCC literature's connection to BAU is mostly via channels already captured in edge A (EW-baryogenesis triggers visible in λ_HHH deviations); a dedicated flavour-CP-FCC-ee edge would require separate literature pass for the specific φ_s precision target and the SM-side CKM-fit comparison value. Skipped rather than added speculatively; queued for a potential follow-up sub-PR if the flavour-FCC-ee literature pass justifies it. NO ELECTROWEAKINO-SPECIFIC EDGE TO DM CELLS: thermal Wino/Higgsino content is captured at the cell-dm-WIMP-canonical level via edge C; authoring separate cells for thermal-Wino and thermal-Higgsino would be a content-pass decision not warranted by Phase C scope.

firewall_self_check.part_1_sensitivity_is_literature_value: PASS. σ(κ_λ) = 3.2–5.7% at 68% CL FCC-hh combined-channel precision is documented in Stapf et al. 2023 arXiv:2312.03513 as the design-completion forecast at √s = 100 TeV with 30 ab⁻¹. The encoded 0.05 is the central-systematic-assumption point in this published range. Cross-validated by the older Abada 2019 FCC-hh CDR (§3.2) ≈ 5% projection and the complementary FCC-ee single-Higgs-NLO indirect σ(δκ_λ) ≈ 33% (combined with HL-LHC) in Bernardi 2022 EPJ Plus. The encoded value is a documented design-completion forecast in a peer-reviewed paper.
firewall_self_check.part_2_per_program_predictions_are_progr: PASS. PPP populated with two programs each citing program-internal calculations: (i) SM at κ_λ = 1 cites Frederix et al. 2014 (di-Higgs NLO QCD, where κ_λ = 1 is structural by SM Higgs-potential definition λ_HHH = 3m_H²/v) and Maltoni et al. 2018 (single-Higgs indirect κ_λ extraction framework). (ii) Composite-Higgs at κ_λ ≈ 0.85 cites Gillioz et al. 2012 (PNGB-Higgs κ_λ = (1-2ξ)/√(1-ξ) explicit ξ-dependence; for ξ = 0.1, κ_λ ≈ 0.84) and Contino et al. 2011 (SILH effective-Lagrangian derivation). Both program-internal calculations.
firewall_self_check.part_3_both_halves_cite: PASS. Sensitivity citations array contains 3 entries (Stapf 2023, Bernardi 2022, FCC-hh CDR 2019). PPP citations arrays contain 2 entries each for SM (Frederix 2014, Maltoni 2018) and composite-Higgs (Gillioz 2012, Contino 2011). All halves cite.
firewall_self_check.section_2_5_self_check: PASS. Question: 'Would I author this resolves edge independent of any pattern observation in the existing dataset? Specifically, if I had only the FCC-hh CDR + Stapf 2023 + Bernardi 2022 + Gillioz 2012 + Frederix 2014 in front of me and the fcc source node's existing description, would the same edge target hierarchy-problem and the same PPP composition be the natural attachment to make from the design literature alone?' Answer: YES — FCC-hh's Higgs self-coupling at 3-5% is named in the CDR §3.2 abstract as one of the program's primary scientific deliverables (alongside direct BSM reach and EW precision); the hierarchy-problem frontier is the natural carrier-level target for a Higgs-potential-shape probe because λ_HHH discriminates SM (κ_λ = 1) from composite-Higgs (κ_λ < 1, ξ-set) and from EW-baryogenesis-relevant BSM scenarios at the hierarchy-problem candidate-foundational-program level.

firewall_self_check.part_1_sensitivity_is_literature_value: PASS. Squark direct-discovery reach at m_squark ≈ 30 TeV (conservative lower edge of the 32–37 TeV range) at FCC-hh is documented in Ellis-Zheng 2015 PRD 92:075034 arXiv:1506.02644 (32 TeV non-compressed, 37 TeV compressed spectra, both at √s = 100 TeV with 3 ab⁻¹); cross-validated by Mangano (ed.) 2017 CERN-2017-003 (program-wide TeV-decade BSM reach summary) and Abada 2019 FCC-hh CDR §4-§6 (gluino 10-14 TeV / squark 30+ TeV at 30 ab⁻¹). The encoded value is a documented design-completion forecast.
firewall_self_check.part_3_both_halves_cite: PASS. Sensitivity citations array contains 3 entries (Ellis-Zheng 2015, Mangano 2017, FCC-hh CDR 2019).
firewall_self_check.section_2_5_self_check: PASS. Question: 'Would I author this resolves edge independent of any pattern observation in the existing dataset?' Answer: YES — FCC-hh's direct BSM mass reach to ~30 TeV is the program's signature deliverable for the hierarchy problem, named in the CDR §4 as the primary scientific motivation for the 100 TeV center-of-mass energy choice; the hierarchy-problem frontier is the natural carrier-level target because the candidate-foundational programs (SUSY, composite-Higgs, extra dimensions) all locate the BSM scale at the TeV-decade where FCC-hh has discovery reach. The exclusion_only=true parameter-space-without-program-point-predictions failure mirrors sub-PR 51 / 52 / 54 / 56-B-C-D precedents.

firewall_self_check.part_1_sensitivity_is_literature_value: PASS. FCC-hh disappearing-track 5σ discovery reach m_χ ≈ 3 TeV (wino) and 1 TeV (higgsino) is documented in Saito et al. 2019 EPJC 79:469 arXiv:1901.02987 — the encoded 3 TeV is the wino-thermal coverage as the higher of the two thermal-WIMP-DM benchmarks. Cross-validated by Han et al. 2018 PRD 97:055007 (complementary higgsino-DM search channels) and Low-Wang 2014 JHEP 08:161 (foundational 100 TeV neutralino-DM study). The encoded value is a documented Rubin-equivalent FCC-hh-Y30ab⁻¹ design-completion forecast in a peer-reviewed paper.
firewall_self_check.part_2_per_program_predictions_are_progr: PASS. PPP populated with two programs each citing program-internal calculations: (i) thermal pure-wino at m_wino^thermal = 2.87 ± 0.04 TeV cites Hisano et al. 2007 PRD 71:063528 (Sommerfeld-corrected wino-DM annihilation), Beneke et al. 2015 JHEP 03:162 (refined NLO calculation with Sommerfeld + bound-state-formation + full NLO matching), and Cohen et al. 2013 JCAP 10:061 (independent cross-validation). (ii) thermal pure-higgsino at m_higgsino^thermal = 1.08 ± 0.02 TeV cites Cirelli-Strumia-Tamburini 2007 Nucl. Phys. B 787:152 (Minimal Dark Matter framework), Beneke et al. 2015 JHEP 03:162 (same refined NLO framework), and Krall-Reece 2018 Chin. Phys. C 42:043105 (current-status assessment). All program-internal cosmological thermal-relic-abundance calculations.
firewall_self_check.part_3_both_halves_cite: PASS. Sensitivity citations array contains 3 entries (Saito 2019, Han 2018, Low-Wang 2014). PPP citations arrays contain 3 entries each for thermal-wino and thermal-higgsino. All halves cite.
firewall_self_check.section_2_5_self_check: PASS. Question: 'Would I author this resolves edge independent of any pattern observation in the existing dataset?' Answer: YES — FCC-hh's disappearing-track DM search is the program's signature WIMP-DM probe, named in the CDR §4 (DM benchmarks) as the primary thermal-WIMP coverage channel; the cell-dm-WIMP-canonical cell is the natural attachment because the cell content explicitly notes 'Heavy-WIMP candidates (TeV-PeV scale, with non-standard freeze-out / non-thermal-relic histories) remain viable' — the thermal-wino-3-TeV and thermal-higgsino-1-TeV are exactly these heavier thermal-WIMP variants. The two-program PPP is the standard candidate-foundational-program partition over WIMP-DM thermal-relic predictions in the literature.

firewall_self_check.part_1_sensitivity_is_literature_value: PASS. FCC-ee m_W precision target σ(m_W) = 0.5 MeV statistical at 12 ab⁻¹ via WW threshold scan at √s ∈ [157, 163] GeV is documented in Azzurri et al. 2021 EPJ Plus 136:1117 arXiv:2106.15438 as the design-completion forecast. Cross-validated by Dam 2016 PoS ICHEP2016:613 arXiv:1601.03849 (companion FCC-ee EW-precision summary). Current-state comparison floor from Aaltonen et al. (CDF II) 2022 Science 376:170 (CDF II m_W ±9.4 MeV, PDG 2024 world-average ±13.3 MeV). The encoded value 0.0005 GeV = 0.5 MeV is a documented design-completion forecast in a peer-reviewed paper.
firewall_self_check.part_2_per_program_predictions_are_progr: PASS. PPP populated with one program: SM EW global fit at m_W^SM = 80.3536 ± 0.0061 GeV cites Awramik et al. 2003 PRD 69:053006 (two-loop SM m_W calculation establishing the structural relation), GFitter 2018 EPJC 78:675 (global EW fit framework), and de Blas et al. 2022 PRL 129:271801 (post-CDF-II SM-fit update). Program-internal: the SM-fit value is derived from (m_t, m_H, α_s, α(m_Z), G_F) via the two-loop electroweak structural relation.
firewall_self_check.part_3_both_halves_cite: PASS. Sensitivity citations array contains 3 entries (Azzurri 2021, Dam 2016, CDF II 2022). PPP citations array contains 3 entries (Awramik 2003, GFitter 2018, de Blas 2022).
firewall_self_check.section_2_5_self_check: PASS. Question: 'Would I author this resolves edge independent of any pattern observation in the existing dataset?' Answer: YES — FCC-ee's m_W precision at 0.5 MeV is a signature EW-precision deliverable named in the FCC-ee CDR Vol 2 (Abada 2019) as one of the program's flagship measurements alongside the Higgs cross-section measurement; the hierarchy-problem frontier is the natural carrier-level target because m_W is the load-bearing observable in the SM EW global fit and any deviation constrains BSM oblique parameters (S, T, U) at the TeV-decade scale via the canonical Δm_W ≈ -α m_W / (4 cos²θ_W (cos²θ_W - sin²θ_W)) × (...) structural relation. The singleton PPP (SM only) is the natural partition: SM is the unique candidate-foundational program with a structural point prediction for m_W; BSM scenarios parameterize the obliques without supplying single point predictions of m_W.

changelog.T_convention_minted: None - sub-PR 57 reuses T33 (sub-PR 53 first-use for precision-measurement sensitivity bound_direction=two-sided exclusion_only=false) on edges A, C, D and uses standard kind=mass bound_direction=lower one-sided exclusion encoding on edge B (precedent: sub-PR 56-C mass-kind lower-bound WDM mass reach). Resolves-edge sensitivity-kind coverage remains 7/8 (energy_scale unused) — the handoff's anticipation of fcc closing 7/8 -> 8/8 via energy_scale anchor was a pattern-completion expectation; the firewall-faithful authoring outcome under the maintainer's binding accuracy/truth-to-literature directive is that energy_scale stays unused until a literature-cited deliverable naturally requires it. None of FCC's literature-cited deliverables encode naturally as kind=energy_scale: Higgs-self-coupling = dimensionless (κ_λ modifier); direct-BSM-reach = mass (particle-mass exclusion); WIMP-thermal-mass = mass (collider mass reach); m_W-precision = mass (W boson mass precision). T-register unchanged at 33 conventions through v94 (T1-T14, T17-T31, T33; T15 retired, T16 dropped). Step 4.4 closes with 7/8 sensitivity-kind coverage; energy_scale closure deferred to whenever Step 4.5 or future content-pass surfaces a literature-cited deliverable that naturally uses it.
changelog.handoff_recommendation_disposition: Handoff sub-PR 57 plan (pre-confirmed fcc lead with 4-7 edges, mixed-exclusion-flavor sub-PR shape per sub-PR 56 rubin-lsst precedent) accepted in part and refined per the binding accuracy/truth-to-literature directive: (1) target count: 4 edges (within the handoff's 4-7 expected range, matching sub-PR 56's count). (2) mixed-exclusion-flavor: yes (3 PPP-populated + 1 exclusion_only, matching sub-PR 56's structure). (3) energy_scale closure 7/8 -> 8/8: REJECTED as authoring rationale — the handoff's anticipation was a pattern-completion expectation, not literature-driven; the firewall-faithful authoring outcome under the maintainer's binding accuracy directive is to encode each FCC deliverable per its natural literature-cited kind (Higgs-self-coupling=dimensionless; BSM-reach=mass; WIMP=mass; m_W=mass) regardless of pattern-completion considerations. Energy_scale stays unused until a literature-cited deliverable naturally requires it. (4) target distribution: 3 edges (A, B, D) into hierarchy-problem carrier + 1 edge (C) into cell-dm-WIMP-canonical — third instance of three-edges-to-a-single-carrier pattern (sub-PR 49 gw-ground-network, sub-PR 54 cmb-s4 prior instances). Three-edges-into-hierarchy-problem is justified by structural distinctness of probes (Higgs-potential shape, direct production, indirect oblique constraint); no precedent question to resolve since the firewall prohibits authoring FOR pattern, not DESPITE pattern. (5) matter-antimatter-asymmetry frontier edge: NOT INCLUDED — earlier draft proposed φ_s precision but the FCC-BAU connection is mostly captured at edge A (EW-baryogenesis triggers visible in λ_HHH deviations); a dedicated flavour-CP-FCC-ee edge would require separate literature pass for the specific φ_s precision target and SM-side CKM-fit comparison value. Deferred rather than added speculatively. All four edges author with one consolidated changelog entry mirroring sub-PR 56's 13-field structure.
changelog.schema_candidates_status_observations: TWO observations recorded by sub-PR 57 authoring, NOT promoted as new v20 schema candidates: (I) The cell-vs-edge encoding-divergence pattern (sub-PR 54 cmb-s4 r-bound first instance, sub-PR 57 edge C second instance) is established as a recurring methodological feature of resolves edges: the same physical content can be observed via distinct measurements with distinct natural encoding kinds (cmb-s4 r-bound: cell=dimensionless from primordial-tensor-to-scalar perturbation amplitude, edge=ratio from CMB-B-mode polarization analysis; fcc-WIMP: cell=length cross-section from direct-detection, edge=mass reach from collider production). The pattern is firewall-faithful when each encoding cites its own literature-internal observable. Not a schema change candidate; documentation candidate only — the v19 spec might benefit from explicit acknowledgment that cell-vs-edge encoding-divergence is first-class. (II) The three-edges-to-a-single-carrier pattern (sub-PR 49 gw-ground-network into compact-astro cells; sub-PR 54 cmb-s4 across CMB cells; sub-PR 57 edges A/B/D into hierarchy-problem) demonstrates that experimental programs spanning multiple structurally-distinct probes of the same frontier produce naturally-multi-edge structures. The v19 spec might benefit from explicit acknowledgment that multiple-edges-per-target from a single program is first-class. Not a schema change candidate; documentation candidate only. The v17 if_real_implies implication kind enum {new_cell, new_axis, forced_edge, promotes_subtype, new_FC} expressibility-gap noted in sub-PR 55 and reaffirmed in sub-PR 56 remains an unaddressed v20 schema-extension candidate; not relevant to sub-PR 57 resolves-edge authoring.
changelog.methodological_observations_recorded: TWO methodological observations recorded by sub-PR 57. (I) THE FIREWALL-FAITHFUL OUTCOME FOR SENSITIVITY-KIND COVERAGE IS NOT FORCED CLOSURE. The handoff's anticipation of sub-PR 57 closing resolves-edge sensitivity-kind coverage 7/8 -> 8/8 via an energy_scale anchor was identified as a pattern-completion expectation. Under the maintainer's binding accuracy/truth-to-literature directive, the firewall-faithful authoring outcome is: encode each FCC deliverable per its natural literature-cited kind, regardless of pattern-completion considerations. Outcome: 7/8 sensitivity-kind coverage at Step 4.4 close. This is a first-class methodological observation about how the firewall interacts with pattern-completion-shaped handoff expectations: the firewall prohibits authoring FOR pattern, which includes authoring under handoff-anticipated structural closure when no literature-cited deliverable naturally supports the kind. Future sub-PRs (and methodology updates) should treat this as the canonical example. Step 4.5 or future content-pass work may close energy_scale to 8/8 if a literature-cited deliverable naturally requires it; until then, the kind stays unused. (II) STEP 4.4 CLOSES WITH 38 RESOLVES EDGES distributed across 12 experimental-program nodes and 15 target nodes (11 phenomenon-FC cells + 4 frontier/totality-approach targets). Cross-FC pattern catalog at sub-PR 57 close: 12 bidirectional-anchor-closures + 6 parallel-anchors + 4 discriminating-resolves-clusters + 1 dual-frontier-program + 1 parallel-resolves-edge-cluster + 3 three-edges-to-a-single-carrier patterns + 2 cell-vs-edge encoding-divergence instances = 29 cumulative cross-FC patterns at Step 4.4 close. The dataset is now structurally enriched for the §6.4 flagship find_discriminating_experiments and find_resolvers queries: fcc joins hyper-k/dune/juno as the fourth-PROGRAM-cluster (FCC + HL-LHC) for the hierarchy-problem frontier via PPP-populated edges (A: SM vs composite-Higgs κ_λ; D: SM m_W vs BSM-oblique m_W). After MCP worker rebuild (Phase C Step N+1), find_discriminating_experiments(fcc, ...) queries will surface FCC's PPP-populated discrimination structure across hierarchy-problem candidate-foundational programs.

skip_decisions.skipped_target: Edge to matter-antimatter-asymmetry frontier via FCC-ee flavour CP-violation observables (φ_s, ε_K, sin(2β), etc.)
skip_decisions.rationale: My earlier draft proposed a flavour-CP-violation edge (φ_s precision at FCC-ee with SM-side CKM-fit comparison value). Skipped under the maintainer's binding accuracy/truth-to-literature directive because: (a) the FCC literature's connection to baryogenesis is mostly captured at edge A (EW-baryogenesis triggers visible in λ_HHH deviations); (b) a dedicated flavour-CP-FCC-ee edge would require separate literature pass for the specific φ_s precision target and SM-side CKM-fit comparison value with verified numerical commitments and citations. Adding speculatively without the literature pass would violate the firewall's authoring-from-literature-only norm. Deferred rather than added speculatively; queued for a potential follow-up sub-PR if the FCC-ee flavour-WG literature (Monteil-Wilkinson 2021, etc.) literature pass justifies it with concrete σ(φ_s) precision target and SM-side CKM-fit prediction citation. Firewall-faithful skip.

skip_decisions.skipped_target: Edge to dark-matter-candidates via heavy-neutral-lepton (right-handed Majorana neutrino) search at FCC-ee Z-pole
skip_decisions.rationale: FCC-ee at the Z-pole produces ~5 × 10¹² Z bosons; rare Z decays Z → ν N (with N a heavy right-handed Majorana neutrino) provide sensitivity to N masses in the GeV–80 GeV range with mixing angles |U|² ~ 10⁻¹¹ — relevant to leptogenesis-via-heavy-Majorana-neutrino scenarios and a potential connection to matter-antimatter-asymmetry. Skipped because: (a) the natural target would be a cell in neutrino-sector-phenomenology (HNL-mass cell) which does not currently exist; authoring a new cell + resolves edge is a content-pass decision deferred outside Phase C scope; (b) authoring an FC-level resolves edge to neutrino-sector-phenomenology directly would not adequately capture the mass-range-specificity of the FCC-ee HNL search. Deferred; queued for future content-pass work that authors a dedicated HNL-mass cell in neutrino-sector-phenomenology. Firewall-faithful skip.

skip_decisions.skipped_target: Edge to a separate Higgs-sector cell or EW-precision cell distinct from the hierarchy-problem carrier target
skip_decisions.rationale: Considered targeting m_W precision (edge D) to a specific EW-sector cell (e.g., a 'cell-EW-mW' cell in some EW-sector FC) rather than the hierarchy-problem carrier. Skipped because: (a) no dedicated EW-sector phenomenon-FC currently exists in the dataset; EW content sits in regime-content sm-electroweak (a regime-content node, not an FC) and in cell-level entries scattered across sm-rep-content; (b) m_W's operational relevance is via the SM EW global fit constraint on BSM oblique parameters at the TeV-decade scale — operationally a hierarchy-problem-frontier probe, not a single-cell observable. The carrier-level attachment is firewall-faithful: m_W precision constrains hierarchy-problem candidate-foundational-program structures (SUSY oblique-T from stop loops, composite-Higgs oblique-S from compositeness scale) without needing a dedicated EW-cell. Decision recorded for future-methodology consideration: if a dedicated EW-sector phenomenon-FC is authored in future content passes (with cells for m_W, m_Z, sin²θ_W^eff, α_s(m_Z²), etc.), the m_W edge could be retargeted to that FC's specific m_W cell. Until then, hierarchy-problem carrier target is the natural attachment.

skip_decisions.skipped_target: Edge to dark-matter-candidates via FCC-hh dark-photon-portal search (cell-dm-dark-photon-portal)
skip_decisions.rationale: FCC-hh can probe dark-photon masses in the 10 GeV - 10 TeV range via direct production and decay, complementing the existing low-mass dark-photon program (NA64, HPS, beam-dump). Skipped because: (a) FCC's primary DM science case is the disappearing-track WIMP search (edge C); the dark-photon search is a secondary deliverable with less-detailed FCC-specific literature; (b) authoring a dark-photon-cell resolves edge from fcc would require separate literature pass for the specific (m_A', ε) reach at FCC-hh with verified numerical commitments. Deferred rather than added speculatively. Firewall-faithful skip.

skip_decisions.skipped_target: Edge E (a fifth edge potentially targeting Higgs Yukawa-coupling precision or top-quark mass precision)
skip_decisions.rationale: Considered authoring a fifth edge targeting FCC's other precision deliverables (Higgs Yukawa couplings to bb, cc, gg, ττ at the 0.3-1% level; top-quark mass to ~50 MeV via threshold scan). Skipped because: (a) Higgs Yukawa coupling precision contributes to the hierarchy-problem candidate-foundational-program space at the same conceptual level as edge A (λ_HHH precision) — adding a fifth edge would partially duplicate edge A's structural target without adding methodological distinctiveness; (b) top-quark mass precision is an input to the SM EW global fit prediction of m_W (edge D's PPP cites the post-CDF-II global fit at m_t = 173.34 GeV) — top-mass precision feeds edge D's PPP comparison value rather than being a separate resolves-edge target. Both are operationally captured at edges A and D via the joint-fit / global-fit methodologies. 4 edges (A, B, C, D) is firmly inside the handoff's 4-7 expected range and matches sub-PR 56's count. Firewall-faithful skip.

changelog: v95: Step 4.5 sweep — quantitative_scale on 12 if_real_implies implications across 12 of 14 carriers. Closes the last open Phase C authoring sweep. Entries by carrier: (1) qg-frontier/string-landscape ~10^500 vacua [ratio, log10, lower]; (2) cc-frontier/anthropic Λ ≈ 10^{-122} [ratio, log10, two-sided]; (3) koide-formula/flavor-symmetry Q = 2/3 exact [dimensionless, two-sided]; (4) chpt/holographic-QCD F_π ≈ 92 MeV [energy_scale, MeV, two-sided]; (5) hierarchy/SUSY m_SUSY ≲ 1 TeV [energy_scale, TeV, upper]; (6) hierarchy/composite-Higgs f ≲ 1 TeV [energy_scale, TeV, upper]; (7) strong-cp/PQ-axion f_a 10^8-10^12 GeV [energy_scale, GeV, log10, uncertainty {2,2}]; (8) flavor/Froggatt-Nielsen ε ≈ 0.22 [dimensionless, two-sided]; (9) measurement/GRW r_C ~ 10^{-7} m [length, m, log10, two-sided]; (10) matter-AM/leptogenesis-Majorana M_1 > 10^9 GeV [energy_scale, GeV, log10, lower] on the Dirac-vs-Majorana cell-promotion implication; (11) DM/QCD-axion f_a 10^{11}-10^{12} GeV [energy_scale, GeV, log10, uncertainty {0.5,0.5}]; (12) DM/PBH M 10^{-17}-10^{-12} M_sun [mass, M_sun, log10, uncertainty {2.5,2.5}]. The 12 implications NOT receiving qs are structural-only (no characteristic numerical scale at the implication level) or kind-enum-mismatched (MOND a_0 lacks an admissible kind in v18 enum — deferred). Cumulative quantitative_scale total: 276 (v93/v94) + 12 = 288. Phase C Step 4.5 CLOSES. Phase C Steps 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5 all complete — Predictive Layer authoring is now complete pending consolidated MCP worker rebuild. Validator clean against schema v19.

Rewrites 38 description fields (36 resolves-type edges + 2 dark-matter-carrier if_real_implies implications) to remove four schema-name leak patterns identified by PHYSICIST_FACING_VOCABULARY.md §3 (exclusion_only, predictions_per_program, bound_direction, bare 'resolves edge(s)' plural-noun) where they appeared as primary nouns describing physics, plus the two implication-description leaks (forced_edge, axis_mapping as primary nouns). 89 distinct edits across the 38 fields; 101 target-pattern occurrences eliminated. Yes — the descriptions are user-facing prose that renders in the explorer's resolves-edge rows and implication trees per sub-PRs E2 / E3 (DISCHARGED 2026-05-27); the rewrites remove schema-name leaks that obscure the physics for a working physicist reader. The rewrites do not commission any new structural pattern, do not change any classification axis, and do not alter any cell content. Firewall-clean.

nodes.label: QFT
nodes.full_name: Quantum Field Theory
nodes.description: Established physics architecture with sustaining target gauge-theoretic content with renormalization-group structure. Closure conditions: renormalizability + unitarity + locality. Internal structure includes perturbative QFT (well-established at small couplings) and lattice / non-perturbative methods (extending QFT's reach where perturbation theory fails). Hosted regimes: SM electroweak content, QCD content, Higgs sector, Yukawa content. v1's worked example for the equations layer — articulated in all four mathematical languages with discharge statuses, loose ends, and clusters explicit.
nodes.internal_structure: perturbative QFT + lattice/non-perturbative methods + EFT extensions

equations.description: Yang-Mills gauge-field Lagrangian; the architecture's core articulation of non-abelian gauge content. The kinetic term sustains gauge-theoretic content as the architecture's definitional commitment.

loose_ends.description: The gauge-coupling values $g_1, g_2, g_3$ are external inputs to QFT; the gauge structure determines the Lagrangian's form but not the coupling magnitudes.
citations: GUT literature, e.g., Pati-Salam 1974
Yang-Mills 1954
Peskin-Schroeder Ch. 15-16
Weinberg 'Quantum Theory of Fields' Vol II

equations.description: Path-integral generating functional. The architecture's probabilistic/quantum content articulated as a measure on field configurations. All correlation functions extracted by functional differentiation.

loose_ends.description: The measure $\\mathcal{D}\\phi$ is well-defined perturbatively but the non-perturbative measure requires lattice regularization or other constructive QFT methods. In curved spacetime backgrounds, the measure's extension is partial.
citations: Glimm-Jaffe 1981
Wilson 1974 (lattice)
Feynman 1948
Peskin-Schroeder Ch. 9

equations.description: Renormalization-group flow of couplings under scale change. The architecture's articulation of how content varies across scales — Wilson's RG insight that physics is scale-stratified.

loose_ends.description: The QCD strong-coupling regime where $\\beta(g)$ becomes non-perturbative. Lattice methods provide partial discharge; analytic non-perturbative QCD remains open.
citations: Wilson 1974
Maldacena 1998
Wilson 1979 'Problems in physics with many scales of length' Sci Am 241(2):158-179
Peskin-Schroeder Ch. 12

equations.description: Chiral anomaly; example of how the architecture's quantum content can break classical symmetries. The anomaly equations are central to the SM's consistency (anomaly cancellation requires specific particle content).

loose_ends.description: Anomaly cancellation imposes the SM's specific particle content but doesn't derive it; why SU(3)×SU(2)×U(1) and not another gauge group is open.
citations: Pati-Salam 1974
Adler 1969
Bell-Jackiw 1969

equations.description: Wightman functions / time-ordered correlation functions; the architecture's articulation of empirically-accessible content. All experimental observables are extracted from correlation functions.
citations: Wightman 1956
Streater-Wightman 1964

equations.description: RG flow as information-flow under coarse-graining. The c-theorem (2D) and a-theorem (4D) articulate monotonically-decreasing information content along Wilsonian trajectories — a decisive information-theoretic articulation of QFT's RG structure.

loose_ends.description: The c-theorem in higher dimensions is partial; the proposed a-theorem in 4D was proved (Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011) but extensions to other dimensions and to non-perturbative regimes remain partial.
citations: Zamolodchikov 1986 (c-theorem)
Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011 (a-theorem)
Casini-Huerta 2007 (entanglement-entropy proof)
Zamolodchikov 1986
Apenko 2012
Beny-Osborne 2015

equations.description: Entanglement entropy of QFT ground states with area-law scaling. The information-theoretic measure of QFT's vacuum structure. In higher dimensions, $S_{\\mathrm{ent}} \\sim \\mathrm{Area}(\\partial A) / \\epsilon^{d-2}$.

loose_ends.description: Entanglement entropy in non-perturbative regimes and in curved-spacetime backgrounds (BH information paradox is a special case) remains partially articulated.
citations: Calabrese-Cardy 2004
Ryu-Takayanagi 2006
Vidal 2007
Calabrese-Cardy 2004
Vidal 2007
Apenko 2012
Beny-Osborne 2015
Calabrese-Cardy 2004
Wheeler 1990 (it-from-bit tradition that QFT-info articulations operationalize)

equations.latex: A = A^a_\mu T^a dx^\mu, \;\; F = dA + A \wedge A
equations.description: Yang-Mills gauge field as connection on principal $G$-bundle with curvature $F$. The geometric articulation: gauge fields are connections-on-bundles content, with the gauge group appearing as the bundle's structure group. The geometric language clusters QFT (gauge theory) with GR (Levi-Civita connection on tangent bundle) — both are connections-on-bundles content; the difference is the bundle's structure group.

loose_ends.description: The geometric language reaches QFT's gauge content cleanly but doesn't articulate QFT's quantum content (path integral, operator algebra) at the same level. Reach-termination at the quantum-content boundary.
citations: Kostant 1970
Souriau 1970
Atiyah-Bott 1982
Kobayashi-Nomizu Vol I-II
Baez-Muniain 1994 'Gauge Fields, Knots and Gravity'
Atiyah-Bott 1982
Kobayashi-Nomizu
Baez-Muniain 1994

equations.description: QFT articulates abstractly as a functor from a cobordism category (n-dimensional manifolds with boundaries) to a category of vector spaces, satisfying specific composition and monoidal axioms. At this abstraction QFT is a functor of categories with structure.

loose_ends.description: The Atiyah-Segal articulation captures topological QFT cleanly but full QFT (with metric structure) requires substantial additional articulation. Gestural at v1 because the functor framework is structurally suggestive but doesn't yet derive QFT's actual physical content.
citations: Lurie 2009 (cobordism hypothesis)
Costello 2011
Atiyah 1988
Segal 1988
Lurie 2009
Atiyah 1988
Segal 1988
Lurie 2009
Peskin-Schroeder
Peskin-Schroeder 'An Introduction to Quantum Field Theory' 1995
Weinberg 'The Quantum Theory of Fields' Vols I-III 1995-2000
Schwartz 'Quantum Field Theory and the Standard Model' 2014
Wilson 1979 'Problems in physics with many scales of length' Sci Am 241(2):158-179

nodes.placement_rationale: Placed at stratum 2a (established architecture) because QFT is a mathematically coherent framework with explicit closure conditions (renormalizability + unitarity + locality) that sustains its own regime content. Classified as architecture rather than regime-content because QFT itself is a framework that hosts content (SM electroweak, QCD, Higgs); established rather than candidate-foundational because of ~80 years of community standing, graduate-curriculum status, and substantial empirical discharge.

nodes.label: GR
nodes.full_name: General Relativity
nodes.description: Established physics architecture with sustaining target gravitational content via pseudo-Riemannian geometry. Closure conditions: general covariance + Einstein field equations. Internal structure: classical GR (well-established at macroscopic scales), with extensions to cosmology, black hole physics, gravitational waves. The architecture's organizing principle (general covariance) does not extend to the quantum content sector (QG frontier as cross-architecture-non-sharing).
nodes.closure_condition: general covariance + Einstein field equations + smooth manifold
nodes.internal_structure: classical GR + cosmological extensions + BH physics + gravitational-wave content

equations.description: Einstein field equations. The architecture's central articulation: spacetime curvature ($G_{\\mu\\nu}$) is sourced by stress-energy ($T_{\\mu\\nu}$), with $\\Lambda$ as the cosmological-term. Sustains gravitational content as geometric-curvature content.

loose_ends.description: The cosmological constant $\\Lambda$ value is an external input; its value $\\sim 10^{-122}$ in Planck units is not derived. Cross-architecture reach-termination at the CC frontier.
citations: Weinberg 1989 'The cosmological constant problem' Rev Mod Phys 61:1

loose_ends.description: Singularity behavior in strong-field regimes (BH interior, early universe near Big Bang) is not determined; classical GR predicts singularities, quantum effects expected to resolve them.

candidate_targeting.program: QG approaches (LQG, string theory, causal sets)

citations: Hawking-Penrose 1970 (singularity theorems)
Wald 'General Relativity' 1984
Einstein 1915
Wald 'General Relativity' 1984
Misner-Thorne-Wheeler 'Gravitation' 1973

equations.description: Geodesic equation; the architecture's articulation of how matter responds to curved spacetime (free-fall trajectories are geodesics).
citations: Wald 1984 Ch. 3

equations.description: Conservation of stress-energy in curved spacetime; the architecture's articulation of what the gravitational coupling preserves.
citations: Wald 1984

equations.latex: S_{\mathrm{BH}} = \frac{k_B c^3 A}{4 \hbar G} \;\;\text{(horizon entropy as information density)}
equations.description: Bekenstein-Hawking horizon entropy as the information-theoretic articulation of gravitational degrees-of-freedom: a black hole's structural content is captured by horizon area, with the proportionality constant fixing the gravitational Newton constant. The information-theoretic articulation extends across general horizons (Bousso bound, holographic principle in asymptotically-AdS settings) but full discharge of GR via information-theoretic principles alone remains partial.

loose_ends.description: Extension to non-equilibrium gravity and dynamical horizons (cosmological evolution, gravitational collapse) requires apparatus beyond the static-horizon Clausius relation; full discharge of time-dependent GR content via information principles remains partial.
citations: Padmanabhan 2010 Rep Prog Phys 73:046901
Verlinde 2011 JHEP 04:029

loose_ends.description: The cosmological-constant value is not derived by information-theoretic principles within GR; holographic-principle articulations in de Sitter backgrounds remain open.

candidate_targeting.program: see cc-frontier for full candidate targeting

citations: Bousso 2002 Rev Mod Phys 74:825
Banks 2009 (de Sitter holography)
Bekenstein 1973 Phys Rev D 7:2333
Hawking 1975 Commun Math Phys 43:199

equations.latex: T \, \delta S = \delta Q \;\Longrightarrow\; G_{\mu\nu} = 8\pi G \, T_{\mu\nu} \;\;\text{(Jacobson)}
equations.description: Jacobson 1995: Einstein equations derived from the local Clausius relation applied to Rindler horizons through every spacetime point. The most substantial information-theoretic discharge of GR — Einstein equations as a thermodynamic equation-of-state. Foundational for the program's apparatus.

loose_ends.description: The Jacobson derivation assumes equilibrium thermodynamics at Rindler horizons; extensions to non-equilibrium states (Eling-Guedens-Jacobson 2006) require additional structure and remain partial.

Eling-Guedens-Jacobson 2006 Phys Rev Lett 96:121301

equations.description: Levi-Civita connection on tangent bundle as the unique torsion-free metric-compatible connection. GR's natural articulation in the geometric language. The geometric language's substantial discharge here makes geometric the primary language for GR.
citations: Kobayashi-Nomizu Vol I

equations.description: Riemann curvature tensor; the geometric articulation of gravitational content as curvature. Curvature sources stress-energy via the Einstein equations; geometric articulation makes the gravitational content's geometric character primary.

equations.description: GR's geometric content articulated within the cohesive ∞-topos of smooth ∞-groupoids (Schreiber DCCT). The cohesive modalities (shape, flat, sharp) recover differential structure abstractly; smooth manifolds appear as locally representable sheaves on the site of Cartesian spaces. The articulation reaches connection-on-bundle content (clustering GR with QFT) but discharge of tensorial-Einstein-equation content within this abstract apparatus is gestural at v1.

loose_ends.description: Bridge from synthetic-differential-geometry / cohesive-topos articulation to Einstein equations at the tensor-equation level is partial; the abstract framework recovers smooth-manifold content but does not yet specifically derive the form of Einstein equations within the topos-theoretic apparatus.
citations: Schreiber 'Differential Cohomology in a Cohesive ∞-Topos'
Kock 'Synthetic Differential Geometry' 2006

loose_ends.description: The category-theoretic language clusters GR with QFT (both have connection-on-bundle structure as their geometric organizing principle), but the language does not articulate the QFT-vs-GR distinction that is operationally central (gauge structure group vs. Levi-Civita connection); reach-termination at the architectural-distinction boundary.

nodes.label: QM-foundational
nodes.full_name: Quantum Mechanics (foundational)
nodes.description: Established physics architecture with sustaining target probabilistic / non-classical correlations via Hilbert-space structure. Closure conditions: linearity + unitarity (between measurements) + Born rule (at measurement). The architecture's organizing structure (unitarity) doesn't extend to articulate why measurement events are non-unitary — the measurement problem is the within-architecture frontier here.
nodes.sustaining_target: probabilistic / non-classical correlations via Hilbert-space structure

equations.description: Fubini-Study symplectic / Kähler structure on the projective Hilbert space $\mathbb{P}\mathcal{H}$. QM-foundational's state-space content articulated geometrically: pure states are points in complex-projective Kähler manifold; observables are real functions (expectation values); dynamics is Hamiltonian flow with respect to the Fubini-Study symplectic form. The Berry phase emerges as holonomy of this geometric structure under adiabatic transport.

loose_ends.description: Projective-geometric reformulation handles state space and unitary dynamics substantially but does not articulate the Born-rule probability assignment or measurement-event content within the geometric language; reach-termination at the measurement interface.

equations.description: Geometric quantization (Kostant-Souriau): a functor from symplectic manifolds to Hilbert spaces with self-adjoint observables. Articulates the classical-to-quantum transition geometrically via prequantum line bundles, polarizations, and metaplectic corrections. Partial discharge — handles many specific cases (cotangent bundles, Kähler quantization, coherent states) but does not provide a universal quantization procedure across all symplectic manifolds.

loose_ends.description: The geometric-quantization functor is non-unique (depends on choice of polarization, metaplectic structure) and does not always exist; uniqueness and existence theorems are partial. This is the apparatus boundary at which classical-to-quantum bridge fails across general symplectic manifolds.

nodes.label: Stat mech
nodes.full_name: Statistical Mechanics
nodes.description: Established physics architecture with sustaining target macroscopic thermodynamic content from microscopic structure via probability- measure articulations. Closure conditions: ergodicity (or its replacements) + entropy maximization at given constraints. Internal structure: equilibrium stat mech (substantial discharge), non- equilibrium (partial discharge with multiple competing programs), stochastic dynamics, large-deviation theory. The Jaynesian foundations articulate the architecture information-theoretically; this articulation is substantial enough that information-theoretic becomes the primary unifying language.

loose_ends.description: Information geometry handles equilibrium and near-equilibrium ensembles substantially, but far-from-equilibrium statistical-mechanical content (driven systems, NESS, large deviations) requires additional geometric structure that remains under active development.

nodes.label: Tensor-net / ER
nodes.full_name: Tensor-Network / Entanglement-Renormalization
nodes.description: Candidate-foundational instance within the information-dynamic family. Articulates QFT ground states (and broader many-body quantum systems) via tensor-network ansätze: MERA (Multi-scale Entanglement Renormalization Ansatz) for critical 2D systems; PEPS (Projected Entangled Pair States) for 2D ground states; broader entanglement- renormalization apparatus extending to higher dimensions and to holographic-correspondence-via-tensor-networks (Swingle 2012). Substantial discharge for specific many-body and CFT-adjacent systems via MERA; partial for general QFT; gestural as universal foundation.

hosting_claims.scope_description: Substantial for specific many-body / CFT-adjacent systems (MERA captures critical 2D system ground states with provable properties; PEPS for gapped 2D ground states). Partial for general QFT — extensions to higher dimensions, to non-critical systems, and to dynamics remain partial. Gestural as universal foundation — the tensor-network claim that all QFT ground states have efficient tensor-network representations is partial-to- gestural for general systems.
