predictions.prediction_citation: El-Showk, Paulos, Poland, Rychkov, Simmons-Duffin, Vichi 2012, Phys. Rev. D 86, 025022; Kos, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Vichi 2016, JHEP 08, 036
predictions.confirmation_citation: Hasenbusch 2010, Phys. Rev. B 82, 174433 (Monte Carlo, η = 0.03627(10) i.e. Δ_σ = 0.51813(5))

citations: Kos, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Vichi 2016, JHEP 08, 036 (precision-islands mixed-correlator bootstrap; abstract gives (Δ_σ, Δ_ε, λ_σσε, λ_εεε) = (0.5181489(10), 1.412625(10), 1.0518537(41), 1.532435(19)) for the 3D Ising universality class; Δ_σ is the leading Z₂-odd scaling dimension recorded here; Δ_ε = 1.412625(10) is the leading Z₂-even partner)

Hasenbusch 2010, Phys. Rev. B 82, 174433 (Monte Carlo cross-check on the improved Blume-Capel model giving η = 0.03627(10), equivalently Δ_σ = 0.51813(5), consistent with the bootstrap value within stated errors)

cells.description: The flagship modern-bootstrap result. After El-Showk et al. (2012) identified the kink in the (Δ_σ, Δ_ε) exclusion plot as the 3D Ising point, Kos-Poland-Simmons-Duffin-Vichi (2014, 2016) sharpened the bound by imposing two-correlator crossing on (σσσσ, σσεε, εεεε) plus the assumption of a single relevant Z_2-odd scalar. The resulting 'island' confines (Δ_σ, Δ_ε) to a region of width ~10⁻⁵ in each direction. The values agree with Monte Carlo to six significant figures and exceed Monte Carlo precision for the central charge ratio.

citations: Kos F, Poland D, Simmons-Duffin D, Vichi A 2016, JHEP 03:120 (arXiv:1603.04436) — 'Precision islands in the Ising and O(N) models' (mixed-correlator conformal bootstrap on the 3D Ising universality class; landmark precision island in (Δ_σ, Δ_ε) space with Δ_σ = 0.5181489(10), Δ_ε = 1.412625(10), c_T/c_T^free = 0.9466930(11); source-value: Δ_ε = 1.412625(10) at the 3D Ising bootstrap precision island; T13 cell-direct first-instance on cell-bs-d3-ising via SPLIT-ATTACHMENT — the existing cell-level predictions[0].quantitative_scale records the companion observable Δ_σ = 0.5181489(10) from the same KPSDV2016 result, so cell-direct records Δ_ε for the second load-bearing exponent on the same bootstrap island; per the sub-PR 8/40 split-attachment precedent, both surfaces carry different observables from the same literature anchor).

El-Showk S, Paulos M F, Poland D, Rychkov S, Simmons-Duffin D, Vichi A 2014, J. Stat. Phys. 157:869 (arXiv:1403.4545) — 'Solving the 3d Ising model with the conformal bootstrap II: c-minimization and precise critical exponents' (earlier precision-bootstrap paper that established Δ_σ ≈ 0.518154(15), Δ_ε ≈ 1.41267(13); precursor to the KPSDV2016 mixed-correlator sharpening).

Chang C-H, Dommes V, Erramilli R S, Homrich A, Kravchuk P, Liu A, Mitchell M S, Poland D, Simmons-Duffin D 2024 (arXiv:2411.15300) — 'Bootstrapping the 3d Ising Stress Tensor' (further sharpening of the 3D Ising spectrum via stress-tensor mixed-correlator bootstrap; consistent with the KPSDV2016 island).

Hasenbusch M 2010, Phys. Rev. B 82:174433 — Monte Carlo high-precision determination of 3D Ising universality-class exponents (ν = 0.63002(10), η = 0.03627(10)); independent cross-check consistent with the bootstrap result at the part-per-million level; T11 stat ⊕ syst quadrature not invoked here since KPSDV2016 reports a single (10)-in-the-last-two-digits combined uncertainty (1.412625 ± 0.000010 = ±1.0e-5); BIDIRECTIONAL-ANCHOR-CLOSURE with real-critical-systems cells cell-crit-Ising-liquid-gas-H2O-CO2-Xe and cell-crit-Ising-antiferromagnet-FeF2-MnF2 (sub-PR 11 v53) plus FC-pred[0] ν = 0.63 across the established `specializes` cross-classification edge.

cells.content: Family of islands parametrized by N. Canonical cases: N=2 (XY universality, Δ_φ ≈ 0.51926(4)); N=3 (Heisenberg, Δ_φ ≈ 0.51875(25)); N=4 (Δ_φ ≈ 0.5180(2)); large-N asymptotic to free-scalar dimension Δ_φ = 1/2 + O(1/N)
realized_examples: λ-transition in liquid 4He (O(2) universality)
ferromagnetic phase transition in EuO, EuS, isotropic Heisenberg magnets (O(3))
finite-temperature chiral symmetry restoration in two-flavour QCD (O(4))

predictions.prediction: O(2)/XY anomalous dimension η = 0.03852(64) for the lambda-transition in liquid 4He superfluid
predictions.prediction_citation: Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2020, Phys. Rev. D 101, 116013
predictions.confirmation_citation: Lipa et al. 2003, Phys. Rev. B 68, 174518 (microgravity measurement on the Space Shuttle giving α = -0.0127(3), in 8σ tension with the bootstrap+MC consensus α ≈ -0.0151)
predictions.description: The 'boson puzzle' or 'lambda-transition puzzle': bootstrap (2020) and Monte Carlo (Hasenbusch et al.) agree on α ≈ -0.0151 for the specific-heat exponent, but the space-station measurement gives -0.0127(3). The discrepancy is many sigma and unresolved as of 2026. Either the experiment has uncontrolled systematics or the theoretical universality class is wrong (e.g. gravitational corrections in the experiment break the O(2) symmetry pattern).

citations: Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2020, Phys. Rev. D 101, 116013 (bootstrap value η = 0.03852(64) for the leading anomalous dimension of the 3D O(2) universality class, the value the prediction text records verbatim; the same paper gives the consensus specific-heat exponent α ≈ -0.0151 that sits in 8σ tension with the Lipa et al. 2003 microgravity ⁴He measurement of α = -0.0127(3))

cells.description: Multiple-correlator bootstrap on (φ_i φ_j, S, T) operators where S is the singlet and T the symmetric-traceless rep of O(N). Kos-Poland-Simmons-Duffin-Vichi (2015) localized islands for N=2,3,4. The O(2)/XY case is the 3D bootstrap's most famous unresolved prediction (the lambda-transition tension).

citations: Chester S M, Landry W, Liu J, Poland D, Simmons-Duffin D, Su N, Vichi A 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125:241601 (arXiv:1912.03324) — 'Carving out OPE space and precise O(2) model critical exponents' (cutting-surface algorithm mixed-correlator bootstrap on the 3D O(2) XY universality class; landmark precision island with Δ_φ = 0.51926(32), Δ_S = 1.51136(22) and the canonical 8σ-tension with the Lipa et al. 2003 microgravity 4He λ-transition measurement; source-value: Δ_φ = 0.51926(4) for the N=2 XY universality class — the canonical XY anchor; T13 cell-direct first-instance on cell-bs-d3-ON-vector via SPLIT-ATTACHMENT — the existing cell-level predictions[0].quantitative_scale records η = 0.03852(64) from the same family, so cell-direct records the N=2 Δ_φ as the load-bearing identification of the XY universality class).

Lipa J A, Nissen J A, Stricker D A, Swanson D R, Chui T C P 2003, Phys. Rev. B 68:174518 — 'Specific heat of liquid helium in zero gravity very near the lambda point' (microgravity Space-Shuttle measurement of the 4He superfluid λ-transition giving α = -0.0127(3), corresponding to ν = (3-3α)/3 = 1/(3 - α) ≈ 0.6709(1); the canonical observed-side counterpart in the 8σ-tension; cell-crit-O2-XY-4He-lambda anchor on real-critical-systems sub-PR 11 v53).

Hasenbusch M 2019, Phys. Rev. B 100:224517 — Monte Carlo cross-check on 3D XY giving ν = 0.6717(1), η = 0.0381(2); consistent with the Chester2020 bootstrap result and with the Lipa2003 measurement, sharpening the tension to its current ~5-8σ form; BIDIRECTIONAL-ANCHOR-CLOSURE with cell-crit-O2-XY-4He-lambda (sub-PR 11 v53) which anchors the experimental side at T_λ = 2.17 K (kind=energy_scale via T24); the Chester2020 bootstrap Δ_φ = 0.51926(4) and the Lipa2003-implied Δ_φ_observed differ across the 8σ-tension cell on cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions FC-pred[1] sigma_deviation = 8 (sub-PR 2 v44) and the 5σ-tension on real-critical-systems FC-pred[1] (sub-PR 11 v53); T26 not invoked — Δ_φ is a continuous bootstrap-precision value, not an exact rational.

cells.content: Fermion-scalar CFT with N_f flavors of Majorana fermion and Yukawa coupling. Bootstrap islands for N_f = 1, 2, 4, 8. Yukawa-relevant scalar dimension Δ_φ ≈ 0.76 at N_f = 1.
realized_examples: Quantum critical point of interacting Dirac fermions on the graphene honeycomb (semimetal-to-insulator transition)
Semimetal-to-superconductor transition in surface states of 3D topological insulators

predictions.prediction: Dirac-fermion anomalous dimensions at the chiral Ising / GNY critical point with N_f = 2 (two-flavor monolayer graphene)
predictions.prediction_citation: Iliesiu, Kos, Poland, Pufu, Simmons-Duffin 2018, JHEP 03, 121

cells.description: First systematic bootstrap of a CFT involving fermions in d > 2. Iliesiu et al. (2017, 2018) developed the necessary tensor structures and exclusion-region machinery. Quantum-critical points in 2D Dirac materials provide a target empirical setting; experimental confirmation of the bootstrap predictions is currently inaccessible because of the difficulty of tuning to the critical point.

cells.content: 4D N=4 super-Yang-Mills bootstrap; central charge c continuous (parametrized by gauge group and 't Hooft coupling); stress-tensor four-point function pinned by superconformal Ward identities, with the unprotected sector probed by Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees (2014, 2017).
realized_examples: N=4 SU(N) super-Yang-Mills at finite N (the canonical 4D superconformal theory)
AdS_5 × S^5 string theory dual (Maldacena 1998)

predictions.prediction: Anomalous dimensions of unprotected operators at all 't Hooft couplings interpolate between weak-coupling perturbation theory and strong-coupling AdS-dual predictions; bootstrap-pinned within a narrow window matching integrability predictions in the planar limit
predictions.prediction_citation: Beem, Rastelli, van Rees 2017, JHEP 06, 086
predictions.confirmation_citation: Cross-check with integrability (Beisert-Eden-Staudacher 2007 cusp anomalous dimension) — bootstrap window agrees within stated error

cells.description: The 4D N=4 SYM bootstrap is the canonical superconformal bootstrap case. The protected sector (1/2-BPS operators) is fixed by chiral algebra; the unprotected sector is constrained by crossing on the stress-tensor multiplet four-point function. Predictions match integrability and weak-coupling expansions in their respective regimes.

cells.content: 4D N=2 SCFT bootstrap; chiral algebra protected sector is a 2D vertex operator algebra (Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees 2015); free-field bound c ≥ 11/30 (a-theorem-compatible) and 24c − 12a ≥ 0
realized_examples: Argyres-Douglas SCFTs (rank-1, isolated points on the Coulomb branch)
N=2 SU(2) SYM with N_f = 4 fundamental hypermultiplets (the conformal point on the Seiberg-Witten plane)
Class-S theories of type A_n (Gaiotto 2009)
cells.description: The 2015 result that 4D N=2 SCFTs have a protected 2D chiral algebra sector embedded in their operator content is one of the most consequential exact results from bootstrap-adjacent methods. The 4D bootstrap can be combined with chiral-algebra constraints to bound 4D conformal anomalies (a, c) and Coulomb-branch dimensions.

citations: Beem C, Lemos M, Liendo P, Peelaers W, Rastelli L, van Rees B C 2015, Commun. Math. Phys. 336:1359 (arXiv:1312.5344) — 'Infinite chiral symmetry in four dimensions' (foundational correspondence between 4D N=2 superconformal field theories and 2D chiral algebras; the chiral-algebra dictionary maps c_2d = -12 c_4d, with chiral-algebra unitarity / interactingness constraints translating into lower bounds on c_4d; canonical-rank-1 Argyres-Douglas (A_1, A_2) theory (H_0) saturates the bound at c_4d = 11/30, the smallest c among interacting rank-1 N=2 SCFTs; source-value: c_4d ≥ 11/30 ≈ 0.3667 lower bound on the 4D N=2 conformal central charge).

Liendo P, Ramirez I, Seo J 2016, JHEP 02:019 (arXiv:1511.07449) — 'Stress-tensor OPE in N=2 superconformal theories' (chiral-algebra bound c_4d ≥ 11/30 derived as a function of flavor central charge k, identifying the canonical Kodaira-list rank-1 SCFTs as bound-saturating; T26 reuse: 11/30 = 0.36666... exact rational encoded as decimal; T10 uncertainty=null because 11/30 is a mathematically exact lower bound, not a measured value; T13 cell-direct first-instance with bound_direction=lower per Rule 35).

Argyres P, Lotito M, Lu Y, Martone M 2016, JHEP 02:001 (arXiv:1601.00011) — 'Geometric constraints on the space of N=2 SCFTs II: Construction of special Kähler geometries and RG flows' (Kodaira classification of rank-1 N=2 SCFTs explicitly tabulates H_0 = (A_1, A_2) with (a, c) = (43/120, 11/30); confirms the c = 11/30 saturation value).

cells.content: Gaussian fixed point: Δ_φ = 1, all OPE coefficients given by Wick contractions; central charge c = 1 in units of free-scalar c
realized_examples: Free massless scalar field theory in 4D (upper critical dimension of φ⁴ theory)
cells.description: The Gaussian fixed point is the bootstrap's trivial-but-foundational reference. All bounds on interacting 4D CFTs are stated relative to this point; many bootstrap exclusion regions have the Gaussian point on their boundary.

citations: Polchinski J 1998, 'String Theory Volume I: An Introduction to the Bosonic String' (Cambridge University Press) Ch 2 — canonical textbook treatment of the 4D free scalar / Gaussian conformal fixed point with operator dimension Δ_φ = (d-2)/2 = 1 in d=4 EXACT, all OPE coefficients given by Wick contractions; source-value: Δ_φ = 1 EXACT at the d=4 free-scalar Gaussian fixed point.

Di Francesco P, Mathieu P, Sénéchal D 1997, 'Conformal Field Theory' (Springer Graduate Texts in Contemporary Physics) §4.2 — canonical CFT textbook on free-scalar fixed points in arbitrary dimension with Δ_φ = (d-2)/2; T10 uncertainty=null for the theoretically-exact dimension at the Gaussian fixed point; T26 reuse: (d-2)/2 at d=4 gives 2/2 = 1 EXACT.

Cardy J L 1996, 'Scaling and Renormalization in Statistical Physics' (Cambridge University Press) Ch 5 — RG treatment of the Gaussian fixed point and the free-scalar field theory as the d≥4 upper-critical-dimension limit of the Wilson-Fisher trajectory; the d=4 Gaussian fixed point is the UV endpoint of the WF trajectory anchored on cell-bs-WF-trajectory.

cells.content: structurally-excluded
cells.description: d = 4 is the upper critical dimension of φ⁴ theory; any 4D Z_2-symmetric scalar CFT is the Gaussian fixed point (cell-bs-d4-free-scalar). Interacting Wilson-Fisher fixed points exist only for d < 4. The Ising-Z2 column is therefore structurally-excluded at d = 4 in the sense that no non-Gaussian Ising universality class exists; the cell records this as positive structural content rather than missing data. (Logarithmic corrections at d = 4 itself are present in the lattice model approaching criticality but do not give a non-Gaussian CFT.)

spacetime-dimension.description: Sample points along the Wilson-Fisher trajectory between d = 2 and d = 4; bootstrap data available at d ∈ [2.5, 3.99] in continuous-d sweeps (Henriksson-Stergiou 2022, Cappelli-Maiani 2018, El-Showk-Paulos-Poland-Rychkov-Simmons-Duffin-Vichi 2014). String-encoded values used (rather than integers) to work around the v15.3 schema's `oneOf: [string, number, integer]` constraint on axis_value_ranges.values items, which fails for integer literals because integers match both `number` and `integer` branches simultaneously (and floats with no fractional part are still treated as integers by Draft-2020-12 validators). v16 schema-clarification candidate — change `oneOf` to `anyOf`.

cells.content: Wilson-Fisher trajectory: Δ_σ(d) interpolates between Δ_σ(d=2) = 1/8 and Δ_σ(d=4) = 1 as d varies continuously; bootstrap islands tracked along the trajectory; at d = 4 − ε with ε small, recovers Wilson-Fisher ε-expansion to high orders
cells.description: The Wilson-Fisher fixed-point trajectory between d = 2 and d = 4. The Ising column extends to non-integer dimensions because the bootstrap equations admit analytic continuation in d. The continuous-d bootstrap (Henriksson, Stergiou 2022) traces the trajectory numerically and matches the ε-expansion of Wilson-Fisher near d = 4. Uses axis_value_ranges with kind=enumerated to record the d-points sampled in published bootstrap data; the same trajectory exists for all real d ∈ (2, 4). This cell is the entry's primary exercise of the continuous axis kind via axis_value_ranges.
citations: Wilson, Fisher 1972, Phys. Rev. Lett. 28, 240
El-Showk, Paulos, Poland, Rychkov, Simmons-Duffin, Vichi 2014, J. Stat. Phys. 157, 869 (continuous-d bootstrap of the Ising universality class)
Henriksson, Stergiou 2022 (ε-expansion to continuous-d bootstrap matching)

spacetime-dimension.description: Above the upper critical dimension d_c = 4 of Ising-class theories. String-encoded per the v16 schema workaround.

cells.content: structurally-excluded
cells.description: For d > 4, the Ising universality class collapses to the Gaussian fixed point; no interacting unitary Z_2-symmetric scalar CFT exists at d = 5 or d = 6 (modulo non-unitary fixed points like the 5D cubic theory of Fei-Giombi-Klebanov-Tarnopolsky 2014, which has imaginary anomalous dimensions and is excluded by unitarity). The cells at d = 5, 6 in the Ising-Z2 column therefore record positive structural content: no non-Gaussian unitary Ising-class CFT exists above d = 4. Uses axis_value_ranges to cover both d = 5 and d = 6 in a single cell.

cells.content: 6D (2,0) SCFT bootstrap; central charge c_T fixed by 1/2-BPS multiplet structure; type-A_n: c_T = (n^3 - n)/(...); type-D_n: similar; type-E_6,7,8: exceptional cases
realized_examples: Theory on N coincident M5-branes in M-theory (type A_{N-1})
Type-D_n (2,0) theory from M5-branes on R^5/Z_2 orbifold
Type-E_6, E_7, E_8 (2,0) theories from compactification of (2,0) on Riemann surfaces (Gaiotto class-S)

predictions.prediction: The 6D (2,0) theory has no Lagrangian description but its observables are pinned by the bootstrap to values consistent with M-theory amplitudes at large N
predictions.prediction_citation: Beem, Rastelli, van Rees 2015, Phys. Rev. Lett. 115, 071601
predictions.confirmation_citation: Chester, Pufu, Yacoby 2018 (large-N AdS/CFT cross-check); Lemos-Liendo-Meneghelli-Mitev 2017 (chiral algebra cross-check via W-algebras)

cells.description: The (2,0) SCFT in 6D is one of the highest-dimensional unitary interacting CFTs allowed by Nahm's theorem. It has no known Lagrangian; the bootstrap and the 2D chiral-algebra approach (which for (2,0) gives W-algebras) are the principal non-perturbative tools. The bootstrap matches M-theory predictions for the M5-brane theory at large N.

cells.content: 3D N=8 superconformal bootstrap; central charge c_T(N) for ABJM theory at level k=1, c_T(1) ≈ 21.0/63 in canonical units; bootstrap islands matching ABJM at small N
realized_examples: ABJM theory at Chern-Simons level k=1 (the 3D N=8 maximally-supersymmetric CFT; Aharony-Bergman-Jafferis-Maldacena 2008)
M2-brane worldvolume theory at large N (dual to AdS_4 × S^7)
cells.description: 3D N=8 is the maximal SCFT in d=3 (Nahm). The bootstrap programme (Chester-Lee-Pufu-Yacoby 2014; Agmon-Chester-Pufu 2017) bounds the spectrum and reproduces the ABJM predictions at small N. The cell extends max-SCFT to its 3D incarnation.

spacetime-dimension.description: d > 2. String-encoded per the v16 schema workaround.

cells.content: structurally-excluded
cells.description: The Virasoro algebra is the d=2 conformal algebra; for d ≥ 3 the conformal group is finite-dimensional so(d,2) and minimal models do not exist. The minimal-models-Virasoro column is structurally-excluded outside d = 2. Records the d=2-exceptionalism of the conformal-bootstrap problem as positive structural content.

spacetime-dimension.description: d ≥ 3 where stress-tensor structure constants are finite-dim parametrized. String-encoded per the v16 schema workaround.

cells.content: Universal bounds on central charges and Hofman-Maldacena ratios (a/c in d=4; t_4, t_2 conformal-collider observables in any d≥3); a/c bounded between 1/3 and 31/18 for unitary 4D CFTs (Hofman-Maldacena 2008), with bootstrap narrowing to a/c ≤ 3/2 + corrections (Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011); central-charge lower bounds from stress-tensor crossing
realized_examples: Bounds saturated by free scalar (a/c = 1/3), free Maxwell (a/c = 1/(36/31 · ...)), and various near-extremal theories

predictions.prediction: Hofman-Maldacena conformal-collider bounds on the angular distribution of energy in 4D CFTs
predictions.prediction_citation: Hofman, Maldacena 2008, JHEP 05, 012
predictions.confirmation_citation: Bootstrap-recovered with tighter window in Komargodski-Zhiboedov 2012, JHEP 11, 140 (causality argument); Faller-Sarkar-Verma 2017 (stress-tensor bootstrap)

cells.description: Stress-tensor four-point function bootstrap with no global symmetry assumed. Yields universal constraints on the central charges (a, c in 4D; analogous in other d) and on Hofman-Maldacena conformal-collider observables. These bounds apply to every unitary CFT in the dimension, providing the most universal bootstrap results.

citations: Hofman D M, Maldacena J 2008, JHEP 05:012 (arXiv:0803.1467) — 'Conformal collider physics: Energy and charge correlations' (foundational conformal-collider derivation of bounds on the a/c ratio for 4D unitary CFTs from positivity of energy flux at infinity; explicit bounds 1/3 ≤ a/c ≤ 31/18 for general 4D unitary CFTs with a unique stress tensor, narrowing to 1/2 ≤ a/c ≤ 3/2 for N=1 superconformal theories; source-value: a/c ≤ 31/18 ≈ 1.7222 upper bound on the 4D unitary-CFT central-charge ratio).

Hofman D M, Li D, Meltzer D, Poland D, Rejón-Barrera F 2016, JHEP 06:111 (arXiv:1603.03771) — 'A proof of the conformal collider bounds' (first complete CFT-first-principles proof of the Hofman-Maldacena bounds via bootstrap methods, valid for any unitary parity-preserving CFT in d ≥ 3 with a unique stress tensor; rigorously establishes 31/18 ≥ a/c ≥ 1/3 in 4D unitary CFTs from crossing + unitarity alone; T26 reuse: 31/18 = 1.7222... exact rational encoded as decimal; T10 uncertainty=null because 31/18 is a mathematically exact bound, not a measured value; T13 cell-direct first-instance with bound_direction=upper per Rule 35).

Komargodski Z, Schwimmer A 2011, JHEP 12:099 (arXiv:1107.3987) — 'On renormalization group flows in four dimensions' (proof of the a-theorem in 4D; provides additional narrowing constraints on (a, c) consistent with the Hofman-Maldacena interval).

Erdmenger J, Osborn H 1997, Nucl. Phys. B 483:431 — earlier derivation of the unitarity constraints on (a, c) from positivity of two-point functions of the stress tensor; consistent precursor to the Hofman-Maldacena collider-flux derivation.

cells.content: Generalized free scalar: Δ_φ = (d−2)/2 = 1/2 in 3D; matches the large-N limit of O(N)-vector
realized_examples: 3D free Klein-Gordon field (massless limit of 3D scalar field theory)
Mean-field theory of phase transitions above the upper critical dimension (for d>4 in Ising-class)
cells.description: Free-scalar fixed points exist in every d. In d=3 the Gaussian fixed point is the universality class of mean-field theory above the upper critical dimension; it is the limit point of the O(N)-vector family at N → ∞ (cell-bs-d3-ON-vector).

citations: Polchinski J 1998, 'String Theory Volume I: An Introduction to the Bosonic String' (Cambridge University Press) Ch 2 — canonical textbook treatment of the free scalar / Gaussian conformal fixed point with operator dimension Δ_φ = (d-2)/2 in any dimension d; source-value: Δ_φ = (3-2)/2 = 1/2 EXACT at the d=3 generalized free scalar / large-N limit of O(N).

Di Francesco P, Mathieu P, Sénéchal D 1997, 'Conformal Field Theory' (Springer Graduate Texts in Contemporary Physics) §4.2 — canonical CFT textbook on free-scalar fixed points giving Δ_φ = (d-2)/2 EXACT in every dimension d; T26 reuse: 1/2 = 0.5 exact rational encoded as decimal; T10 uncertainty=null because (d-2)/2 is a mathematically exact dimension at the Gaussian fixed point, not a measured value.

Cardy J L 1996, 'Scaling and Renormalization in Statistical Physics' (Cambridge University Press) Ch 5 — RG treatment of the free-scalar Gaussian fixed point in arbitrary dimension; the d=3 generalized-free-scalar Δ_φ = 1/2 matches the N→∞ asymptotic limit of the cell-bs-d3-ON-vector island at Δ_φ = 1/2 + O(1/N); T13 cell-direct first-instance.

spacetime-dimension.description: All d in the classification range

cells.content: Generalized free scalar in continuous d: Δ_φ(d) = (d−2)/2; Gaussian Wick contractions for all correlators; valid in every d
cells.description: The free-scalar column is populated for all d ∈ [2,6] by the Gaussian fixed point with continuous-d scaling dimension Δ_φ = (d-2)/2. This is the second cell exercising axis_value_ranges with kind='all' on the continuous spacetime-dimension axis (the first being cell-bs-WF-trajectory's enumeration; this is the full-range version). The d=2 (c=1 line) and d=3, d=4 cells are recorded separately for their specific significance; this cell captures the universal d-dependence.

closure_status.description: Partial. The bootstrap framework is established: the crossing equation + unitarity yield rigorous bounds on operator dimensions and OPE coefficients in any (d, global-symmetry) sector. What is partial: (i) only a finite list of (d, CFT-family) cells has been bootstrap-analysed; many sectors (general N in O(N), arbitrary GUT-style global symmetries, generic supersymmetric theories beyond max-SCFT and N=2) remain to be done. (ii) The bootstrap bounds are increasingly tight but not generally proven optimal; the limit of infinitely many crossing equations and infinitely-high-precision functionals is the actual classification, which is not algorithmically attainable. (iii) Non-unitary CFTs (logarithmic, percolation, non-positivity) are not in scope here but are increasingly studied via modified bootstrap programmes. (iv) Multi-correlator bootstraps with more operators are still being developed; the classification is by no means complete-within-domain. The 'partial' status reflects that the framework is closure-grade but the per-cell exclusion-region determinations are an ongoing research programme.

predictive_yield.prediction: 3D Ising critical exponents at sub-percent precision matching Monte Carlo to ≥ 6 significant figures
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: El-Showk, Paulos, Poland, Rychkov, Simmons-Duffin, Vichi 2012, Phys. Rev. D 86, 025022; Kos, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Vichi 2016, JHEP 08, 036
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Hasenbusch 2010, Phys. Rev. B 82, 174433 (Monte Carlo cross-check)

predictive_yield.prediction: 3D XY model (O(2)) lambda-transition specific-heat exponent α; bootstrap+MC consensus α ≈ -0.0151 in 8σ tension with space-station 4He measurement α = -0.0127(3)
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2020, Phys. Rev. D 101, 116013
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Lipa et al. 2003, Phys. Rev. B 68, 174518 (Space Shuttle Microgravity 4He experiment, in tension)
predictive_yield.description: The 3D O(2) bootstrap's most-cited unresolved prediction. As of 2026 the discrepancy remains unexplained; candidate resolutions include experimental systematics (gravitational gradients during finite-temperature 4He measurement) and a subtle universality-class identification failure (e.g. the experimental system sitting near but not exactly on the O(2) fixed point).

citations: Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2020, Phys. Rev. D 101, 116013 (bootstrap+MC consensus α ≈ -0.0151 for the 3D O(2) specific-heat exponent; abstract reports 'sharpening the existing decades-old 8σ discrepancy between theory and experiment')
Lipa et al. 2003, Phys. Rev. B 68, 174518 (Space Shuttle Microgravity ⁴He measurement of the lambda-transition specific heat giving α = -0.0127(3), in 8σ tension with the bootstrap+MC consensus)

predictive_yield.prediction: Hofman-Maldacena conformal-collider bounds (positive-energy condition on angular distribution in CFTs)
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Hofman, Maldacena 2008, JHEP 05, 012
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Komargodski, Zhiboedov 2012, JHEP 11, 140 (causality argument independently confirms); stress-tensor bootstrap recovers the bounds; ANEC proof by Faulkner-Leigh-Parrikar-Wang 2016

predictive_yield.prediction: 6D (2,0) SCFT central charges and stress-tensor sector match large-N M5-brane amplitudes
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Beem, Rastelli, van Rees 2015, Phys. Rev. Lett. 115, 071601
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Chester, Pufu, Yacoby 2018, JHEP 06, 159 (large-N AdS/CFT cross-check)

predictive_yield.prediction: Wilson-Fisher ε-expansion (1972) interpolation between Ising in d = 2 and Gaussian in d = 4 reproduced by continuous-d bootstrap to high orders in ε
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Wilson, Fisher 1972, Phys. Rev. Lett. 28, 240
predictive_yield.description: The Wilson-Fisher prediction itself predates the modern bootstrap by ≈40 years. The bootstrap's role is structural: it recovers the WF result from first principles (conformal invariance + unitarity + crossing), confirming that the ε-expansion describes a real CFT trajectory in continuous d. Retro-explanatory-only reflects that the bootstrap re-derives rather than first-predicts the WF physics.

predictive_yield.prediction: Quantum critical exponents of 3D Dirac semimetals (Gross-Neveu-Yukawa universality class) at the chiral-Ising critical point
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Iliesiu, Kos, Poland, Pufu, Simmons-Duffin 2018, JHEP 03, 121
predictive_yield.description: GNY exponents are bootstrap-pinned to sub-percent precision but no experimental quantum-critical realization has yet achieved comparable precision; the closest target (electronic semimetal-to-insulator transitions in 2D graphene-like systems) is currently far from the critical point. Experimental verification awaits a cleaner critical-point realization.

predictive_yield.prediction: Lower bound on the number of massless flavors required for an interacting IR-fixed point in 4D SU(N_c) gauge theory ('lower edge of the conformal window'); bootstrap-aided analysis gives N_f / N_c ≥ approximately 2.5 (for SU(3), N_f ≥ 8 or so)
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Poland, Stergiou, Vichi 2018, JHEP 11, 070 (bootstrap analysis); Banks, Zaks 1982, Nucl. Phys. B 196, 189 (original perturbative argument)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Lattice studies of SU(3) with N_f = 8, 10, 12 fundamental flavors: Hasenfratz et al. 2014 onwards (multiple groups; some claim 8-flavor IR-conformality, others find chiral symmetry breaking; tension unresolved)
predictive_yield.description: Lattice QCD studies of near-conformal SU(3) with varying flavor count have produced conflicting reports on the lower edge of the conformal window. The bootstrap provides rigorous lower bounds on operator dimensions that constrain the IR CFT if it exists, but the bootstrap does not directly determine whether such a CFT exists for a given (N_c, N_f); inferring the conformal-window edge requires combining bootstrap with lattice or other input.

predictive_yield.prediction: Universal lower bound on the c-anomaly of any 4D unitary CFT (Cardy formula in 4D): c ≥ 1/30 saturated by free scalar
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Cardy 1988, Phys. Lett. B 215, 749 (a-theorem ancestor); Hofman-Maldacena 2008, JHEP 05, 012
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011, JHEP 12, 099 (a-theorem proof for 4D CFTs); bootstrap-recovered as part of central-charge bound

nodes.label: Modular tensor categories
nodes.full_name: Classification of modular tensor categories (anyonic topological order, rational 2D CFT data, Reshetikhin-Turaev TQFTs)
nodes.description: A modular tensor category (MTC) is a unitary braided fusion category whose modular S-matrix is non-degenerate. MTCs classify three apparently distinct objects: (i) the algebraic data of rational 2D conformal field theories (Moore-Seiberg axioms, 1989); (ii) the anyonic content of (2+1)D topologically-ordered ground states (Kitaev, Wen, Levin, Wang); (iii) the input data for Reshetikhin-Turaev TQFTs in 3D (Reshetikhin-Turaev 1991). These three identifications are theorems, not analogies — Moore-Seiberg established the rational-CFT correspondence, the topological-order correspondence is the consensus framing of intrinsic topological order, and the RT-TQFT construction gives the explicit 3D quantum-invariant. The classification proceeds by rank (number of simple objects / anyon types). Rowell-Stong-Wang (2009) gave the complete classification of unitary MTCs up to rank ≤ 5; partial results at higher rank exist (notably the Drinfeld center construction for double of fusion categories) but a complete classification at general rank remains open. The full data of an MTC are: a finite set of simple objects, associative fusion rules with Frobenius reciprocity, F-symbols satisfying the pentagon axiom, R-symbols satisfying the hexagon axiom, and a modular S-matrix that is non-degenerate. Two MTCs are equivalent iff their fusion-and-braiding data agree up to natural isomorphism of categories.
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Anyonic content of (2+1)D topologically-ordered quantum phases; chiral algebra data of rational 2D conformal field theories; algebraic data of 3D Reshetikhin-Turaev topological quantum field theories. Excludes: SPT phases without intrinsic topological order (these are classified by group cohomology / ten-fold-way / cobordism, not MTC); irrational 2D CFTs (continuous Virasoro modules — bootstrap territory).
nodes.organizing_principle: A finite collection of simple objects (anyon types) with associative tensor product (fusion rules), unitary braiding (R-symbols, equivalently a braided structure on the category), and modular S/T transformation matrices satisfying the modular axioms (modular invariance of the partition function on the torus). The S-matrix non-degeneracy distinguishes MTCs (modular = non-degenerate braiding) from more general braided fusion categories. Two anyon types differ iff at least one of (fusion rule, F-symbol, R-symbol, modular S-row) differs.

classification_axes.name: rank
classification_axes.description: Number of simple objects (distinct anyon types), including the vacuum (which is always present as the monoidal unit). Rank-1 has only the vacuum; rank-2 includes Fibonacci, semion; rank-3 includes Ising; rank-4 includes toric code, three-fermion; etc.
classification_axes.citation: Rowell, Stong, Wang 2009, Commun. Math. Phys. 292, 343 (classification at low rank)

classification_axes.name: mtc-family
classification_axes.description: Family label for canonical MTC examples. SU(2)_k denotes the WZW-derived SU(2) level-k family (rank k+1, central charge c = 3k/(k+2)); these are also the ADE-modular-invariant MTCs at the A-series. Toric-code is the canonical Z_2 gauge theory MTC. Three-fermion (3F) is the rank-4 chiral fermionic MTC with c=4. Doubled-Ising is the Z_2 orbifold of Ising × Ising-bar. Drinfeld-center is the construction Z(C) producing a non-chiral MTC from any fusion category C. Exceptional covers MTCs not in the named series (e.g., Haagerup-related).

cells.content: Trivial MTC: only the vacuum object 1. Fusion rules: 1 ⊗ 1 = 1. S-matrix is the 1×1 identity. Central charge c = 0.
realized_examples: Trivial gapped phase (no topological order)
Vec (the category of complex vector spaces, as a trivial braided fusion category)
cells.description: The unique rank-1 MTC. Every fusion category has the trivial MTC as a sub-category. Realized physically as the gapped trivial phase (a product state) and as the boundary of a (2+1)D trivial phase. All other rank-1 cells are structurally-excluded by the axes' design (rank=1 forces only the vacuum family).
citations: Etingof, Gelaki, Nikshych, Ostrik 2015, AMS Tensor Categories monograph

Etingof, Gelaki, Nikshych, Ostrik 2015, AMS 'Tensor Categories' monograph (canonical reference for the trivial rank-1 MTC, the unique fusion category with only the vacuum object 1; the chiral central charge of the trivial MTC is c = 0 by definition since the trivial MTC corresponds to the trivial gapped phase with no edge degrees of freedom; source-value: vacuum MTC c = 0 exact (trivial-by-definition); T13 cell-direct, T26 reuse for trivial-rational, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form definitional value; non-chiral cluster anchor at c = 0 shared with cells [7] toric-code, [9] doubled-Ising, [12] Drinfeld(Fib))

cells.content: Semion MTC: objects {1, s} with s ⊗ s = 1 and braiding eigenvalue i (the semion's exchange phase is i, hence 'half a fermion'). Chiral central charge c = 1 (mod 8 for bosonic structure). Modular S = (1/√2) [[1,1],[1,-1]].
realized_examples: U(1)_2 Chern-Simons theory
Kalmeyer-Laughlin chiral spin liquid (proposed for triangular antiferromagnets)
Boundary of (2+1)D bosonic IQHE-like phase with σ_xy = 2 (in units of e²/h)
cells.description: The simplest non-trivial chiral MTC. Semions are abelian anyons with statistical phase π/2 (half the fermion phase π). The chirality is captured by c = 1 ≠ 0; the semion MTC has no time-reversal-invariant realization. Conjectured but not definitively observed in materials; Kalmeyer-Laughlin's proposed chiral spin liquid is the canonical candidate.
citations: Kalmeyer, Laughlin 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 59, 2095
Wen 1991, Phys. Rev. B 44, 2664 (Z_2 and U(1) Chern-Simons theories)

Kalmeyer, Laughlin 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 59, 2095 'Equivalence of the resonating-valence-bond and fractional quantum Hall states' + Wen 1991, Phys. Rev. B 44, 2664 'Topological orders and Chern-Simons theory in strongly correlated quantum liquid' (the semion MTC, also denoted U(1)_2, is realized as the canonical chiral abelian topological order with chiral central charge c = 1 mod 8 in the bosonic convention; the semion is an abelian anyon with statistical phase π/2 = half the fermion phase; source-value: semion MTC c = 1 exact; T13 cell-direct, T26 reuse for integer-rational encoded as decimal 1, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value of the chiral central charge)

cells.content: Fibonacci MTC: objects {1, τ} with τ ⊗ τ = 1 ⊕ τ (the Fibonacci fusion rule). Quantum dimension d_τ = φ = (1+√5)/2 (the golden ratio). Central charge c = 14/5. Non-abelian — τ is a non-abelian anyon.
realized_examples: (G_2)_1 Chern-Simons theory
Yang-Lee non-unitary CFT (after a unitarization)
Predicted in certain fractional Chern insulators (no confirmed laboratory realization as of 2026)
cells.description: The simplest non-abelian MTC. Fibonacci anyons are universal for topological quantum computation (Freedman-Kitaev-Larsen-Wang 2003): braiding alone generates a dense subgroup of the unitary group on the topological Hilbert space. Searches for Fibonacci anyons in fractional Chern insulators and certain ν = 12/5 FQHE states are ongoing.
citations: Freedman, Larsen, Wang 2002, Commun. Math. Phys. 227, 605 (universal quantum computation by topological methods)
Trebst, Troyer, Wang, Ludwig 2008, Prog. Theor. Phys. Suppl. 176, 384 (review of Fibonacci-anyon computation)

Freedman, Larsen, Wang 2002, Commun. Math. Phys. 227, 605 'A modular functor which is universal for quantum computation' + Trebst, Troyer, Wang, Ludwig 2008, Prog. Theor. Phys. Suppl. 176, 384 (Fibonacci MTC review) (the Fibonacci MTC has unique non-trivial anyon τ with fusion rule τ ⊗ τ = 1 ⊕ τ; the consistency of this fusion algebra in a modular tensor category forces the quantum dimension d_τ to satisfy d_τ² = 1 + d_τ, whose unique positive solution is d_τ = (1+√5)/2 = φ ≈ 1.6180339887 (the golden ratio); source-value: Fibonacci MTC d_τ = φ = (1+√5)/2 exact, encoded as decimal value 1.6180339887; **T27 FIRST-USE MINT: irrational-structural-constant encoding for closed-form irrationals like the golden ratio φ — encode decimal value with closed-form preserved in source-value parenthetical, no new schema field required (lives within v18 schema's additionalProperties:false on qs object, analogous to T26 mint for rationals in sub-PR 10); the minting case here is the Fibonacci/Lee-Yang structural pair where both cells encode golden-ratio-derived quantum dimensions (φ on Fibonacci, -1/φ on Lee-Yang cell [3]) and the unitary-vs-non-unitary discriminator IS the sign of the quantum dimension**; T13 cell-direct, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value of the quantum dimension; **CLOSES cross-FC pattern signal (iii) by pairing with v52 sub-PR 10 fractional-quantum-hall-states pred[4] d_τ = φ = 1.6180339887 — the `edge-xc-fqh-equiv-mtc` categorically-equivalent partial cross-classification edge is now bidirectionally numerically-anchored on the Fibonacci correspondence**; the Fibonacci anyon is the simplest non-abelian anyon and supports universal topological quantum computation via braiding alone (Freedman-Larsen-Wang 2002); the same d_τ = φ also characterizes the (G_2)_1 Chern-Simons theory and the unitarized Lee-Yang non-unitary CFT)

cells.content: Lee-Yang MTC: non-unitary; objects {1, τ_LY} with τ_LY ⊗ τ_LY = 1 ⊕ τ_LY. Same fusion rule as Fibonacci but negative quantum dimension d_{τ_LY} = -1/φ < 0. Central charge c = -22/5.
realized_examples: Lee-Yang edge singularity in dilute Ising at imaginary magnetic field
Minimal model M(2,5) (non-unitary)
cells.description: The canonical non-unitary MTC. Negative quantum dimension excludes physical realization in unitary quantum systems. Important as an algebraic example demonstrating that non-unitary MTCs exist and have the same fusion-rule data as unitary MTCs (Fibonacci) while differing in braiding signs.
citations: Cardy 1985, Phys. Rev. Lett. 54, 1354 (Lee-Yang edge)
Mussardo 1992, Phys. Rep. 218, 215 (review of non-unitary CFT)

Cardy 1985, Phys. Rev. Lett. 54, 1354 'Conformal Invariance and the Yang-Lee Edge Singularity' + Mussardo 1992, Phys. Rep. 218, 215 'Off-critical statistical models: factorized scattering theories and bootstrap program' (the Lee-Yang MTC is the non-unitary modular tensor category extracted from the M(2,5) Virasoro minimal model with central charge c = -22/5; the unique non-trivial primary τ_LY has fusion rule τ_LY ⊗ τ_LY = 1 ⊕ τ_LY identical to Fibonacci's, but the non-unitarity of M(2,5) forces the quantum dimension to take the OTHER solution of d² = 1 + d, namely d_{τ_LY} = (1-√5)/2 = -1/φ ≈ -0.6180339887; the negative sign signals the absence of a positive-definite inner product and precludes physical realization in unitary quantum systems; source-value: Lee-Yang MTC d_{τ_LY} = -1/φ = (1-√5)/2 exact, encoded as decimal value -0.6180339887; **T27 SECOND-USE: irrational-structural-constant encoding, here for the negative golden-ratio-conjugate -1/φ — structural pair with cell [2] Fibonacci d_τ = +φ; the (Fibonacci, Lee-Yang) cell pair forms a bi-anchored 'same-fusion-different-unitarity' couple within MTC analogous to the (Ising, SU(2)_2) 'same-σ-anyon-different-central-charge' couple at rank 3**; T13 cell-direct, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value of the quantum dimension; the canonical realization is the Lee-Yang edge singularity in dilute Ising at imaginary magnetic field (Cardy 1985))

cells.content: Ising MTC: objects {1, σ, ψ} with fusion σ ⊗ σ = 1 ⊕ ψ, σ ⊗ ψ = σ, ψ ⊗ ψ = 1. Quantum dimensions: d_1 = d_ψ = 1, d_σ = √2. Central charge c = 1/2 (mod 8). Non-abelian — σ is a non-abelian anyon (Majorana-like).
realized_examples: Majorana zero modes at vortex cores of (p+ip) superconductors
Boundary of (2+1)D Kitaev's honeycomb model in the gapped non-abelian (B) phase
Predicted (with partial confirmation) in ν=5/2 FQHE Moore-Read Pfaffian state
cells.description: The Ising MTC is the canonical non-abelian Majorana-anyon system. The σ anyon supports non-abelian braiding via the Majorana zero mode it hosts. Braiding alone is NOT universal for quantum computation (gives only the Clifford group); a non-topological gate is needed to achieve universality. Major experimental relevance: Moore-Read Pfaffian in ν=5/2 FQHE (Willett interferometry experiments and Banerjee thermal-Hall measurements give partial evidence; competing PH-Pfaffian state predicts opposite chirality and remains contested).
citations: Moore, Read 1991, Nucl. Phys. B 360, 362 (Pfaffian state for ν=5/2)
Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2 (anyons in an exactly solved model — honeycomb)
Nayak, Simon, Stern, Freedman, Das Sarma 2008, Rev. Mod. Phys. 80, 1083 (review)

Moore, Read 1991, Nucl. Phys. B 360, 362 'Nonabelions in the fractional quantum hall effect' + Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2 'Anyons in an exactly solved model and beyond' arXiv:cond-mat/0506438 Appendix E (the Ising MTC is the canonical non-abelian Majorana-anyon system at rank 3 with primaries {1, σ, ψ}, fusion σ ⊗ σ = 1 ⊕ ψ, and quantum dimensions d_1 = d_ψ = 1, d_σ = √2; the chiral central charge c = 1/2 mod 8 corresponds to a single chiral Majorana fermion at the edge; this c = 1/2 is THE discriminator from cell [5] SU(2)_2 c = 3/2 — both are rank-3 MTCs with a σ-like anyon of quantum dimension √2, distinguished structurally by chiral central charge alone; source-value: Ising MTC c = 1/2 exact; T13 cell-direct, T26 reuse for rational p/q = 1/2 encoded as decimal 0.5 with the 1/2 rational form preserved in this parenthetical, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value of the chiral central charge; pairs with sub-PR 11 real-critical-systems cell[12] 2D Ising η = 1/4 Onsager-exact across the `edge-xc-crit-derives-mtc-partial` cross-FC edge — 2D Ising universality class is now bidirectionally numerically-anchored on the MTC↔real-critical-systems correspondence)

cells.content: SU(2)_2 Chern-Simons MTC: objects labeled by isospin j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1}. Central charge c = 3·2/(2+2) = 3/2. Distinct from Ising despite both being rank-3 with σ-like anyon — SU(2)_2 has different chiral central charge and different F-symbols.
realized_examples: SU(2)_2 WZW model
Effective theory of a chiral spin liquid with SU(2) global symmetry
cells.description: Rank-3 SU(2)_k MTC at k=2. The j=1/2 anyon is non-abelian with quantum dimension √2 (similar to Ising's σ), but the chiral central charge c=3/2 differs from Ising's c=1/2. This pair (Ising, SU(2)_2) is the canonical example showing that rank does not uniquely determine MTC — two distinct unitary MTCs share rank 3 but differ in chiral central charge.
citations: Knizhnik, Zamolodchikov 1984, Nucl. Phys. B 247, 83 (WZW)
Witten 1989, Commun. Math. Phys. 121, 351 (Chern-Simons gauge theory and Jones polynomial)

Knizhnik, Zamolodchikov 1984, Nucl. Phys. B 247, 83 'Current algebra and Wess-Zumino model in two dimensions' + Bakalov, Kirillov 2001, 'Lectures on Tensor Categories and Modular Functor' (AMS) (the SU(2)_k WZW model has chiral central charge c = k·dim(SU(2))/(k+h^∨_SU(2)) = 3k/(k+2); at k=2 this gives c = 3·2/(2+2) = 3/2; the SU(2)_2 MTC has primaries labeled by isospin j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1} with the j=1/2 anyon having quantum dimension d = √2 — the same as Ising's σ — making c = 3/2 the structural discriminator from the rank-3 Ising MTC; source-value: SU(2)_2 c = 3/2 exact via the Knizhnik-Zamolodchikov WZW central-charge formula; T13 cell-direct, T26 reuse for rational p/q = 3/2 encoded as decimal 1.5 with the 3/2 rational form preserved in this parenthetical, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value)

cells.content: Tricritical Ising MTC (within rank-3 unitary classification): central charge c = 7/10. Three primaries with conformal weights (0, 1/10, 6/10) in the unitary M(4,5) minimal model.
realized_examples: Tricritical Ising phase transition in dilute Ising magnet
Boundary of supersymmetric (2+1)D topological superconductor
cells.description: The tricritical Ising MTC is another rank-3 unitary MTC distinct from both Ising and SU(2)_2. Its primaries arise from the M(4,5) Virasoro minimal model. Demonstrates the rich rank-3 unitary MTC zoo classified by Rowell-Stong-Wang.
citations: Friedan, Qiu, Shenker 1984, Phys. Rev. Lett. 52, 1575 (unitary minimal models)

Friedan, Qiu, Shenker 1984, Phys. Rev. Lett. 52, 1575 'Conformal invariance, unitarity, and critical exponents in two dimensions' (the unitary Virasoro minimal models M(m, m+1) have central charge c = 1 - 6/(m(m+1)); at m = 4 this gives c = 1 - 6/20 = 1 - 3/10 = 7/10 — the tricritical Ising minimal model M(4,5) with three primaries 1, σ, σ' of conformal weights (0, 1/10, 6/10); the tricritical Ising MTC is the third rank-3 unitary MTC alongside Ising c=1/2 and SU(2)_2 c=3/2, completing the rank-3 unitary classification at chiral central charges {1/2, 7/10, 3/2}; source-value: tricritical Ising MTC c = 7/10 exact via the M(4,5) Friedan-Qiu-Shenker minimal-model formula c = 1 - 6/(m(m+1)) at m = 4; T13 cell-direct, T26 reuse for rational p/q = 7/10 encoded as decimal 0.7 with the 7/10 rational form preserved in this parenthetical, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value)

cells.content: Toric code MTC (Z_2 gauge theory): objects {1, e, m, ε} where ε = e⊗m. Fusion: e⊗e = m⊗m = ε⊗ε = 1; e⊗m = ε. Quantum dimensions all 1 (abelian). Chiral central charge c = 0 — non-chiral; time-reversal-invariant.
realized_examples: Kitaev's toric code on a square lattice (experimentally realized in superconducting qubits — Google 2023, IBM 2024 — and in neutral-atom arrays — Lukin group 2021)
Z_2 spin liquids (candidate materials: certain pyrochlores; α-RuCl_3 in narrow regime)
Boundary of (2+1)D Z_2 SPT in the bosonic case
cells.description: The toric code is the canonical Z_2 gauge theory MTC, foundational to topological quantum error correction. Its abelian Z_2×Z_2 anyon content provides the simplest topological code with finite code distance. Experimental realization in quantum-computing hardware is the most concrete confirmed prediction of MTC theory: digital simulation has produced anyonic excitations and braiding signatures consistent with toric-code MTC predictions. Spin-liquid candidates in α-RuCl_3 remain contested as of 2026.
citations: Kitaev 2003, Ann. Phys. 303, 2 (fault-tolerant quantum computation by anyons)
Satzinger et al. (Google Quantum AI) 2021, Science 374, 1237 (realizing topologically ordered states on a quantum processor)
Banerjee et al. 2017, Nat. Mater. 17, 4 (Z_2 spin liquid in α-RuCl_3 — contested)

Kitaev 2003, Ann. Phys. 303, 2 'Fault-tolerant quantum computation by anyons' + Levin, Wen 2005, Phys. Rev. B 71, 045110 'String-net condensation: A physical mechanism for topological phases' (the toric code MTC is the Z_2 gauge theory MTC with four abelian anyons {1, e, m, ε = e ⊗ m}; the chiral central charge c = 0 because the toric code is non-chiral and time-reversal-invariant — the e and m anyons enter symmetrically without preferred orientation; this c = 0 is structurally meaningful — it places toric code in the non-chiral cluster alongside cells [0] vacuum c=0, [9] doubled-Ising c=0, [12] Drinfeld(Fib) c=0, where the c = 0 anchor reflects the absence of net chiral edge degrees of freedom; source-value: toric code MTC c = 0 exact (non-chiral by Z_2-gauge-theory-symmetry-between-e-and-m construction); T13 cell-direct, T26 reuse for zero-rational, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value; toric code is the foundational example of topological quantum error correction (Kitaev 2003) and has been experimentally realized in superconducting qubits (Google Quantum AI 2021), neutral atoms (Harvard/MIT 2021), and trapped ions (Quantinuum 2023))

cells.content: Three-fermion (3F) MTC: objects {1, ψ_1, ψ_2, ψ_3} all fermionic (semion-squared exchange phases). Fusion: ψ_i ⊗ ψ_j = ψ_k for i ≠ j ≠ k, ψ_i ⊗ ψ_i = 1. Central charge c = 4 (mod 8). Chiral, no time-reversal-invariant realization.
realized_examples: Predicted boundary of certain (2+1)D fermionic SPT with three-band structure (no confirmed material realization as of 2026)
U(1)_4 × U(1)_{-4} chiral phase with appropriate K-matrix
cells.description: The 3F MTC is the canonical example of a non-abelian-looking rank-4 abelian MTC. All three non-trivial anyons are fermions; their pairwise fusion is non-trivial. Important as a structural example bridging the toric-code (fully abelian non-chiral) and Ising (non-abelian chiral) rank-domains; 3F is abelian chiral.
citations: Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2 (3F in the honeycomb model spectrum)
Lan, Kong, Wen 2017, Phys. Rev. B 95, 235140 (classification of (2+1)D topological orders)

Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2 'Anyons in an exactly solved model and beyond' arXiv:cond-mat/0506438 + Lan, Kong, Wen 2017, Phys. Rev. B 95, 235140 'Classification of (2+1)-dimensional topological orders and SPT orders for bosonic systems' (the three-fermion MTC 3F appears in the honeycomb-model phase diagram of Kitaev 2006 with central charge c = 4 mod 8; 3F has four objects {1, ψ_1, ψ_2, ψ_3} with all three non-trivial anyons fermionic (statistical phase π) and fusion ψ_i ⊗ ψ_j = ψ_k for i ≠ j ≠ k; despite being abelian with all quantum dimensions equal to 1, 3F is the canonical rank-4 chiral abelian MTC with c = 4 — the discriminator from cells [7] toric-code (c=0) and [9] doubled-Ising (c=0) at the same rank 4; source-value: three-fermion MTC c = 4 mod 8 exact; T13 cell-direct, T26 reuse for integer-rational encoded as decimal 4, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value)

cells.content: Doubled Ising MTC: Z(Ising) = Drinfeld center of the Ising fusion category. Rank 9, but at restricted topological sectors reduces to rank 4 in some constructions. Actually doubled-Ising in its canonical (rank-9) form: objects (a, b̄) where a, b ∈ {1, σ, ψ}. Non-chiral: c = 0. Time-reversal-invariant.
realized_examples: Levin-Wen string-net model with Ising fusion data
Boundary of (3+1)D phase with non-abelian intrinsic topological order on the boundary
cells.description: Note: doubled-Ising MTC is rank 9 (3×3 pairs of (anyon, anti-anyon)) in its full form. The cell here represents the doubled-Ising construction at restricted-rank truncations relevant to specific physical realizations. The full Drinfeld center Z(Ising) is the canonical example of a non-chiral (c=0) MTC realizing non-abelian anyon physics. Realized in Levin-Wen string-net constructions; structurally distinct from chiral Ising in that braiding alone is universal for non-Clifford gates in the doubled context.
citations: Levin, Wen 2005, Phys. Rev. B 71, 045110 (string-net condensation)
Kong, Wen 2014, arXiv:1405.5858 (braided fusion categories of topological order)

Levin, Wen 2005, Phys. Rev. B 71, 045110 'String-net condensation: A physical mechanism for topological phases' + Kong, Wen 2014, arXiv:1405.5858 'Braided fusion categories, gravitational anomalies, and the mathematical framework for topological orders in any dimensions' (the doubled-Ising MTC Z(Ising) is the Drinfeld center of the Ising fusion category; the Drinfeld center construction always yields a non-chiral MTC — every chiral central charge of the input fusion category is matched by its negative in the centered MTC — giving c = 0 for any Z(C) construction; in its canonical rank-9 form Z(Ising) has objects (a, b̄) for a, b ∈ {1, σ, ψ}; the cell axis-value 'rank 4' here represents a restricted truncation relevant to specific Levin-Wen string-net constructions; source-value: doubled-Ising MTC c = 0 exact (non-chiral by Drinfeld-center-construction theorem); T13 cell-direct, T26 reuse for zero-rational, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value; non-chiral cluster anchor at c = 0 shared with cells [0] vacuum, [7] toric-code, [12] Drinfeld(Fib))

cells.content: SU(2)_3 Chern-Simons MTC: objects labeled by isospin j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1, 3/2}. Central charge c = 3·3/(3+2) = 9/5. The j=1/2 and j=3/2 anyons are non-abelian; j=1 is abelian (the Z_2 grading). Equivalent to a Fibonacci-extended construction.
realized_examples: Read-Rezayi parafermion states (k=3 case) in FQHE
Proposed at ν = 12/5 FQHE plateau
SU(2)_3 WZW model
cells.description: Rank-4 SU(2)_k MTC at k=3. Contains a Fibonacci sub-MTC (the integer-spin sector {j=0, j=1}). The non-abelian j=3/2 anyon supports universal topological quantum computation (universality inherited from Fibonacci sub-content). Read-Rezayi state at k=3 is the canonical FQHE realization candidate; ν=12/5 plateau is the prime experimental target but evidence as of 2026 is partial.
citations: Read, Rezayi 1999, Phys. Rev. B 59, 8084 (parafermion FQHE states)
Xia et al. 2004, Phys. Rev. Lett. 93, 176809 (observation of ν=12/5 plateau)

Knizhnik, Zamolodchikov 1984, Nucl. Phys. B 247, 83 + Read, Rezayi 1999, Phys. Rev. B 59, 8084 'Beyond paired quantum Hall states: parafermions and incompressible states in the first excited Landau level' (the SU(2)_3 WZW model has chiral central charge c = 3·3/(3+2) = 9/5 via the Knizhnik-Zamolodchikov WZW formula c = 3k/(k+2); the SU(2)_3 MTC has primaries labeled by isospin j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1, 3/2}, contains a Fibonacci sub-MTC (the integer-spin sector {j=0, j=1} is closed under fusion and equivalent to Fibonacci), and is proposed at the ν = 12/5 FQH plateau (Xia et al. 2004 PRL 93:176809) — making SU(2)_3 the rank-4 SU(2)_k step that connects MTC structural classification to FQH plateau phenomenology; source-value: SU(2)_3 c = 9/5 exact via Knizhnik-Zamolodchikov WZW central-charge formula 3k/(k+2) at k=3; T13 cell-direct, T26 reuse for rational p/q = 9/5 encoded as decimal 1.8 with the 9/5 rational form preserved in this parenthetical, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value)

cells.content: SU(2)_4 Chern-Simons MTC: objects labeled by j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1, 3/2, 2}. Central charge c = 3·4/(4+2) = 2.
realized_examples: SU(2)_4 WZW model
Proposed in higher-Landau-level FQHE constructions
cells.description: SU(2)_k MTC at k=4. Contains the doubled-Ising and three-fermion MTCs as truncations. Continues the SU(2)_k family pattern (rank-(k+1), central-charge 3k/(k+2)) into rank 5 and beyond.
citations: Bakalov, Kirillov 2001, Lectures on Tensor Categories and Modular Functor (AMS) — SU(2)_k tables

Knizhnik, Zamolodchikov 1984, Nucl. Phys. B 247, 83 + Bakalov, Kirillov 2001, 'Lectures on Tensor Categories and Modular Functor' (AMS) — SU(2)_k tables (the SU(2)_4 WZW model has chiral central charge c = 3·4/(4+2) = 12/6 = 2 via the Knizhnik-Zamolodchikov WZW formula c = 3k/(k+2); SU(2)_4 contains both doubled-Ising and three-fermion MTCs as truncations and continues the SU(2)_k family pattern of rank-(k+1) at chiral central charge 3k/(k+2); the SU(2)_k family hits exact integer chiral central charges at k=4 (c=2), k=10 (c=5/2 — wait, 30/12 = 5/2 not integer; correct integer-c points are k=4 c=2 and the asymptote c→3 as k→∞); source-value: SU(2)_4 c = 2 exact via Knizhnik-Zamolodchikov WZW central-charge formula 3k/(k+2) at k=4; T13 cell-direct, T26 reuse for integer-rational encoded as decimal 2, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value)

cells.content: Fibonacci × Fibonacci-bar MTC (Drinfeld-center-like construction): rank 4 actually (1, τ, τ̄, τ⊗τ̄). Or alternatively a rank-5 exceptional from doubled SO(3)_3 truncation. Central charge c = 0 (non-chiral, if doubled construction).
realized_examples: Drinfeld center Z(Fib) of Fibonacci fusion category
Levin-Wen string-net with Fibonacci input
cells.description: A canonical doubled-Fibonacci construction: non-chiral MTC of rank 4-5 (depending on construction) used in non-abelian topological code proposals. Important for topological quantum computation: gives universal gate set via braiding alone in the doubled (non-chiral) context, unlike chiral Fibonacci which is universal in the chiral context.
citations: Levin, Wen 2005, Phys. Rev. B 71, 045110 (string-net construction)
Bonderson 2007, PhD thesis Caltech (non-abelian anyons and topological quantum computation)

Levin, Wen 2005, Phys. Rev. B 71, 045110 'String-net condensation: A physical mechanism for topological phases' + Bonderson 2007, PhD thesis Caltech 'Non-Abelian Anyons and Interferometry' (the Drinfeld center Z(Fib) of the Fibonacci fusion category is the canonical doubled-Fibonacci MTC, used in Levin-Wen string-net constructions of non-abelian topological codes; the Drinfeld-center construction always yields a non-chiral MTC, giving c = 0; rank is 4 in the simplest Z(Fib) form with objects {1, τ, τ̄, τ ⊗ τ̄} and 5 in some doubled SO(3)_3 truncations; the c = 0 anchor places Drinfeld(Fib) in the non-chiral cluster alongside cells [0] vacuum, [7] toric-code, [9] doubled-Ising; source-value: Drinfeld(Fib) MTC c = 0 exact (non-chiral by Drinfeld-center-construction theorem); T13 cell-direct, T26 reuse for zero-rational, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value; the Fibonacci input makes Drinfeld(Fib) non-abelian while remaining non-chiral, supporting universal topological quantum computation via braiding in a time-reversal-symmetric architecture)

cells.content: SU(2)_5 Chern-Simons MTC: rank 6, j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1, 3/2, 2, 5/2}. Central charge c = 3·5/(5+2) = 15/7.
realized_examples: SU(2)_5 WZW model
cells.description: Continuation of the SU(2)_k family at k=5. Higher-rank SU(2)_k MTCs have not been the focus of experimental searches but are well-classified mathematically. Notable: SU(2)_k for k → ∞ approaches the irrational free-boson regime, which exits MTC into general bootstrap-allowed CFT territory.
citations: Kac 1990, Infinite-Dimensional Lie Algebras (Cambridge)

Kac 1990, 'Infinite-Dimensional Lie Algebras' (Cambridge University Press) + Knizhnik, Zamolodchikov 1984, Nucl. Phys. B 247, 83 (the SU(2)_5 WZW model has chiral central charge c = 3·5/(5+2) = 15/7 ≈ 2.142857 via the Knizhnik-Zamolodchikov WZW formula c = 3k/(k+2); SU(2)_5 has rank 6 with primaries j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1, 3/2, 2, 5/2}; higher-k SU(2)_k MTCs are well-classified mathematically (the limit k → ∞ approaches c → 3 of the bosonized SU(2) current algebra) but have not been the focus of experimental searches; source-value: SU(2)_5 c = 15/7 exact via Knizhnik-Zamolodchikov WZW central-charge formula 3k/(k+2) at k=5, encoded as decimal 2.142857142857; T13 cell-direct, T26 reuse for rational p/q = 15/7 with the 15/7 rational form preserved in this parenthetical, T10 uncertainty=null for the closed-form algebraic value)

cells.content: structurally-excluded
cells.description: Structurally excluded: rank 1 uniquely fixes mtc-family = vacuum (the only rank-1 MTC is the trivial one). All non-vacuum families require rank ≥ 2.

cells.content: structurally-excluded
cells.description: Structurally excluded: toric code is intrinsically rank-4 (vacuum + e + m + ε). No rank-2 MTC has the toric-code fusion structure.

cells.content: structurally-excluded
cells.description: Structurally excluded: toric-code rank is 4, not 3.

cells.content: structurally-excluded
cells.description: Structurally excluded: three-fermion MTC is intrinsically rank 4 (vacuum + 3 fermions).

closure_status.description: Partial. The classification framework (unitary braided fusion categories with non-degenerate modular S-matrix) is established and rigorously defined. Complete classification at rank ≤ 5 is established (Rowell-Stong-Wang 2009 plus subsequent refinements). At rank ≥ 6 the classification is partial: SU(2)_k and other Lie-group Chern-Simons families are well-understood, the Drinfeld center construction Z(C) gives non-chiral MTCs from any fusion category, but exhaustive lists at general rank are open. Non-unitary MTCs and modular categories over non-trivially-graded contexts (super-MTCs, fermionic MTCs) are partial classifications with active mathematical work. The closure-status `partial` reflects this: well-classified at low rank, framework-complete but list-incomplete at high rank.

predictive_yield.description: Theoretical prediction (Freedman-Larsen-Wang 2002): the braid group representation on the Fibonacci MTC's topological Hilbert space generates a dense subgroup of the unitary group, hence braiding alone universal. No laboratory realization of Fibonacci anyons confirmed as of 2026; searches continue in fractional Chern insulators and certain FQHE states (notably ν=12/5).
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Freedman, Larsen, Wang 2002, Commun. Math. Phys. 227, 605
predictive_yield.prediction: Fibonacci anyons support universal topological quantum computation via braiding alone.

predictive_yield.description: Multiple platforms (superconducting qubits — Google Quantum AI 2021; neutral atoms — Harvard/MIT 2021; trapped ions — Quantinuum 2023) have prepared toric-code ground states and demonstrated anyonic excitations consistent with MTC predictions. The most stringent test — explicit braiding of e and m anyons to confirm mutual statistics — was achieved by Satzinger et al. (Google Quantum AI 2021) and refined in subsequent work.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Kitaev 2003, Ann. Phys. 303, 2
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Satzinger et al. (Google Quantum AI) 2021, Science 374, 1237; Bluvstein et al. (Harvard) 2022, Nature 604, 451; Iqbal et al. (Quantinuum) 2023, Nature 626, 505
predictive_yield.prediction: Toric code Z_2 anyons (e, m, ε) realized in digital quantum simulation with measurable braiding signatures.

predictive_yield.description: Moore-Read 1991 prediction: the ν=5/2 FQHE plateau hosts Ising-MTC anyons (Pfaffian or anti-Pfaffian state). Experimental status 2026: Willett interferometry experiments (2009-2013) and Banerjee thermal-Hall measurements (2018) provide partial evidence consistent with non-abelian Ising statistics. The PH-Pfaffian state (Son 2015) gives opposite chiral central charge and is favored by some thermal-conductance experiments. As of 2026 the correct topological state remains contested; the question is open whether Pfaffian, anti-Pfaffian, or PH-Pfaffian is realized.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Moore, Read 1991, Nucl. Phys. B 360, 362
predictive_yield.prediction: Non-abelian Ising anyons realized in ν=5/2 FQHE with Pfaffian topological order.
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Willett, Pfeiffer, West 2010, Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. 107, 8853 (interferometry evidence); Banerjee et al. 2018, Nature 559, 205 (thermal Hall conductance, half-quantized — partial confirmation, contested by PH-Pfaffian interpretation)

predictive_yield.description: Banerjee et al. (2017) reported half-quantized thermal Hall conductance in α-RuCl_3 under in-plane magnetic field, interpreted as evidence for Kitaev-honeycomb-model-like non-abelian spin liquid. Subsequent re-measurements (e.g., Czajka et al. 2021) showed the half-quantization is not universal across samples and may be phonon-thermal-Hall not topological. As of 2026 the spin-liquid identification in α-RuCl_3 is contested.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2
predictive_yield.prediction: Z_2 spin liquid topological order (toric-code MTC at low temperatures) in α-RuCl_3.
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Banerjee et al. 2017, Nat. Mater. 17, 4 (initial evidence); Czajka et al. 2021, Nat. Phys. 17, 915 (counter-evidence: non-universality, phonon contribution) — observations contested

predictive_yield.description: Read-Rezayi 1999 prediction: the ν=12/5 FQHE plateau hosts SU(2)_3-like MTC. Observed plateau (Xia et al. 2004) but no detailed topological characterization. Universal topological quantum computation accessible via braiding if the prediction holds.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Read, Rezayi 1999, Phys. Rev. B 59, 8084
predictive_yield.prediction: Read-Rezayi parafermion state at ν=12/5 FQHE.

predictive_yield.description: Moore-Seiberg 1989 established the categorical-equivalence between rational 2D CFTs and MTCs. This is structurally a classification theorem about a pre-existing object (rational CFT, established since the 1970s minimal-model era) rather than a prediction of new phenomena. The MTC framework reorganizes and clarifies rational CFT structure but does not predict new rational CFTs to be observed. Retro-explanatory.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Moore, Seiberg 1989, Commun. Math. Phys. 123, 177
predictive_yield.prediction: Moore-Seiberg classification: every rational 2D CFT has the algebraic data of an MTC.

predictive_yield.description: Reshetikhin-Turaev 1991 construction produces a 3D TQFT from any MTC, yielding quantum invariants of 3-manifolds and links (the Jones polynomial generalizes to all MTCs). This is a mathematical confirmation rather than a physical experiment, but it's rigorously established — every MTC produces well-defined 3-manifold and link invariants, computable in polynomial time.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Reshetikhin, Turaev 1991, Invent. Math. 103, 547
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Bakalov, Kirillov 2001 (textbook proof)
predictive_yield.prediction: Reshetikhin-Turaev TQFTs as 3-manifold invariants from MTC data.

Reshetikhin, Turaev 1991, Invent. Math. 103, 547 (RT-TQFT from MTC)
