B 166:196 (foundational kinetic-mixing paper, already cited on this cell; introduces the L ⊃ -(ε/2) F^μν F'_μν term mixing two abelian gauge groups, with ε arising from loop integration over heavy bi-charged matter; the natural loop-induced scale of ε is ε ~ (g g'/16π²) ln(Λ/M) for heavy bi-charged states of mass M with UV cutoff Λ, giving ε ~ 10⁻³ at one loop with O(1) couplings and ε ~ 10⁻⁶ at two-loop / extra-gauge-coupling-suppression, which sets the natural-loop-mixing scale spanning the cell-content window 10⁻³ - 10⁻⁹; encoded value 1e-6 sits at the two-loop-suppressed / α²-natural center of the cell-content range and coincides with the geometric mean of the window edges; T13 direct cells[i].quantitative_scale; T10 uncertainty=null since 1e-6 is the model-internal benchmark center of a parameter window rather than a measurement; bound_direction two-sided since both the 10⁻³ upper edge — set by Holdom-one-loop-natural upper boundary jointly with BaBar/NA64/LHCb visible-dark-photon experimental exclusion — and the 10⁻⁹ lower edge — set by current dedicated-experiment sensitivity floor — are literature-cited boundaries of the same canonical kinetic-mixing-benchmark window; benchmark-selection precedent from sub-PR 30 cell-dm-gravitino-LSP where the canonical-most-cited overlap center of multiple SUSY-mediation scenarios was selected as the qs anchor)

Pospelov 2009 'Secluded U(1) below the weak scale' Phys. Rev. D 80:095002 arXiv:0811.1030 (sub-GeV dark sector with U(1)' kinetic-mixing, already cited on this cell; identifies benchmarks ε ~ 10⁻³ to 10⁻⁴ for the visible-decay regime relevant to the muon g-2 anomaly and various sub-GeV dark sector phenomenology; this upper-end-of-window range is the Holdom-natural one-loop regime that sets the upper edge of the cell-content range; the canonical center 1e-6 sits below this regime and corresponds to multi-loop-suppressed / extra-gauge-coupling-suppressed kinetic-mixing scenarios that are the primary experimental target for invisible-decay channels in B-factory and beam-dump searches; the broader picture: 1e-6 is the central order-of-magnitude where current and proposed dark-sector experiments achieve sensitivity reach for kinetically-mixed dark photons of MeV-GeV mass)

Battaglieri et al. 2017 'US Cosmic Visions: New Ideas in Dark Matter 2017 — Community Report' arXiv:1707.04591 (recent compendium already cited on this cell; the dark-photon section maps the m_{A'} vs ε plane with the kinetically-mixed dark photon benchmark targets running ε ~ 10⁻³ (Holdom-natural one-loop / experimental-frontier-of-visible-decay) down to ε ~ 10⁻⁸ - 10⁻⁹ (sensitivity floor of dedicated proposed experiments DarkLight, HPS phase-II, LDMX, Belle II in invisible-decay channels); the thermal-freeze-in regime at ε ~ 10⁻¹¹ - 10⁻¹² for sub-MeV freeze-in dark-photon-DM is shown as a separate parameter region but lies outside the canonical kinetic-mixing-portal window the cell content names — the cell content range 10⁻³ - 10⁻⁹ explicitly excludes the thermal-freeze-in deep regime and is dedicated to the kinetically-mixed portal scenarios this cell records; recent-compendium / review citation per §4 admissibility test benchmark-of-range convention)

cells.content: Gravitino LSP in SUSY scenarios — TeV-PeV mass, produced from heavy-NLSP late decays
realized_examples: Big-bang-nucleosynthesis (BBN) bounds constrain late gravitino-producing decays — abundant gravitinos produced by NLSP decays during BBN would disrupt light-element abundances. Gravitino-LSP scenarios are restricted to specific NLSP-mass / lifetime windows.
LHC searches for gravitino-LSP signatures (mono-photon + missing energy, displaced vertices) provide complementary bounds in the high-scale-SUSY-mediation parameter space.
cells.description: In SUSY scenarios with gravity / gauge / anomaly mediation, the gravitino can be the lightest superpartner (LSP). Cosmologically, gravitino-LSP-DM is produced from late decays of next-to-lightest-superpartner (NLSP). Status `parameter-space-partly-excluded` — BBN bounds rule out wide regions, but specific mass/lifetime windows remain viable.
citations: Ellis, Nanopoulos, Olive 1984, Phys. Lett. B 147, 99 (gravitino DM)
Steffen 2006, JCAP 09, 001 (gravitino DM review)

Ellis, Nanopoulos, Olive 1984 'Phenomenological implications of cosmological gravitino constraints' Phys. Lett. B 147:99 (foundational gravitino-DM cosmological-framework paper, already cited on this cell; canonical TeV-scale gravitino mass benchmark for gravity-mediated and anomaly-mediated SUSY-breaking gravitino-LSP-DM where m_{3/2} ~ F/M_Pl with F the SUSY-breaking F-term and M_Pl the reduced Planck mass; for F ~ (10^{11} GeV)^2 SUSY-breaking-scale at the GUT scale this gives m_{3/2} ~ 1 TeV; 1 TeV taken as benchmark center of the canonical TeV-PeV mass range that the cell description specifies — gravity-mediated SUSY scenarios with NLSP-decay-induced production cluster around m_{3/2} ~ 100 GeV–10 TeV, anomaly-mediated SUSY scenarios cluster around m_{3/2} ~ 10–1000 TeV, with 1 TeV in the canonically-most-cited overlap region; T13 direct cells[i].quantitative_scale; T5 uncertainty=null since 1 TeV is the model-internal benchmark of a parameter range, not a measurement)

Steffen 2006 'Gravitino dark matter and cosmological constraints' JCAP 09:001 arXiv:hep-ph/0605306 (gravitino-DM cosmology review compendium already cited on this cell; BBN bounds + thermal-relic + NLSP-decay-induced production restrict m_{3/2} to specific mass-lifetime windows; canonical TeV-scale gravitino with charged-slepton NLSP and high-scale SUSY-breaking-mediation is treated as the central benchmark scenario)

Bolz, Brandenburg, Buchmuller 2001 'Thermal Production of Gravitinos' Nucl. Phys. B 606:518 arXiv:hep-ph/0012052 (thermal-relic gravitino abundance calculation: Ω_{3/2} h^2 ≈ 0.27 × (T_R / 10^{10} GeV) × (100 GeV / m_{3/2}) × (m_{gluino} / 1 TeV)^2 — TeV-scale gravitino-as-100%-DM benchmark requires reheating temperature T_R ~ 10^{10} GeV with TeV-scale gluino mass; consistent with anomaly-mediated SUSY-breaking gauge-mediation benchmark)

Ibe, Kitano, Murayama 2009 'A viable supersymmetric model with UV insensitive anomaly mediation' Phys. Rev. D 79:115016 arXiv:0903.2275 (anomaly-mediated SUSY-breaking gravitino: canonical mass m_{3/2} ~ 10–1000 TeV with squark/slepton/gaugino spectra following anomaly-mediation pattern; the TeV-PeV mass range the cell description names is centered in this anomaly-mediation regime)

cells.content: MACHOs as primary halo DM — brown dwarfs, faint stars, planetary-mass compact objects — falsified by microlensing surveys
realized_examples: EROS-2 (2007) and OGLE-IV microlensing surveys bound f_MACHO < 0.08 (8% of halo DM) across the planetary-to-stellar mass range. Combined with BBN bound that total baryonic mass is far less than Ω_DM, MACHOs cannot constitute the bulk of dark matter.

Distinguishes MACHO-baryonic-compact from primordial-black-hole-compact: PBHs are non-baryonic (formed from gravitational collapse of cosmological density fluctuations) and not subject to the BBN constraint on baryonic matter.
cells.description: Pre-1990s MACHO hypothesis: DM is mostly faint baryonic compact objects (brown dwarfs, planets, faint stars) in galactic halos. EROS-2 and OGLE microlensing surveys decisively ruled out as a major DM fraction. Distinct from PBH cells because MACHOs are baryonic; the BBN bound on baryons constrains MACHOs but not PBHs (which formed from radiation-era gravitational collapse).
citations: Tisserand et al. (EROS-2) 2007, Astron. Astrophys. 469, 387
Wyrzykowski et al. (OGLE-IV) 2011, Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 416, 2949

Tisserand et al. (EROS-2 Collaboration) 2007 'Limits on the Macho content of the Galactic Halo from the EROS-2 Survey of the Magellanic Clouds' Astron. Astrophys. 469:387 arXiv:astro-ph/0607207 (EROS-2 LMC/SMC microlensing: f_MACHO < 0.08 in MACHO mass range 6 × 10⁻⁸ M_sun to 15 M_sun at 95% CL; combined with BBN baryon-density bound MACHOs definitively cannot constitute major DM fraction; T4 upper bound, T5 uncertainty=null, T10 falsified-context uncertainty=null, T13 direct cells[i].quantitative_scale; distinct from PBH cells in that MACHOs are baryonic while PBHs formed from radiation-era gravitational collapse and are not baryon-budget-counted)
Wyrzykowski et al. (OGLE-IV) 2011, Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 416:2949 (OGLE-IV complementary constraint at smaller radii within the same MACHO mass range)

cells.content: DAMA/LIBRA annual modulation interpretation as WIMP signal — FALSIFIED by XENON/LZ/PandaX null results
realized_examples: DAMA/NaI (1996-2002), DAMA/LIBRA (2003-2024) reported ~12σ annual modulation in NaI(Tl) scintillator detector, consistent with expected WIMP-Earth-velocity-modulation signal. The interpreted parameter space (m_WIMP ~ 10 GeV, σ_SI ~ 10⁻⁴⁰ cm²) was excluded by XENON1T, XENONnT, LZ at multiple orders of magnitude below the implied cross-section. ANAIS-112 (2024) and COSINE-100 results using NaI(Tl) target have not reproduced the modulation signal.
cells.description: Long-running specific-claim with clean falsification of the WIMP interpretation. The annual modulation itself is observed at high significance; the WIMP interpretation is excluded by other experiments at compatible WIMP parameter space. The modulation might be due to systematics (seasonal cosmic-ray variations, muon contamination) or some other non-WIMP physics. Recorded as `falsified-specific-claim` for the WIMP-interpretation only; the modulation signal itself remains an open puzzle.
citations: Bernabei et al. (DAMA/LIBRA) 2018, Universe 4, 116 (latest DAMA result)
Aprile et al. (XENON1T) 2018, Phys. Rev. Lett. 121, 111302 (DAMA exclusion)
Adhikari et al. (ANAIS-112) 2024, arXiv:2404.17348 (no modulation in compatible parameter space)

Bernabei et al. (DAMA/LIBRA Collaboration) 2018 'First model independent results from DAMA/LIBRA-phase2' Universe 4:116 arXiv:1805.10486 (DAMA WIMP-interpretation benchmark fit: m_WIMP ~ 10 GeV with σ_SI ~ 10⁻⁴⁰ cm² for SI elastic scattering on NaI; specific parameter region depends on velocity-distribution / channeling assumptions, with light-WIMP ~10 GeV and heavy-WIMP ~50 GeV interpretations both ruled out by XENON1T / XENONnT / LZ / ANAIS-112; T8 pre-falsification-window m_WIMP, T10 falsified-interpretation uncertainty=null, T13 direct cells[i].quantitative_scale; the 12.9σ modulation significance is encoded separately at FC-level predictive_yield[1].quantitative_scale per the split-attachment maneuver this sub-PR introduces — first sub-PR to use both cell-level (T13) and FC-prediction-level (in established use since sub-PR 1 hadronic-states) qs attachment surfaces on the same observation-pair; distinct from T7 sub-PR 4 within-attachment-point convention)
Aprile et al. (XENON1T Collaboration) 2018, Phys. Rev. Lett. 121:111302 (XENON1T DAMA exclusion in the relevant parameter region)
Adhikari et al. (ANAIS-112 Collaboration) 2024, arXiv:2404.17348 (ANAIS-112 NaI-target null result on annual modulation at compatible parameter space; six-year exposure)
Kang, Scopel, Tomar, Yoon 2019 'DAMA/LIBRA-phase2 in WIMP effective models' arXiv:1804.07528 (model-effective theory fits documenting the 10-50 GeV ambiguity of DAMA WIMP-interpretation)

cells.content: CDMS-II silicon detector 2010 3-event excess — FALSIFIED by subsequent SuperCDMS exclusions
realized_examples: CDMS-II 2010 reported 3 candidate events vs ~1 expected background in silicon detectors, interpreted as a hint of low-mass WIMP at m_WIMP ~ 8 GeV. Subsequent CDMS-Lite (2014) and SuperCDMS (2018+) silicon-detector exclusions ruled out the parameter space implied by the 3-event hint.
cells.description: Small-statistics positive-detection hint (3 events vs ~1 expected) cleanly excluded by subsequent CDMS-collaboration follow-ups. Less prominent than DAMA/LIBRA but exemplary of how low-significance hints get excluded by increased exposure.
citations: Agnese et al. (CDMS-II) 2013, Phys. Rev. Lett. 111, 251301 (3-event report)
Agnese et al. (SuperCDMS) 2018, Phys. Rev. D 97, 022002 (exclusion)

Agnese et al. (CDMS-II Collaboration) 2013 'Silicon detector dark matter results from the final exposure of CDMS II' Phys. Rev. Lett. 111:251301 arXiv:1304.4279 (CDMS-II silicon 3-event excess: best-fit low-mass WIMP interpretation gave m_WIMP ≈ 8.6 GeV with σ_SI ~ 2 × 10⁻⁴¹ cm² assuming standard halo and SI elastic scattering on Si; 3 events observed vs ~1 background expected; T8 pre-falsification interpretation window, T10 falsified-interpretation uncertainty=null, T13 direct cells[i].quantitative_scale; encoded as 8 GeV per cell description rounding; the 8.6 GeV value in the paper is the maximum-likelihood best-fit)

Agnese et al. (SuperCDMS Collaboration) 2018, Phys. Rev. D 97:022002 (SuperCDMS Soudan + SuperCDMS at SNOLAB later exclusion: ruled out the implied (m_WIMP, σ_SI) region; exemplary of low-significance positive-hint exclusion by increased exposure)

cells.content: String-axiverse — multitude of ALPs predicted by string compactifications across decades of mass scales
realized_examples: No specific axiverse-ALP has been detected. Theoretical motivation: string compactifications give large numbers of axion-like pseudoscalars with masses log-distributed across many orders of magnitude (Arvanitaki et al. 2010). Some specific candidates: heavy ALPs from Calabi-Yau compactifications, ultra-light axions from string moduli, fuzzy-DM-like axions.
cells.description: Predicted-only cell. String-theoretic compactifications generically predict many ALPs distributed across mass scales — the 'axiverse.' Specific candidates would populate cells in this FC and the QCD-axion / ALP cells. The axiverse prediction is not yet tested at the level of finding a string-theoretic axion; current limits constrain regions of the predicted parameter space but the full axiverse remains a research target.
citations: Arvanitaki, Dimopoulos, Dubovsky, Kaloper, March-Russell 2010, Phys. Rev. D 81, 123530 (string axiverse)
Hui, Ostriker, Tremaine, Witten 2017, Phys. Rev. D 95, 043541 (axiverse cosmology)

A. Arvanitaki, S. Dimopoulos, S. Dubovsky, N. Kaloper, J. March-Russell 2010, Phys. Rev. D 81:123530 (arXiv:0905.4720) — 'String axiverse', foundational prediction of a logarithmically-distributed spectrum of ALPs spanning roughly 10⁻³³ eV (Hubble scale, where m_a < H makes the axion frozen and effectively cosmological-constant-like) to ~10⁰ eV (upper end of viable QCD-axion mass), produced from compactification moduli in generic string vacua. (T28 multi-prediction-envelope encoding: value = log10 midpoint of literature window = -16.5; uncertainty = half-width = 16.5 in log10 space, recovering the 10^-33 eV to 10^0 eV range when expanded.)

P. Svrcek, E. Witten 2006, JHEP 06:051 (arXiv:hep-th/0605206) — 'Axions in string theory', source for the decay-constant range f_a ~ 10¹⁶–10¹⁸ GeV in string-theoretic compactifications and the natural logarithmic spread of axion masses across the axiverse window.

M. J. Stott, D. J. E. Marsh 2018, Phys. Rev. D 98:083006 (arXiv:1805.02016) — axiverse phenomenology including Type IIB and M-theory compactifications producing dozens-to-hundreds of ALPs distributed across the [Hubble-scale, eV] mass window with characteristic logarithmic density.
PDG 2024 (S. Navas et al., Phys. Rev. D 110:030001), 'Axions and other very light bosons' review — section on string-axiverse and ultralight-ALP cosmology.

cells.content: Supermassive PBHs as seeds for galactic-center SMBHs (cross-reference to compact-astro SMBH cells)
realized_examples: High-redshift SMBH formation (z > 6 quasars with M_BH > 10⁹ M_sun observed by JWST 2023) is challenging for stellar-collapse seed scenarios. PBH-seeded SMBH growth from massive primordial seeds (M ~ 10⁵-10⁶ M_sun) is one of several proposed solutions.
cells.description: Cross-reference cell to compact-astrophysical-objects cell-compact-SMBH-galactic-center and SMBH-AGN cells. From the DM perspective, supermassive-mass-window PBHs are not DM candidates per se (the abundance is too small for major Ω_DM contribution) but they connect to PBH formation physics. Status `viable-targeted` for the seed-formation hypothesis.
citations: Carr, Silk 2018, Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 478, 3756 (PBH-SMBH connection)
Larson et al. (JWST CEERS) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 953, L29 (high-z SMBH observation)

Carr, Silk 2018 'Primordial Black Holes as Generators of Cosmic Structures' Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 478:3756 arXiv:1801.00672 (foundational PBH-as-SMBH-seed paper, already cited on this cell; the 'seed' effect section argues that if PBHs have a similar mass and make a small contribution to dark matter the seed effect dominates on small scales, in which case PBHs could generate the supermassive black holes in galactic nuclei; the canonical 10^5-10^6 M_sun seed-mass window for growth to M_BH ~ 10^9 M_sun by z ~ 6-7 under Eddington-limited accretion over ~700 Myr is the central benchmark of the paper's seed-effect analysis; encoded value 1e5 M_sun is the lower edge of this canonical window, the minimum seed mass quoted as required to solve the early-SMBH problem; T13 direct cells[i].quantitative_scale; T10 uncertainty=null since 1e5 M_sun is the model-internal benchmark of a window-bounded range, not a measurement; bound_direction two-sided since both the 1e5 lower edge and the 1e6 upper edge are literature-cited boundaries of the same canonical seed-mass window)

Larson, Finkelstein, Kocevski et al. (JWST CEERS) 2023 'A CEERS Discovery of an Accreting Supermassive Black Hole 570 Myr after the Big Bang: Identifying a Progenitor of Massive z > 6 Quasars' Astrophys. J. Lett. 953:L29 arXiv:2303.08918 doi:10.3847/2041-8213/ace619 (observational motivation for the massive-seed scenario: discovery of an accreting SMBH at z=8.679 with M_BH ~ 10^7 M_sun in CEERS_1019 570 Myr after the Big Bang challenges stellar-collapse seed scenarios since Pop-III remnants at ~10^2 M_sun cannot reach 10^7 M_sun by z=8.679 under Eddington-limited growth; massive primordial seeds in the 10^5-10^6 M_sun window solve the timing problem and are the most directly-cited motivator for the PBH-seed scenario this cell records; note: this cell's existing citations field attributes the paper to JWST UNCOVER, which is incorrect — Larson et al. 2023 ApJL 953:L29 is the CEERS paper [CEERS=Cosmic Evolution Early Release Science], while UNCOVER is a separate JWST survey; the discrepancy is flagged for a future docs-only cleanup PR and the corrected attribution is used here per the disposition principle 'true to the literature, accurate')

Carr, Kühnel 2020 'Primordial Black Holes as Dark Matter: Recent Developments' Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 70:355 arXiv:2006.02838 (PBH-as-DM review compendium across mass scales, already cited on the companion cell cell-dm-PBH-asteroid-mass-viable; the section on 'cosmic structure formation' summarizes the seed-effect and Poisson-effect mechanisms and reports that PBHs larger than ~10^3 M_sun can generate cosmological structures, with the 10^5-10^6 M_sun window being the most-cited benchmark for galactic-nucleus SMBH seeds; recent-compendium / review citation per the §4 admissibility test's benchmark-of-range convention requiring at minimum foundational + recent review)

predictive_yield.prediction: Canonical WIMP miracle: thermal-relic GeV-TeV particle with weak-scale annihilation cross-section gives Ω_DM ≈ 0.27 naturally and is detectable by direct-detection experiments
predictive_yield.description: Lee-Weinberg 1977 established the WIMP-miracle relic-abundance calculation. SUSY-LSP and analogous candidates motivated three decades of WIMP-search programs. LZ 2024 (Aalbers et al. Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 041002) and XENONnT 2023 placed σ_SI bounds orders of magnitude below typical WIMP-miracle predictions. Canonical SUSY-neutralino-LSP parameter space is essentially excluded; non-canonical heavy-WIMP variants remain viable. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` rather than falsified because (a) heavy-WIMP / non-thermal-WIMP scenarios remain alive, (b) the community uses 'constrained' or 'largely excluded' rather than falsified, (c) the WIMP-miracle as a class prediction is weakened but specific WIMP subclasses survive.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Lee, Weinberg 1977, Phys. Rev. Lett. 39, 165
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Aalbers et al. (LZ) 2024, Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 041002 (most stringent constraint)

predictive_yield.prediction: DAMA/LIBRA NaI(Tl) annual modulation is a WIMP signal
predictive_yield.description: Bernabei et al. 1996-2024 observe ~12σ annual modulation in NaI(Tl) detector at DAMA/LNGS. Interpreted as WIMP-Earth-velocity modulation with m_WIMP ~ 10 GeV, σ_SI ~ 10⁻⁴⁰ cm². XENON1T (2018), XENONnT (2023), and LZ (2024) all excluded the parameter space orders of magnitude below the DAMA-implied cross-section. ANAIS-112 (2024) and COSINE-100, using NaI(Tl) targets similar to DAMA, have not reproduced the modulation in WIMP-compatible parameter space. The WIMP interpretation is falsified; the modulation itself remains an open puzzle (possible systematics: cosmic-ray seasonal variation, muon contamination, environmental).
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Bernabei et al. (DAMA/NaI / LIBRA) 1996-present
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Aprile et al. (XENON1T) 2018, Phys. Rev. Lett. 121, 111302; Adhikari et al. (ANAIS-112) 2024, arXiv:2404.17348 (NaI-target null result)

citations: Bernabei et al. (DAMA/LIBRA Collaboration) 2018 'First model independent results from DAMA/LIBRA-phase2' Universe 4:116 arXiv:1805.10486 (DAMA/LIBRA-phase2 annual-modulation full-exposure significance: 12.9σ over 2.46 ton·yr in 2-6 keV combining DAMA/NaI + LIBRA-phase1 + LIBRA-phase2; modulation amplitude 0.0103 ± 0.0008 cpd/kg/keV, phase 145 ± 5 days well-matched to expected June-2 maximum, period 0.999 ± 0.001 yr; DAMA/LIBRA-phase2 alone achieves 9.5σ; the modulation observable itself is established at high significance even though the WIMP-interpretation is falsified by null results at compatible parameter space from XENON1T / XENONnT / LZ / ANAIS-112; T13 — first FC-prediction-level qs in this sub-PR; T5 uncertainty=null since the significance is the integrated-likelihood test statistic, not a measurement with associated ± error bars; split-attachment maneuver: 12.9σ modulation significance attaches to the FC-level predictive_yield[1] entry (the DAMA-modulation prediction surface, predictive_yield[i].quantitative_scale), while the falsified WIMP-interpretation m_WIMP ~ 10 GeV attaches separately to cell-dm-DAMA-LIBRA-falsified (the cell-direct surface, cells[i].quantitative_scale, T13) — same observation, two attachment points, first sub-PR to use both surfaces on a single observation-pair; distinct from T7 (sub-PR 4 cells[i].predictions[j] within-attachment-point choice convention for cells with internal predictions[] arrays))
Bernabei et al. (DAMA/LIBRA) 1996-2024 successive Phys. Lett. B and Nucl. Phys. publications spanning 14 + 6 = 20 annual cycles of operation at Gran Sasso (long-baseline modulation record)

predictive_yield.prediction: MACHOs (faint baryonic compact objects) constitute the bulk of galactic-halo dark matter
predictive_yield.description: Pre-1990s MACHO hypothesis. EROS-2 (Tisserand et al. 2007) and OGLE-IV (Wyrzykowski et al. 2011) microlensing surveys bound f_MACHO < 0.08 of halo DM in the planetary-to-stellar mass range. Combined with BBN bound on baryonic matter, MACHOs cannot be the bulk of DM. Type-A falsified prediction (a specific BSM-or-near-BSM hypothesis ruled out).
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Paczynski 1986, Astrophys. J. 304, 1 (MACHO proposal)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Tisserand et al. (EROS-2) 2007, Astron. Astrophys. 469, 387

predictive_yield.prediction: QCD axion in DFSZ / KSVZ band with m_a ~ μeV-meV and g_aγγ ~ 10⁻¹⁵ GeV⁻¹ is the dark matter
predictive_yield.description: Theoretical prediction from Peccei-Quinn 1977 + Weinberg 1978 + Wilczek 1978 plus misalignment cosmology gives axion-DM with mass linked to PQ-breaking scale. ADMX-G2 has excluded m_a in 2.66-4.2 μeV at DFSZ sensitivity (Bartram et al. 2021); the full cosmologically-allowed window (m_a from ~ neV to ~ meV) requires years more of haloscope scanning. Status `unconfirmed-not-yet-tested` — the prediction is well-defined and the experiment is in progress.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Preskill, Wise, Wilczek 1983, Phys. Lett. B 120, 127 (misalignment cosmology); Abbott, Sikivie 1983

predictive_yield.prediction: Sterile neutrino at m_s ~ 7 keV decays to γ + ν producing a detectable X-ray line in galaxy clusters
predictive_yield.description: Bulbul et al. 2014 and Boyarsky et al. 2014 reported a ~3.5 keV unidentified X-ray line in stacked observations of galaxy clusters, interpretable as decaying 7 keV sterile-ν dark matter. Subsequent observations (Dessert-Rodd-Safdi 2020 Science 367, 1465; Foster et al. 2020) significantly weakened the line claim using deeper observations and improved background modeling. The sterile-ν-DM interpretation is not falsified but the specific 3.5 keV line claim is substantially weakened.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Dodelson, Widrow 1994, Phys. Rev. Lett. 72, 17 (sterile-ν DM); Bulbul et al. 2014 (3.5 keV line)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Dessert, Rodd, Safdi 2020, Science 367, 1465 (weakening of 3.5 keV line)

predictive_yield.prediction: PBHs in the LIGO mass window (10-100 M_sun) constitute the bulk of dark matter
predictive_yield.description: v21 falsified prediction (cell-compact-PBH-LIGO-window-excluded). LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA binary-merger rates incompatible with PBH-as-100%-DM in this mass window — implied PBH merger rates exceed observed rates by orders of magnitude. Hütsi-Raidal-Vaskonen 2021 derived f_PBH ≲ 10⁻³ in LIGO window. Cross-reference: same falsified prediction recorded in compact-astrophysical-objects v21; here it appears as falsified prediction for `excluded-as-major-fraction` cell.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Carr, Kühnel, Sandstad 2016, Phys. Rev. D 94, 083504 (PBH-DM in LIGO window)
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Hütsi, Raidal, Vaskonen 2021, Phys. Rev. D 103, 063510

predictive_yield.prediction: CDMS-II silicon-detector 2010 3-event excess is a WIMP signal at m_WIMP ~ 8 GeV
predictive_yield.description: Agnese et al. (CDMS-II) 2013 reported 3 candidate events vs ~1 background in silicon detectors, hinting at low-mass WIMP at m_WIMP ~ 8 GeV. SuperCDMS subsequent exclusions (Agnese et al. 2018 Phys. Rev. D 97, 022002) ruled out the parameter space implied by the 3-event hint at high significance. Type-B falsified-specific-claim — low-statistics hint excluded by increased exposure.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Agnese et al. (CDMS-II) 2013, Phys. Rev. Lett. 111, 251301
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Agnese et al. (SuperCDMS) 2018, Phys. Rev. D 97, 022002

predictive_yield.prediction: Fuzzy / ultralight dark matter at m_φ ~ 10⁻²² eV explains galaxy-scale small-scale-structure anomalies (core-cusp, missing satellites)
predictive_yield.description: Hu-Barkana-Gruzinov 2000 proposed fuzzy DM. Iršič et al. 2017 Lyman-α bounds m_φ ≳ 2 × 10⁻²¹ eV, in tension with the original 10⁻²² eV motivation. Galaxy rotation-curve analyses partially favor m_φ ~ 10⁻²² eV but Lyman-α and Milky Way satellite counts disfavor. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` — different observations point in different directions.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Hu, Barkana, Gruzinov 2000, Phys. Rev. Lett. 85, 1158
predictive_yield.confirmation_citation: Iršič et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 031302 (Lyman-α tension)

predictive_yield.prediction: Self-interacting dark matter with velocity-dependent σ/m solves galactic core-cusp + dwarf-galaxy-diversity problems
predictive_yield.description: Spergel-Steinhardt 2000 motivated SIDM. Observational evidence for SIDM-style halo structure is suggestive but not conclusive; specific particle-physics realizations (Yukawa-mediated dark sector) remain to be confirmed. Status `unconfirmed-not-yet-tested` — the framework is internally consistent but requires identifying specific particle realization.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Spergel, Steinhardt 2000, Phys. Rev. Lett. 84, 3760

predictive_yield.prediction: Asymmetric dark matter explains the Ω_DM / Ω_b ≈ 5 coincidence via shared asymmetry-generation
predictive_yield.description: Nussinov 1985 + subsequent work. Connects baryogenesis to DM-genesis. Predicts m_DM ~ 1-15 GeV. Sub-GeV-DM direct-detection programs (SuperCDMS, SENSEI, DAMIC) target the predicted mass range; no signal so far.
predictive_yield.prediction_citation: Nussinov 1985, Phys. Lett. B 165, 55; Petraki, Volkas 2013 review

closure_status.description: The dark-matter candidate space is well-classified at the framework level — particle and non-particle candidates are organized by mass scale, production mechanism, and parameter-space status. The classification is `partial` because: (i) the central question (what DM IS) remains open after 50 years of search; (ii) several candidate classes have been only partially excluded — e.g., axions in DFSZ/KSVZ band remain `viable-targeted`, asteroid-mass PBHs remain `viable-targeted`, dark-photon-coupled candidates have viable regions; (iii) the canonical WIMP-miracle parameter space, while largely excluded, has not been formally declared `falsified` because non-canonical WIMP variants survive; (iv) the type-B falsified-specific-claim cells (DAMA/LIBRA WIMP-interpretation, CDMS-II Si excess, MACHO-as-major-fraction) provide clean empirical anchors but specific positive-detection of DM has not been achieved. Multiple search programs (LZ, XENONnT, PandaX next-generation; ADMX-Sidecar / MADMAX higher-mass axion; LiteBIRD / CMB-S4 cosmological constraints; LISA stochastic-GW PBH constraints) will substantially update this map over the next decade.

related_entries: sm-rep-content (the primary structural-FC underlying particle-DM candidates; sterile-ν, dark-photon, and various BSM-particle candidates derive from extensions of SM rep content)
spacetime-symmetry-groups (all DM candidates are gravitating matter and satisfy Poincaré invariance + diffeomorphism invariance)
su5-gut-rep-content (specific candidates — sterile-ν from SO(10) 16-plet, SUSY-LSP from GUT-extended frameworks, monopole DM from GUT symmetry breaking — derive from GUT-rep extensions)

neutrino-sector-phenomenology (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm and cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV correspond to neutrino-phen cells cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM, cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED, cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension — same physical sterile neutrinos, different organizing FCs)

compact-astrophysical-objects (CELL-LEVEL OVERLAP: cell-dm-PBH-asteroid-mass-viable, cell-dm-PBH-LIGO-mass-excluded, cell-dm-PBH-supermassive-formation correspond to compact-astro PBH cells — same physical PBHs, different organizing FCs)

PLANNED FUTURE PHENOMENON-FC: cosmological-observations — would organize the cosmological-Ω_DM observational data, hubble-tension data, structure-formation observations that constrain DM candidates as a separate FC; this DM-candidates FC organizes the candidate space, not the observational data on Ω_DM itself
PLANNED FUTURE: modified-gravity-alternatives (MOND, TeVeS, ...) — would be the alternative phenomenon-FC for DM-replacement scenarios; intentionally excluded from this FC's domain
citations: Bertone, Hooper, Silk 2005, Phys. Rep. 405, 279 (canonical DM review)
Bertone, Hooper 2018, Rev. Mod. Phys. 90, 045002 (DM history)
Battaglieri et al. 2017, arXiv:1707.04591 (US Cosmic Visions: New Ideas in Dark Matter — community report)
Carr, Kühnel 2020, Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 70, 355 (PBH-as-DM)
Tulin, Yu 2018, Phys. Rep. 730, 1 (SIDM review)
Adams et al. 2023, Snowmass2021 Cosmic Frontier (community white paper)

nodes.label: Cosmological observations
nodes.full_name: Classification of cosmological observables — CMB anisotropies, large-scale structure, BBN abundances, dark-energy probes, Hubble constant measurements, primordial relics — by observable class, probe epoch, and observational status (consistent, multi-experiment tension, bounded-only, primordial-not-detected, or falsified-specific-claim)
nodes.description: Cosmology has entered a tension-rich precision era. The ΛCDM concordance model — with six basic parameters (Ω_b h², Ω_c h², 100θ_*, τ_reio, A_s, n_s) — fits the bulk of cosmological data at the few-percent level, but multiple ~3-5σ tensions have emerged between independent measurement methods over the past decade. This FC organizes the cosmological observational landscape: the precisely-measured cosmological parameters and their consistencies; the multi-experiment tensions where different probes give incompatible values for the same physical quantity (most prominently the Hubble tension between CMB-derived and local-distance-ladder-derived H_0); the upper-bound cells where the predicted physical effect is not yet detected (primordial gravitational waves, primordial non-Gaussianity); the falsified-specific-claim cells (EDGES 2018 21cm absorption); and the persistent long-standing tensions like the BBN ⁷Li problem. Cells correspond to specific cosmological observables or measurement programs; axes are observable-class × probe-or-epoch × observational-status. The classification is dynamic — the Hubble tension has migrated from ~2σ (2010) through ~3.5σ (2018) to ~5σ (2024) with more-precise local-ladder and CMB measurements, while DESI 2024 BAO results have introduced a new evolving-dark-energy hint that may eventually displace ΛCDM. Cell-level overlaps with dark-matter-candidates (Ω_DM) and neutrino-sector-phenomenology (Σm_ν cosmological bound) are noted but expressed via related_entries cross-references rather than FC-level edges (consistent with v23 / v25 observation that current edge subtypes don't fit phen↔phen cell-overlap cleanly).
nodes.domain_of_applicability: Observational probes of the universe at cosmological scales (Mpc to Hubble-radius) and across cosmic history from inflation (z → ∞) through recombination (z ~ 1100), reionization (z ~ 10), galaxy formation (z ~ 1-20), to present-day large-scale structure (z < 2). Includes: CMB anisotropy spectra (TT, TE, EE, BB polarization) and their derived parameters; BAO sound-horizon measurements across redshift; type-Ia supernovae as standardizable distance candles; local distance-ladder calibrations (Cepheids, TRGB); weak-lensing-derived structure-amplitude measurements; cluster-count cosmology; BBN-era light-element abundance measurements; 21cm-cosmology probes of reionization and cosmic dawn; gravitational-wave standard sirens (multi-messenger and dark sirens); high-z galaxy properties as ΛCDM tests; primordial gravitational-wave searches via CMB B-modes; primordial non-Gaussianity searches via CMB and LSS bispectra. Excludes: (i) the candidate-space for dark matter (that lives in dark-matter-candidates v25); (ii) the candidate-space for dark energy / modified-gravity alternatives (a separate phenomenon-FC if authored); (iii) compact-object catalogs at cosmological distances (those live in compact-astro / gravitational-wave-event-catalog); (iv) specific neutrino-cosmology observables that are organized in neutrino-sector-phenomenology (Σm_ν bound, cosmological-neutrino-background).
nodes.organizing_principle: Each cell corresponds to a specific cosmological observable, measurement program, or upper-bound search. The classification axes capture: (i) what KIND of observable — distance-measure, anisotropy-spectrum-feature, cosmological-parameter, primordial-relic-or-non-detection, or multi-experiment-tension-pair (which is itself an axis-of-tension between paired cells); (ii) what PROBE or EPOCH provides the measurement — CMB-epoch (z ~ 1100, Planck / ACT / SPT), BAO-LSS-epoch (z < 2, BOSS / eBOSS / DESI), distance-ladder-local (z < 0.1, SH0ES / TRGB / TFR), GW-standard-siren-era (z ~ 0.1, GW170817 + future), BBN-epoch (z ~ 10⁹, primordial-D / He / Li abundance measurements), inflation-era (z → ∞, B-mode searches), reionization-21cm-epoch (z ~ 10-30, EDGES / SARAS / HERA), JWST-galaxy-formation-epoch (z ~ 5-15); (iii) the observational status with a richer enum than prior phenomenon-FCs because cosmology has unique structural features — measured-precise-consistent (single-method precision agreement), measured-with-multi-experiment-tension (the Hubble cluster, σ_8 cluster, ⁷Li problem), bounded-with-open-range (cosmological mass bounds, equation-of-state evolution), primordial-not-detected (primordial-GW, primordial-NG), falsified-specific-claim (EDGES 21cm). The framework is constrained from above by GR + standard-model neutrino + dark-matter + dark-energy physics; from below by precision measurements that have been driving the field into tension over the past decade.

classification_axes.name: observable-class
classification_axes.description: The category of cosmological observable. distance-measure: BAO sound-horizon (r_s), supernova-Ia distance-redshift relation, GW standard-siren distance. anisotropy-spectrum-feature: CMB acoustic-peak positions/amplitudes, polarization features, lensing reconstruction. cosmological-parameter: Ω_b h², Ω_c h², τ_reio, n_s, A_s, w_0, w_a, Ω_K — fundamental ΛCDM and extended-model parameters. primordial-relic-or-non-detection: primordial gravitational waves (B-modes from inflation), primordial non-Gaussianity (f_NL), primordial-magnetic-field bounds. multi-experiment-tension-pair: when the same physical quantity is measured by different methods giving incompatible values (Hubble tension between CMB and local ladder; σ_8 tension between CMB and weak-lensing). BBN-light-element-abundance: primordial D/H, ⁴He/H, ⁷Li/H — fixed by BBN to specific values given Ω_b h² + N_eff. high-z-structure-observable: JWST galaxy properties at z > 6 testing structure-formation predictions.

classification_axes.name: probe-or-epoch
classification_axes.description: The cosmological epoch or probe-method providing the measurement. CMB-epoch (z ~ 1100): Planck, ACT, SPT, future LiteBIRD / CMB-S4. BAO-LSS-epoch (z < 2): BOSS / eBOSS / DESI sound-horizon-via-galaxy-correlation. distance-ladder-local (z < 0.1): SH0ES Cepheid-based, TRGB-based, surface-brightness-fluctuations. GW-standard-siren (z ~ 0.001-0.1): GW170817 multi-messenger H_0; future LISA + Einstein Telescope. BBN-epoch (z ~ 10⁹): primordial-D/H from quasar absorption spectra, ⁴He from H II regions, ⁷Li from metal-poor stars. inflation-era (z → ∞): CMB B-mode polarization searches (BICEP/Keck, future LiteBIRD). reionization-21cm-epoch (z ~ 10-30): EDGES, SARAS, future SKA / HERA. JWST-galaxy-formation-epoch (z ~ 5-15): galaxy mass functions, UV luminosity functions. weak-lensing-LSS-epoch (z ~ 0.5-1.5): KiDS / DES / HSC / future Rubin / Euclid.

classification_axes.name: observational-status
classification_axes.description: Observational status with cosmology-specific richness. measured-precise-consistent: quantity precisely measured with multiple independent probes giving consistent values (CMB acoustic-peak positions, recombination-epoch optical depth, spectral index n_s, spatial flatness Ω_K ≈ 0). measured-with-multi-experiment-tension: quantity precisely measured by individual methods but methods disagree (Hubble tension cluster; σ_8 cluster; BBN ⁷Li problem). bounded-with-open-range: cosmological bound established, true value below current sensitivity (Σm_ν cosmological bound; primordial-magnetic-field upper bound). primordial-not-detected: predicted signal of primordial origin not yet detected, only upper-bounded (primordial gravitational waves r < 0.036; primordial non-Gaussianity f_NL bounds). falsified-specific-claim: a specific observation claim has been retracted or independently failed to reproduce (EDGES 2018 21cm absorption claim; potentially the original BICEP2 2014 gravitational-wave claim, though that was a calibration / foreground correction rather than a falsification per se). anomaly-not-yet-resolved: observation challenges ΛCDM but interpretation is open (JWST high-z mature galaxies; DESI 2024 evolving-DE hint).

cells.content: CMB temperature anisotropy acoustic-peak structure (Planck 2018 / ACT DR6 2024)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 (Aghanim et al. 2020 Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6): seven well-resolved acoustic peaks in the temperature power spectrum, positions and amplitudes consistent with ΛCDM at the sub-percent level for the first three peaks. ACT DR6 (2025): independent ground-based measurement of the same acoustic structure at smaller angular scales, consistent with Planck. The 100θ_* parameter is measured to ~0.03% precision.
cells.description: The most precisely-measured cosmological observable. The acoustic-peak positions encode the angular size of the sound horizon at last scattering, which fixes a combination of cosmological parameters with extraordinary precision. Confirmed across multiple independent CMB experiments (WMAP, Planck, ACT, SPT) at sub-percent precision. Foundational for CMB-derived cosmological parameter inference.
citations: Aghanim et al. (Planck Collaboration) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6 (Planck 2018 cosmological parameters)
Aiola et al. (ACT) 2020, JCAP 12, 047 (ACT cosmology)

Aghanim N. et al. (Planck Collaboration) 2020 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters' Astron. Astrophys. 641:A6 arXiv:1807.06209 (source-value: 100θ_* = 1.04109 ± 0.00030 from Table 2 base-ΛCDM TT,TE,EE+lowE+lensing; 0.03% angular-acoustic-scale precision is the most-precisely-measured cosmological observable)

cells.content: BAO sound-horizon measurements across z < 2 (BOSS / eBOSS / DESI) — including DESI 2024 evolving-DE hint
realized_examples: BOSS DR12 (Alam et al. 2017) gave BAO measurements at z = 0.38, 0.51, 0.61 consistent with Planck-LCDM. eBOSS extended to z = 1.5-2.3. DESI 2024 Year-1 BAO results (Adame et al. 2024) reach 3σ tension with ΛCDM when combined with CMB + SN-Ia, hinting at a time-evolving dark-energy equation of state w(z) = w_0 + w_a · z/(1+z) away from w = -1, w_a = 0. The DESI hint is currently the most prominent ΛCDM-extension anomaly.
cells.description: BAO measurements are the second-most-precise cosmological probe after CMB. The DESI 2024 Year-1 result is a recent and prominent departure from ΛCDM consistency — the evidence for evolving dark energy is at ~2-3σ alone and ~3-4σ when combined with CMB + SN-Ia (BAO + supernovae prefers w_0 > -1 with w_a < 0). DESI Year-3 and Year-5 will further test. Listed `anomaly-not-yet-resolved` because the result is real and significant but the interpretation (modified ΛCDM vs systematic vs new physics) is open.
citations: Alam et al. (BOSS) 2017, Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 470, 2617
Adame et al. (DESI Collaboration) 2024, JCAP 04, 026 (DESI Year-1 BAO + cosmology)
DES Collaboration 2024 supernova cosmology combined analysis

Aghanim N. et al. (Planck Collaboration) 2020 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters' Astron. Astrophys. 641:A6 arXiv:1807.06209 (source-value: r_drag = 147.09 ± 0.26 Mpc from Table 2 base-ΛCDM TT,TE,EE+lowE+lensing; sound horizon at end of baryon-drag epoch — the BAO standard ruler)

cells.content: Hubble constant from CMB inverse-distance-ladder — H_0 = 67.4 ± 0.5 km/s/Mpc (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 (assuming ΛCDM): H_0 = 67.4 ± 0.5 km/s/Mpc. ACT DR6 + Planck combined gives H_0 = 67.81 ± 0.55 km/s/Mpc, fully consistent with the inverse-distance-ladder value. The CMB-derived H_0 is inferred from acoustic-peak positions + BAO sound-horizon calibration + assumed ΛCDM expansion history.
cells.description: One half of the Hubble tension. The CMB-derived H_0 value is precise but assumes ΛCDM is the correct cosmological framework — modifications to early-universe physics (added relativistic species N_eff, early-dark-energy, etc.) can shift the CMB-inferred H_0 upward. Cell content is the *CMB-inferred H_0* — paired with cell-cosm-H0-local-ladder via the tension-pair axis kind.
citations: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6
Aiola et al. (ACT) 2020 (consistent independent CMB result)

Aghanim N. et al. (Planck Collaboration) 2020 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters' Astron. Astrophys. 641:A6 arXiv:1807.06209 (source-value: Hubble time t_H = 1/H_0 = 14.52 ± 0.11 Gyr derived from Planck 2018 H_0 = 67.4 ± 0.5 km/s/Mpc base-ΛCDM TT,TE,EE+lowE+lensing; conversion factor 1 Mpc·s/km = 978.46 Myr)

cells.content: Hubble constant from local distance ladder — H_0 = 73.04 ± 1.04 km/s/Mpc (SH0ES 2022)
realized_examples: Riess et al. (SH0ES Collaboration) 2022, Astrophys. J. Lett. 934, L7: H_0 = 73.04 ± 1.04 km/s/Mpc via Cepheid-Type-Ia-supernova distance ladder, anchored to Gaia parallaxes + LMC detached eclipsing binaries + masers (NGC 4258).

TRGB-based H_0 (Freedman et al. 2019, Astrophys. J. 882, 34): H_0 ≈ 69.8 ± 1.7 km/s/Mpc — intermediate between CMB and Cepheid-based, with active debate about Cepheid metallicity dependence and TRGB tip identification.
Surface-brightness-fluctuations (Blakeslee et al. 2021): consistent with SH0ES.
cells.description: Other half of the Hubble tension. The local-ladder value disagrees with the CMB-inferred value by ~5σ. SH0ES uses Cepheids; TRGB-based ladders (Freedman et al.) give somewhat lower values. The tension has grown from ~2σ (2010) through ~3.5σ (2018) to ~5σ (2024) with more-precise local-ladder and CMB measurements. Resolutions span (a) systematics in either method, (b) new pre-recombination physics changing CMB-inferred H_0 (early-dark-energy, extra relativistic species), (c) late-time new physics. Cell paired with cell-cosm-H0-CMB-Planck via the multi-experiment-tension structure.
citations: Riess et al. (SH0ES) 2022, Astrophys. J. Lett. 934, L7
Freedman et al. 2019, Astrophys. J. 882, 34 (TRGB)
Verde, Treu, Riess 2019, Nature Astron. 3, 891 (tension review)

Riess A.G. et al. (SH0ES Collaboration) 2022 'A Comprehensive Measurement of the Local Value of the Hubble Constant with 1 km s⁻¹ Mpc⁻¹ Uncertainty from the Hubble Space Telescope and the SH0ES Team' Astrophys. J. Lett. 934:L7 arXiv:2112.04510 (source-value: Hubble time t_H = 1/H_0 = 13.40 ± 0.19 Gyr derived from SH0ES H_0 = 73.04 ± 1.04 km/s/Mpc via Cepheid-TypeIa-SN distance ladder anchored to Gaia parallaxes + LMC eclipsing binaries + NGC4258 maser)

cells.content: Hubble constant from gravitational-wave standard sirens — GW170817 multi-messenger H_0 ≈ 70⁺¹⁹₋₈ km/s/Mpc
realized_examples: Abbott et al. (LVC + EM partners) 2017, Nature 551, 85: GW170817 binary-neutron-star merger combined with electromagnetic identification of host galaxy NGC 4993 gave H_0 = 70⁺¹⁹₋₈ km/s/Mpc (low precision, single event). Future LISA + Einstein Telescope will measure H_0 with multiple standard-siren events at percent precision.
cells.description: Third independent H_0 probe. Current precision too low to resolve the Hubble tension, but the method is systematically distinct from both CMB and Cepheid-based ladders, providing a future tiebreaker. Listed `bounded-with-open-range` rather than entering the tension pair because the precision is insufficient to discriminate.
citations: Abbott et al. (LIGO-Virgo + EM partners) 2017, Nature 551, 85 (GW170817 standard siren)
Schutz 1986, Nature 323, 310 (original standard-siren proposal)

Abbott B.P. et al. (LIGO/Virgo + EM partners) 2017 'A gravitational-wave standard siren measurement of the Hubble constant' Nature 551:85 arXiv:1710.05835 (source-value: Hubble time t_H = 1/H_0 = 13.97 +1.80 -2.98 Gyr derived from GW170817 standard-siren H_0 = 70 +19 -8 km/s/Mpc; asymmetric uncertainty propagated through inverse-mapping H_0 → 1/H_0 which flips sign convention)

cells.content: σ_8 structure-amplitude from CMB — σ_8 = 0.811 ± 0.006 (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018: σ_8 = 0.811 ± 0.006 (or equivalent S_8 = σ_8 (Ω_m/0.3)^0.5 ≈ 0.832 ± 0.013). ACT DR6 + Planck: consistent.
cells.description: First half of the σ_8 tension. CMB-derived σ_8 measures structure amplitude extrapolated forward from recombination using ΛCDM growth-of-perturbations. Paired with cell-cosm-sigma8-weak-lensing via multi-experiment-tension.
citations: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6

Aghanim N. et al. (Planck Collaboration) 2020 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters' Astron. Astrophys. 641:A6 arXiv:1807.06209 (source-value: σ_8 = 0.811 ± 0.006 from Table 2 base-ΛCDM TT,TE,EE+lowE+lensing; structure-amplitude inferred via CMB extrapolated forward to z=0)

cells.content: σ_8 from weak-lensing surveys — KiDS / DES / HSC give S_8 ≈ 0.77, ~2-3σ lower than CMB-inferred
realized_examples: KiDS-1000 (Asgari et al. 2021): S_8 = 0.766⁺⁰·⁰²⁰₋₀.₀₁₄. DES Y3 (Abbott et al. 2022): S_8 = 0.776 ± 0.017. HSC Year 3 (Dalal et al. 2023): S_8 = 0.776 ± 0.020. All three surveys consistently report S_8 ~2-3σ below the Planck-CMB-inferred value.
cells.description: Second half of the σ_8 tension. Weak-lensing surveys probe structure growth at lower redshift (z ~ 0.5-1.5) than CMB. Multiple independent surveys (KiDS, DES, HSC) consistently give S_8 lower than CMB-inferred — the tension is less significant than Hubble (2-3σ vs 5σ) but persistent across independent surveys. Possible resolutions: lensing systematics; baryonic-feedback effects in matter power spectrum; new physics (massive neutrinos, modified gravity).
citations: Asgari et al. (KiDS-1000) 2021, Astron. Astrophys. 645, A104
Abbott et al. (DES Y3) 2022, Phys. Rev. D 105, 023520
Dalal et al. (HSC Y3) 2023, Phys. Rev. D 108, 123519

Abbott T.M.C. et al. (DES Collaboration) 2022 'Dark Energy Survey Year 3 results: Cosmological constraints from galaxy clustering and weak lensing' Phys. Rev. D 105:023520 arXiv:2105.13549 (source-value: S_8 = σ_8(Ω_m/0.3)^0.5 = 0.776 ± 0.017 from DES Y3 3×2pt combined cosmic-shear + galaxy-galaxy lensing + galaxy clustering; ~2σ below Planck-CMB-inferred value)

cells.content: Primordial deuterium abundance D/H from quasar absorption spectra — D/H = (2.527 ± 0.030) × 10⁻⁵
realized_examples: Cooke, Pettini, Steidel 2018, Astrophys. J. 855, 102: D/H = (2.527 ± 0.030) × 10⁻⁵ from high-resolution Keck HIRES + VLT UVES observations of metal-poor damped Lyman-α absorbers. Implies η_b ~ 6 × 10⁻¹⁰, consistent with CMB-derived Ω_b h² at sub-percent precision.
cells.description: Most precise BBN abundance measurement. D/H is exponentially sensitive to η_b (baryon-to-photon ratio), providing an independent measurement of Ω_b h² that agrees with CMB at sub-percent precision — one of the most successful cross-epoch ΛCDM consistency tests.
citations: Cooke, Pettini, Steidel 2018, Astrophys. J. 855, 102
Cyburt, Fields, Olive, Yeh 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 015004 (BBN review)

J. 855:102 arXiv:1710.11129 (source-value: D/H = (2.527 ± 0.030) × 10⁻⁵ from Keck HIRES + VLT UVES high-resolution observations of seven metal-poor damped Lyman-α absorbers at z=2.5-3.1; consistent with η_b inferred from Planck-CMB at sub-percent precision — the most precise non-CMB constraint on Ω_b h²)

cells.content: Primordial ⁷Li/H — BBN-predicted (5 × 10⁻¹⁰) is ~3× observed in metal-poor stars (1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰)
realized_examples: BBN prediction (Cyburt et al. 2016 review): ⁷Li/H ≈ 5.0 × 10⁻¹⁰ for η_b inferred from CMB + D/H. Observation in metal-poor halo stars (Spite plateau, Spite & Spite 1982 + many subsequent): ⁷Li/H ≈ 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰. The discrepancy is ~3-4σ persistent for 25+ years.

Possible resolutions: stellar destruction (turbulent mixing); BBN-era new physics modifying Li nuclear reactions; uncertain Li nuclear cross-sections (partly addressed). No resolution accepted by consensus.
cells.description: Longest-standing BBN tension. Persistent for 25+ years; multiple proposed resolutions debated. Listed `measured-with-multi-experiment-tension` though the 'tension' is between BBN-theory and stellar-observation rather than two independent measurements — captures the same essential structure of independent-method disagreement.
citations: Spite, Spite 1982, Astron. Astrophys. 115, 357 (Spite plateau)
Cyburt, Fields, Olive, Yeh 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 015004 (review of BBN, including ⁷Li problem)

Sbordone L. et al. 2010 'The metal-poor end of the Spite plateau. I. Stellar parameters, metallicities, and lithium abundances' Astron. Astrophys. 522:A26 arXiv:1003.4510 (source-value: A(Li) = log[N(Li)/N(H)] + 12 ≈ 2.199 ± 0.086 for metal-poor halo turn-off stars on the Spite plateau, corresponding to ⁷Li/H = (1.58 ± 0.31) × 10⁻¹⁰; ~3× lower than BBN-predicted 5 × 10⁻¹⁰ given η_b from CMB + D/H — the persistent ~3-4σ ⁷Li problem unresolved for 25+ years)

cells.content: Primordial gravitational waves from inflation — tensor-to-scalar ratio bounded r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck Array 2021)
realized_examples: BICEP/Keck Array 2021 (Ade et al. Phys. Rev. Lett. 127, 151301): r < 0.036 at 95% CL from CMB B-mode polarization at degree angular scales, after foreground subtraction.
Future: LiteBIRD (JAXA, ~2030 launch) targets r ~ 10⁻³ sensitivity; CMB-S4 ground-based targets similar.
cells.description: Major Mendeleev empty cell. Primordial gravitational waves are the prediction of inflation; their detection would establish the inflation framework and measure the inflation energy scale. Current upper bound rules out the simplest large-field inflation models (specifically m²φ² inflation is excluded). Detection is the top observational target of next-generation CMB experiments.
citations: Ade et al. (BICEP/Keck) 2021, Phys. Rev. Lett. 127, 151301 (r < 0.036)
Hazumi et al. (LiteBIRD) 2019, J. Low Temp. Phys. 194, 443 (LiteBIRD design)

Lett. 127:151301 arXiv:2110.00483 (source-value: r < 0.036 at 95% CL on the tensor-to-scalar ratio at pivot k=0.05 Mpc⁻¹ from CMB B-mode polarization at degree angular scales after multi-frequency foreground subtraction; excludes the simplest large-field inflation models — m²φ² is ruled out)

cells.content: Primordial non-Gaussianity — f_NL^local bounded |f_NL^local| < 5.0 (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 NG constraints: f_NL^local = -0.9 ± 5.1 (consistent with zero); f_NL^equilateral = -26 ± 47; f_NL^orthogonal = -38 ± 24. Future galaxy surveys (DESI, Rubin, Roman, SKA) target f_NL ~ O(1) sensitivity, distinguishing multi-field vs single-field inflation models.
cells.description: Second Mendeleev empty cell. Detection of f_NL^local ≠ 0 would falsify single-field slow-roll inflation; non-detection is consistent with simplest inflation models. Active observational target for next-generation LSS surveys.
citations: Akrami et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A9 (NG constraints)
Maldacena 2003, JHEP 05, 013 (single-field inflation NG bound)

Astrophys. 641:A9 arXiv:1905.05697 (source-value: f_NL^local = -0.9 ± 5.1 at 68% CL from Planck temperature + polarization KSW estimator; consistent with zero — single-field slow-roll inflation prediction f_NL^local = O(slow-roll) << 1 not yet falsifiable; future LSS surveys target f_NL ~ O(1) sensitivity)

cells.content: Reionization optical depth τ_reio = 0.054 ± 0.007 (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 from large-scale E-mode polarization: τ_reio = 0.054 ± 0.007, implying reionization centered at z_reio ≈ 7.7 ± 0.7. Consistent with high-z quasar Gunn-Peterson trough observations (z ~ 6-7) and JWST z > 7 galaxy spectroscopy.
cells.description: Recombination/reionization-history parameter. Precisely measured from CMB polarization and consistent with quasar and JWST observations. Stable across recent updates.
citations: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6

Aghanim N. et al. (Planck Collaboration) 2020 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters' Astron. Astrophys. 641:A6 arXiv:1807.06209 (source-value: τ_reio = 0.054 ± 0.007 from Planck large-scale E-mode polarization base-ΛCDM TT,TE,EE+lowE+lensing; implies reionization midpoint z_reio ≈ 7.7 ± 0.7 — consistent with quasar Gunn-Peterson trough z~6-7 and JWST z>7 spectroscopy)

cells.content: Spatial curvature Ω_K consistent with zero — |Ω_K| < 0.005 (Planck 2018 + BAO)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO: Ω_K = 0.001 ± 0.002 (95% CL upper bound |Ω_K| < 0.005). Consistent with the inflationary prediction of spatial flatness.
cells.description: Strong inflation-test prediction confirmed at sub-percent precision. The flatness is one of the cleanest ΛCDM-inflation success stories.

cells.content: Scalar spectral index n_s = 0.965 ± 0.004 (Planck 2018) — slightly red, as inflation predicts
realized_examples: Planck 2018: n_s = 0.965 ± 0.004, robustly different from scale-invariance (n_s = 1) by >7σ. ACT DR6 consistent.
cells.description: Inflation-test prediction confirmed. The slightly-red spectral index (n_s < 1) is a generic prediction of slow-roll inflation and is now established at high significance.

cells.content: JWST observations of unexpectedly massive / mature galaxies at z > 10
realized_examples: Labbé et al. 2023 Nature 616, 266: JWST CEERS+UNCOVER observations of 6 candidate galaxies at z > 7 with stellar masses ~10¹⁰-10¹¹ M_sun, larger than easily explained by standard ΛCDM galaxy-formation. Subsequent spectroscopic follow-ups (Curtis-Lake et al. 2023; Labbé et al. 2024) have refined some masses downward but the high-z mass-function tension persists.

GN-z11 confirmed at z = 10.6 with mature stellar population (~10⁹ M_sun stellar mass; Bunker et al. 2023). At z > 14 several photometric candidates with stellar masses challenging ΛCDM at current modeling precision.
cells.description: Current cosmology anomaly. JWST observations of mature massive galaxies at z > 10 challenge ΛCDM structure-formation predictions; the tension is partial (some galaxy masses refined downward upon spectroscopic confirmation) but persistent. Active observational and theoretical work ongoing.

cells.content: EDGES 2018 21cm absorption feature at z ~ 17 — claim contested by SARAS 3 null result
realized_examples: Bowman et al. (EDGES) 2018, Nature 555, 67: reported absorption feature at 78 MHz (z ~ 17, cosmic dawn) with depth ~500 mK — about 2-3× deeper than standard cosmology predicts. Stimulated speculative explanations (DM-baryon scattering cooling). SARAS 3 (Singh et al. 2022, Nature Astron. 6, 607): independent attempt found no evidence for the EDGES signal at the claimed amplitude.
The EDGES result has not been formally retracted; the team maintains the detection. The signal is currently contested with one independent null result.
cells.description: Most prominent contested observational claim in current cosmology. The 2018 EDGES signal would have been a major discovery — first detection of 21cm cosmic-dawn absorption. SARAS 3 (2022) does not see the signal at the claimed level. Listed `falsified-specific-claim` for the specific EDGES amplitude though formal retraction has not occurred; the contested status is the second clean type-B-like case in cosmology (alongside any potential BICEP2-2014 dust-foreground story, though that was a calibration issue rather than retraction).
citations: Bowman et al. (EDGES) 2018, Nature 555, 67
Singh et al. (SARAS 3) 2022, Nature Astron. 6, 607 (null result)

predictive_yield.description: ΛCDM-prediction for CMB acoustic peaks (Hu, Sugiyama, Silk 1995-97 era; Spergel et al. WMAP 2003+) has been spectacularly confirmed by Planck 2018 and ACT DR6. Six-parameter fit matches data over many orders of magnitude in multipole. Foundational ΛCDM-success.

predictive_yield.prediction: Hubble constant is consistent across CMB-inverse-distance-ladder and local-Cepheid-ladder measurements
predictive_yield.description: ΛCDM requires consistency. CMB + BAO + Cepheid-ladder + TRGB-ladder + GW-standard-sirens should all give the same H_0 within their uncertainties. Observed: ~5σ disagreement between Planck CMB-inferred 67.4 ± 0.5 and SH0ES local 73.04 ± 1.04. The Hubble tension has grown since 2018 and is now the most prominent ΛCDM-disagreement. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because neither measurement is falsified — both are precise within their methods — but they disagree.

closure_status.description: Cosmology has entered a phase where the ΛCDM framework is precision-tested at the few-percent level and producing consistent results in many places (CMB + BAO + BBN D/H + spatial flatness + spectral index) but simultaneously producing multiple ~3-5σ tensions in others (Hubble, σ_8, dark-energy evolution per DESI, JWST high-z, ⁷Li). The classification of cosmological observables is well-developed; what remains open is whether the tensions reflect (a) systematics in specific measurement methods that will be resolved by better data, (b) new pre-recombination physics (early dark energy, extra relativistic species, non-standard recombination), (c) new late-universe physics (modified gravity, interacting DE-DM, time-varying DE), or (d) some combination. Listed `partial` because the framework is settled but multiple cells are in active tension. The next-generation observational programs (DESI Year-5, Euclid, Rubin LSST, LiteBIRD, CMB-S4, SKA, Einstein Telescope) will resolve or sharpen most current tensions over the next decade.
