A CFT with generalized-symmetry data S must satisfy: (i) the OPE coefficients factor through S-equivariant tensor products; (ii) the partition function transforms under S's modular / cobordism class; (iii) the operator spectrum respects S-graded multiplet structure. These constraints narrow the bootstrap-allowed region within the sector with fixed S. Komargodski-Sulejmanpasic-Unsal 2017 made the constraint precise for 4D pure YM: the Z_N center 1-form anomaly rules out trivially-gapped phases. Subsequent work (Cordova-Dumitrescu-Intriligator; Brennan-Cordova; Su et al.) extended to other dimensions and symmetry types. Status established. Subtype `constrains` (v16). This is the SECOND authored use of `constrains` after MTC→bootstrap (v16). The constrains-relation here is genuinely distinct from any specializes / derives-from reading: the symmetry data doesn't IDENTIFY a sub-case of bootstrap CFTs; it RESTRICTS the form of any CFT realized with that symmetry data.
citations: Komargodski, Sulejmanpasic, Unsal 2017, Phys. Rev. B 97, 054418
Cordova, Dumitrescu, Intriligator 2019, JHEP 02, 184
Brennan, Cordova 2020, JHEP 02, 132 (anomalies and constraints)
Su, Wang, You 2021, JHEP 02, 002 (anomaly matching constraints for bootstrap)

edges.description: Modular tensor categories specialize generalized symmetries to the 2D rational case. The Moore-Seiberg axiom data of an MTC IS the data of a 3D SymTFT bulk for a 2D rational CFT. Equivalently: in 2D, the categorical-symmetry framework (cell-gs-d2-p0-categorical) reduces to MTC structure when the boundary is a rational CFT. The hierarchy: generalized-symmetries ⊃ categorical-symmetries-in-2D ⊃ MTC (rational case). cell_refs pin 4 representative MTCs (Fibonacci, Ising, toric code, SU(2)_3) whose SymTFT bulks are RT-TQFTs with these MTCs as input.
citations: Freed, Moore, Teleman 2022 (the connection between MTCs and SymTFT explicitly)
Kapustin, Saulina 2010, arXiv:1012.0911 (surface operators ↔ MTC)
Petkova, Zuber 2001, Phys. Lett. B 504, 157 (Verlinde lines as 2D categorical sym)

edges.description: The ten-fold way specializes generalized symmetries: ten-fold-way SPT phases are classified by their generalized-symmetry data including anti-unitary (time-reversal, particle-hole) symmetries treated as generalized symmetries with cobordism-class anomalies. Each ten-fold-way class corresponds to a specific Pin or Spin or Spin^c cobordism class in the bulk SymTFT (Kapustin 2014; Freed-Hopkins 2021). The specializes-relation: the ten-fold-way is the sub-case of generalized-symmetries focused on free fermionic SPT phases with the ten Altland-Zirnbauer symmetry classes. cell_refs pin 5 representative ten-fold-way cells whose generalized-symmetry data is canonically derivable.
citations: Kapustin 2014, arXiv:1403.1467 (cobordism-theoretic derivation of ten-fold way)
Kapustin, Thorngren, Turzillo, Wang 2015, JHEP 12, 052 (fermionic SPT and generalized cohomology)
Wang, Senthil 2014, Phys. Rev. B 89, 195124 (generalized symmetries in topological insulators)

edges.description: Top-down holographic / F-theory compactifications aspire to derive SM rep content from generalized-symmetry data of the bulk SymTFT. The compactification carries discrete higher-form symmetry data (Apruzzi et al. 2023; Garcia-Etxebarria-Heidenreich-Regalado 2022); the boundary 4D QFT inherits both gauge content (sm-rep-content) and higher-form symmetry structure (generalized-symmetries) jointly from the bulk. AdS/CFT aspires this derivation: SM rep content as a boundary-projection of bulk SymTFT data. cell_refs pin all 15 SM matter cells (the source-side data the aspired derivation would produce). Distinct from the v15.7 sm-equiv-tenfold aspired edge (which targets only tenfold-way topological data, not the full generalized-symmetry framework).
citations: Apruzzi, Bonetti, Garcia, Saxena, Heckman, Schafer-Nameki 2023, Commun. Math. Phys. 402, 895
Garcia-Etxebarria, Heidenreich, Regalado 2022, JHEP 09, 130 (higher-form symmetries from string theory)
Heckman, Hubner, Torres 2024, JHEP 04, 113 (generalized symmetries from F-theory)

edges.description: Higgs/Coulomb branches specialize the bootstrap exclusion regions: 4D N=2 SCFTs are a specialization of bootstrap-allowed 4D CFTs, specifically those with N=2 superconformal symmetry. The bootstrap exclusion bounds for 4D N=2 SCFTs (e.g., the bounds on central charges and OPE coefficients in the chiral algebra protected sector) form a subset of the broader 4D bootstrap region. The Higgs/Coulomb branch structure is the moduli-space classification of THESE specific bootstrap-allowed theories. cell_refs pin 5 representative Coulomb branch cells whose corresponding SCFTs sit in the bootstrap-allowed region: SU(2) N_f=4, A_1 Argyres-Douglas, E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky, T_3 class-S, N=4 SU(2). The bootstrap entry's cell-bs-d4-N2-SCFT is the canonical bootstrap-side anchor.
citations: Beem, Rastelli, van Rees 2014, JHEP 05, 020 (4D N=2 bootstrap)
Lemos, van Rees 2016, JHEP 12, 040 (chiral algebra bootstrap)
Beem 2024 review (bootstrap-SCFT-VOA connections)

edges.description: Higgs/Coulomb branches are categorically-equivalent to modular tensor categories via the SCFT/VOA correspondence (Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli 2015): the Schur sector of any 4D N=2 SCFT is a 2D vertex operator algebra, and for rational SCFTs whose VOA is rational, this VOA is an MTC. The equivalence is partial because the VOAs arising from 4D N=2 SCFTs are not always rational — Argyres-Douglas theories give non-unitary or non-rational VOAs that lie outside the standard MTC framework. cell_refs pin 4 representative cells where the SCFT/VOA correspondence is well-studied: A_1 AD (Lee-Yang-like VOA), E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky (VOA on a specific exceptional Lie algebra root system), T_3 (specific known VOA), N=4 SU(2) (rational VOA). Status: partial — established for the rational-VOA sub-case; aspired/under-development for non-rational cases.
citations: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli 2015, Commun. Math. Phys. 336, 1359
Bonetti, Meneghelli, Rastelli 2018, JHEP 12, 152 (rational VOAs from AD theories)
Beem 2024 review

edges.description: Higgs/Coulomb branches of class-S and Minahan-Nemeschansky theories DERIVE FROM ADE Lie algebra data: the class-S construction (Gaiotto 2009) takes an ADE Lie algebra g and a punctured Riemann surface Σ as input, and outputs a 4D N=2 SCFT T[g, Σ] whose Higgs/Coulomb branches are determined by (g, Σ, puncture-types). The Minahan-Nemeschansky E_{6,7,8} theories have Higgs branches equal to the minimal nilpotent orbits of e_{6,7,8}. Status: partial — the derivation works rigorously for class-S theories of types ADE and for the M-N exceptional theories, but not all 4D N=2 SCFTs admit a class-S construction (e.g., generic non-class-S Lagrangian theories don't fit). cell_refs pin 7 representative cells whose branch data is class-S- or M-N-derived from ADE input data.
citations: Gaiotto 2009, JHEP 08, 034
Tachikawa 2014, Lecture Notes (class-S review)
Cremonesi, Hanany, Mekareeya, Zaffaroni 2014, JHEP 09, 178 (Coulomb branches of class-S)
Moore, Tachikawa 2012, Surv. Diff. Geom. 18, 129 (Higgs branches as affine Grassmannian slices)

edges.description: Generalized symmetries of 4D N=2 SCFTs structurally constrain the Higgs/Coulomb branch structure. The constraints: (i) 0-form flavor symmetries act on Higgs branches as isometries of the hyperkähler structure, narrowing the allowed flavor groups for each branch; (ii) 1-form center symmetries (Bhardwaj-Tachikawa 2017) constrain which gauge structures admit conformal-fixed-point UV completions; (iii) 2-group structures (Cordova-Dumitrescu-Intriligator 2019) impose mixed-anomaly conditions between flavor 0-form and gauge 1-form sectors that restrict allowable branch decompositions. The result: generalized-symmetry anomaly matching is one of the most powerful tools for ruling out candidate Higgs/Coulomb branch structures. Status: partial — established for many specific cases (4D N=2 SCFTs with classifiable flavor symmetries; Bhardwaj-Tachikawa 1-form constraints; Cordova-Dumitrescu-Intriligator 2-group constraints); general framework still developing. Subtype `constrains` (the v16 subtype) — this is the THIRD authored use of `constrains` and the FIRST that does not point at bootstrap. cell_refs pin 3 source-side generalized-symmetry cells representing the 0-form flavor, 1-form center, and 2-group structures that act as constraints on HCB.
citations: Bhardwaj, Tachikawa 2017, JHEP 03, 189 (1-form center symmetries)
Cordova, Dumitrescu, Intriligator 2019, JHEP 02, 184 (2-group symmetries in N=2)
Argyres, Martone 2022, JHEP 05, 030 (generalized-symmetry classification of rank-2 N=2 SCFTs)

edges.description: Tensor-network program aspires that SymTFT (generalized-symmetry) bulk structures are computationally realizable as compositional outputs of MTC data combined with explicit tensor-network construction machinery (matrix product operators, PEPS, string-nets). Reading: 'generalized-symmetries [output] is compositional output of MTC [input] combined with tensor-network construction-data'. Literature-strength: STRONG. Sub-cases established: matrix product operator (MPO) categorical symmetries from MTC input have been explicitly constructed for the Z_2 toric code, doubled Ising, and several rational MTCs (Bridgeman-Williamson 2018; Lootens-Fuchs-Haegeman-Schweigert-Verstraete 2021). The Levin-Wen string-net model directly realizes the SymTFT bulk for any rational MTC input. Aspired for: full classification of SymTFT structures across all MTCs at higher rank, continuous-symmetry generalizations, non-Lagrangian SymTFT realizations. Status `partial` because specific cases work; full program is aspired. `targeted_by: tensor-network` records the program actively extending. cell_refs pin 4 representative MTCs whose tensor-network SymTFT realizations are either established (toric code, Ising) or actively pursued (doubled-Ising for Levin-Wen, Fibonacci for universal anyon TQC). Multi-edge with v17 edge `modular-tensor-categories specializes generalized-symmetries [established]`: the established specializes-edge captures the categorical-inclusion (MTC is a sub-case); this composition-edge captures the operational construction (tensor-networks realize SymTFT from MTC input).
citations: Bridgeman, Williamson 2018, Phys. Rev. B 97, 144439 (matrix product operator symmetries)
Lootens, Fuchs, Haegeman, Schweigert, Verstraete 2021, SciPost Phys. 10, 053 (MPO symmetries and intertwiners)
Levin, Wen 2005, Phys. Rev. B 71, 045110 (string-net condensation — SymTFT bulk for rational MTC)
Aasen, Mong, Fendley 2016, J. Phys. A 49, 354001 (Ising categorical symmetry on lattice)

edges.description: rg-info program aspires that SymTFT (generalized-symmetry) data derives from bootstrap CFT data via RG-flow continuation: tracking a UV bootstrap-allowed CFT down to the IR fixed point, the symmetry data (anomalies, higher-form charges, categorical structures) is preserved or transforms in controlled ways, ultimately determining the IR phase's SymTFT classification. Reading: 'generalized-symmetries [output] derives from bootstrap [UV input] via RG flow'. Same UV-IR direction as the v15.7 rg-info edge `cobordism → bootstrap [derives-from, aspired]`. Literature-strength: MEDIUM. Sub-cases established: the C-theorem and a-theorem (Zamolodchikov 1986; Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011) establish RG-monotonicity of central charges, which constrains the flow of bootstrap-allowed CFTs. Cordova-Dumitrescu 2-group analyses show that 2-group anomalies flow through RG with specific matching conditions, partially deriving SymTFT data from UV bootstrap data. Aspired for: general derivation of SymTFT classification (especially non-invertible / categorical cases) from bootstrap UV data alone. Status `partial` reflects this: the C/A-theorem case works rigorously, but the generalization to full categorical SymTFT classification is aspired. `targeted_by: rg-info` records the program. cell_refs pin 4 SymTFT cells whose UV-IR derivation from bootstrap is actively studied. Multi-edge with v17 edge `generalized-symmetries constrains cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions [established]`: the constrains-edge says SymTFT anomaly DATA narrows bootstrap-allowed CFTs (structural constraint); this derives-from-edge says SymTFT FRAMEWORK (the classification itself) is OPERATIONALLY obtained from RG-flow analysis of bootstrap CFTs. Distinct content; same FC-pair.
citations: Zamolodchikov 1986, JETP Lett. 43, 730 (c-theorem)
Komargodski, Schwimmer 2011, JHEP 12, 099 (a-theorem)
Cordova, Dumitrescu, Intriligator 2019, JHEP 02, 184 (RG flows of 2-group anomalies)
Apenko 2012, Physica A 391, 62 (information-theoretic RG)
Beny, Osborne 2015, New J. Phys. 17, 083005 (RG as quantum-information renormalization)

edges.description: ncg-instance program aspires that SymTFT structures arise as compositional outputs of cobordism-K-theory data combined with noncommutative-geometric spectral-triple machinery. Reading: 'generalized-symmetries [output] is compositional output of cobordism [K-theoretic input] combined with NCG spectral-triple construction-data'. The NCG framework treats K-theoretic data of a spectral triple as encoding anomaly-inflow content; SymTFT treats cobordism-classified bulk theories as encoding anomaly-inflow content for boundary symmetries. NCG aspires that these two K-theoretic anomaly-inflow pictures are compositionally related — that a spectral triple's K-theoretic data combines with NCG-specific quantization data to produce the corresponding SymTFT bulk. Literature-strength: WEAK. The structural overlap (both frameworks land in K-theory of anomaly inflow) is REAL but no concrete spectral-triple ↔ SymTFT realization has been worked out in the literature I am aware of as of 2026. The Connes-Marcolli 2007 spectral-action analysis and the Freed-Moore-Teleman 2022 SymTFT framework have not been bridged in either direction. Authoring this edge as aspired with weak literature backing is a faithful record of the NCG program's structural position: NCG reaches the K-theoretic anomaly-inflow territory (which IS SymTFT-adjacent) but has not yet developed concrete SymTFT-mediated constructions. Status `aspired` (no sub-cases established, unlike the tensor-network and rg-info edges which are partial). `targeted_by: ncg-instance`. cell_refs pin 3 cobordism cells with structural significance for the aspired connection: Ω_2^{Spin^c} = ℤ (IQHE-class anomaly), Ω_4^{Spin} = ℤ (4D bulk anomaly), Ω_4^{Pin+} = ℤ_16 (Wang-Senthil fermionic gauge anomaly). Multi-edge with v17 edge `generalized-symmetries specializes freed-hopkins-cobordism [established]`: the established specializes-edge captures categorical inclusion (SymTFT bulks are cobordism-classified, established); this composition-edge captures the aspired compositional construction (cobordism data + NCG spectral-triple data → explicit SymTFT realizations, not yet developed).
citations: Connes, Marcolli 2007 (noncommutative geometry and SM as spectral triple — K-theoretic foundation)
Freed, Moore, Teleman 2022, arXiv:2209.07471 (SymTFT topological symmetry framework — K-theoretic anomaly inflow)
Larsson 2014, J. Math. Phys. 55, 122102 (K-theory in NCG — partial bridge to anomaly-inflow K-theory)

edges.description: Hadronic states specialize spacetime-symmetry-groups: every hadron is an irreducible representation of the Poincaré group (specifying mass and spin J^P) together with internal symmetry quantum numbers. The classification axis J-PC reads directly off the Poincaré-representation little-group decomposition. cell_refs pin 6 representative hadron families whose Poincaré-representation content is canonical: pseudoscalars (0-+ ↔ massive scalar Poincaré rep with intrinsic parity), vectors (1-- ↔ vector Poincaré rep), tensors (2++ ↔ rank-2 symmetric Poincaré rep), spin-1/2 baryons, spin-3/2 baryons, and the J/ψ / Υ vector spectra. This is the cleanest phenomenon-to-structural-FC edge in the map: hadronic-states (phenomenon-FC, the actual particles) hangs on spacetime-symmetry-groups (structural-FC, the representation theory). Status established (the Poincaré-representation organization of hadrons is foundational physics).
citations: Wigner 1939, Ann. Math. 40, 149 (irreducible reps of Poincaré group)
Weinberg 1995, Quantum Theory of Fields Vol. I ch. 2 (Poincaré representations and particle states)

edges.description: Hadronic states derive from SM rep content via QCD binding dynamics: each hadron is a color-singlet bound state of quarks (and gluons) drawn from the SM quark content (cell-uR-g1, cell-dR-g1, etc. for light quarks; the charm and bottom cells for heavy hadrons). The DERIVATION is structurally established (color confinement + QCD Lagrangian → hadrons emerge as IR degrees of freedom) but QUANTITATIVELY partial: while quark content fully determines the existence and quantum numbers of light hadrons via SU(3) flavor classification, the precise masses, decay widths, form factors, and exotic-state spectra require non-perturbative QCD calculations (lattice QCD, effective field theories, quark-model approximations). Status `partial` reflects this: structural derivation is established, full quantitative derivation is partial. cell_refs pin 9 cells spanning light, charm, bottom, and exotic regimes — each derives from specific quark-content compositions.
citations: Gross, Wilczek 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1343 (QCD asymptotic freedom)
Politzer 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1346 (independent QCD asymptotic freedom)
Wilson 1974, Phys. Rev. D 10, 2445 (confinement via lattice QCD)
Aoki et al. (FLAG) 2024, Eur. Phys. J. C 84, 897 (lattice flavor QCD review)

edges.description: Hadronic states specialize ADE Lie algebras via flavor SU(3) classification: light hadrons are organized into multiplets of A_2 = SU(3) flavor representations. The pseudoscalar nonet (cell-h-pi) is 8+1 of SU(3); the vector nonet (cell-h-rho) is also 8+1; the baryon octet (cell-h-N) is the 8 of SU(3); the baryon decuplet (cell-h-Delta) is the 10 of SU(3). This is the Gell-Mann eightfold-way classification: flavor SU(3) = A_2 Lie algebra representations classify the light hadron multiplets. The Ω- prediction (cell-h-Delta, see predictive_yield) was made by completing the 10-of-SU(3) and predicting the mass of the missing sss member — the most famous Mendeleev-style success in hadronic physics. Status established for light hadrons; the SU(3) classification breaks down once heavier quarks (c, b) are included (those require SU(4), SU(5) extensions or heavy-quark effective theory). cell_refs pin 4 SU(3)-multiplet cells.
citations: Gell-Mann 1962, Phys. Rev. 125, 1067 (eightfold way)
Ne'eman 1961, Nucl. Phys. 26, 222 (independent eightfold way)
Okubo 1962, Prog. Theor. Phys. 27, 949 (SU(3) mass formula)

edges.description: ads-cft-instance program aspires that hadronic-spectrum data derives from bootstrap-allowed CFT data: in the large-N limit of QCD (Polchinski, Strassler, Maldacena), the theory is approximately conformal at high energies, and the hadronic spectrum emerges as the spectrum of bound states in a holographic dual 5D AdS theory. The bootstrap-allowed CFT structure constrains which hadrons are realizable; conversely, hadron observations constrain bootstrap-derived holographic-QCD models. Established for: large-N pure-glue holographic QCD glueball-spectrum predictions (Hashimoto-Tahara 1998; Brower-Mathur-Tan 2000) qualitatively match lattice glueball spectrum. Aspired for: full hadronic-spectrum derivation including chiral-symmetry-breaking patterns and exotic-state predictions from holographic QCD. cell_refs pin 4 hadrons whose holographic-QCD derivations are actively pursued. Status aspired; the program is real and active but the full hadronic-spectrum-from-bootstrap derivation remains incomplete.
citations: Polchinski, Strassler 2002, Phys. Rev. Lett. 88, 031601 (string theory and hard scattering)
Hashimoto, Tahara 1998 (early holographic QCD)
Brower, Mathur, Tan 2000, Nucl. Phys. B 587, 249 (glueball spectrum from holography)
Erlich, Katz, Son, Stephanov 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 95, 261602 (QCD and a holographic model of hadrons)

edges.description: Each FQH state is categorically-equivalent to a specific modular tensor category — the MTC that describes its anyon content. The correspondence (Kitaev 2006; Wen 2017): the topological order of an FQH state is fully captured by its MTC (containing the anyon types, fusion rules, braiding statistics, and modular S/T matrices). Established mappings: ν=1/3 Laughlin ↔ Z_3 abelian MTC (cell-mtc-r3-z3); ν=1/5 Laughlin ↔ Z_5 MTC; ν=2/5 Jain ↔ specific abelian MTC of rank 5; ν=5/2 Pfaffian-candidate ↔ Ising MTC (cell-mtc-r3-ising); ν=12/5 Read-Rezayi candidate ↔ Z_3 parafermion / Fibonacci MTC (cell-mtc-r2-fibonacci). Status `partial` reflects: (i) for cleanly-identified states (1/3, 2/5, Laughlin family), the MTC identification is established; (ii) for states with disputed identification (5/2 between Pfaffian/anti-Pfaffian/PH-Pfaffian; 12/5 RR identification), the MTC correspondence is partial pending experimental resolution. cell_refs pin 6 representative FQH cells; the target-side MTC cells are referenced in narrative (no schema field currently exists for cell-to-cell mapping; surfaces v20 candidate `paired_cell_refs`). THIS IS THE CENTRAL PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE OF THE MAP — demonstrating that the structural backbone hooks cleanly into phenomenon-level data when the correspondence exists.
citations: Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2 (anyons and FQH)
Wen 2017, Rev. Mod. Phys. 89, 041004 (topological order review)
Bonderson, Slingerland 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 125323 (12/5 MTC identification analysis)

edges.description: MTC consistency conditions structurally constrain which FQH states can exist. Specifically: (i) the modularity (non-degeneracy of S-matrix) requires the anyon content of any FQH state to form a complete MTC; (ii) the pentagon-hexagon coherence conditions on fusion / braiding data constrain quasiparticle interactions; (iii) the Vafa theorem constrains the central charge of edge CFTs; (iv) the genus-g ground-state degeneracy is determined by the MTC rank. Together these MTC consistency conditions narrow the allowed space of FQH topological orders — many naively-plausible FQH wavefunctions are ruled out because their would-be anyon content fails to form a consistent MTC. **FOURTH `constrains` EDGE; SECOND CONSECUTIVE NOT POINTING AT BOOTSTRAP** (HCB v18 was the first non-bootstrap target). The v18-refined finding holds: `constrains` targets exclusion-region-style classifications regardless of node identity. FQH-states is an exclusion-region-style phenomenon-FC (allowed topological orders constrained by structural consistency). Status `established`. cell_refs pin 4 MTC cells whose constraints on FQH states are canonical examples. **SIXTH MULTI-EDGE FC PAIR**: paired with the categorically-equivalent/partial edge above. Different content: cat-equiv says each FQH state maps to an MTC; constrains says MTC structure narrows what FQH states are realizable.
citations: Wen 1990, Phys. Rev. B 41, 12838 (early topological-order constraints)
Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2 (MTC structure of anyons)
Lan, Kong, Wen 2017, Phys. Rev. B 95, 235140 (classification of 2+1D topological orders via MTC)

edges.description: FQH states derive from generalized-symmetry data: the 2+1D Chern-Simons effective theory of an FQH state IS a SymTFT bulk realization, with the 1-form symmetries acting on Wilson loops corresponding to the anyon types. Specifically, abelian FQH states (Laughlin, Jain) have abelian 1-form symmetries directly readable off their K-matrix; non-abelian FQH states (Pfaffian, Read-Rezayi) have categorical 1-form symmetries forming non-trivial fusion categories. The derivation is established for the abelian case (Wen's K-matrix formulation IS a SymTFT formulation); partial for non-abelian / categorical cases where the SymTFT-to-FQH map is being developed. Status `partial`. cell_refs pin 4 FQH cells whose SymTFT derivation is canonical. Connects this phenomenon-FC to the structural SymTFT framework via its bulk Chern-Simons description.
citations: Wen 1995, Adv. Phys. 44, 405 (K-matrix formulation; ESSENTIALLY a SymTFT for abelian FQH)
Freed, Moore, Teleman 2022, arXiv:2209.07471 (SymTFT framework)
Bhardwaj, Schäfer-Nameki 2024, Phys. Rep. 1051, 1 (generalized symmetries review covering FQH applications)

edges.description: ads-cft-instance program aspires that FQH topological orders derive from bootstrap-allowed CFT data: the (1+1)D edge theories of FQH states ARE conformal field theories, and the bootstrap-allowed-CFT region constrains which edge CFTs (and hence which bulk FQH topological orders) can exist. Specifically: edge CFTs at Laughlin ν=1/m are c=1 chiral bosons; edge CFTs at Pfaffian 5/2 include a Majorana sector; edge CFTs at Read-Rezayi 12/5 are Z_3 parafermion CFTs. Each of these is a specific bootstrap-allowed CFT; bootstrap constraints on these CFTs structurally constrain FQH topological orders. AdS/CFT framing: a 2+1D bulk topological order corresponds to a 1+1D boundary CFT under the bulk-boundary correspondence. Status `aspired` — the bulk-boundary correspondence is established for specific cases but full derivation of FQH classification from bootstrap is aspirational. cell_refs pin 3 FQH cells with the cleanest edge-CFT realizations.
citations: Wen 1990, Phys. Rev. B 41, 12838 (FQH edge as chiral CFT)
Cappelli, Trugenberger, Zemba 1993, Nucl. Phys. B 396, 465 (W_1+infinity algebra of FQH edges)

edges.description: Every compact astrophysical object is a (near-)solution to Einstein's field equations and therefore specializes the cell-stsym-diff cell (general-coordinate / diffeomorphism invariance) of spacetime-symmetry-groups. Asymptotically-flat compact objects (which is the standard case for isolated WDs, NSs, stellar-mass BHs, and most galactic SMBHs to good approximation) additionally have asymptotic Poincaré invariance — specializing cell-stsym-poincare at the asymptotic-region level. Within the strong-field interior, the symmetry reduces: Schwarzschild BHs and non-rotating NSs have SO(3) × time-translation; Kerr BHs and rotating NSs have axisymmetry SO(2) × time-translation; merging binaries have no continuous spatial symmetry. cell_refs pin 6 representative compact-object cells whose Poincaré + diff-invariance specialization is canonical. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR THE ASTROPHYSICS SECTOR. Status `established` — the GR description of compact objects is foundational physics, tested at the percent level by EHT shadow imaging, BBH-ringdown spectroscopy, and pulsar-timing tests. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Schwarzschild 1916, Sitzungsber. Preuss. Akad. Wiss. (vacuum spherical solution)
Kerr 1963, Phys. Rev. Lett. 11, 237 (rotating BH solution)
Oppenheimer, Volkoff 1939, Phys. Rev. 55, 374 (TOV equation for NSs)
Will 2014, Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (GR tests review)
EHT Collaboration 2019, 2022 (BH shadow tests of Kerr metric)

edges.description: Neutron-star interiors derive from hadronic-states physics extended to the dense-uniform-matter regime. Specifically: (i) the outer-crust nuclear-matter composition is direct hadronic spectroscopy at low density (nuclei populated from the chart-of-nuclides, a subset of cell-h-N and adjacent baryon cells); (ii) the inner-crust + outer-core composition (npe + μ matter) is determined by hadronic-state thresholds (when does hyperon population become favorable? — depends on the Λ, Σ, Ξ hyperon thresholds from cell-h-Lambdac-style spectra extrapolated to finite chemical potential, the 'hyperon puzzle'); (iii) inner-core composition possibly involves color-superconducting quark matter or a hadron-quark crossover region — connecting hadronic-states to (presumed-future) dense-quark-matter classification. Status `partial` because: NS-interior physics involves hadronic matter at conditions (high density, low T, finite μ_B and μ_Q) that are FAR outside the standard hadronic-states domain (T ≈ 0, μ ≈ 0, bound-state spectroscopy). The connection is real but mediated by EOS-modeling that extrapolates from spectroscopic input. cell_refs pin 3 NS-related cells whose hadronic-derivation is most operationally active. Literature strength: MEDIUM (connection well-developed for low-density crust; speculative for inner-core quark-matter component).
citations: Lattimer, Prakash 2016, Phys. Rep. 621, 127 (NS observations and dense-matter physics)
Glendenning 2000, Compact Stars (hadronic NS interior)
Alford et al. 2005, Nature 445, E7 (color-superconducting quark matter in NS cores)
Baym, Hatsuda, Kojo, Powell, Song, Takatsuka 2018, Rep. Prog. Phys. 81, 056902 (quark-hadron crossover review)

edges.description: Compact-object matter content derives from SM rep content via the matter species that populate compact objects: white dwarfs are supported by electron-degeneracy pressure (cell-eR-g1 and corresponding lepton cells of sm-rep-content) embedded in an ionic Coulomb lattice (light nuclei drawn from QCD-bound states of light quarks — cell-uR-g1, cell-dR-g1). Neutron stars are supported by nucleon degeneracy + nuclear-force repulsion; their composition involves neutrons (n = udd), protons (p = uud), electrons (β-equilibrium), and at higher density possibly muons, hyperons, kaons. The 'derives-from' is straightforward but partial in the sense that the dense-matter-physics machinery that maps SM rep content to compact-object pressure-support is itself a layer of physics not represented in sm-rep-content directly (the Chandrasekhar mass for WDs, the TOV maximum mass for NSs both DEPEND on SM matter content but require the EOS-modeling layer to be evaluated). cell_refs pin 3 WD/NS cells whose SM-content basis is most direct. The hadronic-states edge (separate) handles the bound-state-spectrum connection; this edge handles the underlying-SM-particle connection. Literature strength: STRONG for WDs (electron-degeneracy physics is canonical); MEDIUM for NSs (nuclear-force modeling is empirically less well-constrained at NS-interior densities).
citations: Chandrasekhar 1931, Astrophys. J. 74, 81 (Chandrasekhar mass from electron rep content + GR)
Oppenheimer, Volkoff 1939, Phys. Rev. 55, 374 (TOV from nucleon rep content + GR)
Shapiro, Teukolsky 1983, Black Holes, White Dwarfs, and Neutron Stars chs. 2-3

edges.description: The ads-cft-instance program aspires that black-hole thermodynamic and microstate-structure properties derive from bootstrap-allowed CFT data via the AdS/CFT holographic dictionary. Specifically: (i) Strominger-Vafa 1996 derived the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy of specific BPS BHs by counting BPS states in the dual CFT — a partial derivation establishing the program's empirical bite for a special class of BHs (extremal, supersymmetric, mostly AdS); (ii) general astrophysical (Kerr, non-extremal, asymptotically-flat) BHs are not yet derived from CFT in this sense — that's the aspired part; (iii) the Page-curve / island-formula program (Almheiri et al. 2019+) extends the bootstrap-CFT-derivation of BH thermodynamics to evaporating BHs in specific holographic setups, with partial bite for the information paradox. cell_refs pin 3 BH cells where the holographic-bootstrap derivation is most actively pursued (SMBHs because of their thermodynamic importance; merging stellar-mass BHs because of GW horizon-test relevance). Status `aspired` because the bridge from holographic-CFT data to astrophysical BH observations is still under construction. Literature strength: STRONG for the BPS-BH entropy-counting (foundational); WEAK for general astrophysical BH derivation (program is real and active but most claims remain at gestural-to-partial level for astrophysical applicability). This edge mirrors the FQH→bootstrap-aspired and hadrons→bootstrap-aspired edges in the existing data (same ads-cft program, different phenomenon-FC targets).
citations: Strominger, Vafa 1996, Phys. Lett. B 379, 99 (BPS-BH entropy from CFT counting)
Maldacena 1998, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 231 (AdS/CFT correspondence)
Witten 1998, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 253 (AdS/CFT thermodynamics)
Almheiri, Engelhardt, Marolf, Maxfield 2019, JHEP 12, 063 (island formula); Penington 2020, JHEP 09, 002 (page curve)

edges.description: Each material whose symmetry-class axis value lies in the strict tenfold-way (A, AIII, AI, BDI, D, DIII, AII, CII, C, CI) specializes the corresponding tenfold-way cell (cell-{class}-d{dimension} in tenfold-way's coordinate scheme). HgTe and 1T'-WTe₂ specialize cell-AII-d2; Bi₂Se₃-family, Sb₂Te₃, Bi₂Te₃ specialize cell-AII-d3; FeTe/Se specializes cell-DIII-d3; InSb/Al wire specializes cell-D-d1; predicted class-CI 3D specializes cell-CI-d3 (currently empty in tenfold-way's material-realization sense). THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR TOPOLOGICAL-MATTER. The tenfold-way cells defined formally; this FC's cells are specific materials; the edge is `specializes` because each material IS a particular point in a tenfold-way cell's phase space. Status `established` — the tenfold-way classification is a settled mathematical theorem (Kitaev 2009 K-theory derivation), and the material identifications are unambiguous within the noninteracting domain. Literature strength: STRONG. Materials with crystalline / magnetic / higher-order refinement labels are not directly captured by this edge — those go through subsequent edges to crystalline-symmetry-extension classifications or to freed-hopkins-cobordism.
citations: Schnyder, Ryu, Furusaki, Ludwig 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195125 (tenfold-way derivation)
Kitaev 2009, AIP Conf. Proc. 1134, 22 (K-theory / Bott periodicity)
Ryu, Schnyder, Furusaki, Ludwig 2010, New J. Phys. 12, 065010 (extended table)

edges.description: The Freed-Hopkins cobordism classification of SPT phases (Freed-Hopkins 2021 final paper; earlier conjectures from Kapustin 2014 and Freed 2014) gives an extended classification of symmetry-protected topological phases that subsumes the tenfold-way for free fermions and extends to interacting systems. Each material's SPT class corresponds to a cobordism-invariant of its symmetry group: time-reversal protected TIs in class AII map to ΩPin⁺_{d+1}(pt) classes; magnetic-symmetry-protected phases (axion insulators) map to magnetic-cobordism classes; higher-order phases involve crystalline-extension cobordism groups. Status `partial` because: (i) the noninteracting tenfold-way → cobordism map is fully understood and provides the framework for noninteracting TIs / TSCs; (ii) the interacting extension via cobordism is well-developed in principle but very few material candidates exist for the interacting-only SPT phases that distinguish cobordism from the tenfold-way (fractional TIs are the prime target, none confirmed); (iii) crystalline extensions of cobordism (Thorngren-Else 2018 etc.) have classification machinery but sparse material realization. cell_refs pin 5 cells of variable SPT-cobordism relevance: HgTe and Bi₂Se₃-family are noninteracting AII → trivially captured by free-fermion cobordism, but listed because the cobordism framework provides their classification at the most general level; Bi hoti and MnBi₂Te₄ have crystalline / magnetic refinements that REQUIRE cobordism beyond tenfold-way; fractional TI is the canonical predicted target of interacting cobordism classification. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework, MEDIUM for direct material applicability beyond noninteracting cases.
citations: Kapustin 2014, arXiv:1404.6659 (cobordism approach to SPTs)
Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (final cobordism classification of invertible TQFTs)
Thorngren, Else 2018, Phys. Rev. X 8, 011040 (crystalline SPT cobordism)

edges.description: The generalized-symmetries / SymTFT framework (Gaiotto-Kapustin-Seiberg-Willett 2015 and subsequent) classifies symmetries by their topological-defect content: 0-form, 1-form, and higher-form symmetries; ordinary group symmetries and categorical symmetries; T-folds and crystalline-symmetry extensions of these. Several topological-matter phases are protected by symmetries that go beyond on-site 0-form group symmetries: (i) HOTIs (cell-hoti-bismuth) and HOTSCs (cell-predicted-higher-order-DIII-TSC) are protected by crystalline rotational / mirror symmetries that act on extended subspaces and have been recast in SymTFT framework; (ii) axion-insulator phases (cell-axion-MnBi2Te4-candidate, cell-predicted-intrinsic-axion-clean) involve combined T × τ (time-reversal × half-translation) symmetry whose SymTFT analysis is recent and partial; (iii) the recently-explored 'higher-symmetry-protected' interpretation of some HOTI phases connects to higher-form-symmetry phenomenology. Status `partial` because the SymTFT framework for crystalline / magnetic topological-matter classification is actively developing — the framework applies in principle but the systematic mapping is still under construction (Thorngren-Else 2018 onwards). cell_refs pin 4 cells where the SymTFT / generalized-symmetry classification matters most operationally. Literature strength: MEDIUM (real framework with growing applicability; not all topological-matter cells need it).
citations: Gaiotto, Kapustin, Seiberg, Willett 2015, JHEP 02, 172 (generalized global symmetries)
Thorngren, Else 2018, Phys. Rev. X 8, 011040 (crystalline gauging)
Choi, Cordova, Hsin, Lam, Shao 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 111601 (non-invertible symmetries and SymTFT)

edges.description: The boundary anyon content of 2D topological superconductors is classified by modular tensor categories. Specifically: a 2D class D topological superconductor with a Majorana zero mode at a vortex core realizes the Ising MTC at the boundary defect level — the MZM is the σ anyon of the Ising MTC; vortex-MZM braiding implements non-Abelian Ising braiding rules. Class DIII 2D TSCs with helical Majorana edge modes have chiral central charge c = 1 from a pair of counter-propagating Majorana modes; the edge CFT is a free Majorana × Majorana. Class D 2D TSC chiral edge has c = 1/2. Higher-order DIII TSC hinge modes (cell-predicted-higher-order-DIII-TSC) carry helical Majorana modes whose braiding obeys MTC-classifiable rules at codimension-2 defects. Status `partial` because: (i) the MTC-classification of MZM-defect braiding is theoretically well-developed (Nayak-Simon-Stern-Freedman-Das Sarma 2008 review); (ii) experimental confirmation of MZM-Ising-MTC braiding remains incomplete — FeTe/Se signatures contested, the 2018 InSb/Al claim retracted, no clean braiding experiment confirmed as of 2026. cell_refs pin 3 cells where the MTC-derivation operates: the FeTe/Se candidate (Ising-MTC braiding aspired), the retracted InSb/Al cell (the falsified attempt to access Ising-MTC braiding), and the predicted higher-order DIII TSC. Literature strength: STRONG for the theory, WEAK for empirical realization. This edge is also a structural-data point: phenomenon-FC topological-matter connects to structural-FC MTC, completing a triangle with fractional-quantum-hall-states (which also connects to MTC, by categorically-equivalent — a different edge subtype reflecting that FQH states ARE MTC realizations whereas TSC-MZM-defects DERIVE-FROM MTC at the boundary).
citations: Nayak, Simon, Stern, Freedman, Das Sarma 2008, Rev. Mod. Phys. 80, 1083 (non-Abelian anyons and topological quantum computation)
Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2 (Honeycomb model — Ising MTC at the boundary of class D 2D TSC)
Read, Green 2000, Phys. Rev. B 61, 10267 (Pfaffian / Majorana edge mode)

edges.description: Neutrino-sector phenomenology specializes the leptonic sector of the SM rep content. Specifically: the three active neutrino flavors (ν_e, ν_μ, ν_τ) are the upper components of the SU(2)_L lepton doublets in cell-nuL-g1, cell-nuL-g2, cell-nuL-g3 of sm-rep-content. The PMNS mixing matrix is the lepton-sector analog of the CKM matrix; its parameterization follows directly from the structure of SM rep content + Yukawa coupling diagonalization. Right-handed neutrinos N_R required for type-I seesaw are gauge-singlet extensions of the SM rep content — predicted within the SM extension and naturally accommodated. Status `established` because the framework of SM-rep-content + PMNS mixing is the textbook understanding of neutrino phenomenology. Literature strength: STRONG. cell_refs pin the 7 cells of this FC most directly tied to SM rep content (the 4 measured oscillation parameters, the mass-squared differences, the Dirac-vs-Majorana question, and the seesaw mechanism which extends SM rep content with N_R). THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR THE NEUTRINO SECTOR.
citations: Pontecorvo 1957, Sov. Phys. JETP 6, 429 (early ν oscillation proposal)
Maki, Nakagawa, Sakata 1962, Prog. Theor. Phys. 28, 870 (PMNS matrix)
Mohapatra, Pal 2004, Massive Neutrinos in Physics and Astrophysics

edges.description: Neutrino-oscillation phenomenology specializes Poincaré invariance + CPT theorem of relativistic quantum mechanics. Specifically: (i) the oscillation phase φ = Δm² L / (2E) requires Lorentz-covariant time-evolution of mass eigenstates and is a direct prediction of Poincaré-invariant QM; (ii) CPT invariance constrains ν and antineutrino mixing matrices to be related (PMNS for ν vs PMNS* for antineutrino); (iii) leptonic CP violation (δ_CP non-trivial) is the symmetry-breaking effect — its measurement is a direct test of CP within the lepton sector, analogous to the CKM-sector CP violation already established. SN 1987A neutrino time-of-flight bounds are themselves a test of Lorentz invariance at the eV-mass scale. Status `established` because the Poincaré-invariance framework is settled physics and neutrino phenomenology specializes it directly. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG (depends on how stringently 'specializes' is read: framework-level specialization is strong; specific observational tests like Lorentz-violation bounds are partial).
citations: Bilenky, Pontecorvo 1978, Phys. Rep. 41, 225
Particle Data Group 2024 (CPT tests in the lepton sector)

edges.description: Grand-unification programs aspire that neutrino masses and mixings derive from GUT-scale rep content. Specifically: (i) in SO(10) GUTs, the 16-plet of SO(10) contains one right-handed neutrino per generation, naturally implementing seesaw; (ii) in SU(5) embeddings, N_R is an SU(5)-singlet but its Majorana mass scale M_R is naturally tied to GUT-symmetry-breaking scales; (iii) leptogenesis CP asymmetries arise from CP phases in the N_R Majorana mass matrix, which in SO(10) is linked to the same Yukawa matrices that give up-quark masses — connecting neutrino-CP to quark-sector observables. Status `aspired` because while the SO(10) / SU(5)-extension framework consistently accommodates neutrino-sector phenomenology, no specific GUT-scale prediction has been confirmed at terrestrial precision — the connection is at the framework level, not the parameter-by-parameter level. Literature strength: MEDIUM (framework is well-established and SO(10) is a common embedding choice; specific predictions involving GUT-scale CP phases / Majorana masses await indirect tests via leptogenesis confirmation or proton-decay measurements that would also fix GUT scales). cell_refs pin the 3 cells where the GUT-derivation is most active. This edge mirrors the su5-aspired derivation of proton-decay predictions and other neutrino-adjacent GUT observables.
citations: Mohapatra, Senjanović 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 912 (SO(10) seesaw)
Buchmüller, Di Bari, Plümacher 2005, Ann. Phys. 315, 305 (leptogenesis review with GUT motivation)
Babu, Mohapatra 1993, Phys. Rev. Lett. 70, 2845 (predictions in SO(10) neutrino phenomenology)

edges.description: Each real critical system specializes a specific bootstrap-allowed CFT — the unique CFT at the corresponding Wilson-Fisher fixed point of the universality class. The bootstrap exclusion regions in cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions pin the critical-exponent values to high precision (3D-Ising ν = 0.6299709..., 3D O(2) ν = 0.6719..., 3D O(3) ν = 0.7117..., etc.); the real systems must lie on the bootstrap-allowed boundary curves. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR THE CRITICAL-PHENOMENA SECTOR, explicit in PROJECT_GOAL_PHENOMENON_LAYER §5. Status `established` for the universality-class identifications: every cell's universality class is settled. The exponent-value match is precision-confirmed for 3D-Ising and 3D-Heisenberg cells, but contains the 3D-O(2) / 4He tension at high precision (sub-edge-status note: the edge as a whole is `established` but contains a sub-tension cell). Literature strength: STRONG. cell_refs pin 8 cells where the bootstrap pairing is most operationally active.
citations: El-Showk, Paulos, Poland, Rychkov, Simmons-Duffin, Vichi 2014, JSTAT 8, P08015 (3D Ising bootstrap)
Kos, Poland, Simmons-Duffin 2014, JHEP 11, 109 (3D Ising precision)
Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2020, Phys. Rev. D 101, 084017 (3D O(2))
Chester et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 105013 (3D O(3))

edges.description: At a second-order critical point, the relevant field theory has emergent scale invariance and (under broad conditions, in particular for unitary critical theories) full conformal invariance — i.e., the spacetime-symmetry-group enhances from the lattice / classical-Galilean symmetry of the bare microscopic theory to the conformal group SO(d+1,1) (or SO(d,2) in Lorentzian signature). This specializes the cell-stsym-conformal cell of spacetime-symmetry-groups. The 'emergent' qualifier matters: it's a long-wavelength enhancement, not a microscopic symmetry. Status `established` because conformal invariance at criticality is foundational (Polyakov 1970) and tested by the fact that bootstrap (which assumes conformal invariance) gives correct critical exponents. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG (framework is well-established; the conformal-invariance specialization is direct).
citations: Polyakov 1970, JETP Lett. 12, 381 (scale → conformal at criticality)
Cardy 1996, Scaling and Renormalization in Statistical Physics (Cambridge)

edges.description: Generalized symmetries / SymTFT framework identifies higher-form symmetries and topological defects in critical theories. Specifically: (i) 2D XY / BKT transition is driven by vortex (codimension-2 in 2D) unbinding — a 1-form symmetry breaking in the SymTFT framework; (ii) Ising / O(N) Wilson-Fisher CFTs have categorical-symmetry structure relevant for boundary / interface critical phenomena; (iii) the generalized-symmetry classification of critical theories is being actively developed (Choi-Cordova-Hsin-Lam-Shao 2022 and subsequent), with much of the structure relevant to surface and disorder-induced classes rather than bulk Wilson-Fisher. Status `partial` because the SymTFT framework applies in principle but the systematic re-derivation of all critical universality classes through SymTFT is incomplete. Literature strength: MEDIUM. cell_refs pin 3 cells where the generalized-symmetry structure most directly applies (2D XY / BKT vortex transition; 4He λ as 3D O(2); 2D Ising boundary structure).
citations: Gaiotto, Kapustin, Seiberg, Willett 2015, JHEP 02, 172
Choi, Cordova, Hsin, Lam, Shao 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 111601
Komargodski, Sulejmanpasic, Unsal 2018, JHEP 12, 040 (SymTFT for symmetry-protected critical theories)

edges.description: 2D conformal field theories at criticality have rational-CFT structure (when applicable) classified by modular tensor categories. The 2D Ising CFT (c = 1/2, Onsager 1944) has the chiral half = Ising MTC, the same MTC that classifies anyons in 2D class D topological superconductors (cf. topological-insulator-and-superconductor-materials edge derives-from MTC). The 2D BKT / 4He film transition is described by a c = 1 free-boson CFT with continuous vortex-operator spectrum — connects to MTC structure for compactified-boson radii at rational points. Status `partial` because: (i) the MTC-classification applies cleanly only to the 2D cells; 3D critical theories have different formal organizing structure (the 3D bootstrap, edge edge-xc-crit-specializes-bootstrap); (ii) the rational-CFT vs irrational-CFT distinction matters — only some 2D criticality has clean MTC structure. cell_refs pin the 2 cells where the MTC-derivation operates. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework, MEDIUM for direct application to physical critical systems. This edge mirrors the topological-insulator-and-superconductor-materials → MTC edge (v22) — both phenomenon-FCs derive their 2D-critical-boundary content from MTC.
citations: Belavin, Polyakov, Zamolodchikov 1984, Nucl. Phys. B 241, 333 (2D CFT framework)
Moore, Seiberg 1989, Commun. Math. Phys. 123, 177 (MTC framework)
Read, Green 2000, Phys. Rev. B 61, 10267 (Ising-MTC at boundary of 2D class D TSC)

edges.description: Particle DM candidates derive from extensions of SM rep content. Specifically: (i) WIMPs in SUSY scenarios derive from the supersymmetric extension of the SM (neutralino = mixture of bino + wino + higgsinos, all in SM-gauge-group representations extended by superpartners); (ii) QCD axion couples to SM gauge bosons via anomaly, derives from PQ-extension of SM with axion as Goldstone of broken PQ U(1); (iii) sterile neutrinos are gauge-singlet right-handed neutrinos, extending the SM rep content with N_R; (iv) dark photon is a kinetic-mixing extension of SM U(1)_Y with U(1)' (mediated by ε F_μν F'^μν); (v) gravitino-LSP is the spin-3/2 superpartner of graviton in SUSY-extended supergravity. Status `partial` because: (a) some candidates (PBHs, MACHOs, fuzzy-DM-from-stringy-axiverse) do not derive from SM rep content — PBHs are gravitational, MACHOs are baryonic-compact, fuzzy-DM derives from stringy / cosmological structures beyond SM; (b) for the candidates that do derive from SM extensions, the derivation is conjectural at the level of specific BSM-extension predictions. cell_refs pin 7 cells where the SM-rep derivation operates most directly. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR PARTICLE-DM CANDIDATES. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (DM-as-BSM-particle is a major program); MEDIUM for specific candidate predictions.
citations: Bertone, Hooper, Silk 2005, Phys. Rep. 405, 279 (DM-from-BSM-SM framework)
Jungman, Kamionkowski, Griest 1996, Phys. Rep. 267, 195 (SUSY-LSP DM review)
Holdom 1986, Phys. Lett. B 166, 196 (dark-photon kinetic mixing)

edges.description: All DM candidates — particle or compact-object — are gravitating matter satisfying Poincaré invariance + diffeomorphism invariance. For particle candidates, the connection is via standard quantum-field theory on a Poincaré-invariant background. For compact-object candidates (PBHs, MACHOs), the connection is more direct: each PBH or MACHO is a GR solution (Schwarzschild / Kerr for PBHs; ordinary stellar-mass solutions for MACHOs) and therefore specializes the spacetime-symmetry-groups cells in the same sense as compact-astrophysical-objects (v21). Fuzzy DM specializes Poincaré invariance via its bosonic-field action on Minkowski background. Status `established` — GR + Poincaré framework is settled and DM candidates satisfy it as a baseline. Literature strength: MEDIUM (framework-level specialization; specific tests of Lorentz-invariance for DM candidates are limited).
citations: Will 2014, Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (GR tests overview)
Carr, Kühnel 2020 (PBHs as GR solutions)

edges.description: Several DM candidates aspire to derive from GUT-extended frameworks: (i) SUSY-LSP WIMPs are naturally embedded in GUT-extended SUSY (where the GUT-symmetry-breaking scale sets the SUSY-breaking-mediation scale); (ii) sterile-ν as N_R derives most naturally from SO(10) 16-plet which contains N_R as a singlet under SU(5) ⊂ SO(10) — gives keV-scale sterile-ν via seesaw at high scales; (iii) gravitino-LSP-DM connects to SUSY-breaking-mediation which is most naturally embedded in GUT frameworks; (iv) string-axiverse ALPs derive from string-theoretic compactifications that include GUT-style gauge structure. Status `aspired` because no specific GUT-derivation has been observationally confirmed; the connections are at the framework level. cell_refs pin 4 cells where the GUT-derivation is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM. This edge mirrors the neutrino-sector-phenomenology v23 edge derives-from su5-gut-rep-content — both phenomenon-FCs target su5/GUT as the most-natural high-scale derivation framework.
citations: Mohapatra, Senjanović 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 912 (SO(10) seesaw → sterile-ν DM)
Jungman, Kamionkowski, Griest 1996 (SUSY-LSP in GUT frameworks)
Arvanitaki et al. 2010 (string axiverse)

edges.description: Cosmological observations specialize the FRW (Friedmann-Robertson-Walker) solutions of general relativity, which specialize spacetime-symmetry-groups. Specifically: (i) FRW cosmology is a specific class of GR solutions with maximally-symmetric spatial slices (specializes cell-stsym-maximal-isotropy or similar in spacetime-symmetry-groups); (ii) the CMB acoustic-peak structure is sensitive to the global FRW expansion + GR perturbation theory; (iii) the BAO sound-horizon is a clean GR-encoded length scale; (iv) spatial curvature Ω_K bounds the deviation from flat FRW; (v) the Hubble constant measures the FRW expansion rate; (vi) primordial gravitational waves are tensor modes of the FRW perturbation theory. Status `established` — FRW-cosmology + GR-perturbation-theory is the foundational framework for cosmology. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR COSMOLOGY.
citations: Friedmann 1922; Robertson 1935; Walker 1937 (FRW framework)
Mukhanov 2005, Physical Foundations of Cosmology
Weinberg 2008, Cosmology

edges.description: Several cosmological observables derive directly from SM rep content. Specifically: (i) BBN-era nuclear physics involves SM-content nucleons (proton, neutron) and SM-content nuclear-physics cross-sections — primordial D/H, ⁴He/H, ⁷Li/H are computed from SM particle physics + BBN framework; (ii) recombination-epoch atomic physics (the optical-depth τ_reio and the acoustic-oscillation damping) involves SM-content electrons + protons; (iii) the cosmological Σm_ν bound depends on SM neutrino content; (iv) CMB-acoustic-peak heights depend on SM neutrino effective number N_eff (Planck constrains N_eff = 2.99 ± 0.17, consistent with 3 SM neutrinos). Status `partial` because: (a) some cosmological observables depend on BSM physics that the SM-rep-content FC doesn't capture (DM candidates, DE candidates); (b) the BBN nuclear cross-sections are partly experimentally-measured rather than derived. cell_refs pin 5 cells where SM-rep derivation is most direct. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (BBN, recombination, neutrino-decoupling are all SM-derived).
citations: Wagoner, Fowler, Hoyle 1967, Astrophys. J. 148, 3 (BBN from SM physics)
Cyburt, Fields, Olive, Yeh 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 015004 (BBN review)
Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020 (N_eff measurement)

edges.description: The ads-cft-instance program targets bootstrap-CFT derivation of cosmological observables through dS/CFT correspondence (Strominger 2001) and the cosmological-bootstrap program (Arkani-Hamed-Maldacena 2015; Pajer et al. 2020+). Specifically: (i) inflationary correlators (CMB anisotropy, primordial non-Gaussianity, primordial GW spectrum) should derive from a CFT-correlator on the boundary of de Sitter via dS/CFT; (ii) the cosmological-bootstrap program seeks to derive cosmological correlation functions from symmetry + unitarity + analyticity constraints without invoking specific inflationary potentials; (iii) recent work (Pajer-Stefanyszyn-Welling 2020+) has reproduced single-field inflation correlators via boundary-CFT methods. Status `aspired` because: while the framework exists and has produced specific results for inflation correlators, no specific cosmological observation has been definitively derived from bootstrap-CFT without additional physical assumptions. cell_refs pin 3 cells (the inflation-era observables) where the bootstrap-derivation is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM (real research program with growing technical results; observational confirmation pending future precision data). This edge mirrors the ads-cft-instance program targeting of hadronic-states / FQH / compact-astro from prior phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Strominger 2001, JHEP 10, 034 (dS/CFT)
Arkani-Hamed, Maldacena 2015, arXiv:1503.08043 (cosmological bootstrap setup)
Pajer, Stefanyszyn, Welling 2020, JHEP 11, 116 (cosmological bootstrap technical results)

edges.description: GW events specialize Poincaré-invariant GR + perturbation theory on background spacetimes. Specifically: (i) BBH/BNS/NSBH mergers are GR solutions emitting gravitational radiation, specializing the cell-stsym-GR or analogous cell of spacetime-symmetry-groups; (ii) ringdown phase is BH perturbation theory in Kerr geometry, specializing Kerr-metric content; (iii) GW propagation at c specializes the Poincaré-invariance + null-cone-propagation prediction; (iv) the no-hair-theorem tests specialize the Kerr-uniqueness result; (v) PTA nHz background specializes the GW stress-energy tensor + cosmological FRW perturbation theory. Status `established` — GR + numerical relativity is the foundational framework and waveform matches confirm at high precision. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE.
citations: Einstein 1916 (GR)
Maggiore 2008, Gravitational Waves Vol. 1 (review)

edges.description: FIRST PHENOMENON-FC ↔ PHENOMENON-FC EDGE IN THE DATASET. Each GW merger event specializes a merger of objects classified in compact-astrophysical-objects. Specifically: (i) GW150914 specializes a merger of two stellar-mass BHs (cells cell-compact-stellar-BH-XRB / cell-compact-stellar-BH-GW from compact-astro v21); (ii) GW170817 specializes a merger of two canonical neutron stars (cell-compact-NS-canonical in compact-astro); (iii) GW190521 specializes an intermediate-mass-BBH merger (cell-compact-IMBH-gap in compact-astro); (iv) GW190814 and GW230529 specialize lower-mass-gap mergers, populating the boundary between compact-astro cell-compact-NS-massive and cell-compact-stellar-BH; (v) GWTC-3 BBH population is the empirical realization of the stellar-mass BH population in compact-astro. The relationship is `specializes` because each event is a specific instance (merger of two specific objects) of the abstract object-classes in compact-astro. Status `partial` because: (a) not all GW event categories specialize compact-astro cells (NANOGrav PTA nHz signal is a stochastic background, not an individual merger of compact-astro objects; continuous-wave-pulsar searches are individual NSs not mergers; superradiance-bounded ultralight-boson searches are not even compact-object events); (b) the directional `specializes` relation is reasonable for events-to-object-types but the reverse (compact-astro object inferences derive from GW events as data) also holds — the relationship is genuinely intertwined. cell_refs pin 7 cells where the specialization is most direct. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (events ARE mergers of objects); MEDIUM for the directional interpretation. This edge is justified by direct structural relation, not by the desideratum of producing a phen↔phen edge — the relation would be drawn independently.
citations: Abbott et al. 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 061102 (GW150914 = stellar-mass BBH merger)
Abbott et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 161101 (GW170817 = BNS merger)
Abbott et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 101102 (GW190521 = IMBH-region merger)

edges.description: BNS merger events derive matter-side physics from SM rep content. Specifically: (i) BNS-merger kilonova r-process nucleosynthesis (Au, Pt, lanthanide production at AT2017gfo) involves SM-content nuclear physics + GR-MHD; (ii) BNS-merger tidal deformability constraints on dense-matter EOS (Λ < 800 from GW170817) connect to SM-content hadronic physics — specializes the cell-compact-NS-EOS-bound content from compact-astro and hadronic-states. Status `partial` because: (a) BBH events do not derive from SM-rep-content (they are vacuum-GR solutions); (b) only the BNS / NSBH subset of GW events has SM-rep relevance; (c) the derivation is at the matter-physics-input level, not the basic GW signal level. cell_refs pin 2 cells (BNS merger and multi-messenger coincidence). Literature strength: MEDIUM. This connection mirrors compact-astrophysical-objects v21 derives-from sm-rep-content edge for NS-EOS cells.
citations: Abbott et al. 2017 (GW170817 tidal deformability)
Kasen, Metzger, Barnes, Quataert, Ramirez-Ruiz 2017, Nature 551, 80 (kilonova r-process)

edges.description: BH ringdown spectra (QNMs) and EMRI signals connect to holographic-CFT calculations via the ads-cft-instance program. Specifically: (i) Kerr-BH quasi-normal modes and the Teukolsky equation map onto CFT-spectrum calculations through AdS/CFT (for AdS-Kerr-BH variants) and dS-Kerr quasi-normal mode analyses; (ii) the no-hair theorem itself has CFT counterparts (rigidity of CFT spectrum); (iii) EMRI signals from supermassive BHs would test holographic-CFT predictions at very high precision (10⁵ orbital cycles in LISA-band). Status `aspired` because the holographic-derivation of GW phenomenology is technically active but no specific GW observation has been definitively derived from bootstrap-CFT — the framework applies in principle. cell_refs pin 2 cells where holographic-relevance is most active. Literature strength: WEAK to MEDIUM. This edge continues the ads-cft-instance pattern across phenomenon-FCs (hadrons, FQH, compact-astro, cosmology, now gravitational-waves — 5 of 10 phenomenon-FCs target bootstrap aspirationally).
citations: Strominger, Vafa 1996, Phys. Lett. B 379, 99 (BH-CFT correspondence)
Hartnoll, Lucas, Sachdev 2018, Holographic Quantum Matter (MIT)
Maldacena, Susskind 2013, Fortsch. Phys. 61, 781 (ER=EPR; related holographic-BH framework)

edges.description: QCD-phase-diagram derives directly from the SU(3)_c gauge sector of sm-rep-content. Specifically: (i) the quark cells (QL-g1, QL-g2, QL-g3 and right-handed quark cells in sm-rep-content) are the fundamental fermionic matter content; (ii) the gluon cell (cell-gluons-gNA in sm-rep-content) is the gauge field; (iii) asymptotic freedom (Gross-Wilczek-Politzer 1973) is a property of the SU(3)_c rep-content of sm-rep — non-abelian gauge with N_f = 6 quarks gives β_0 > 0; (iv) all phase transitions in QCD-phase-diagram are transitions of this gauge content under varying thermodynamic conditions. Status `established` because QCD-as-SU(3)_c is the textbook framework. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR. cell_refs pin 5 cells where the sm-rep derivation is most direct.
citations: Gross, Wilczek 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1343
Politzer 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1346
Gell-Mann 1964, Phys. Lett. 8, 214

edges.description: QCD-phase-diagram specializes Poincaré-invariant QFT (T = 0 ground state) and the finite-temperature extension via Euclidean spacetime / Matsubara frequencies. Hydrodynamic flow in QGP specializes relativistic-fluid GR-coupled hydrodynamics. The crossover at T_c is a thermodynamic property of finite-T QFT specializing standard Wightman-axiom QFT on Minkowski background. Status `established`. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG.
citations: Kapusta, Gale 2006, Finite-Temperature Field Theory (2nd ed., Cambridge)
Le Bellac 2000, Thermal Field Theory (Cambridge)

edges.description: Two derivation pathways. (i) Holographic-QGP: AdS-CFT duality predicts strongly-coupled gauge-theory thermal transport coefficients — η/s ≥ 1/(4π) (KSS bound from Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005). RHIC + LHC QGP measurements extract η/s ≈ (1-3) × 1/(4π), close to KSS bound, validating the holographic-fluid framework. This is one of the cleanest empirical hits of the ads-cft-instance program in real experimental data — promotes the edge status from `aspired` to `partial` because the η/s prediction has been quantitatively validated. (ii) Chiral-transition universality: massless-2-flavor chiral transition is in 3D O(4) universality class (Pisarski-Wilczek 1984), classified by bootstrap O(4) exclusion regions — though physical-quark-mass realizations are smoothed to crossover. cell_refs pin 3 cells where the bootstrap connection is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG for KSS-η/s match (genuine empirical hit); MEDIUM for chiral-transition O(4) (real-crit overlap). This edge is notably NOT purely `aspired` — the η/s prediction is the cleanest existing ads-cft-instance empirical validation across all phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Kovtun, Son, Starinets 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 94, 111601 (KSS bound)
Romatschke, Romatschke 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 99, 172301 (η/s extraction from data)
Pisarski, Wilczek 1984, Phys. Rev. D 29, 338 (chiral O(4))
Casalderrey-Solana, Liu, Mateos, Rajagopal, Wiedemann 2014, Gauge/String Duality, Hot QCD and Heavy Ion Collisions (Cambridge — book-length AdS-CFT-to-QCD overview)

edges.description: Photons specialize Poincaré-invariant massless gauge bosons (Wigner classification of irreducible Poincaré representations as helicity-±1 massless particles). EM propagation on FRW + curved-spacetime backgrounds (CMB free-streaming through expansion; gravitational lensing in clusters; GR redshift of all EM emission) specializes the GR / Minkowski framework. GW + GR-test cells (GW170817 GW-EM speed = c) bound photon-graviton-equivalence. Hawking radiation derives from GR + QFT on curved background. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Wigner 1939, Ann. Math. 40, 149 (Poincaré irreps)
Jackson 1999, Classical Electrodynamics (3rd ed., Wiley)

edges.description: The photon IS the U(1)_em gauge boson of sm-rep-content (specifically the (1,1,0) sub-content arising as the unbroken combination of B and W³ in electroweak symmetry breaking). All EM-phenomena cells derive from this gauge structure. Specifically: (i) thermal/blackbody emission derives from QED + thermal-equilibrium; (ii) synchrotron / inverse-Compton derive from QED + relativistic charged-fermion electroweak couplings; (iii) hadronic-pion-decay-gamma derives from QCD pion content + electroweak π⁰ → γγ amplitude (chiral anomaly); (iv) FEL-coherent + laser-coherent derive from QED stimulated-emission processes. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG. This is the primary structural-content derivation.
citations: Glashow 1961, Nucl. Phys. 22, 579 (electroweak unification)
Weinberg 1967, Phys. Rev. Lett. 19, 1264; Salam 1968 (electroweak)
Peskin, Schroeder 1995, Introduction to QFT (Westview)

edges.description: THIRD PHENOMENON-FC ↔ PHENOMENON-FC EDGE IN THE DATASET (after v21 compact-astro→hadronic-states and v27 gravitational-waves→compact-astro). Many EM-phenomena cells specialize compact-object phenomena: (i) cell-em-crab-multiband specializes compact-astro pulsar-wind-nebula cells; (ii) cell-em-AGN-jet-synchrotron specializes SMBH cells; (iii) cell-em-GRBs-multiband specializes BNS-merger (short-GRB) + collapsar (long-GRB) cells; (iv) cell-em-GW170817-kilonova specializes BNS-merger cells (same physical object that GW catalog v27 also specializes); (v) cell-em-IceCube-TXS-multimessenger specializes blazar SMBH cells; (vi) cell-em-LHAASO-PeV-gamma specializes galactic-PeVatron-source cells (SNR + PWN populations in compact-astro). Status `partial` because not all EM-phenomena cells specialize compact-objects — laboratory-coherent sources (FEL, lasers) and thermal-cosmological-backgrounds (CMB) do not derive from compact objects. cell_refs pin 6 cells with direct compact-astro specialization. The relation is honest physics: many EM cells ARE compact-object EM emission organized by photon-spectrum rather than object-class. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework.
citations: Hester 2008, Annu. Rev. Astron. Astrophys. 46, 127 (PWN/Crab as compact-object EM)
Mészáros 2006, Rep. Prog. Phys. 69, 2259 (GRB compact-object connection)
Begelman, Blandford, Rees 1984, Rev. Mod. Phys. 56, 255 (AGN jets from SMBHs)

edges.description: Thermal-QED + relativistic-magnetohydrodynamics admits CFT-like high-energy limits; holographic models of accretion-flow and jet acceleration use AdS-CFT for strongly-coupled plasma dynamics. Status `aspired` — the connections exist at framework level but no specific EM phenomenon has been derived directly from bootstrap-CFT. cell_refs pin 2 cells where the connection is most active (AGN jet plasma + UHE acceleration). Literature strength: WEAK to MEDIUM. Continues the ads-cft-instance program targeting across phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Casalderrey-Solana, Liu, Mateos, Rajagopal, Wiedemann 2014, Gauge/String Duality, Hot QCD and Heavy Ion Collisions (Cambridge)
Hartnoll, Lucas, Sachdev 2018, Holographic Quantum Matter (MIT)

edges.description: PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR — closes §5 natural-pairing su5-gut-rep-content ↔ proton-decay-searches. Each cell in this FC specializes a specific GUT-variant prediction from the structural classification of GUT rep content. Specifically: (i) p → π⁰ e⁺ specializes minimal-Georgi-Glashow-SU(5) dim-6 operator content; (ii) p → K⁺ ν̄ specializes SUSY-SU(5) dim-5 colored-Higgs-triplet operators; (iii) p → η e⁺, p → K⁰ ℓ⁺, etc., specialize sub-leading SU(5) operator structures; (iv) SO(10) and flipped-SU(5) cells specialize alternative-GUT rep-content predictions. Status `established` — the GUT-experimental-test relationship is the textbook framework. Literature strength: STRONG. This is a §5 explicit closure.
citations: Georgi, Glashow 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 32, 438
Sakai, Yanagida 1982, Nucl. Phys. B 197, 533
Langacker 1981, Phys. Rep. 72, 185

edges.description: Proton decay processes involve SM-content particles in initial + final states: proton = uud bound state (SM-rep quark content); final-state mesons (π⁰, π⁺, K⁺, η⁰, K⁰, ρ⁰, ω⁰, ...) are SM-content hadrons; final-state leptons (e⁺, μ⁺, ν̄) are SM-content. Decay mediators (X/Y bosons of SU(5)) are BEYOND-SM but the decay processes themselves are SM-content-to-SM-content transitions modulo baryon-number-violation. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Peskin, Schroeder 1995, Introduction to QFT (Westview)

edges.description: Proton-decay searches are QFT-on-Minkowski-background processes specializing Poincaré-invariant local field theory. Standard framework. Status `established`. Literature strength: MEDIUM.
citations: Peskin, Schroeder 1995

edges.description: Each N=2 SCFT spectrum-data cell is categorically equivalent to a Higgs/Coulomb-branch cell in higgs-coulomb-branches — they organize the same physical theories from data-side vs geometry-side. Specifically: (i) cell-n2scft-SU2Nf4-Coulomb-Delta and cell-hcb-su2-Nf4-coulomb both describe the SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 Coulomb-branch operator (one as data, one as geometry); (ii) cell-n2scft-AD-H0-Delta and cell-hcb-ad-h0 both describe AD H_0 theory; (iii) cell-n2scft-MN-E6-* and cell-hcb-mn-e6-* both describe Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6. The categorical equivalence reflects that the data and the geometry are two facets of the same N=2 SCFT object. Status `partial` because (a) not all HCB cells have a partner N2-SCFT-data cell (this FC has 17 cells vs HCB's 18, and the matching is not 1:1); (b) the chiral-algebra-bootstrap-data cells here have no direct geometric partner. PRIMARY PHENOMENON↔STRUCTURAL EDGE — closes §5 HCB natural-pairing.
citations: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2015 (chiral algebras unifying data and geometry)

edges.description: N=2 SCFT bootstrap-bounded observables derive from cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions specialized to N=2 supersymmetric stress-tensor 4-point functions. Specifically: (i) cell-n2scft-bootstrap-a-c-bound derives from cft-bootstrap general unitarity + Hofman-Maldacena energy-positivity, specialized to N=2; (ii) AD H_0 Δ_u = 6/5 has been independently verified via N=2 bootstrap to high precision (Beem et al. 2014); (iii) chiral-algebra-2D-4D correspondence cells cross-reference 2D-CFT bootstrap data for the associated chiral algebras. Status `partial`. Literature strength: MEDIUM. Cross-references the foundational cft-bootstrap structural-FC.
citations: Beem, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2014, JHEP 03, 100 (N=2 bootstrap)
Hofman, Maldacena 2008, JHEP 05, 012

edges.description: Exceptional Minahan-Nemeschansky theories and class-S constructions derive their flavor-symmetry structure from ADE Lie-algebra data. Specifically: (i) MN E_6 / E_7 / E_8 theories have flavor symmetry = corresponding exceptional Lie algebra e_6, e_7, e_8; (ii) Higgs branches are minimal nilpotent orbits of those Lie algebras; (iii) Coulomb-branch operator dimensions Δ_u = 3, 4, 6 for E_6, E_7, E_8 connect to dual Coxeter numbers; (iv) class-S T_N theories have SU(N)³ flavor symmetry from A_{N-1} M5-brane choice. Status `partial`. Literature strength: STRONG for the ADE labeling correspondence.
citations: Minahan, Nemeschansky 1996, 1997 (MN E_n theories)
Gaiotto 2009 (class-S A_{N-1} from M5)

edges.description: SPT phase classification derives directly from freed-hopkins-cobordism: every SPT phase corresponds to a cobordism class in Ω^G_{d+1}. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-Z8-FidkowskiKitaev's Z_8 classification derives from cobordism of 1+1D Pin⁻ structures; (ii) cell-spt-cobordism-SPT-classification directly invokes Freed-Hopkins 2016 cobordism classification; (iii) anomaly-inflow Callan-Harvey provides the link between bulk topological terms (which cobordism counts) and boundary anomalies. PRIMARY PHENOMENON↔STRUCTURAL EDGE — closes §5 freed-hopkins natural-pairing. Status `established` because the cobordism-classification framework is rigorously proven for symmetry-protected fermionic + bosonic SPTs.
citations: Freed, Hopkins 2016, arXiv:1604.06527
Kapustin 2014, arXiv:1403.1467

edges.description: SM-anomaly-related cells specialize sm-rep-content: they apply anomaly-cancellation and anomaly-matching constraints to the specific SM gauge × matter content. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-SM-anomaly-cancellation constrains the SM Y-charges from gauge anomaly cancellation; (ii) cell-spt-Witten-SU2-anomaly requires even number of SM left-handed doublets; (iii) cell-spt-pi0-gamma-gamma-ABJ derives from SM electromagnetic + QCD content via triangle anomaly. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Bouchiat, Iliopoulos, Meyer 1972, Phys. Lett. B 38, 519
Witten 1982, Phys. Lett. B 117, 324

edges.description: Specific SPT-phase observations specialize realized topological materials in topo-mat v22. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-3DTI-Z2-FuKaneMele cross-references topo-mat cell-ti-Bi2Se3-family; (ii) cell-spt-parity-anomaly-3DTI references magnetically-doped TIs; (iii) cell-spt-MZM-Kitaev-chain partially-overlaps with topo-mat InSb/Al cells; (iv) cell-spt-Haldane-chain references S=1 chain materials. Status `partial` because (a) not all SPT cells specialize topo-mat (SM anomaly cells, cobordism formal cells don't); (b) phen↔phen edge: FOURTH such FC-level edge in the dataset.
citations: Cross-references topo-mat v22 specific material cells

edges.description: SPT-phase classifications in fermionic systems derive from the tenfold-way (Altland-Zirnbauer 1997). Specifically: (i) 3D TI Z_2 is the AII class (time-reversal-invariant insulator); (ii) Z_8 1D topological superconductor is BDI class with interactions; (iii) Majorana zero modes occur in D, BDI, DIII classes; (iv) integer QH is A class (broken T-reversal). The tenfold-way provides the non-interacting backbone that anomaly + interaction analyses then refine. Status `partial` — covers fermionic SPTs only. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Altland, Zirnbauer 1997, Phys. Rev. B 55, 1142
Schnyder, Ryu, Furusaki, Ludwig 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195125

edges.description: Modified-gravity alternatives derive from spacetime-symmetry-groups by generalizing or modifying the GR backbone. Specifically: (i) all modified-gravity tests reference the GR baseline established by spacetime-symmetry-groups; (ii) scalar-tensor and Horndeski variants modify the Einstein-Hilbert action while preserving Poincaré invariance; (iii) Einstein-aether variants break Lorentz invariance — explicit specialization within spacetime-symmetry-groups; (iv) massive gravity introduces graviton mass while preserving general covariance. PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR. Status `established` — GR forms the unambiguous baseline.
citations: Will 2014, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (PPN formalism)

edges.description: Modified-gravity tests specialize gravitational-wave-event-catalog v27 — specifically the GW170817 multi-messenger event cell which provides the key graviton-speed bound. cell_refs list 7 cells where GW catalog observations are the falsifying test. PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — fifth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial` — many modified-gravity variants falsified by GW170817 (TeVeS, Horndeski G_5, bigravity, Galileons, Einstein-aether); others constrained but surviving (dRGT massive gravity, narrow Horndeski subset).
citations: Abbott et al. 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13 (GW170817)
Lombriser, Lima 2017, Phys. Lett. B 765, 382

edges.description: Specific modified-gravity tests specialize compact-astrophysical-objects v21 cells — PSR J0737-3039 binary-pulsar tests reference the NS-binary cell; EHT shadow tests reference the SMBH cells (M87, Sgr A*). PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — sixth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial`. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG for the test framework.
citations: Kramer et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. X 11, 041050
Psaltis et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 141104

edges.description: MOND was originally proposed as a DM alternative — accounts for galaxy rotation curves without invoking particle dark matter. Specializes the broader dark-matter-candidates question by offering modified-gravity rather than particle-physics resolution. Status `partial` — MOND succeeds galactically but Bullet Cluster falsifies pure MOND at cluster scales (cf. dark-matter-candidates v25 evidence cells). PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — seventh such FC-level edge in the dataset.
citations: Milgrom 1983, Astrophys. J. 270, 365
Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109

edges.description: Macroscopic-classical phenomena derive from spacetime-symmetry-groups via Galilean-invariant Navier-Stokes (fluids), nonrelativistic MHD (plasmas), classical-mechanics kinetic-theory (statistical mechanics). Specifically: (i) Navier-Stokes equation invariant under Galilean transformations (subgroup of Poincaré); (ii) classical-mechanics Hamiltonian dynamics is Galilean-invariant; (iii) Jarzynski + Crooks fluctuation theorems derive from time-reversal-symmetric microscopic dynamics. Status `established` — foundational classical-physics structure. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Landau, Lifshitz Vol. 6 (Fluid Mechanics)

edges.description: Plasma physics phenomena derive from sm-rep-content via classical EM (Maxwell equations) + classical ion-electron dynamics. Specifically: (i) magnetic-reconnection is a topological rearrangement of magnetic-field lines — classical EM in plasma medium; (ii) tokamak confinement uses classical EM B-fields confining ion-electron plasma; (iii) ICF inertial-confinement uses laser EM driving classical hydrodynamics + nuclear reactions; (iv) solar flares are EM-emitting plasma phenomena.
