Status `partial`. Literature strength: STRONG for EM-driven plasma physics derivation. Cell_refs pin specific plasma-related cells.
citations: Krall, Trivelpiece 1973, Principles of Plasma Physics (McGraw-Hill)

edges.description: Specific macroscopic-classical cells overlap with EM-phenomena cells via the electromagnetic emission of plasma processes. Specifically: (i) cell-mc-solar-flare-reconnection produces observable hard X-ray (RHESSI) + EUV (SDO) emissions — characterized in EM-phenomena AGN-and-Galactic-source cells; (ii) Parker Solar Probe measures in-situ plasma but the solar wind emits across the radio-to-UV spectrum. PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — eighth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial` — only specific plasma-EM cells participate.
citations: Shibata, Magara 2011, Living Rev. Solar Phys. 8, 6

edges.description: Closure constraint on the SM rep-content table. The five fermion multiplets (Q_L, u_R, d_R, L_L, e_R) replicate over exactly three generations; the 'N/A' generation value is structurally excluded for every fermion multiplet by construction of the SM matter content. The five (fermion-multiplet, generation=N/A) cells are forced empty by this closure. Self-edge on sm-rep-content with subtype 'derives-from': the forced cells' forbidden-by-pattern status is read off the SM's definition of the fermion sector as three generations of identical-gauge-rep copies.
citations: Particle Data Group, Review of Particle Physics, latest revision (live document at pdg.lbl.gov)
Weinberg 1996, The Quantum Theory of Fields Vol. II, §21.3

axis_mapping.description: Closure operates along the generation axis. For particle-multiplet ∈ {Q_L, u_R, d_R, L_L, e_R}, the permitted generation values are exactly {1, 2, 3}; the value 'N/A' is structurally excluded.

edges.description: Closure constraint on the SM rep-content table. SM gauge bosons (eight gluons of SU(3)_c, three W's of SU(2)_L, one B of U(1)_Y) are connection 1-forms on the G_SM gauge bundle whose multiplicities are fixed by dim(G_SM) = 8 + 3 + 1 = 12. Gauge fields do not replicate over the fermion generation index; the generation values 1, 2, 3 are structurally excluded for gauge-boson multiplets. The nine (gauge-multiplet, generation∈{1,2,3}) cells are forced empty by this closure. Self-edge on sm-rep-content with subtype 'derives-from': the forced cells' forbidden-by-pattern status is read off the bundle-theoretic structure of gauge fields as connections on a principal G_SM bundle, independent of matter content.
citations: Particle Data Group, Review of Particle Physics, latest revision (live document at pdg.lbl.gov)
Weinberg 1996, The Quantum Theory of Fields Vol. II, §21.3

axis_mapping.description: Closure operates along the generation axis. For particle-multiplet ∈ {gluons, W, B}, the permitted generation value is exactly {N/A}; the values 1, 2, 3 are structurally excluded.

edges.description: Closure constraint on the SM rep-content table. The minimal Standard Model contains a single scalar Higgs SU(2)_L doublet, not replicated over the fermion generation index; the generation values 1, 2, 3 are structurally excluded for the Higgs multiplet. The three (H, generation∈{1,2,3}) cells are forced empty by this closure. Self-edge on sm-rep-content with subtype 'derives-from': the forced cells' forbidden-by-pattern status is read off the SM's minimal Higgs-sector definition (one scalar doublet), as distinct from extended scalar sectors such as the two-Higgs-doublet model that lie beyond the SM.
citations: Particle Data Group, Review of Particle Physics, latest revision (live document at pdg.lbl.gov)
Weinberg 1996, The Quantum Theory of Fields Vol. II, §21.3

axis_mapping.description: Closure operates along the generation axis. For particle-multiplet = H, the permitted generation value is exactly {N/A}; the values 1, 2, 3 are structurally excluded.

edges.description: Closure constraint on the SU(5) rep-content table. The matter representations 5̄ and 10 of SU(5) house one Standard Model generation each: 5̄ ⊃ (d_R^c, L_L) and 10 ⊃ (Q_L, u_R^c, e_R^c). They replicate over the three SM generations under the SU(5) → SM breaking that preserves the SM matter content. The generation value 'N/A' is structurally excluded for matter reps. The two (matter-rep, generation=N/A) cells are forced empty by this closure. Self-edge on su5-gut-rep-content with subtype 'derives-from': the forced cells' forbidden-by-pattern status is read off SU(5)'s definition of the matter sector as three generations of (5̄+10), mirroring the analogous Step 7 closure on sm-rep-content where the SM matter multiplets Q_L, u_R, d_R, L_L, e_R replicate over the same three generations.
citations: Georgi, Glashow 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 32, 438
Langacker 1981, Phys. Rep. 72, 185

axis_mapping.description: Closure operates along the generation axis. For su5-rep ∈ {5̄, 10}, the permitted generation values are exactly {1, 2, 3}; the value 'N/A' is structurally excluded.

edges.description: Closure constraint on the SU(5) rep-content table. The 24 (adjoint) is the SU(5) gauge-boson representation; gauge fields are connection 1-forms on the SU(5) principal bundle whose multiplicity is fixed by dim(SU(5)) = 24, independent of the fermion-generation index. Under SU(5) → SM the 24 decomposes into the 12 SM gauge bosons (8 gluons + 3 W + 1 B) plus 12 X/Y leptoquarks that mediate proton decay; the generation values 1, 2, 3 are structurally excluded for the 24 because the gauge sector is not generation-indexed. The three (24, generation∈{1,2,3}) cells are forced empty by this closure. Self-edge on su5-gut-rep-content with subtype 'derives-from': the forced cells' forbidden-by-pattern status is read off the bundle-theoretic structure of SU(5) gauge fields as connections on a principal SU(5) bundle, mirroring the analogous Step 7 closure on sm-rep-content where gauge-boson multiplicity is fixed by dim(G_SM) = 12.
citations: Georgi, Glashow 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 32, 438
Langacker 1981, Phys. Rep. 72, 185

axis_mapping.description: Closure operates along the generation axis. For su5-rep = 24 (adjoint), the permitted generation value is exactly {N/A}; the values 1, 2, 3 are structurally excluded.

edges.description: Both sterile-neutrino-class dark-matter candidates (cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV and cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm on the source-FC side) derive their structure from the sterile-neutrino flavor framework organized in neutrino-sector-phenomenology: given the sterile-neutrino mass eigenstate + active-sterile mixing parameter space established on the neutrino-sector side (with experimental constraints from short-baseline appearance + reactor + atmospheric + gallium-anomaly + X-ray-line searches), the DM-candidate classification reads off the cosmological-relic-abundance and observational-signature consequences (production mechanism, parameter-space-status). Per v15 convention, 'A derives-from B' = A's structure is read off B; here the DM-side structure (candidate-class × production-mechanism × observational-status axes for sterile-neutrino DM) is read off the neutrino-side flavor framework + experimental result network. Target-side cells where this derivation applies: cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED and cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension (cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV reads off both eV-sterile parameter-space regions — the LSND-specific 3+1 region now-excluded, and the BEST+gallium remaining-tension region); cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM (cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm reads off the keV-sterile decay-line signature framework via E_γ = m_s/2 two-body-decay kinematics). Status `partial` because only the sterile-neutrino-class DM cells participate; WIMPs, axions, primordial black holes, dark-photon-portal, gravitino-LSP and other DM candidate classes do not derive from neutrino-sector-phenomenology. cell_refs pins the source-side (DM-side) cells per v15.1 source-side-pinning convention (cf. edge-xc-stsym-derives-from-ade-lie). The relation formalizes the cell-content cross-references already explicit in both DM-side cells' content and description fields ('shared with neutrino-phen cells cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED and cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension'; 'shared with neutrino-phen cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM — same physical particle, classified here as DM candidate and there as neutrino-sector observable'). It also accounts structurally for the cross-FC parallel-anchor pattern surfaced in sub-PRs 41+43+44: cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV and cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED both encode quantitative_scale at kind=mass val=1.0 eV unc=0.5 eV with shared LSND 2001 + MiniBooNE 2018 + Dentler 2018 + IceCube 2020 + PDG 2024 literature (≥ 4 shared citations) — the parallel-anchor is the same-particle-different-FC-classification pattern this edge expresses; cell-dm-sterile-nu-keV-warm encodes the particle-mass anchor (m_s ≈ 7.1 keV from Bulbul 2014 sterile-ν → γ + ν two-body decay) while cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM encodes the complementary observational-signature anchor (E_γ = 3.55 ± 0.03 keV from the same 2014 stacked-galaxy-cluster X-ray observation), the two anchors related by E_γ = m_s/2 — the cross-observable encoding pattern this edge also formalizes.
citations: Dodelson, Widrow 1994, Phys. Rev. Lett. 72:17 — 'Sterile neutrinos as dark matter' (foundational sterile-neutrino DM production framework via Dodelson-Widrow active-sterile mixing; the DM-candidate classification reads off the sterile-active mixing structure organized on the neutrino-sector side).

Shi, Fuller 1999, Phys. Rev. Lett. 82:2832 — 'New Dark Matter Candidate: Nonthermal Sterile Neutrinos' (resonant production mechanism; alternative cosmological history derivation also reading off neutrino-sector mixing structure).

Boyarsky, Ruchayskiy, Shaposhnikov 2009, Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 59:191 (arXiv:0901.0011) — 'The Role of Sterile Neutrinos in Cosmology and Astrophysics'. Primary review explicitly framing keV sterile-neutrino DM as a derivation from the sterile-active mixing framework of neutrino-sector phenomenology; explicit derivation chain from neutrino-side flavor framework to DM-side candidate-class assignments.

Drewes, Lasserre, Merle, Mertens, Adhikari et al. 2017, JCAP 01:025 (arXiv:1602.04816) — 'A White Paper on keV Sterile Neutrino Dark Matter'. Comprehensive review of how the neutrino-sector sterile-mass + mixing parameter space maps onto the keV-DM cosmological-relic-abundance + observational-signature parameter space.

Abazajian 2017, Phys. Rep. 711-712:1 (arXiv:1705.01837) — 'Sterile neutrinos in cosmology'. Explicit derivation chain from neutrino-sector flavor framework to DM-candidate-class assignments across eV/keV mass scales.

Bulbul, Markevitch, Foster, Smith, Loewenstein, Randall 2014, Astrophys. J. 789:13 (arXiv:1402.2301) — 'Detection of an Unidentified Emission Line in the Stacked X-Ray Spectrum of Galaxy Clusters'. 3.55 keV X-ray line interpretation: DM-side observational signature E_γ = m_s/2 derived from neutrino-sector two-body-decay framework; co-cited with Boyarsky et al. 2014 PRL 113:251301.

Acero et al. (MicroBooNE Collaboration) 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128:241801 — 'Search for an Excess of Electron Neutrino Interactions in MicroBooNE Using Multiple Final State Topologies'. DM-side eV-sterile parameter-space-largely-excluded status derives from neutrino-sector accelerator-experiment + reactor-experiment + atmospheric-experiment constraints organized on the neutrino-sector side.

Barinov et al. (BEST Collaboration) 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128:232501 (arXiv:2201.07360) — 'Results from the Baksan Experiment on Sterile Transitions (BEST)'. Gallium-anomaly result establishing the remaining-tension eV-sterile parameter space on the neutrino-sector side, which the cell-dm-sterile-nu-eV DM-side cell reads off for its 'remaining parameter space from BEST gallium-anomaly results' framing.

Hyper-Kamiokande Proto-Collaboration 2018 'Hyper-Kamiokande Design Report' arXiv:1805.04163, §10 (nucleon decay): 10-year-exposure 3σ discovery reach for τ(p → e⁺π⁰) ≈ 6.3 × 10³⁴ yr; 20-year sensitivity reaches τ > 10³⁵ yr at 90% CL.

Bian, Di Lodovico, Horiuchi et al. 2022 'Hyper-Kamiokande Experiment: A Snowmass White Paper' arXiv:2203.02029, §II (Nucleon Decay and Dark Matter): 'With 20 years of data, Hyper-K will reach a proton decay sensitivity of 10³⁵ years for p → π⁰e⁺ and 3 × 10³⁴ years for p → ν̄K⁺.'

predictions_per_program.program: Minimal non-SUSY (Georgi-Glashow) SU(5)

citations: Langacker 1981 'Grand Unified Theories and Proton Decay' Phys. Rep. 72:185 — minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) prediction τ(p → e⁺π⁰) in the 10²⁸⁻³¹ yr range from M_X ≈ 10¹⁴⁻¹⁵ GeV.
Murayama, Pierce 2002 'Realistic Grand Unification' hep-ph/0108104, Phys. Rev. D 65:055009 — refined non-SUSY minimal SU(5) predicts τ(p → e⁺π⁰) ≲ 10³⁰ yr; already falsified.

predictions_per_program.description: Minimal non-SUSY (Georgi-Glashow) SU(5) predicts τ(p → e⁺π⁰) ≲ 10³⁰ yr (M_X ≈ 10¹⁵ GeV from sin²θ_W running), already excluded by the Super-K bound τ > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr (2020). Hyper-K's 10³⁵-yr reach widens the exclusion of any non-SUSY-minimal-SU(5) revival.
citations: Langacker 1981 Phys. Rep. 72:185 (program-internal calculation)
Murayama, Pierce 2002 hep-ph/0108104, Phys. Rev. D 65:055009 (refined program-internal calculation)

predictions_per_program.program: SUSY SU(5) (dimension-6 via X-boson exchange, M_GUT ≈ 2 × 10¹⁶ GeV)

citations: Hisano 2022 'Proton decay in SUSY GUTs' PTEP 2022:12B104 (arXiv invited review) — since the SUSY-unification GUT scale rises to ∼ 2 × 10¹⁶ GeV, the X-boson-induced τ(p → e⁺π⁰) prediction sits at 10³⁵⁻³⁶ yr.
Hisano, Murayama, Yanagida 1992 'Probing GUT scale mass spectrum through precision measurements on the weak scale parameters' Phys. Rev. Lett. 69:1014 (original SUSY GUT scale derivation).

predictions_per_program.description: SUSY SU(5)'s elevated M_GUT ≈ 2 × 10¹⁶ GeV (from successful coupling unification under MSSM running) pushes the X-boson-induced τ(p → e⁺π⁰) prediction into the 10³⁵⁻³⁶ yr band. Hyper-K's 10³⁵-yr reach lies in the lower half of this window: a positive HK observation would point to SUSY-SU(5)-style dim-6; a null result through HK's full exposure pushes the GUT scale toward 10³⁶ yr and out of HK reach.
citations: Hisano 2022 PTEP 2022:12B104 (program-internal review of SUSY-SU(5) p → e⁺π⁰ lifetime prediction)

predictions_per_program.program: Minimal non-SUSY SO(10) with Pati-Salam intermediate scale

citations: Babu, Khan 2015 'A Minimal Non-Supersymmetric SO(10) Model: Gauge Coupling Unification, Proton Decay and Fermion Masses' arXiv:1507.06712, Phys. Rev. D 92:075018 — minimal non-SUSY SO(10) with 54 + 126 + 10 Higgs and a Pati-Salam intermediate scale M_I ≈ 10¹³⁻¹⁴ GeV predicts τ(p → e⁺π⁰) bounded above by a few × 10³⁵ yr; cf. abstract: 'the model generally predicts an upper bound of few times 10³⁵ yrs for proton lifetime, which is not too far from the present Super-Kamiokande limit'.

predictions_per_program.description: Minimal non-SUSY SO(10) with a Pati-Salam intermediate scale predicts τ(p → e⁺π⁰) bounded above at ∼ few × 10³⁵ yr — squarely within Hyper-K's reach. The model's authors explicitly identify Hyper-K and axion-search experiments as the verdict-rendering tools for the scenario.
citations: Babu, Khan 2015 arXiv:1507.06712, Phys. Rev. D 92:075018 (program-internal calculation)

edges.description: Hyper-Kamiokande (258-kton fiducial water-Cherenkov, J-PARC narrow-band beam, Tochibora mine; first physics 2027 per Design Report) extends Super-Kamiokande's reach in the p → e⁺π⁰ channel by approximately an order of magnitude: from the current SK bound τ > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr (2020 enlarged-FV analysis, arXiv:2010.16098) to a target τ > 10³⁵ yr after 20 years of exposure. The cell cell-pd-pi0-eplus-falsified-GG on proton-decay-searches anchors the empirical state of this channel (τ > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr as a lower bound on the lifetime) and records that minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) is already falsified. Hyper-K's reach lands in the band where SUSY-SU(5) (10³⁵⁻³⁶ yr) and minimal non-SUSY SO(10) with Pati-Salam intermediate scale (few × 10³⁵ yr upper bound) sit, while extra-dimensional / orbifold SU(5) variants (∼ 10³⁶ yr) remain beyond reach. This edge formalizes the experiment-to-channel-cell relationship the parallel cell cell-pd-Hyper-K-future already describes descriptively (target lifetime τ > 10³⁵ yr as a lower bound). The three per-program competing predictions span the discrimination space: a positive HK signal would point toward SUSY-SU(5)-style or SO(10)-style dim-6 mediation; a null result through full exposure narrows the surviving GUT variants toward extra-dimensional embeddings.
citations: Hyper-Kamiokande Proto-Collaboration 2018 'Hyper-Kamiokande Design Report' arXiv:1805.04163
Bian et al. 2022 'Hyper-Kamiokande Experiment: A Snowmass White Paper' arXiv:2203.02029

Super-Kamiokande Collaboration (Takhistov et al.) 2020 'Search for proton decay via p → e⁺π⁰ and p → μ⁺π⁰ with an enlarged fiducial volume in Super-Kamiokande I-IV' arXiv:2010.16098, Phys. Rev. D 102:112011 — current bound τ > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr.
Hyper-Kamiokande Proto-Collaboration 2018 'Hyper-Kamiokande Design Report' arXiv:1805.04163, §10 — p → ν̄K⁺ channel sensitivity targeted at τ > 3 × 10³⁴ yr (90% CL, 20-yr exposure).
Bian et al. 2022 'Hyper-Kamiokande Experiment: A Snowmass White Paper' arXiv:2203.02029, §II — 'Hyper-K will reach a proton decay sensitivity of ... 3 × 10³⁴ years for p → ν̄K⁺.'

predictions_per_program.program: Minimal SUSY SU(5) (dimension-5 colored-Higgsino exchange, M_scalar ≲ 30 TeV)

citations: Babu, Gogoladze, Ün 2022 'Proton Lifetime in Minimal SUSY SU(5) in Light of LHC Results' arXiv:2012.14411, JHEP 02:164 — 'when the SUSY scalar masses are required to be ≤ 30 TeV ... we find that proton lifetime for the decay p → ν̄K⁺ is bounded by τ(p → ν̄K⁺) ≤ 1.1 × 10³⁵ yrs.'
Ellis, Evans, Nagata, Olive, Velasco-Sevilla 2020 'Supersymmetric proton decay revisited' arXiv:1912.04888, Eur. Phys. J. C 80:332.
Hisano, Murayama, Yanagida 1993 'Nucleon decay in the minimal supersymmetric SU(5) grand unification' Nucl. Phys. B 402:46, hep-ph/9207279 (original SUSY-SU(5) dim-5 calculation).

predictions_per_program.description: Minimal SUSY SU(5) with sfermions accessible to next-generation colliders (M_scalar ≲ 30 TeV) bounds τ(p → ν̄K⁺) above at 1.1 × 10³⁵ yr through colored-Higgsino dim-5 operators. Hyper-K's 3 × 10³⁴-yr reach therefore sits one half-decade below the upper edge of the minimal-SUSY-SU(5) prediction window: a positive HK observation would confirm minimal SUSY SU(5); a null result through full exposure pushes the scalar-mass scale further upward.
citations: Babu, Gogoladze, Ün 2022 arXiv:2012.14411, JHEP 02:164 (program-internal upper-bound calculation)
Hisano, Murayama, Yanagida 1993 Nucl. Phys. B 402:46 (foundational program-internal dim-5 calculation)

predictions_per_program.program: SUSY SU(5) with split-spectrum / Peccei-Quinn-suppressed dim-5 (heavy sfermions M_scalar ≫ TeV)

citations: Hisano, Kobayashi, Nagata 2012 'Enhancement of Proton Decay Rates in Supersymmetric SU(5) Grand Unified Models' arXiv:1204.6274 — with heavy sfermions, the SUSY p → ν̄K⁺ lifetime is prolonged into the 10³⁶ yr range.

Ibe, Kobayashi, Nagata 2024 'Proton Lifetime in Minimal Supersymmetric SU(5) with GMSB' arXiv:2409.06239 — messenger-scale-dependent prolongation pushes τ beyond Hyper-K reach for M_scalar at the heaviest end.

predictions_per_program.description: SUSY SU(5) variants in which Peccei-Quinn symmetry suppresses or split-spectrum sfermions push dim-5 contributions toward 10³⁶ yr lifetimes, beyond Hyper-K's reach. A null result at HK is fully consistent with this class of models. The split-spectrum/PQ branch is therefore the SUSY family that survives HK's null verdict.
citations: Hisano, Kobayashi, Nagata 2012 arXiv:1204.6274 (program-internal calculation, heavy-sfermion enhancement of suppression)

edges.description: Hyper-Kamiokande's p → ν̄K⁺ channel reach (τ > 3 × 10³⁴ yr targeted; current SK bound τ > 5.9 × 10³³ yr) discriminates SUSY-SU(5) variants by sfermion mass scale. The cell cell-pd-Kplus-nubar-SUSY on proton-decay-searches anchors the SK bound (τ > 5.9 × 10³³ yr as a lower bound on the lifetime) and identifies the SUSY-SU(5)-favored character of this channel (which proceeds via colored-Higgsino dim-5 operators, distinctively favored over the dim-6 X-boson contribution dominant in p → e⁺π⁰). Hyper-K's 3 × 10³⁴-yr reach sits between minimal SUSY SU(5)'s upper bound (1.1 × 10³⁵ yr for M_scalar ≲ 30 TeV) and split-spectrum SUSY (~10³⁶ yr): the experiment cleanly discriminates between these two SUSY families. The complementary DUNE liquid-argon-TPC reach (τ > 1.3 × 10³⁴ yr, recorded on cell-pd-DUNE-K-channel-sensitivity) provides cross-detector confirmation. Note Pati-Salam GUTs render the proton stable in their minimal form and are not represented in the per-program prediction list because no quantitative prediction can be encoded for an infinite lifetime; their incompatibility with any HK observation is recorded only at the description level.

Bian et al. 2022 'Hyper-Kamiokande Experiment: A Snowmass White Paper' arXiv:2203.02029

Hyper-Kamiokande Proto-Collaboration 2018 'Hyper-Kamiokande Design Report' arXiv:1805.04163, §6 (long-baseline oscillation): with a 10-year run at the J-PARC 1.3 MW upgraded beam, Hyper-K achieves ≥5σ CL discovery of CP violation in the leptonic sector for approximately 57% of true δ_CP values; precision σ(δ_CP) ≤ 7° (≈12.2° in radians/100) at 1σ for δ_CP near maximal CPV.

Bian et al. 2022 'Hyper-Kamiokande Experiment: A Snowmass White Paper' arXiv:2203.02029, §VII — 'The Hyper-K and J-PARC neutrino beam measurement of neutrino oscillation is more likely to provide a 5-sigma discovery of CP violation than any other existing experiment.'

edges.description: Hyper-Kamiokande's headline neutrino-oscillation result is precision measurement of the leptonic CP-violating phase δ_CP. The cell cell-nu-deltaCP anchors the present empirical state (best-fit δ_CP ≈ -1.97 rad ≈ 3.0892 rad in the [0, 2π) convention, observational-status: 'bounded-with-open-range'). Hyper-K achieves ≥5σ CL discovery of CPV in approximately 57% of the true δ_CP parameter space within a 10-year run, with σ(δ_CP) precision ≲ 7° near maximal CPV. This edge is recorded as bounds-setting rather than measurement-discriminating: leptonic δ_CP is a Standard-Model-MNS-matrix parameter being measured, not a discriminator between candidate-foundational programs that make competing single-valued predictions of its value. Various BSM frameworks (leptogenesis flavor-symmetry models, A4/S4 family symmetries, anarchic neutrino mass models) correlate δ_CP with other parameters but do not in general fix it to a single citable value; admissibility under §4 part (2) would require program-internal calculations that produce specific δ_CP point predictions, which the leading frameworks do not generally offer. The bounds-setting framing is therefore the appropriate one.

Bian et al. 2022 'Hyper-Kamiokande Experiment: A Snowmass White Paper' arXiv:2203.02029

Abe et al. (T2K Collaboration) 2020 'Constraint on the matter-antimatter symmetry-violating phase in neutrino oscillations' Nature 580:339 — establishes the present empirical anchor that Hyper-K extends.

Hyper-Kamiokande Proto-Collaboration 2018 'Hyper-Kamiokande Design Report' arXiv:1805.04163, §8 (atmospheric neutrinos): with a 10-year atmospheric-ν run, Hyper-K resolves the neutrino mass ordering at ≥3σ CL for both NH and IH true values (resolution improves up to ≤5σ when combined with the beam-ν data, depending on the true θ_23 octant).

edges.description: Hyper-Kamiokande's atmospheric-ν programme leverages the wide range of baselines (∼100 km to ∼1.3 × 10⁴ km Earth-traversing) to extract the mass-ordering-dependent matter-effect resonance in P(ν_μ → ν_e). The cell cell-nu-mass-hierarchy anchors the current empirical state (current global-fit preference for NH at ≈2.5σ, recorded as an upper bound of 2.47σ on the deviation from null ordering). Hyper-K's 10-yr atmospheric-ν reach achieves ≥3σ resolution of NH vs IH for both true orderings; the joint atmospheric + J-PARC-beam analysis pushes to ≥5σ for favorable θ_23 octant. This edge is recorded as bounds-setting rather than measurement-discriminating: NH vs IH is a binary determination of which mass ordering nature realizes, not a discriminator between candidate-foundational programs that make competing predictions of the ordering. Specific flavor-symmetry models (A4, sequential-dominance type-I seesaw, anarchic) prefer one ordering or the other under additional assumptions but do not generically produce program-internal predictions admissible under §4 part (2). The bounds-setting framing is therefore the appropriate one. JUNO at ∼53 km from reactor sources provides matter-effect-free complementary determination of the ordering (a future Step-4.4 sub-PR can author the parallel JUNO experimental-coverage edge).

predictions_per_program.description: Minimal non-SUSY (Georgi-Glashow) SU(5) predicts τ(p → e⁺π⁰) ≲ 10³⁰ yr (M_X ≈ 10¹⁵ GeV from sin²θ_W running), already excluded by the Super-K bound τ > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr (2020). Hyper-K's 10³⁵-yr reach widens the exclusion of any non-SUSY-minimal-SU(5) revival.

predictions_per_program.program: SUSY SU(5) (dimension-6 via X-boson exchange, M_GUT ≈ 2 × 10¹⁶ GeV)

citations: Hisano 2022 'Proton decay in SUSY GUTs' PTEP 2022:12B104 (arXiv invited review) — since the SUSY-unification GUT scale rises to ∼ 2 × 10¹⁶ GeV, the X-boson-induced τ(p → e⁺π⁰) prediction sits at 10³⁵⁻³⁶ yr.
Hisano, Murayama, Yanagida 1992 'Probing GUT scale mass spectrum through precision measurements on the weak scale parameters' Phys. Rev. Lett. 69:1014 (original SUSY GUT scale derivation).

predictions_per_program.description: SUSY SU(5)'s elevated M_GUT ≈ 2 × 10¹⁶ GeV (from successful coupling unification under MSSM running) pushes the X-boson-induced τ(p → e⁺π⁰) prediction into the 10³⁵⁻³⁶ yr band. Hyper-K's 10³⁵-yr reach lies in the lower half of this window: a positive HK observation would point to SUSY-SU(5)-style dim-6; a null result through HK's full exposure pushes the GUT scale toward 10³⁶ yr and out of HK reach.

predictions_per_program.program: Minimal non-SUSY SO(10) with Pati-Salam intermediate scale

citations: Babu, Khan 2015 'A Minimal Non-Supersymmetric SO(10) Model: Gauge Coupling Unification, Proton Decay and Fermion Masses' arXiv:1507.06712, Phys. Rev. D 92:075018 — minimal non-SUSY SO(10) with 54 + 126 + 10 Higgs and a Pati-Salam intermediate scale M_I ≈ 10¹³⁻¹⁴ GeV predicts τ(p → e⁺π⁰) bounded above by a few × 10³⁵ yr; cf. abstract: 'the model generally predicts an upper bound of few times 10³⁵ yrs for proton lifetime, which is not too far from the present Super-Kamiokande limit'.

predictions_per_program.description: Minimal non-SUSY SO(10) with a Pati-Salam intermediate scale predicts τ(p → e⁺π⁰) bounded above at ∼ few × 10³⁵ yr — squarely within Hyper-K's reach. The model's authors explicitly identify Hyper-K and axion-search experiments as the verdict-rendering tools for the scenario.

edges.description: DUNE's 40-kt LAr TPC far detector is sensitive to the p → e⁺π⁰ channel through the back-to-back positron + two-photon (from π⁰ → γγ) topology, with O(10³⁴ yr) 90% CL sensitivity at 400 kt·yr exposure (10 years at full FD mass). The water-Cherenkov technique favors EM-shower-rich channels over LAr's ionization-imaging strength, so DUNE's p → e⁺π⁰ reach (~10³⁴ yr) is roughly one decade below Hyper-K's (~10³⁵ yr at 20-year exposure) — the two experiments form a discriminating-resolves-pair on this channel, with HK the stronger probe and DUNE providing complementary background-suppressed cross-check. Per-program predictions are taken without modification from the matching Hyper-K edge edge-rs-hyper-k-pd-pi0-eplus per the T30 detector-independence principle: minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) (already excluded), SUSY SU(5) (predicts 10³⁵·⁵±⁰·⁵ yr), and minimal non-SUSY SO(10) with Pati-Salam intermediate scale (predicts 10³⁵·⁵±⁰·³ yr) each give the same lifetime band regardless of which detector is doing the measuring; what differs across the HK and DUNE edges is only the per-channel detector sensitivity.

predictions_per_program.description: SUSY SU(5) variants in which Peccei-Quinn symmetry suppresses or split-spectrum sfermions push dim-5 contributions toward 10³⁶ yr lifetimes, beyond Hyper-K's reach. A null result at HK is fully consistent with this class of models. The split-spectrum/PQ branch is therefore the SUSY family that survives HK's null verdict.

edges.description: DUNE's 40-kt LAr TPC far detector probes p → ν̄K⁺ through clean ionization-track identification of the K⁺ followed by its delayed decay (K⁺ → μ⁺ν or K⁺ → π⁺π⁰), reaching τ > 1.3 × 10³⁴ yr at 400 kt·yr (10-year FD exposure) per the TDR Vol II projection. Liquid argon's ionization-imaging strength provides quasi-background-free detection for this channel — a detector-technology advantage over Hyper-K's water Cherenkov technique (where the K⁺ is below Cherenkov threshold and must be inferred from its decay daughters). In 90% CL sensitivity terms, DUNE's 1.3 × 10³⁴ yr reach at 10 yr is modestly below Hyper-K's 3 × 10³⁴ yr reach at 20 yr, but DUNE's near-zero-background regime makes any DUNE detection a clean discovery rather than a statistical excess against irreducible background. The two experiments form a discriminating-resolves-pair on this channel: HK provides the larger raw exposure, DUNE provides the cleaner per-event identification. Per-program predictions are taken without modification from the matching Hyper-K edge edge-rs-hyper-k-pd-Kplus-nubar per the T30 detector-independence principle: minimal SUSY SU(5) (predicts τ ≤ 1.1 × 10³⁵ yr) and split-spectrum SUSY (predicts 10³⁶±⁰·⁵ yr) give the same lifetime bounds regardless of which detector is measuring.

Super-Kamiokande Collaboration (Abe et al.) 2014 'Search for proton decay via p → ν̄K⁺ using 260 kiloton·year data of Super-Kamiokande' Phys. Rev. D 90:072005, arXiv:1408.1195 — current best experimental lower limit τ > 5.9 × 10³³ yr (90% CL), the empirical floor DUNE doubles.

Abi et al. (DUNE Collaboration) 2020 'Long-baseline neutrino oscillation physics potential of the DUNE experiment' Eur. Phys. J. C 80:978, arXiv:2006.16043, Abstract: 'It has the potential to observe charge-parity violation in the neutrino sector to a precision of 3σ (5σ) after an exposure of 5 (10) years, for 50% of all δ_CP values.' Full simulation, reconstruction, and event selection of the FD with detailed flux/cross-section/detector uncertainties.

DUNE Collaboration 2020 'Deep Underground Neutrino Experiment (DUNE), Far Detector TDR Volume II: DUNE Physics' arXiv:2002.03005, §3 (Long-baseline neutrino oscillation): the TDR companion analysis specifies the same 5σ-at-10-yr / 50%-coverage benchmark used in the EPJ C paper.

edges.description: DUNE's headline long-baseline-oscillation result is precision measurement of the leptonic CP-violating phase δ_CP via the asymmetry between P(ν_μ → ν_e) and P(ν̄_μ → ν̄_e) over the 1285-km Fermilab–SURF baseline. The cell cell-nu-deltaCP anchors the present empirical state (T2K + NuFIT global fit). DUNE reaches 5σ CPV discovery for 50% of true δ_CP values at 10 years of nominal beam running (3σ at 5 years) per Abi et al. 2020 EPJ C 80:978. This is comparable to Hyper-K's reach (5σ for ~57% of δ_CP at 10 years) but is achieved with a fundamentally different systematics profile: DUNE's wide-band on-axis beam and matter-effect-rich long baseline make its δ_CP measurement complementary to HK's off-axis narrow-band shorter-baseline approach. The two experiments form a discriminating-experiments pair on this observable in a fairness-preserving sense: their systematic uncertainties are largely orthogonal (DUNE's matter effects vs HK's reduced matter-effect dependence; DUNE's argon cross-section vs HK's water cross-section), so joint analyses reach 5σ for substantially larger δ_CP coverage at lower combined exposure. This edge is recorded as bounds-setting rather than measurement-discriminating: leptonic δ_CP is a Standard-Model-MNS-matrix parameter being measured, not a discriminator between candidate-foundational programs that make competing single-valued predictions of its value. Specific BSM frameworks (leptogenesis with flavor symmetries, A4/S4 family symmetries, anarchic neutrino mass models) correlate δ_CP with other parameters but do not in general fix it to a single citable value; per-program admissibility under §4 part (2) would require single-valued δ_CP predictions, which the leading frameworks do not generally offer. The bounds-setting framing is therefore the appropriate one, mirroring the Hyper-K edge.

DUNE Collaboration 2020 'DUNE Far Detector TDR Volume II: DUNE Physics' arXiv:2002.03005

Esteban, Gonzalez-Garcia, Maltoni, Schwetz, Zhou (NuFIT 5.2) 2022 JHEP 09:178 — current global-fit δ_CP best-fit and bounds.

Abi et al. (DUNE Collaboration) 2020 'Long-baseline neutrino oscillation physics potential of the DUNE experiment' Eur. Phys. J. C 80:978, arXiv:2006.16043, Abstract: 'DUNE will resolve the neutrino mass ordering to a precision of 5σ, for all δ_CP values, after 2 years of running with the nominal detector design and beam configuration.' The 1285-km baseline produces strong matter-induced asymmetry that distinguishes NH from IH essentially independently of δ_CP — DUNE's reach is the fastest published 5σ-for-all-δ_CP timeline of any next-generation experiment.
DUNE Collaboration 2020 'Deep Underground Neutrino Experiment (DUNE), Far Detector TDR Volume II: DUNE Physics' arXiv:2002.03005, §3 (Long-baseline neutrino oscillation), §8 (Atmospheric neutrinos).

edges.description: DUNE's 1285-km Fermilab → SURF baseline traverses ~30 g·cm⁻² of Earth matter, producing a large matter-induced ν_μ → ν_e (vs ν̄_μ → ν̄_e) asymmetry that is opposite-sign for NH vs IH. Per Abi et al. 2020 EPJ C 80:978, DUNE resolves the mass ordering to ≥5σ for ALL true δ_CP values after only 2 years of beam running — significantly faster than Hyper-K's atmospheric-ν-led approach (HK ≥3σ at 10 yr from atmospheric-ν alone; ≥5σ in joint atmospheric + J-PARC beam with favorable θ_23 octant). The mass-ordering resolution is matter-effect-driven at DUNE, statistical-power-driven at HK — the two experiments form a discriminating-experiments pair on this observable in the strongest sense: DUNE's baseline gives it a systematic advantage on mass ordering that exposure-scaling cannot close for HK. JUNO at ~53 km from reactor sources provides matter-effect-free complementary determination via vacuum-oscillation interference patterns (a future Step-4.4 sub-PR can author the parallel JUNO experimental-coverage edge, completing a three-way discriminating-experiments cluster on this cell). This edge is recorded as bounds-setting rather than measurement-discriminating: NH vs IH is a binary determination of which mass ordering nature realizes, not a discriminator between candidate-foundational programs that make competing predictions of the ordering. Specific flavor-symmetry models (A4 type-I seesaw with sequential dominance; certain anarchic models; some μ-τ-symmetric models) prefer one ordering or the other under additional assumptions but do not generically produce program-internal predictions admissible under §4 part (2). The bounds-setting framing is therefore the appropriate one, mirroring the Hyper-K edge.

DUNE Collaboration 2020 'DUNE Far Detector TDR Volume II: DUNE Physics' arXiv:2002.03005
Esteban, Gonzalez-Garcia, Maltoni, Schwetz, Zhou (NuFIT 5.2) 2022 JHEP 09:178 — present global-fit NH/IH preference (~2.5σ NH).

Abusleme et al. (JUNO Collaboration) 2025 'Potential to identify neutrino mass ordering with reactor antineutrinos at JUNO' Chin. Phys. C 49:033104, arXiv:2405.18008 — abstract: 'a 3σ median sensitivity to reject the wrong mass ordering hypothesis can be reached with an exposure to approximately 6.5 years × 26.6 GW thermal power.' This supersedes the Yellow Book's earlier 3-4σ at 6-yr estimate and is the canonical current JUNO mass-ordering projection.

edges.description: JUNO's headline physics result is the determination of the neutrino mass ordering via the fine interference pattern between the solar (Δm²_21) and atmospheric (Δm²_31, Δm²_32) oscillation frequencies in the survival probability of reactor antineutrinos at a 52.5-km baseline from the Yangjiang and Taishan reactor complexes. Per the JUNO Collaboration's latest published projection (arXiv:2405.18008, Chin. Phys. C 49:033104), JUNO reaches 3σ median sensitivity at approximately 6.5 years × 26.6 GW thermal exposure — an updated estimate that supersedes the Yellow Book's earlier 3-4σ-at-6-yr value. The cell cell-nu-mass-hierarchy anchors the present empirical state (current NuFIT 6.0 global-fit preference for NH at ~2.5σ when SK-atmospheric data is included, recorded as an upper bound of 2.47σ on the deviation from null ordering). JUNO is the third experimental program added to the discriminating-experiments cluster on this cell (after HK at sub-PR 46 and DUNE at sub-PR 47), and the JUNO mechanism is methodologically ORTHOGONAL to both predecessors: (a) HK uses statistical-power with matter-effect resonance in atmospheric ν over wide-baseline Earth-traversing path-lengths (~100 km to ~13000 km); (b) DUNE uses matter-effect-driven beam-ν oscillations over the 1285-km Fermilab → SURF baseline; (c) JUNO uses quasi-vacuum oscillation interference at 52.5-km baseline, COMPLETELY INDEPENDENT of the CP violating phase δ_CP and the mixing angle θ_23 (Abusleme et al. 2025 abstract). The three-way discriminating-experiments cluster therefore exposes the mass ordering through three independent physical channels — matter-driven beam (DUNE), statistical-power atmospheric (HK), and vacuum-interference reactor (JUNO) — with three independent systematic dependencies. A unanimous result across all three would carry exceptional confidence; a discrepancy would localize the systematic explanation. This edge is recorded as bounds-setting rather than measurement-discriminating, mirroring the HK and DUNE edges on this cell: NH vs IH is a binary determination of which mass ordering nature realizes, not a discriminator between candidate-foundational programs that make competing predictions of the ordering. Specific flavor-symmetry models (A4 type-I seesaw with sequential dominance; certain anarchic models; some μ-τ-symmetric models) prefer one ordering or the other under additional assumptions but do not generically produce program-internal predictions admissible under §4 part (2). The bounds-setting framing is therefore the appropriate one, mirroring the HK and DUNE edges.
citations: Abusleme et al. (JUNO Collaboration) 2025 'Potential to identify neutrino mass ordering with reactor antineutrinos at JUNO' Chin. Phys. C 49:033104, arXiv:2405.18008 — abstract: 'a 3σ median sensitivity to reject the wrong mass ordering hypothesis can be reached with an exposure to approximately 6.5 years × 26.6 GW thermal power.' This supersedes the Yellow Book's earlier 3-4σ at 6-yr estimate and is the canonical current JUNO mass-ordering projection.

Detection efficiency 36.9% ± 4.9% via three-fold coincidence signature (K⁺ pulse + K⁺ → μ⁺ν delayed pulse + μ⁺ → e⁺ν̄ν delayed pulse).

citations: Hisano, Kobayashi, Nagata 2012 'Enhancement of Proton Decay Rates in Supersymmetric SU(5) Grand Unified Models' arXiv:1204.6274 — with heavy sfermions, the SUSY p → ν̄K⁺ lifetime is prolonged into the 10³⁶ yr range.

Ibe, Kobayashi, Nagata 2024 'Proton Lifetime in Minimal Supersymmetric SU(5) with GMSB' arXiv:2409.06239 — messenger-scale-dependent prolongation pushes τ beyond next-generation experimental reach for M_scalar at the heaviest end.

predictions_per_program.description: SUSY SU(5) variants in which Peccei-Quinn symmetry suppresses or split-spectrum sfermions push dim-5 contributions toward 10³⁶ yr lifetimes, beyond JUNO's reach. A null result at JUNO is fully consistent with this class of models. The split-spectrum/PQ branch is therefore the SUSY family that survives JUNO's null verdict (same conclusion as for HK and DUNE — the program-internal prediction is detector-independent per T30).
citations: Hisano, Kobayashi, Nagata 2012 arXiv:1204.6274 (program-internal calculation, heavy-sfermion enhancement of suppression)

edges.description: JUNO's 20-kt liquid-scintillator far detector probes p → ν̄K⁺ through a clean THREE-FOLD COINCIDENCE signature: (1) prompt scintillation pulse from the K⁺ ionization track + neutral hadronic remnant of the bound proton's nuclear wavefunction; (2) delayed pulse from K⁺ → μ⁺ν (BR 63.6%) or K⁺ → π⁺π⁰ (BR 20.7%); (3) further-delayed pulse from μ⁺ → e⁺ν̄ν (lifetime 2.2 μs). Per the JUNO Collaboration's dedicated proton-decay paper (Abusleme et al. 2023 Chin. Phys. C 47:113002, arXiv:2212.08502), the detection efficiency is 36.9% ± 4.9% with background 0.2 ± 0.05(syst) ± 0.2(stat) events after 10 years of data collection, projecting a 90% CL sensitivity τ > 9.6 × 10³³ yr at the 10-year / 200 kt·yr exposure benchmark. The cell cell-pd-Kplus-nubar-SUSY anchors the present empirical state (Super-K 2014 bound τ > 5.9 × 10³³ yr). JUNO is the third experimental program added to the discriminating-experiments cluster on this cell (after HK at sub-PR 46 with τ > 3 × 10³⁴ yr targeted, and DUNE at sub-PR 47 with τ > 1.3 × 10³⁴ yr targeted). The three experiments form a discriminating-experiments cluster across three orthogonal detector technologies: (a) water-Cherenkov (HK) where the K⁺ is below threshold and must be inferred from decay daughters; (b) liquid-argon TPC (DUNE) where the K⁺ ionization track is imaged directly and the channel becomes quasi-background-free; (c) liquid scintillator (JUNO) where the three-fold timing-coincidence signature provides clean per-event identification. JUNO's 90% CL reach (9.6 × 10³³ yr) is the lowest of the three but the systematic profile is independent, and any positive observation in JUNO would carry exceptional confidence through the temporal-coincidence signature. Per-program predictions are taken without modification from the matching HK and DUNE edges per the T30 detector-independence principle: minimal SUSY SU(5) (predicts τ ≤ 1.1 × 10³⁵ yr) and split-spectrum SUSY (predicts 10³⁶±⁰·⁵ yr) give the same lifetime bounds regardless of which detector is measuring. Pati-Salam GUTs render the proton stable in their minimal form and are not represented in the per-program prediction list because no quantitative prediction can be encoded for an infinite lifetime; their incompatibility with any HK/DUNE/JUNO observation is recorded at the description level.
citations: Abusleme et al. (JUNO Collaboration) 2023 'JUNO Sensitivity on Proton Decay p → ν̄K⁺ Searches' Chin. Phys. C 47:113002, arXiv:2212.08502 — abstract: 'The estimated sensitivity based on 200 kton-years exposure is 9.6 × 10³³ years, which is competitive with the current best limits on the proton lifetime in this channel and complements the use of different detection technologies.' Detection efficiency 36.9% ± 4.9% via three-fold coincidence signature (K⁺ pulse + K⁺ → μ⁺ν delayed pulse + μ⁺ → e⁺ν̄ν delayed pulse).
Abusleme et al. (JUNO Collaboration) 2022 'JUNO Physics and Detector' Prog. Part. Nucl. Phys. 123:103927, arXiv:2104.02565 — comprehensive 2022 update

Super-Kamiokande Collaboration (Abe et al.) 2014 'Search for proton decay via p → ν̄K⁺ using 260 kiloton·year data of Super-Kamiokande' Phys. Rev. D 90:072005, arXiv:1408.1195 — current best experimental lower limit τ > 5.9 × 10³³ yr (90% CL); the empirical floor.

Abusleme et al. (JUNO Collaboration) 2022 'Sub-percent precision measurement of neutrino oscillation parameters with JUNO' Chin. Phys. C 46:123001, arXiv:2204.13249 — abstract: 'the Δm²_21 and sin²θ_12 oscillation parameters will be determined to 0.5% precision or better in six years of data collection.' Joint JUNO + TAO analysis.

edges.description: JUNO is the world's leading precision experiment for the solar mixing angle sin²θ_12. The dedicated Sub-percent Precision paper (Abusleme et al. 2022 Chin. Phys. C 46:123001, arXiv:2204.13249) projects sin²θ_12 will be determined to ≤0.5% relative precision (≈0.0015 absolute on the current best-fit value 0.307) in six years of joint JUNO + TAO data collection, improving on the current NuFIT 6.0 global-fit uncertainty (~4% relative; ±0.012 absolute) by roughly an order of magnitude. The cell cell-nu-theta12-solar already anchors the present value (NuFIT 6.0: 0.307 +0.012 −0.011 at 1σ). This edge is recorded as bounds-setting rather than measurement-discriminating because sin²θ_12 is a Standard-Model PMNS-matrix parameter being measured to higher precision, not a discriminator between candidate-foundational programs that make competing single-valued predictions of its value. BSM frameworks (family symmetries A4, S4, anarchic models, certain seesaw mechanisms) generally predict relations among the PMNS parameters rather than the single sin²θ_12 value; admissibility under §4 part (2) would require program-internal calculations of the specific point value, which the leading frameworks do not generally offer at the precision JUNO will probe. The bounds-setting framing is therefore the appropriate one. JUNO is the only experimental program currently adding a precision-measurement probe to this cell; no discriminating-experiments cluster forms here. The sensitivity is recorded as a dimensionless precision target — central value 0.307 (matching the cell's existing dimensionless encoding) with achievable projected precision ±0.0015 (0.5% relative) — with no direction-of-bound encoded since this is a precision target rather than a discovery floor.

edges.description: JUNO is the world's leading precision experiment for the solar mass-squared difference Δm²_21. The dedicated Sub-percent Precision paper (Abusleme et al. 2022 Chin. Phys. C 46:123001, arXiv:2204.13249) projects Δm²_21 will be determined to ≤0.5% relative precision (≈3.75 × 10⁻⁷ absolute on the current best-fit value 7.49 × 10⁻⁵ eV²) in six years of joint JUNO + TAO data collection, improving on the current NuFIT 6.0 global-fit uncertainty (~2.5% relative; ±1.9 × 10⁻⁶ absolute) by roughly an order of magnitude. The cell cell-nu-Dm21-squared already anchors the present value (NuFIT 6.0: 7.49 +0.19 −0.19 × 10⁻⁵ eV² at 1σ). This edge is recorded as bounds-setting rather than measurement-discriminating because Δm²_21 is a Standard-Model PMNS-matrix-related parameter being measured to higher precision, not a discriminator between candidate-foundational programs. The bounds-setting framing is the appropriate one for the same reasons as edge-rs-juno-nu-theta12-solar. The sensitivity encoding follows T31 (sub-PR 48 mint): kind=mass + units="eV²" mirrors the cell's T17 encoding convention (kind=mass for squared-dimensional mass quantities with units carrying the dimensional state); the value records the current central value (7.49 × 10⁻⁵), uncertainty records the achievable projected precision (3.75 × 10⁻⁷ eV² = 0.5% relative), with no direction-of-bound encoded since this is a precision target rather than a discovery floor.

Abbott et al. (LIGO Scientific Collaboration, Virgo, KAGRA) 2020 'Prospects for observing and localizing gravitational-wave transients with Advanced LIGO, Advanced Virgo and KAGRA' Living Rev. Relativ. 23:3 — Table 2 lists BBH detection ranges; the canonical 30+30 M_sun nonspinning binary has detection range ≈ 2.6 Gpc at the A+/O5 design sensitivity (90% confidence sky-averaged inclination). The horizon (face-on optimally-oriented) is ≈ 2.3 × detection range.

Abbott et al. (LIGO Scientific Collaboration, Virgo) 2023 'GWTC-3: Compact Binary Coalescences Observed by LIGO and Virgo During the Second Part of the Third Observing Run' Phys. Rev. X 13:041039, arXiv:2111.03606 — 90 confident compact-binary detections through O3 (76 BBH-dominated; redshifts up to z≈0.8); the realized catalog as of sub-PR 49 authoring.

edges.description: The LIGO–Virgo–KAGRA network is the experimental program that realizes cell-compact-stellar-BH-GW. As of GWTC-3 (Abbott et al. 2023 Phys. Rev. X 13:041039) the catalog contains 90 confident compact-binary detections, of which the great majority are stellar-mass BBH mergers covering 5–80 M_sun primary masses out to z≈0.8. A+/O5 design sensitivity extends BBH detection range for the canonical 30+30 M_sun source to ≈2.6 Gpc; the projected next-generation upgrades (Cosmic Explorer 40-km arms, Einstein Telescope underground triangle) reach z>10 for the same source, effectively the full observable cosmological volume. The cell content is therefore actively being populated and refined by ground-network observations on a year-by-year basis. This edge is bounds-setting rather than measurement-discriminating because the catalog-enumeration cell does not directly discriminate between candidate-foundational programs: candidate-program discrimination via stellar-mass-BBH mergers happens through (i) parameterized-post-Einsteinian waveform-deviation tests recorded on separate test-of-GR observables and (ii) population-level mass–spin distribution analyses recorded at the cell-population level; both are downstream of merger-catalog enumeration itself. The discriminating-experiments pair structure for this cell is created by LISA's complementary early-inspiral edge (edge-rs-lisa-stellar-BH-early-inspiral): ground-network resolves the final inspiral–merger–ringdown phase at 10 Hz – 1 kHz; LISA resolves the early-inspiral phase at 10⁻³ – 10⁻¹ Hz, ≈10⁴ yr before LIGO-band coalescence, on the same source population. Sensitivity reach: detection-horizon upper bound ~2.6 Gpc (the distance to which the detector can probe a canonical source).

edges.description: BNS inspirals observed by the ground-network constrain the dense-nuclear-matter equation of state via the binary tidal-deformability parameter Λ̃, which enters the post-Newtonian inspiral waveform at fifth post-Newtonian order. GW170817 (Abbott et al. 2017 PRL 119:161101) provided the first measurement, setting Λ̃(M_chirp≈1.186 M_sun) < 720 at 90% CL low-spin prior. With ≈1–10 confident BNS detections expected per year at A+/O5 design sensitivity (Abbott et al. 2020 LRR 23:3), the combined-event reach on the canonical 1.4 M_sun NS tidal deformability is projected at Λ₁.₄ ≲ 400 (90% CL). The three per-program competing-prediction entries record the predicted Λ₁.₄ for stiff, intermediate, and soft EOS families respectively, each citing the program-internal nuclear-physics calculation. The cell cell-compact-NS-massive anchors the observational constraint M_max > 2 M_sun from pulsar timing (PSR J0740+6620, PSR J0348+0432); GW tidal-deformability bounds add complementary EOS information on top of the M_max constraint. Together they jointly discriminate between stiff/intermediate/soft EOS classes. The discriminating-resolves structure here is between EOS-class programs (a microphysical-program family), not between candidate-foundational programs in the cosmology-or-particle-physics sense; this matches sub-PR 48's precedent of using descriptive program strings rather than node ids for the program field. Sensitivity reach: dimensionless upper bound Λ̃ ≲ 400 (projected A+/O5 90% CL upper bound; Λ̃ is dimensionless).

Abbott et al. (LIGO Scientific, Virgo, KAGRA) 2020 'Prospects for observing and localizing GW transients with Advanced LIGO, Advanced Virgo and KAGRA' Living Rev. Relativ. 23:3, Table 2 — A+/O5 BNS detection range ≈ 330 Mpc (90% confidence sky-averaged inclination). For post-merger HMNS detection the relevant sensitivity is in the 2–4 kHz band, where Advanced LIGO design has limited sensitivity and only the loudest nearby (D ≲ 50 Mpc) BNS would yield a detectable post-merger waveform; CE/ET design specifically targets this band with order-of-magnitude strain improvement.

Branchesi et al. 2023 'Science with the Einstein Telescope: a comparison of different designs' JCAP 07:068, arXiv:2303.15923 — ET post-merger sensitivity projections for 2–4 kHz HMNS-oscillation detection at Mpc–Gpc scales.

edges.description: Hypermassive neutron-star (HMNS) post-merger oscillations following BNS coalescence have not yet been observed: GW170817 was detected up to merger but the high-frequency (2–4 kHz) post-merger phase was below the Advanced LIGO O2 noise floor. The post-merger waveform carries the f_peak (dominant post-merger frequency) and f_2 (secondary modes) which encode the supranuclear EOS at densities above the inspiral-tidal-deformability regime; different EOS classes predict f_peak in the 2–4 kHz range. The A+/O5 design BNS range of ≈330 Mpc enables only nearby loud BNS detections for post-merger study; Cosmic Explorer and Einstein Telescope, with order-of-magnitude strain-sensitivity improvement in the 2–4 kHz band, are specifically designed to resolve post-merger oscillations at routine SNR. This edge is bounds-setting rather than measurement-discriminating because the per-program HMNS post-merger frequency predictions overlap heavily within EOS uncertainty (different EOS classes predict 2.5–3.5 kHz f_peak ranges that broadly overlap), and the cleanest current discriminator is the inspiral tidal-deformability Λ̃ already recorded on edge-rs-gw-ground-network-NS-EOS. The post-merger edge complements but does not duplicate that information: post-merger spectroscopy probes the EOS at higher densities (≳2× nuclear saturation) than inspiral tidal-deformability. CE/ET-era detections will populate this cell with confirmed-individual entries beyond the current candidate-individual status. Sensitivity reach: detection-horizon upper bound ~330 Mpc — the A+/O5 BNS detection range as the relevant distance scale for nearby loud events accessible to post-merger study.

edges.description: LISA opens the 10³–10⁵ M_sun IMBH window — currently the largest empty zone in the observed BH mass distribution — by directly detecting IMBH-IMBH mergers across cosmic time. Its 10⁻³–10⁻¹ Hz band is optimally tuned to the merger frequencies of canonical 10⁴ M_sun pairs (f_merger ≈ 0.01–0.1 Hz). The detection horizon for canonical IMBH-IMBH mergers extends to z≈15–20, comoving distance ≈30 Gpc — effectively the full cosmologically-accessible volume. The per-program competing-prediction list records the discriminating IMBH-merger-rate predictions for the three leading seed-formation scenarios: light-seed (Pop III remnant) predicts ≈20/yr dominated by high-z events; heavy-seed (direct collapse) predicts ≈3/yr at intermediate z; hierarchical-cluster predicts ≈5/yr at low z. LISA's first ≈2 years of data will provide the cleanest empirical test of SMBH seed origin. Sensitivity reach: detection-horizon upper bound ~30 Gpc (LISA can probe IMBH–IMBH mergers up to comoving distances of ≈30 Gpc, equivalent to z ≈ 15).

edges.description: LISA directly observes SMBH-SMBH mergers across cosmic time — the dynamical-merger counterpart to the steady-state SMBH-galactic-center observations (Sgr A* dynamics, EHT imaging, reverberation-mapping for nearby AGN) that anchor this cell. The 10⁻³–10⁻¹ Hz LISA band is optimally tuned to merger frequencies of 10⁵–10⁷ M_sun SMBHB pairs; detection horizon extends to z≈20 (≈40 Gpc comoving) at SNR > 100. The per-program competing-prediction list records discriminating SMBHB-merger-rate predictions for the two leading seed-formation scenarios: light-seed (Pop III remnant) predicts ≈30/yr at high z with characteristic M_total ≈ 10⁵–10⁶ M_sun; heavy-seed (direct collapse) predicts ≈10/yr at intermediate z with characteristic M_total ≈ 10⁶–10⁷ M_sun. The hierarchical-cluster channel that contributes to IMBH-gap mergers does not significantly populate the SMBH-galactic-center merger budget at LISA-detectable mass scales, so it is not included here. LISA's ≈4-year mission yields ≈40–200 SMBHB merger detections depending on scenario, providing the statistically powerful population-level seed-formation test. This edge does not form a discriminating-resolves-pair with any other Step-4.4 source since SMBHB mergers are uniquely accessible to LISA in the 10⁻³–10⁻¹ Hz band; ground-network and pulsar-timing-array bands probe orthogonal mass ranges. Sensitivity reach: detection-horizon upper bound ~40 Gpc (LISA reach to z ≈ 20 for canonical 10⁶ M_sun SMBHB).

edges.description:
LISA observes the EARLY-INSPIRAL phase of stellar-mass binary black holes ≈10⁴ yr before their coalescence in the LIGO/Virgo/KAGRA band: the same source population, observed at a different epoch and frequency band. Canonical 30+30 M_sun GW150914-like BBH systems sweep through LISA's 10⁻³–10⁻¹ Hz band over 10⁴–10⁵ yr before reaching ground-band coalescence; the LISA-band horizon for SNR≥8 detection is ≈3 Gpc, well within the ground-network catalog's reach (≈2.6 Gpc detection range at A+/O5). LISA early-inspiral detections provide (i) source-localization advance warning (sky-position to ≈1 deg² weeks before coalescence) enabling targeted electromagnetic-counterpart searches and ground-network sensitivity tuning, (ii) independent measurement of the inspiral phase coefficients for tests of GR (parameterized post-Einsteinian phase coefficients constrained at lower-PN-order than achievable from ground alone), and (iii) population-level orbital-eccentricity measurements that discriminate astrophysical formation channels (isolated-binary-evolution predicts circular orbits; dynamical-formation in dense clusters and AGN-disk channels predict residual eccentricity at LISA-band orbital separations).

THIS EDGE CREATES THE FIRST GW-DOMAIN DISCRIMINATING-RESOLVES-PAIR with edge-rs-gw-ground-network-stellar-BH-GW: the same target cell (cell-compact-stellar-BH-GW), two source experimental programs (ground-network and LISA), two orthogonal frequency bands (10 Hz – 1 kHz vs 10⁻³ – 10⁻¹ Hz), two orthogonal epochs of the same source's gravitational-wave signal (final inspiral–merger–ringdown vs early inspiral ≈10⁴ yr earlier). The structural discriminator is the frequency-band + epoch complementarity, not a per-program prediction difference; therefore this edge is bounds-setting rather than measurement-discriminating at the experimental-coverage level. Astrophysical-formation-channel discrimination via eccentricity or population statistics happens at the cell-population level, downstream of the catalog-enumeration. This is also the project's FIRST same-target discriminating-resolves-pair across two physics-domain-distinct experimental programs (ground-network and LISA share the multiband-GW-astronomy programme but are operationally and methodologically distinct), extending the discriminating-resolves-pair pattern (introduced for HK↔DUNE at sub-PR 47, deepened to three-way for HK↔DUNE↔JUNO at sub-PR 48) into the gravitational-wave physics domain. Sensitivity reach: detection-horizon upper bound ~3 Gpc.

DESI Collaboration (Levi et al.) 2013 'The DESI Experiment, a whitepaper for Snowmass 2013' arXiv:1308.0847, §2 — the DESI Science Requirements anchor a Stage-IV Dark-Energy Task Force figure-of-merit FoM = 1/[σ(w_p) × σ(w_a)] ≳ 70 with a CMB prior; the design exposure was specified to detect deviations from a cosmological constant (w_0 = -1, w_a = 0) at ≥5σ if dark energy is dynamical at the (w_0+0.5 w_a, w_a) shifts the (w_0, w_a) phase plane permits.

Adame et al. (DESI Collaboration) 2024 'DESI 2024 VI: Cosmological Constraints from the Measurements of Baryon Acoustic Oscillations' JCAP 02:021 (2025) arXiv:2404.03002, §8 — DESI Year-1 BAO combined with CMB (Planck 2018) and SN Ia (Pantheon+ / Union3 / DES-SN5YR) reports a deviation from ΛCDM of 2.6σ (Pantheon+) / 3.5σ (Union3) / 3.9σ (DES-SN5YR) under the w_0 w_a CDM parameterization; the full 5-year DESI dataset is projected to reach ≥5σ discrimination or definitive consistency with ΛCDM at the design FoM precision.

Aghanim et al. (Planck Collaboration) 2020 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters' Astron. Astrophys. 641:A6 arXiv:1807.06209 — the CMB prior anchoring the DESI design FoM, with ΛCDM consistency at Planck-precision base-ΛCDM in the absence of late-time data.

edges.description:
DESI's flagship cosmological measurement — sub-percent BAO standard-ruler distances across 0.1 < z < 3.5, combined with CMB priors and Type-Ia-SN luminosity distances — directly constrains the dark-energy equation-of-state (w_0, w_a) and through it, the cosmological-constant problem (cc-frontier). The DESI Year-1 BAO release (Adame et al. 2024, arXiv:2404.03002) reports a 2.6–3.9σ preference (depending on SN dataset) for an evolving dark-energy equation-of-state over a cosmological constant when BAO + CMB + SN are combined under the w_0 w_a CDM parameterization. The full 5-year DESI dataset is designed to either confirm an evolving-DE detection at ≥5σ or close out the current tension as a statistical fluctuation — either outcome materially advances the cosmological-constant frontier's empirical content. Sensitivity reach: 5σ lower bound on (w_0, w_a) deviation from ΛCDM under CMB + SN-combined constraint — the experiment's design-exposure discovery floor.

This is the first experimental-coverage edge in the project to target an open frontier directly rather than attaching to a specific cell — appropriate when the experimental program's most direct structural payoff is the frontier's empirical content rather than a specific cell's value. The v18 spec §3 Rule 32 explicitly permits open-frontier and totality-approach ids as targets for such edges; this sub-PR is the first instance.

This is a bounds-setting probe rather than measurement-discriminating because the candidate-foundational dark-energy programs DESI's measurement bears on (ΛCDM with w ≡ -1, quintessence-class models with thawing or freezing potentials, early-dark-energy proposals, modified-gravity Horndeski/DHOST classes) do not, in their currently-published forms, supply point-predictive program-internal calculations of (w_0, w_a) that satisfy §4 admissibility test part (2). ΛCDM predicts (w_0, w_a) = (-1, 0) exactly; the evolving-DE programs are hypothesis classes with parameter freedom rather than point predictors. The discriminating power lives in DESI's sensitivity to deviations from the cosmological-constant point, not in pre-computed program-internal predicted values. A future update may record specific point-predictive quintessence-potential benchmarks for the competing programs (e.g., axion-quintessence with cosmologically-constrained potential parameters, or specific early-DE realizations with fixed pre-recombination physics) if the firewall test admits per-program predictions citing program-internal calculation papers.

Adame et al. (DESI Collaboration) 2024 'DESI 2024 III: Baryon Acoustic Oscillations from Galaxies and Quasars' arXiv:2404.03000, §6 — DESI Year-1 reports BAO standard-ruler distance measurements (D_M/r_d and D_H/r_d) across seven redshift bins at 0.5–1.4% precision per bin; the implied constraint on r_d via the CMB-anchored inverse-distance-ladder combination is consistent with the Planck 2018 base-ΛCDM value 147.09 ± 0.26 Mpc, with DESI Year-5 + CMB combination projected to reach an r_d-equivalent precision of ≈0.5 Mpc at the design exposure.

DESI Collaboration 2016 'The DESI Experiment Part I' arXiv:1611.00036, §2 — 5-year design forecast for the BAO standard-ruler measurement program: combined isotropic and anisotropic distance precision at <0.3% over the survey, sufficient to anchor the sound horizon at drag epoch to ≈30 times the FoM achievable from BOSS.

edges.description:
DESI is the unique current source for the BAO sound-horizon-at-drag-epoch standard-ruler measurement at the precision recorded in cell-cosm-BAO-sound-horizon-DESI. The cell anchors r_d ≈ 147.09 ± 0.26 Mpc as the Planck-CMB-derived value; DESI's BAO measurements provide the late-time complementary anchor that, in CMB+BAO combination, tightens r_d's effective precision below 0.5 Mpc at the full 5-year design exposure. The Year-1 BAO release (Adame et al. 2024, arXiv:2404.03000) already delivers D_M/r_d and D_H/r_d at 0.5–1.4% per redshift bin, with the seven-bin coverage (z = 0.295, 0.510, 0.706, 0.930, 1.317, 1.491, 2.330) spanning the BAO standard-ruler from low-z galaxies through high-z Lyman-α forest. Sensitivity reach: precision-improvement target on r_d, with central value 147.09 Mpc (mirroring the cell's existing value) and projected DESI Y5 + CMB combined precision ±0.5 Mpc; no direction-of-bound recorded since the uncertainty is non-null (the precision interval is two-sided around the central value).

This is a bounds-setting probe rather than measurement-discriminating: DESI's role on this cell is to deliver the measurement at sub-percent precision — a precision-improvement target, not a discriminating measurement between point-predictive candidate-foundational programs. The BAO sound horizon at drag epoch is a derived quantity from pre-recombination physics (baryon/photon ratio, matter/radiation equality, recombination history) that all standard cosmological models share within Planck-precision; no candidate-foundational programs predict r_d at distinguishably different values that DESI could discriminate at this surface. Future cosmological-models extensions (e.g., extra-radiation N_eff scenarios, early-dark-energy modifications to recombination history) that DO shift r_d in predictable ways could be recorded as per-program competing predictions in a downstream sub-PR if they satisfy §4 part (2) admissibility.

(DESI Collaboration) 2024 'DESI 2024 VI: Cosmological Constraints' JCAP 02:021 (2025) arXiv:2404.03002, §6 — DESI Year-1 BAO + CMB (Planck) base-ΛCDM combination tightens H_0 to 68.52 ± 0.62 km/s/Mpc; the implied Hubble-time precision is ≈0.13 Gyr at Y1. The 5-year design forecast (DESI 2016, arXiv:1611.00036, Table 2.3) projects an H_0 precision of ≈0.3 km/s/Mpc from BAO+CMB, equivalent to a Hubble-time uncertainty ≈0.07 Gyr at H_0 ≈ 67.5 km/s/Mpc.

DESI Collaboration 2016 'The DESI Experiment Part I' arXiv:1611.00036, §2 — the design exposure was specified to anchor the BAO+CMB inverse-distance-ladder H_0 at sub-half-percent precision, the empirical floor for the 'low-side' anchor of the Hubble tension against SH0ES-class local distance-ladder measurements.

edges.description:
DESI's BAO measurements, combined with CMB priors (Planck 2018 or post-Planck successors such as ACT DR6 and Simons Observatory), tighten the inverse-distance-ladder Hubble-constant measurement that the cell cell-cosm-H0-CMB-Planck anchors. The cell records the Planck-2018 base-ΛCDM H_0 = 67.4 ± 0.5 km/s/Mpc (equivalently Hubble time t_H = 14.52 ± 0.11 Gyr); DESI's 5-year design exposure projects the combined BAO+CMB H_0 precision to ≈0.3 km/s/Mpc (Hubble-time precision ≈0.07 Gyr), establishing the precision floor for the 'low-side' anchor of the Hubble tension. The DESI Year-1 BAO + Planck CMB combination already returns H_0 = 68.52 ± 0.62 km/s/Mpc (Adame et al. 2024 §6), well within the Planck-only 1σ envelope and remaining in ≈5σ tension with SH0ES's local distance-ladder H_0 = 73.04 ± 1.04 km/s/Mpc (Riess et al. 2022 ApJL 934:L7). DESI tightens the CMB-anchored side; whether the tension is genuinely cosmological or driven by yet-unaccounted-for systematics on either side requires this precision floor. Sensitivity reach: precision-improvement target on the Hubble time, with central value 14.52 Gyr (mirroring the cell's existing value) and DESI Y5 + CMB projected precision ±0.07 Gyr at H_0 ≈ 67.5 km/s/Mpc; no direction-of-bound recorded since the uncertainty is non-null per Rule 34.

This is a bounds-setting probe rather than measurement-discriminating: DESI's contribution to this cell is precision-improvement on the CMB-anchored H_0, not a discriminating measurement between point-predictive candidate-foundational programs. ΛCDM predicts H_0 ≈ 67.5 km/s/Mpc from CMB+BAO at Planck-precision; the candidate Hubble-tension resolutions (early-dark-energy raising the CMB-inferred H_0 by 5–10%, varying-fundamental-constants scenarios, modified pre-recombination physics) are hypothesis classes with parameter freedom rather than point predictors. DESI's role is to determine the CMB-side anchor as precisely as possible; the discriminating physics between candidate resolutions of the Hubble tension lives on a different observational axis (e.g., late-time distance ladders, gravitational-wave standard sirens, time-delay cosmography) where the candidate programs DO supply point-predictive H_0 calculations admissible under §4 part (2). A future sub-PR pairing DESI with Rubin LSST or SH0ES via per-program competing predictions for specific early-DE benchmarks could populate the discriminating-experiments structure across the Hubble-tension cluster.

Sikivie 1983 'Experimental Tests of the Invisible Axion' Phys. Rev. Lett. 51:1415 — original derivation of the haloscope concept: a galactic-halo axion converts to a microwave photon in a high-Q resonant cavity immersed in a strong static magnetic field, with conversion power proportional to g_aγγ² × B² × V × Q × ρ_DM; the program's foundational design paper.

Bartram et al. (ADMX Collaboration) 2021 'Search for invisible axion dark matter in the 3.3–4.2 μeV mass range' Phys. Rev. Lett. 127:261803 (arXiv:2110.06096) §3 — ADMX Run 1B achieved DFSZ-coupling sensitivity g_aγγ ≲ 4 × 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹ (log10 ≈ −15.4) over the 3.3–4.2 μeV band at 90% CL; the program's currently-deepest published QCD-axion-coupling-floor exclusion in the few-μeV band, with the broader few-μeV-to-meV envelope reaching g_aγγ ≲ 10⁻¹⁵ GeV⁻¹ across the program's active mass coverage.

Stern et al. (ADMX Collaboration) 2015 'Cavity design for high-mass axion dark matter searches' Rev. Sci. Instrum. 86:123305 — ADMX Gen-2 design report establishing the few-μeV scan plan at DFSZ sensitivity and the resonator-array roadmap to higher masses through the late 2020s, the design-document anchor for the program's currently-running operations.

edges.description: Axion-haloscope-network's structural bear-down on the strong-CP-problem frontier proceeds via direct detection of the QCD axion postulated by the Peccei-Quinn mechanism. The Peccei-Quinn-axion candidate-targeting entry on strong-cp-problem (discharge_status: gestural-to-partial) explicitly names ADMX, ABRACADABRA, and IAXO as the experimental discharge channel; this edge formalizes the apparatus→structural-claim relation. Sensitivity reach: discovery-floor upper bound g_aγγ ≲ 10⁻¹⁵ GeV⁻¹ — the broad few-μeV-band haloscope program reach (ADMX Run 1B Bartram et al. 2021 published DFSZ exclusion ≈ 3.5 × 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹ over 3.3–4.2 μeV; HAYSTAC, MADMAX, ABRACADABRA, and DMRadio extend coverage to higher and lower m_a; the value −15 is a robust rounded reach across the program's active mass-coupling envelope). This edge is recorded as bounds-setting rather than measurement-discriminating, following the desi-cc-frontier precedent (sub-PR 50): the candidate axion models (KSVZ, DFSZ, generalized invisible-axion variants per Di Luzio et al. 2020) predict relations between f_a, m_a, and g_aγγ rather than single point values discriminable at fixed m_a in the §4 part (2) sense; no per-program point predictions are recorded — the §4-admissible posture, mirroring the (w_0, w_a) hypothesis-class structure of evolving-DE alternatives at desi-cc-frontier. The structural payoff if the haloscope program detects an axion signal is documented downstream in the peccei-quinn-axion if_real_implies entry on strong-cp-problem and the qcd-axion-as-dark-matter if_real_implies entry on dark-matter (both Phase B). Second experimental-coverage edge in the project to target an open frontier directly (after edge-rs-desi-cc-frontier in sub-PR 50).

citations: Bartram et al. (ADMX Collaboration) 2021 'Search for invisible axion dark matter in the 3.3–4.2 μeV mass range' Phys. Rev. Lett. 127:261803 (arXiv:2110.06096) §3 — ADMX Run 1B achieved DFSZ-coupling sensitivity g_aγγ ≈ 3.5–4.5 × 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹ over the 3.3–4.2 μeV mass range at 90% CL exclusion; log10(g_aγγ) ≈ −15.4 is the published floor at the DFSZ benchmark in the canonical QCD-axion-as-DM mass band.

Du et al. (ADMX Collaboration) 2018 'Search for Invisible Axion Dark Matter with the Axion Dark Matter Experiment' Phys. Rev. Lett. 120:151301 (arXiv:1804.05750) — ADMX Gen-2 first DFSZ-sensitivity result at 2.66–2.81 μeV, the first haloscope result reaching the DFSZ benchmark for the QCD axion as 100% of dark matter; the foundational empirical anchor for the cell's realized_examples list.

Sikivie 1983 'Experimental Tests of the Invisible Axion' Phys. Rev. Lett. 51:1415 — the haloscope-concept derivation linking g_aγγ to f_a via g_aγγ = (α / 2π f_a) × (E/N − 1.92), with model-dependent E/N (KSVZ: 0; DFSZ: 8/3); the m_a–g_aγγ relation that anchors the cell's structural content and pegs the DFSZ benchmark coupling at the few-μeV mass band.

Preskill, Wise, Wilczek 1983 'Cosmology of the Invisible Axion' Phys. Lett. B 120:127 — misalignment-mechanism relic abundance Ω_a h² ≈ 0.01 θ²_mis (f_a / 10¹¹ GeV)¹¹∕2⁶⁶, the canonical QCD-axion-as-DM mechanism whose validity is what the cell's existing energy_scale qs (f_a ~ 10¹¹ GeV) records and what this resolves edge would discharge via direct detection in the haloscope band.

edges.description: Axion-haloscope-network directly targets the canonical QCD-axion-as-dark-matter cell. The cell's realized_examples already cites ADMX, HAYSTAC, ABRACADABRA, and DMRadio as the apparatus inventory; this edge formalizes the empirical discharge of the cell's structural content (Peccei-Quinn QCD axion at f_a ~ 10¹¹ GeV producing the observed Ω_DM ≈ 0.26 via misalignment, with m_a ≈ 6 μeV via m_a × f_a ≈ √(m_u m_d) Λ_QCD²). Sensitivity reach: discovery-floor upper bound g_aγγ ≲ 4 × 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹ (equivalently log₁₀(g_aγγ /GeV⁻¹) ≲ −15.4), the ADMX Run 1B Bartram et al. 2021 published DFSZ-benchmark exclusion at 90% CL in the 3.3–4.2 μeV band. The cell's existing energy_scale qs (f_a ~ 10¹¹ GeV) places canonical QCD-axion-DM mass squarely in the ADMX/HAYSTAC mass coverage. This edge is recorded as bounds-setting rather than measurement-discriminating: while DFSZ (E/N = 8/3) and KSVZ (E/N = 0) predict distinct g_aγγ values at fixed m_a, neither is a point predictor in the §4 part (2) sense — both are model classes with implementation-dependent fermion content (Di Luzio et al. 2020) producing benchmark coupling bands; haloscope sensitivity excludes the entire DFSZ/KSVZ band over its mass coverage rather than discriminating two competing point predictions at a single m_a.

citations: Armengaud et al. (IAXO Collaboration) 2019 'Physics potential of the International Axion Observatory (IAXO)' JCAP 06:047 (arXiv:1904.09155) §3–4 — IAXO is the next-generation helioscope with design sensitivity to g_aγγ ≈ 6 × 10⁻¹² GeV⁻¹ at low m_a (helioscope channel) and supporting RADES/IAXO-RF haloscope cavities probing g_aγγ ≈ 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹ in the few-100 μeV mass region; the IAXO physics paper anchors the network's wider m_a coverage.

Brun et al. (MADMAX Collaboration) 2019 'A new experimental approach to probe QCD axion dark matter in the mass range above 40 μeV' Eur. Phys. J. C 79:186 (arXiv:1901.07401) §2 — MADMAX dielectric-haloscope design report: m_a coverage 40–400 μeV at DFSZ-band sensitivity g_aγγ ≈ 1–6 × 10⁻¹⁵ GeV⁻¹ across the band, extending haloscope coverage to high-mass QCD axions and intermediate-mass ALPs.

Salemi et al. (ABRACADABRA Collaboration) 2021 'Search for low-mass axion dark matter with ABRACADABRA-10cm' Phys. Rev. Lett. 127:081801 (arXiv:2102.06722) — ABRACADABRA broadband lumped-element approach: m_a coverage 0.31–8.3 neV (sub-μeV) with published g_aγγ sensitivity ≈ 3 × 10⁻¹⁰ GeV⁻¹ in the 10-cm prototype, and successor DMRadio-50L / DMRadio-m³ designs targeting g_aγγ ≈ 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹ in the same low-mass band.

Bähre et al. (ALPS-II Collaboration) 2013 'Any light particle search II — Technical Design Report' JINST 8:T09001 (arXiv:1302.5647) §2 — ALPS-II light-shining-through-walls design: m_a coverage neV–meV via photon regeneration, with first-physics-run-2023 sensitivity g_aγγ ≈ 2 × 10⁻¹¹ GeV⁻¹ across the low-mass ALP region and successor LSW programs (STAX, JURA) targeting deeper coverage.

Arvanitaki, Dimopoulos, Dubovsky, Kaloper, March-Russell 2010 'String Axiverse' Phys. Rev. D 81:123530 (arXiv:0905.4720) — theoretical foundation for the ALP parameter space the cell organizes: string compactifications generically produce many pseudoscalars with masses log-distributed over 30+ decades, motivating the wide-mass haloscope-network coverage.

edges.description: Axion-haloscope-network targets the generic-ALP cell — pseudoscalars in the wider m_a–g_aγγ parameter space that share haloscope phenomenology with the QCD axion but are not constrained to the KSVZ/DFSZ benchmark lines and need not solve strong-CP. Coverage spans neV to meV via complementary apparatus: ABRACADABRA / DMRadio broadband lumped-element approaches (m_a < 1 μeV), ADMX / HAYSTAC microwave cavities (μeV–tens of μeV), MADMAX dielectric haloscopes (40–400 μeV), and ALPS-II / IAXO LSW + helioscope channels (broad m_a coverage at lower g_aγγ sensitivity). Sensitivity reach: discovery-floor upper bound g_aγγ ≲ 10⁻¹⁶ GeV⁻¹ (equivalently log₁₀(g_aγγ /GeV⁻¹) ≲ −16), mirroring the cell's existing coupling-strength bound as the program-wide forward-looking sensitivity floor combining MADMAX, IAXO/RADES, and DMRadio design reaches across the ALP-haloscope envelope. This is a bounds-setting probe rather than measurement-discriminating: ALPs are explicitly hypothesis-class candidates (m_a and g_aγγ unlinked, parameter space broader than the QCD-axion line); no candidate-foundational program in the dataset commits to a point prediction in the (m_a, g_aγγ) plane that haloscope sensitivity could discriminate at the §4 part (2) admissibility bar. The string-axiverse cell (cell-dm-predicted-string-axiverse) provides theoretical motivation for the broad ALP parameter space but does not commit to specific point predictions; no per-program point predictions are recorded — the §4-admissible posture for this edge.

citations: Abel et al. (PSI nEDM Collaboration) 2020 'Measurement of the Permanent Electric Dipole Moment of the Neutron' Phys Rev Lett 124:081803 — the program's currently-deepest published bound |d_n| < 1.8 × 10⁻²⁶ e·cm at 90% CL, obtained with the Ramsey-resonance ultracold-neutron apparatus at PSI; this paper is the design-anchor citation for the strong-cp-problem frontier's existing quantitative_scale (|θ_QCD| < 10⁻¹⁰).

Ayres et al. (n2EDM Collaboration) 2021 'The design of the n2EDM experiment' Eur Phys J C 81:512 §2 — next-generation n2EDM successor apparatus at PSI commissioned with target sensitivity |d_n| ≲ 10⁻²⁷ e·cm at 90% CL across the program's projected operating period, the design-document anchor for the program's currently-running deeper-bound trajectory.

Pendlebury et al. 2015 'Revised experimental upper limit on the electric dipole moment of the neutron' Phys Rev D 92:092003 — the immediate predecessor RAL/Sussex/ILL nEDM result (re-analyzed 2015) at |d_n| < 3.0 × 10⁻²⁶ e·cm 90% CL, establishing the apparatus-method continuity that the PSI nEDM and n2EDM programs continue.

Pospelov-Ritz 2005 'Electric dipole moments as probes of new physics' Annals Phys 318:119 — comprehensive review establishing the EDM-method's role as the leading low-energy probe of fundamental CP violation, including the Crewther-DiVecchia-Veneziano-Witten translation from |d_n| bound to θ̄_QCD constraint that underlies the strong-cp-problem frontier discharge.

edges.description:
